<a id="customer-support-policy"></a>
## Customer Support Policy

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/customer-support-policy_

### Table of Contents

- Overview
- Contact support
- How CartonCloud customer support works
- Free online support fair use policy
- Before you contact support

### Overview

- For customer support, use the Knowledge Base to find the answer immediately or if you need further help with CartonCloud, contact our Service Desk team.
- Free online customer support for common or basic queries is part of your CartonCloud subscription.
- This is subject to the fair use policy (below) so we can support as many customers as possible, as effectively as possible.
- Paid support time with our team is available for more complex and detailed queries specific to your account.
- You can contact us in the same way for both free and paid support (below). We will always let you know if a paid support call is required.
- For urgent issues, we offer a call back service. Simply call, leave a voicemail and we will get back to you ASAP.

This applies to support queries related to both the CartonCloud software and integrations to the CartonCloud software that have been created or managed by the CartonCloud team.

### Contact support

- Click New Ticket .
- Enter your Email address
- Enter the Subject of the issue or query.
- Describe the issue or query you are experiencing in the Ticket Description box.
- Upload a relevant file if necessary.
- Click Submit.

Alternatively, simply send an email to support@cartoncloud.com.au and provide as much relevant information as possible. [Here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-write-a-cartoncloud-support-case) is a guide for providing useful information that will give our team the best chance of getting to a resolution quickly.

### How CartonCloud customer support works

- CartonCloud supports users in several ways, including extensive support articles, tutorial videos and online support.
- Search the Knowledge Base for the information you need instantly without having to wait for a response from our team.
- If you can't find an article that answers your question, lodge a ticket via CartonCloud Help here .
- CartonCloud provides free online support to all subscribers, subject to our fair use policy. Any user within an organisation can Contact Support , however, CartonCloud support is available to CartonCloud tenants only.
- You can view and manage the support tickets you have raised via the CartonCloud Help Portal .
- When you contact us, we direct your request to the team best suited to answer your question and make sure they have all the necessary information to help you.
- For urgent issues, we offer a phone back service. Call your local number at Contact Us and follow the prompts to leave a voicemail that will go through to the next available support agent.
- Using the CartonCloud Help Portal , support email and phone number rather than individual contact's emails and phone numbers will ensure a better outcome for you. All of the support channels are monitored by multiple staff during business hours and will mean a faster response from the team most equipped to assist.
- For requests that require detailed explanations relating to your account specifically or tailored advice for how best to configure settings for your operation, we may need to schedule a paid support call with the most appropriate team member (details below).
- You can contact us in the same way for both free and paid support. We will always let you know if a paid support call is required prior to booking anything in or charging you.

### Free online support fair use policy

Free online support is intended to provide answers and resolutions for common or basic queries or to respond to any suspected bugs or issues with the system. This free online support is not intended for detailed questions that require an in-depth understanding of your particular use ticket or existing implementation. This would reduce our ability to respond to all of the support requests in a timely manner, but also in us providing potentially incomplete or incorrect information for the operational queries. Free online support can help explain functionality and processes in a general manner and help with basic troubleshooting.

For requests that require detailed explanations relating to your account specifically or tailored advice for how best to configure settings for your operation, we may need to schedule a paid support call with the most appropriate team member from our Customer Success team. This will ensure the best outcome for you and that your request can be fully resolved. Where a ticket falls into this category, our Service Desk team will let you know and arrange for a call to be booked at the next suitable opportunity. These calls are billable as per the *Paid Support, Training and Project Management* rates found [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/services-pricing#paid-support). If you would like to book a paid support call, you can [Contact Support](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport)in the same way as above and we will arrange for a session with the most appropriate person from our team.

Fair use also applies to the frequency and volume of tickets raised by an organisation. If an organisation raises an excessive number of tickets in a given period, our support team will communicate this with the primary contacts at that organisation and arrange for further support tickets to be handled in a paid support call (subject to the same charges as above).

Fair use does not apply to bugs or issues with the system.

Below are some examples of Free vs Paid support queries:

| **Free** | **Paid** |
| --- | --- |
| How do I create a new sales order? | Can you design a custom workflow for our multi-warehouse 3PL operation? |
| Why is this pick showing an error message? | Can you review and optimise our Picking and Replenishment configuration? |
| Where do I update a customer’s address details? | Can you create and upload a full list of Warehouse Locations for us? |
| We think we’ve found a bug — can you investigate? | Can you assist with building out a complete set of Income Zone Sets configurations? |

### Before you contact support

If you have a customer support query, we recommend searching the [Knowledge Base](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/topiccatalog) for immediate answers to your questions before you [Contact Support.](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport)

If you’re unable to CartonCloud, noticing slow performance, or observing unexpected behaviour in the system, please try the following steps to help resolve the issue:

- If you are using the web application, ensure that you are using the latest version of Google Chrome as your web browser.
- If you are using the mobile application, please ensure that you have the latest version of the CartonCloud application . For best performance, we also recommend updating to the latest version of Android or iOS available for your device.
- Clear your web browser's cookies and cache . This removes old data and allows your browser to perform better, which might resolve the issue.
- Check our System Status page to see if CartonCloud is experiencing any system issues.

<a id="product-policies"></a>
## Product Policies

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/product-policies_

In this section, you'll find the CartonCloud product policies which apply to your account. Choose the relevant page below to find out more:

- [Technical support & bug fixing policy](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/technical-support-bug-fixing-policy)
- [Customer Support Policy](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/customer-support-policy)
- CartonCloud Development Policy & Philosophy
- [Terms of service](https://app.cartoncloud.com.au/terms-of-service)
- [Privacy policy](https://app.cartoncloud.com.au/privacy-policy)

**Overview**

- There are two types of support requests, technical support and customer support.
- Any system issues or outages relating to the CartonCloud software are treated as the highest priority and rectified urgently.
- For customer support, use the Knowledge Base or Q&A Forum to find the answer immediately or if you need further help with CartonCloud, contact our support team.
- Free online customer support for common or basic queries is part of your CartonCloud subscription.
- This is subject to the fair use policy so we make sure we can help as many customers as possible, as best as possible.
- Paid support time with our team is available for more complex and detailed queries specific to your account.
- For urgent issues, we offer a call back service. Simply call, leave a voicemail and we will get back to you ASAP.

This applies to support queries related to both the CartonCloud software and integrations to the CartonCloud software that have been created or managed by the CartonCloud team.

<a id="rate-limiting-in-the-cartoncloud-web-application"></a>
## Rate Limiting in the CartonCloud Web Application

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/rate-limiting-in-the-cartoncloud-web-application_

### Table of Contents

- Background
- Why we use Rate Limiting
- What it means for you

### Background

Rate limiting controls how often a single user can use the system. For you, this means how often you can load new pages with the CartonCloud Web Application.

### Why we use Rate Limiting

Rate limiting is a way to manage high traffic and make sure no one user (or system) unintentionally slows things down for others.

Implementing Rate Limiting is aimed at ensuring all users get a fast, reliable experience when using the Web Application.

### What it means for you

For users using the system as intended, rate limiting should have**no impact** on normal operation. However, if you do exceed the limit, you'll see this message:

**403 Error: "Whoa there! You're sending requests a bit too quickly. Please wait a moment and try again."**

If this happens:

- **Wait 60 seconds**, then try again. The page you are attempting to access should load successfully

If you believe you are reaching the rate limit in error, please contact our Service Desk team [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/new-support-ticket).

<a id="services-pricing"></a>
## Services Pricing

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/services-pricing_

### Table of Contents

- Overview of fees and charges
- Weekly license fees and initial implementation fee
- Development charges
- Paid support, training and project management
- Paid add-ons
- Definitions
- Other

### Overview of fees and charges

Fees and charges for CartonCloud can be summarised into four different categories:

1. [Weekly License Fees (plus any paid add-ons)](https://help.cartoncloud.com#weekly)
2. Initial Implementation (paid at the time of joining with CartonCloud)
3. Integrations
4. Ongoing Paid Support (when going over plan inclusions)

Free online support is available to all CartonCloud customers (see our [Customer Support Policy](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Customer-Support-Policy) for details).

The prices below are the same for both AUD and NZD and are charged in either AUD or NZD depending on where you are based (Australia or New Zealand).

### Weekly License Fees & Initial Implementation Fee

Please contact us at [sales@cartoncloud.com](mailto:sales@cartoncloud.com.au) for more information.

Integration Charges (valid From 1 July 2020)

Our current integration charges are available [here](https://www.cartoncloud.com/hubfs/Pricing%20Documents/CartonCloud%20Integration%20Pricing%202020.pdf).

**The above rates exclude the following:**

Third-party FTP server setup : ***$540 + GST flat fee (no charge for using CartonCloud FTP server)***

Testing / Support / Training not included in the above pricing: ***$220 + GST Per Hour***

For further information on integration charges, capabilities and support, please use CartonCloud Help and lodge a case with our integrations team.

### Development Charges

Please see [CartonCloud Development Policy & Philosophy](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/CartonCloud-Development-Policy-Philosophy) for full details on how we approach our product development roadmap.

Many minor workflow customisations we're able to perform with Bolts. Customisation requests or change to existing integrations or bolts require gathering, scoping, and then developing.

| Description | UOM | AUD (ex GST) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Development** |  |  |
| Customising Work-Flow or Pricing through Bolts. | Per Hour | $540 per year. |
| Customised Development for features that cannot be solved with Bolts Examples include:   Creating customised export formats for Run Sheets or Reports   Customised workflows and/or features that the software requires. | Per Hour | Minimum Requirements Gathering / Scoping Fee: $500 Minimum Development Fee: $2,500 |
| **Labels & Document Templates** |  |  |
| Label format Change (either PDF or ZPL). | Per Hour | $220 Per Hour |

### Paid Support, Training and Project Management

**Remote (phone, video, screen-share)**

For requests that require detailed explanations relating to your account specifically or tailored advice for how best to configure settings for your operation, we may need to schedule a paid support call with the most appropriate team member from our Customer team. This will ensure the best outcome for you and that your request can be fully resolved (see our [Customer Support Policy](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Customer-Support-Policy) to understand inclusions in our free online support).

Some examples are:

- Advising on the best way to implement barcode scanning for your warehouse to incorporate location, product and batch number scanning;
- Configuring CartonCloud to match the requirements of a new customer that you have signed up that requires a detailed understanding of their requirements, your workflow preferences and existing implementation.

The below bundles are available after you have exhausted your initial implementation and training package when you start your account with CartonCloud.

| **Description** | **UOM** | **AUD (ex GST)** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Remote Support (phone, screen-share etc)** |  |  |
| Ad-Hoc Support / Training | Per Hour | $220 (charged in 15-minute blocks) |
| **Existing Customer Additional Bundles** |  |  |
| 3 Hours of Support / Training | Pre-paid | $500 (25% saving) |
| 10 Hours of Support / Training | Pre-paid | $1,440 (35% saving) |
| Need larger blocks?   Reach out to the team via [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport) . |  |  |

**On-site (a person on the ground at your premises)**

If you require further training and assistance with implementing CartonCloud, training staff, or implementing process changes, we have expert staff available to assist at your premises. Please note that this is subject to availability with our Implementation team and the best individual to assist you with your requirements.

| **Description** | **UOM** | **AUD (ex GST)** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **On-site training (note: excludes travel costs).** |  |  |
| Hourly - (note: travel time charged at 50% rate) | Per Hour | $220 (charged in 1-hour blocks) |
| Day Rate (8 hours) - note, travel time is additional | Per Day | $1580 (10% discount) |
| **Travel Costs** |  |  |
| Driving (Personal or CartonCloud Vehicle) Car Rental | Per KM (AU) Per Day | $0.66 (AU) / $0.70 (NZ) Vehicle costs are passed on |
| Flights | Per Flight | Airline costs are passed on |
| Accommodation | Per Night | Accommodation costs are passed on |
| Food and incidentals | Per Event | Food and incidentals are passed on at cost |
| Administration Fee | One-Off | $180 |

- Please note you will be invoiced in two stages.
  - Set labour hours and known fixed costs (please note flights will not be booked until the initial invoice has been paid).
  - Travel incidentals and additional labour hours.
- Cancellation of onsite training will result in any unavoidable costs (such as airline cancellation levies) being passed on along with the Administration Fee.
- Cancellation with less than two working days notice will incur 33% of set labour hours, unavoidable costs being passed on and the Administration Fee.

Initial Setup / Training

Our current setup fees are available from [sales@cartoncloud.com](mailto:sales@cartoncloud.com.au.)

Initial setup hours are available for depletion for 26 weeks from the first weekly invoice.

### Paid Add-Ons

Special software features are available for an additional fee.

| Add-on | Price (ex GST) |
| --- | --- |
| EDI Gateway Connectivity | Contact the Service Desk by lodging a case at [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/) . |
| SSCC label generation | $500 per year, per customer (payable 12 months in advance) |

### Definitions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Per Year, Per Customer | Within each CartonCloud account, each customer which has the functionality enabled will invoke another charge. For example, if you're a 3PL warehouse with 10 customers, and 2 of them require SSCC label generation, you would enable SSCC label generation within each of the two customers requiring it. CartonCloud would then bill you $1000 + GST ($500 x 2) for enabling this feature, and it will remain available for 12 months before being invoiced again. |

### Other

**Account Cancellation**

Unless otherwise specified, as per our [Terms of Service](https://app.cartoncloud.com.au/terms-of-service)accounts can be cancelled with 30 days notice (invoices generated during the following 30 days are payable).
Once an account is cancelled, the account is deactivated (no longer accessible), however, it can be reactivated within 12 months and data reinstated. After this time, the account and all of the associated data are deleted and cannot be recovered.
Account reactivation is charged at the current account setup fee and includes, and is treated as a new account setup.

**Overdue Accounts / Account Deactivation**

Accounts overdue by more than 14 days will be temporarily disabled until payments are up to date.
Weekly charges for the system will continue to be generated until we receive a written notification requesting account cancellation (30 days notice). Note: This means if your account becomes more than 14 days overdue, account access will be disabled, however, the system will continue to import chargeable jobs via Email, FTP, API etc. This prevents data loss from occurring during the deactivation period.

**Implementation Support Time - Engagement**

Your on-boarders are busy people who are working with multiple customers every week. This means their schedules are carefully managed to make sure they are available to assist you at agreed times.

When requesting time with one of the on-boarding team please provide as much notice as is possible; commonly, these guys are operating on a back-to-back schedule for several days. If your request is urgent you can also reach out to the Service Desk by lodging a case at [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com)   / Australia 1300 138 871 / New Zealand 0800 004 133.

Conversely, if an appointment needs to be cancelled or re-booked - please provide as much notice as is possible to allow us time to communicate with other customers to take your allotted spot.

**Do please note:**

We understand that not everything goes to plan in a fast-paced environment like the Logistics Industry. Therefore, we appreciate you may need to cancel your scheduled meeting. If you cannot attend, we require a**minimum of 2 hours' notice.** If missed, your **implementation hours will be depleted by 30 minutes.** This ensures business continuity and keeps the onboarding process on track.

**To reschedule your meeting, please contact your Implementation Consultant.**

<a id="technical-support-bug-fixing-policy"></a>
## Technical support & bug fixing policy

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/technical-support-bug-fixing-policy_

### Table of Contents

- Technical support & bugs
- Contact support

### Technical support & bugs

- We have monitors and alerts set up 24/7 for any system issues or outages relating to the CartonCloud software.
- If there is a system issue, we will display a banner at the top of the CartonCloud web application and update our System Status page .
- If you want to raise a technical issue or a bug, you can Contact Support .
- All technical issues and bugs are triaged when raised and prioritised based on various factors, including the severity of impact and the estimated number of customers impacted.
  - Critical - The application is degraded, users aren't able to perform their job function, and there are no workarounds. We begin working on the highest priority tickets immediately and resolve them as soon as reasonably possible.
  - High - A feature is unavailable, application performance is significantly degraded, or users' job functions are impaired. We begin working on high-priority tickets following any highest-priority tickets.
  - Medium - The application or specific feature isn't working as expected, but a workaround is available. Users' experience is impacted, but their job function is not impaired. Medium priority tickets are typically worked on after all high and highest priority tickets are resolved, and there are no other critical priorities.
  - Low - Non-critical functionality not behaving as expected that is minorly impacting product usability. Low priority bugs are worked on at CartonCloud's discretion and typically will be resolved when one of our Developers is already working in that product area and can easily resolve the problem identified.
  - No impact - Smaller paper cuts such as cosmetic errors, or non-critical functionality not behaving as expected that don't impact product usability. No impact bugs are worked on at CartonCloud's discretion and typically will be resolved when one of our Developers is already working in that product area and can easily resolve the problem identified.

### Contact support

Please visit the Contact Support page for full details if you need to get in touch with our support team.

---

<a id="guided-cartoncloud-set-up-videos"></a>
## Guided CartonCloud Set Up Videos

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/guided-cartoncloud-set-up-videos_

### Table of Contents

- What are the Guided CartonCloud Set Up Videos?
- CartonCloud Set Up Video Series
- How do you use the Guided CartonCloud Set Up Videos?

### What are the Guided CartonCloud Set Up Videos?

The CartonCloud set up videos have been designed and created to assist users in setting up their CartonCloud accounts successfully and ensure they maximise the benefits CartonCloud can offer. There are a number of different video series. The series you watch will depend on what stage of set up you are in and if you are using the Transport, Warehouse or both systems.

### How do you use the Guided CartonCloud Set Up Videos?

There are two options when following the CartonCloud Set Up Videos. You can either:

1. Follow the YouTube Playlist - each video series is on our YouTube channel. The playlist will play each video, one after the other.
2. Follow on the Knowledge Base page - the page lists each video with the corresponding Knowledge Base pages for that particular video.

### CartonCloud Set Up Video Series

| **Series** | **Description** | **Who should use this series?** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Basic Warehouse Set Up](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Basic-Warehouse-Set-Up) | This series will step through how to set up your account for WMS. You will cover all the basic Warehouse concepts and ensure you are ready to start using CartonCloud. | First time user of CartonCloud (new employee or CartonCloud has just launched in your organisation) Your CartonCloud account has just been created.  You need to refresh on some of the key WMS concepts |
| [Basic Transport Set Up](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Basic-Transport-Set-Up) | This series will step through how to set up your account for TMS. You will cover all the basic Transport concepts and ensure you are ready to start using CartonCloud. | First time user of CartonCloud (new employee or CartonCloud has just launched in your organisation) Your CartonCloud account has just been created.  You need to refresh on some of the key TMS concepts |
| [Transport Charging](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Transport-Charging) | This series will explain the different rating methods you can utilise to charge your customers. It will help you set up your first Rate Card and ensure you are charging customers correctly and maximising revenue potential. | First time user of CartonCloud (new employee or CartonCloud has just launched in your organisation) Your CartonCloud account has just been created (and you have already completed the TMS Basic Setup Series) You are responsible for invoicing at your company |
| [Warehouse Charging](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Warehouse-Charging) | This video will explain the different warehouse rating methods you can utilise to charge your customers.  Please note that this video was produced on V1 of CartonCloud; therefore, Customer Charges refers to Rate Cards in V2 of the app. In addition, some charging methods have been made available since this video and can be found [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/eAH8Hw) for storage charges and [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/gQH8Hw) for handling charges. | First time user of CartonCloud (new employee or CartonCloud has just launched in your organisation) Your CartonCloud account has just been created (and you have already completed the WMS Basic Setup Series) You are responsible for invoicing at your company |
| [Charging and Invoicing](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Charging-and-Invoicing) | This video will explain how invoicing and charging work in CartonCloud.  Please note that this video was produced on V1 of CartonCloud; therefore, Customer Charges refers to Rate Cards in V2 of the app. | First time user of CartonCloud (new employee or CartonCloud has just launched in your organisation) You are responsible for invoicing at your company |
| [Mobile App - Transport](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Mobile-App-Transport)[https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/-AS_Hw](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/-AS_Hw) | This video will explain how to use the transport mobile app. | First time user of CartonCloud (new employee or CartonCloud has just launched in your organisation) You are a driver user role You need to refresh on how to use the mobile app |
| [Mobile App - Warehouse](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Mobile-App-Warehouse)[https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/-gS_Hw](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/-gS_Hw) | This video will explain how to use the warehouse mobile app. | First time user of CartonCloud (new employee or CartonCloud has just launched in your organisation) You are a packer user role  You need to refresh on how to use the mobile app |
| [Automated Data Entry](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Automated-Data-Entry)[https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/8QS_Hw](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/8QS_Hw) | This video will explain how to set up your account to be more automated and minimise manual data entry. | First time user of CartonCloud (new employee or CartonCloud has just launched in your organisation) You would like to automate more processes in your operations  You need to refresh on what Parsers are and how to use them effectively |
| [Onforwarders](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Onforwarders) | This video explains how to use the onforwarders functionality in CartonCloud. | If you use onforwarders in your operation |
| [Business Intelligence (BI) Tool](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Business-Intelligence-BI-Tool) | This video explains what the Business Intelligence (BI) tool is and how to use it in your CartonCloud account. |  |

# Account Management > Users

<a id="adding-removing-customer-access-to-user-profiles"></a>
## Adding/Removing Customer access to User Profiles

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/adding-removing-customer-access-to-user-profiles_

- In some instances, you may have a user that requires access to multiple customers that have been set up in CartonCloud.
- To make changes to a users profile follow the steps below.
- Select Customers in the main menu, followed by Users in the drop-down menu.
- Then select Edit .
- By clicking into the Customer Box, you can now add or remove the accessible customer's access as required.
- Also, check if charging information should or shouldn't be visible.

- Select Save and the changed will be applied immediately.
- If a user has access to multiple customer's they will now be able to select them as shown in the animation below (the top option is the selected tenancy, the bottom option is the selected Customer within that tenancy).

---

<a id="adding-removing-warehouses-to-user-profiles"></a>
## Adding/Removing Warehouses to User Profiles

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/adding-removing-warehouses-to-user-profiles_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to modify the warehouses a user has access to

### Purpose

In some cases, you may need to add or remove warehouses to a user's access. To do so, you can edit the user and add or remove warehouses to their profile.

### How to modify the warehouses a user has access to

- Navigate to the Users page, Contacts > Users .
- Select the relevant User.
- Select the blue Edit button.
- Scroll down to the Warehouses section.
- Select the Warehouses you wish to give the user access to and/or unselect the Warehouses you want to remove from the user's access.
- Scroll to the bottom of the page and select Save .

![adding-removing-warehouses-to-user-profiles-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/adding-removing-warehouses-to-user-profiles-0.png?width=670&height=156&name=adding-removing-warehouses-to-user-profiles-0.png)

---

<a id="bulk-importing-users"></a>
## Bulk Importing Users

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/bulk-importing-users_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to import users in bulk

### Purpose

To import Users in bulk.

### How to import users in bulk

- Navigate to the Users page, **Contacts**>**Users**.
- Select **More** and then **Import CSV**.

![bulk-importing-users-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/bulk-importing-users-0.png?width=447&height=106&name=bulk-importing-users-0.png)

- Select **Download Sample Data**.

![bulk-importing-users-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/bulk-importing-users-1.png?width=446&height=209&name=bulk-importing-users-1.png)

- Open the downloaded file and edit accordingly using the below table as a guide. Column Header

Description

Detail

Name

User Name

Email

User Email Address

Create

Create User Now

Yes
No

Password

User Password

Roles

Roles - if assigning more than one role then separate each with a semi-colon.

Administrator;Packer;Customer;Data Entry;Onforwarder; Driver

Customers

If creating a 'Customer' type user you need to type the relevant Customer Name in. Note, these are the Customers to which the User will have access. If assigning more than one role then separate each with a semi-colon.

Where User is of type 'Customer'

Warehouses

The warehouses the user will have access to. If assigning more than one role then separate each with a semi-colon.
- Save the file.
- Navigate back to CartonCloud and select **Choose file**.
- Select the relevant file.
- Click **Upload File**.

![bulk-importing-users-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/bulk-importing-users-2.png?width=670&height=314&name=bulk-importing-users-2.png)

- A success message will appear. If you selected Yes for Create User Now you will see their password on this page. Ensure you select the Download List of Generated Passwords option.

![bulk-importing-users-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/bulk-importing-users-3.png?width=670&height=89&name=bulk-importing-users-3.png)

If you need to hide charging information from the created Users you will need to manually select each newly created user and enable this setting.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/Zm1z94iyE4g

---

<a id="creating-or-inviting-new-users"></a>
## Creating or Inviting New Users

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/creating-or-inviting-new-users_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to invite/create a new user
- Additional settings
- User role specific settings
- Accepting a CartonCloud user invitation
- Troubleshooting

### Purpose

For someone to access CartonCloud they need to be created or invited as a User first.

Follow along Vincent as he steps through how to add a new User to your CartonCloud account.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/NxMJZ6zhI08

How to invite/create a new User

- Navigate to the Users page (Contacts>Users).
- Click on **+Invite New User**. The below screen will appear.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-or-inviting-new-users-0-png.png?width=510&height=681&name=creating-or-inviting-new-users-0-png.png)

- Fill in the name and email field with the user's details.

It is recommended that for staff who do not have their own email address that you select the Create User option and use a placeholder email instead (the email is only used as login details).

- You have two options when generating a new User, you can either **invite** or **create** the User.  To create a user tick the **Create User Now** box.
  - **Invite User:** select this option if you wish to send an email invitation to the user and have them create their own password. This option is best for Customers and staff with Administrator access.
  - **Create User:**selectthis option if you wish to create the user immediately and set up their password on their behalf. This option is best for internal staff such as Packers and Drivers.
- Fill in the remaining fields using the below field descriptions.

| **Field** | **Field Description** |
| --- | --- |
| **Additional Settings** | Provides the option to hide all charging information from this user. |
| **Create User Now** | Tick the box next to **Create User Now** if you wish to **create** the user rather than invite them.  Invite user: do not tick this box if you wish to invite the user.  Create user: tick the box to create the user and password immediately. If **Create User Now** is ticked an extra option **Generate Password Automatically**will appear. This will automatically set a password up for the user and display it on the screen upon submitting the form. |
| **Email** | **Invite user:** this is where the invitation will be sent. Upon accepting the email the user can then change the email address if they wish. **Create user:**if the user does not have an email address, a placeholder email can be utilised instead. |
| **Roles** | Select the Roles you wish to enable for the user. There is no limit to the number of roles a user can have. A brief overview of each role is provided on the page. For more information on each role see [User Roles Explained](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/User-Roles-Explained). Note, if you would like the user to have access to the mobile application, the Packer or Driver role must be ticked. |
| **Subscribe** | If you wish the user to be subscribed to CartonCloud Newsletters. We recommend leaving this ticked so the User will receive important product updates related to CartonCloud. They are able to unsubscribe at any time if they wish. |
| **Warehouses** | Tick the applicable warehouses the user will be operating within. See [Setting up Warehouses (Multiple Sites)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Setting-up-Warehouses-Multiple-SitesSetting-up-Warehouses-Multiple-Sites)  for more information. |

### Additional Settings

For each user, you have the option to hide all charging information. This includes:

- The Charges tab will be hidden on Sale Orders, Purchase Orders and Consignments
- Rate Cards will not be visible.
- Invoices will not be visible.

### User Role Specific Settings

Some User Roles have specific settings that you can choose to apply.

| **User Role** | **Setting** | **Explanation** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Driver | List user on summary sheets even if they have no delivery runs to do | When enabled, the driver will appear on summary sheets regardless of whether they have delivery runs to complete. |
| Driver | Automatically Emal Run Sheets to Driver | When enabled, the driver will be emailed their Run Sheets automatically. |
| Driver | Enable this Driver to Add or Edit Consignment Items via the Mobile Device | When enabled, the driver will have the ability to add and edit Consignment Items from the mobile app.  **Setting options:** **Use Organisation Settings:**will apply the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level. **Enabled:**regardless of the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level, the driver will be able to add and edit Consignment Items via the mobile app.  **Disabled:** regardless of the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level, the driver will NOT be able to add and edit Consignment Items via the mobile app. Please see [Add/edit Consignment Items for Drivers on the Mobile App](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Add-edit-Consignment-Items-for-Drivers-on-the-Mobile-App) for more information. |
| Administrator | Allow this user to edit other users | When enabled the user will be able to edit other users.  If the user has this permission enabled it will appear as Yes on the Users list view screen under the heading 'Can edit users'. |
| Administrator | Allow access to the BI Dashboard | *This setting is only available if the user has 'Allow this user to edit other users' enabled.* This will give the user access to the [Business Intelligence Tool](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Business) if the account has the BI Dashboard enabled. The BI Dashboard is a paid add-on feature.  Note, that [WMS Premium](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/WMS-Premium) accounts will have access to unlimited BI Dashboard users, however, **for non-WMS Premium accounts** **additional users will be an additional cost.**Please reach out to our [Service Desk](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/new-support-ticket)team if you have any questions. |
| Onforwarder | Carrier(s) | Choose which Carriers the user will have access to. |

### Accepting a CartonCloud User Invitation

If needed, new users can get assistance with accepting their invitation from the video below.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/pn0iPdU7e1g

What to do if the user accepts the invite but cannot seem to access the Tenancy or Customer

One thing to keep in mind is that a CartonCloud User has a single login to view all the tenancies (3PL warehouses) and customers they have access to.

This means if you invite them to your tenancy they will need to manually select to view your tenancy from the drop-down menu.

If a user you have invited is reporting not being able to access your Tenancy or Customer. Please first ask them to double-check they have attempted to select it in the drop-down menus shown below.

![creating-or-inviting-new-users-1-png](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-or-inviting-new-users-1-png.gif?width=398&height=164&name=creating-or-inviting-new-users-1-png.gif)

- The top drop-down menu allows the user to move between tenancies.
- The bottom drop-down menu allows the user to move between Customers within those tenancies.

### Troubleshooting

It's very common for invitation emails to end up in spam or junk folders, so it's always a good idea to have the User check those locations as well for their invitation.

**What to do if the user accepts the invite but cannot seem to access the Tenancy or Customer:**

- One thing to keep in mind is that a CartonCloud User has a single login to view all the tenancies (3PL warehouses) and customers they have access to.
- This means if you invite them to your tenancy they will need to manually select to view your tenancy from the drop-down menu.
- If a user you have invited is reporting not being able to access your Tenancy or Customer. Please first ask them to double-check they have attempted to select it in the drop-down menus shown below.
- The top drop-down menu allows the user to move between tenancies.
- The bottom drop-down menu allows the user to move between Customers within those tenancies.

**What to do if the user is deactivated due to an unverified email address:**

CartonCloud automatically deactivates users whose email addresses remain unverified after account creation. This is a security measure to ensure only verified users can access the system.

- To resolve this issue: ✅ **Email verification must be completed to prevent automatic deactivation in the future.**
  - **Re-enable the user account (You may need to reach out to [CartonCloud Support](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/new-support-ticket) to re-enable the User)**:
    - Navigate to **Contacts > Users**.
    - Use the **Filters** to locate the deactivated user.
    - Click **Edit** next to the user and set **Active** to **Yes**.
    - Click **Save**.
  - **Ask the user to verify their email**:
    - Once the user is re-enabled, they need to log in.
    - Direct them to **Settings** at:
[https://app.cartoncloud.com/settings](https://app.cartoncloud.com/settings)
    - From their **Settings** page, they can complete the **email verification** process.

---

<a id="disabling-users"></a>
## Disabling Users

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/disabling-users_

Please note, you must have permission to edit other users to be able to disable a user. To have this permission you must have administrator access. See [User Roles Explained](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/User-Roles-Explained)for more information.

Once a CartonCloud user has been created it cannot be deleted, however, it can be disabled. This is because CartonCloud Users may have access to multiple CartonCloud Organisations (tenancies). When disabling them from within your account they can still log in to CartonCloud, but can no longer access your Organisation.

- To deactivate a user, in the Main Menu click on Contacts , then click on Users in the drop-down menu.
- Next, find the User you want to disable and select them.
- Finally, click Disable User .

The animation below demonstrates how to disable and re-enable a User.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/disabling-users-0-png.gif?width=1852&height=1208&name=disabling-users-0-png.gif)

Once a user is disabled you will not see them in the All tab. Instead, you will need to use the Filter tab and select Inactive to Yes, then click Go!

---

<a id="drivers"></a>
## Drivers

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/drivers_

### Table of Contents

- What is a Driver?
- How to create a Driver
- Driver List View
- View Driver
  - Details
  - Run Sheets
  - Cash on Deliveries
  - Consignments & Statistics
  - Optimise Map
  - Location History

### What is a Driver?

A Driver in CartonCloud had access to the mobile app to complete deliveries and the web app to view their assigned Consignments and Run Sheets.

### How to create a Driver

To create a new Driver follow the [Setting up Drivers in CartonCloud](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Setting-up-Drivers-in-CartonCloud) article.

### Driver List View

The Driver's page provides a full list of all active drivers within your Organisation. An active driver is a driver that has had a Consignment allocated to them during that week (including deleted ones).

From the Driver's page you can see which drivers are currently online (indicated by a green or red circle next to their name) and if they have location services enabled (indicated by a green or red pin next to their name).

![image-png-Mar-03-2026-01-55-19-0377-AM](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Mar-03-2026-01-55-19-0377-AM.png?width=670&height=152&name=image-png-Mar-03-2026-01-55-19-0377-AM.png)

### View Driver

By selecting a driver you are able to view and edit that Driver's details. The below describes what options and features are available from each tab on the View Driver screen.

#### Details Tab

To view the relevant details of the Driver, including:

- Driver: the driver's name.
- Email: the driver's email.
- Online: if the driver is online.
- Location Access Available: If the driver had enabled location access.

#### Run Sheets Tab

The Run Sheets assigned to the Driver.

You can email the Run Sheets to the Driver using the **Email Run Sheets to Driver** button.

#### Cash On Deliveries Tab

The  [Cash on Deliveries](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Cash-on-Deliveries) associated with the Driver.

#### Consignments & Statistics Tab

The Consignments assigned with the Driver.

The statistics for the Driver are summarised in a table showing the **Total Consignments**, **Total Completed** and**Total Incomplete**.

#### Optimise Map

Optimise the route of the driver according to the Consignments allocated to the driver.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/drivers-1-png.png?width=670&height=352&name=drivers-1-png.png)

#### Location History

The location history of the driver.

---

<a id="edit-user"></a>
## Edit User

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/edit-user_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to edit a user
- Additional settings
- User role specific settings

### Purpose

The edit User page allows you to update an already existing User's information and permissions.

### How to Edit a User

Note, to be able to edit a User you need the 'Allow this User to edit other users' enabled against your own user. This will need to be enabled by someone with this permission.

- Navigate to the Users page, Contacts>Users.
- Select the User you wish to edit.
- Click Edit .

![edit-user-0-png](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/edit-user-0-png.png?width=670&height=262&name=edit-user-0-png.png)

- Edit the fields using the below field descriptions as a guide.
- Select Save.

![Edit_User_2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hubfs/Edit_User_2.avif)

| **Name** | The user's full name |
| --- | --- |
| **Email** | The User's contact email. This is where the invite will be sent to. If you invite rather than create the User, the User will be able to change this themselves when they accept the invitation. |
| **Password** | Change the User's password. |
| **Warehouses** | These are the warehouse the User will have access to. |
| **Roles** | The roles assigned to the User. This will dictate what information and functionality the User will have access to. For more information please see [User Roles Explained](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/User+Roles+Explained) |
| **Additional Settings** | Provides the option to hide all charging information from this user. |
| **Subscribe** | We recommend leaving this ticked so the User will receive important product updates related to CartonCloud. They are able to unsubscribe at any time if they wish. |

### Additional Settings

For each user, you have the option to hide all charging information. This includes:

- The Charges tab will be hidden on Sale Orders, Purchase Orders and Consignments
- Rate Cards will not be visible.
- Invoices will not be visible.

### User Role Specific Settings

Some User Roles have specific settings that you can choose to apply.

| **User Role** | **Setting** | **Explanation** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Adminstrator | Allow this User to edit other Users | Give access to the Admin user to edit other Users. |
| Adminstrator | Allow access to the BI Dashboard | *This setting is only available if the user has 'Allow this user to edit other users' enabled.* This will give the user access to the [Business Intelligence Tool](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Business) if the account has the BI Dashboard enabled. The BI Dashboard is a paid add-on feature.  Note, that [WMS Premium](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/WMS-Premium) accounts will have access to unlimited BI Dashboard users, however, **for non-WMS Premium accounts** **additional users will be an additional cost.**Please reach out to our [Service Desk](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport)team if you have any questions. |
| Driver | List user on summary sheets even if they have no delivery runs to do | When enabled, the driver will appear on summary sheets regardless of whether they have delivery runs to complete. |
| Driver | Automatically Email Run Sheets to Driver | When enabled, the driver will be emailed their Run Sheets automatically. |
| Driver | Enable this Driver to Add or Edit Consignment Items via the Mobile Device | When enabled, the driver will have the ability to add and edit Consignment Items from the mobile app.  **Setting options:** **Use Organisation Settings:**will apply the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level. **Enabled:**regardless of the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level, the driver will be able to add and edit Consignment Items via the mobile app.  **Disabled:** regardless of the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level, the driver will NOT be able to add and edit Consignment Items via the mobile app. **Please see [Add/edit Consignment Items for Drivers on the Mobile App](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=577767258) for more information.** |
| Onforwarder | Carrier(s) | Choose which Carriers the Onforwarder will have access to. |

<a id="overview-of-cartoncloud-s-user-interface"></a>
## Overview of CartonCloud's User Interface

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/overview-of-cartoncloud-s-user-interface_

### Table of Contents

- User Interface Video Overview
- Search for anything!

Becoming familiar with CartonCloud's interface will help in ensuring a smooth onboarding process. As you use CartonCloud more, naturally you will become more familiar with the user interface, however, until then you can use the below video to assist you in the initial stages of using CartonCloud.

### User Interface Video Overview

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/UzWd5BpYFlQ

### Search for anything!

In CartonCloud, you can use our global search tool to search for anything. This means Menu's, references and much more. Some examples of this include; Consignments, Sale Orders, Purchase Orders and Addresses.

The Search for Anything bar is only visible to Administrator and Customer Users.

The Search Box appears up the top next to your Organisation name:

![User Interface_1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/User%20Interface_1.jpeg?width=670&height=38&name=User%20Interface_1.jpeg)

**Refine your Results:**
You can also refine your search to a particular 'type', using keywords:

Address
Consignment
Customer
Delivery
Delivery Run
Manifest
Product
Purchase Order
Sale Order
User
Warehouse Location

For example, to search Consignments going to IGA Burleigh Heads:
"Consignment IGA Burleigh Heads "

<a id="setting-up-drivers-in-cartoncloud"></a>
## Setting up Drivers in CartonCloud

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/setting-up-drivers-in-cartoncloud_

For a driver to use CartonCloud (including the Mobile App) they must be set up as a User.

To set up a driver as a user, please follow the [Creating or Inviting New Users](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Creating+or+Inviting+New+Users) article, ensuring you tick the Driver role within it.

Once completed, the Driver can then login to the Web App via the website [https://app.cartoncloud.com](https://app.cartoncloud.com.au/)  or login to the Mobile App by downloading it through the Apple App Store (iPhone) or Google Play Store (Android). We will cover CartonCloud Driver training in a future section.

This also now allows you to allocate Consignments to Drivers. Steps on how to allocate will also be covered in future sections of this guide.

It is also possible to allocate consignments on a Delivery Run automatically to a Driver upon the Consignments creation.

Next Step: [Setting up Vehicles in CartonCloud (optional)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=530055403)

---

<a id="user-modes"></a>
## User Modes

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/user-modes_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Relevant user roles
- Limits
- Choose user mode
- Switch user mode

### Purpose

The CartonCloud mobile app offers two user modes, "Delivering" and "Picking". Depending on what roles a user has been assigned via the CartonCloud web app, one or both of these user modes may be available.

### Relevant user roles

- Driver
- Packer

These user roles are assigned to each user within the CartonCloud web app by a system administrator. To be able to log into the CartonCloud mobile app at least one of these roles must have been assigned to you. Should you only have one role assigned to you, you will not be presented with all of the options to choose or switch your user mode mentioned below. You will simply be operating in your assigned space (e.g. Packers will not have the *Delivering*option unless they are also a Driver).

### Limits

Each user role does come with some limitations in regards to what functionality is accessible within the app while you are operating in the corresponding user mode.

For example, while in "Picking" mode you will not be able to optimise routes, allocate jobs or collect PODs. Similarly, while in "Delivering" mode you won't be able to pack Sale Orders.

### Choose user mode

Upon logging into the CartonCloud app you will be presented with a dialogue allowing you to choose the initial user mode you want to be operating in. If you choose to cancel this view, the default selection of "Delivering" will be applied (or Picking if you do not have Driver privileges).

Should you only have been assigned a Driver role, you will not be given an option to choose your user mode but will be directly forwarded to the appropriate user mode (*Delivering*).

![User Mode_1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/User%20Mode_1.jpeg?width=195&height=386&name=User%20Mode_1.jpeg)

### Switch user mode

To switch the current user mode, open the main slide-in menu and choose "Switch Mode". This will then open a view with user-mode options. Select the save button (blue ticks in the top right corner) to confirm your selection and update the app content accordingly.

Should you only have been assigned one user role, you will not have the option to switch the current user mode.

![User Mode_2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/User%20Mode_2.jpeg?width=229&height=450&name=User%20Mode_2.jpeg)

---

<a id="user-roles-explained"></a>
## User Roles Explained

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/user-roles-explained_

### Table of Contents

- User roles explained
- Additional settings
- User roles web or mobile access

### User roles explained

When you create a User, you can select the role you wish to assign to them. This role will determine what access they have within your CartonCloud account. The below describes the different roles a user can be assigned to.

| Administrator | Selecting this option provides access to all areas within CartonCloud, however there is an additional settings that can restrict this user. When you select the Administrator role tick box there is an option that appears to allow this user to add and edit other Users. If this is not selected the user will not have the ability to add or edit other users and their settings. This is typically only enabled for the System Administrator within your organisation for CartonCloud. |
| --- | --- |
| Customer | This role restricts the user to only login as a specific Customer. Please note that Order entry screens and features are different and restricted for these users. Note you must select at least one Customer. Note if you wish to provide a user with Administrator role access to login as a customer they will be provided with a drop down when the login to select which Customer they wish to emulate. |
| Data Entry | This role provides the user with the ability to add, edit and view consignments, Manifests and upload consignment invoices (hard copy POD's & other paperwork). As a data entry user if you add documents to a Consignment it then needs to be approved by an admin user before the document is uploaded. |
| Driver | Select this role for all users who need to login to the mobile app for Delivering and to login to the Web App to view their Consignments and Run Sheets. You can assign one or more Carriers to a Driver user. When a Carrier is assigned, the Driver is only visible to Onforwarder users who share the same Carrier — Onforwarders cannot allocate consignments, Run Sheets, or Delivery Runs to Drivers outside their assigned Carriers. |
| Onforwarder | Enable this role for external Onforwarders who need to login to the system to update consignment status, attach additional paperwork and view their allocated consignments. Choose which Carriers the Onforwarder will have access to. |
| Packer | Select this option for users who need to view, edit and pack Purchase Orders and Sales Orders. This is also required for users who wish to use the mobile app for Putaway, Picking and Wave Picking features |

As a guide, the following user types would have these roles selected.

| User Type | Administrator | Packer | Customer | Driver | Data Entry | Onforwarder |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| System Admin | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Operations | Yes |  |  |  |  |  |
| Driver |  |  |  | Yes |  |  |
| Administration | Yes |  |  |  |  |  |
| Warehouse Manager | Yes | Yes |  |  |  |  |
| Customer Service |  |  |  |  | Yes |  |
| Onforwarder/ Subcontractor |  |  |  |  |  | Yes |
| Customer |  |  | Yes |  |  |  |
| Warehouse Picker |  | Yes |  |  |  |  |

### Additional settings

For each user, you have the option to hide all charging information. This includes:

- The Charges tab will be hidden on Sale Orders, Purchase Orders and Consignments
- Rate Cards will not be visible.
- Invoices will not be visible.

### User Roles Web or Mobile Access

Below shows the various user roles and which interface that user role can access.

![User Roles Explained_1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/User%20Roles%20Explained_1.jpeg?width=344&height=348&name=User%20Roles%20Explained_1.jpeg)
