<a id="public-zapier-integrations"></a>
## Public Zapier Integrations

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/public-zapier-integrations_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Prerequisites
- How to access CartonCloud's public Zapier
- Most common connections
- How to set up a Zapier integration with CartonCloud
- API Clients for Zapier

### Purpose

Zapier allows you to integrate other applications with CartonCloud. Using your own Zapier account and CartonCloud's Public Zapier Integration, you can connect applications straight into CartonCloud yourself.

### Prerequisites

- You will need your own Professional Zapier Account (if you are using Premium apps).
- You will need to create a CartonCloud API Client for each integration you wish to set up in Zapier (e.g. if you want to set up a Shopify integration for one customer and another Shopify integration for another customer, you would need to create two different API Clients or if you want to set up a Shopify integration and a Starshipit integration for a customer you would need to set up two different API Clients).
- Meet the integration requirements linked in the below table.
- Access your customer's connection account (e.g. full access to the customer's Shopify account).

### How to access CartonCloud's Public Zapier

You can access CartonCloud's Public Zapier [here](https://zapier.com/apps/cartoncloud/integrations).

### Most common connections

Please note, these are just the most common ones we see users building. You can use Zapier to easily build an integration between CartonCloud and thousands of other applications.

| **Connection** | **Link to template** | **Requirements to set up the integration** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Xero pushing Purchase Orders into CartonCloud | [Template in Zapier](https://workflows.zapier.com/?attempt_id=0307c199-4e81-4fdf-85a8-8c133ccd6675&template=857867) | [Xero Integration Requirements](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Xero-Integration) |
| Starshipit pushing Sale Orders into CartonCloud | [Template in Zapier](https://workflows.zapier.com/?attempt_id=87823599-665f-4c44-9ff2-dae65b68559f&template=841677) | [Starshipit Integration Requirements](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Starshipit-Integration) |
| Xero pushing Sale Orders into CartonCloud | [Template in Zapier](https://workflows.zapier.com/?attempt_id=99eaf49d-481a-4124-bb76-2043b2b85d99&template=70199) | [Xero Integration Requirements](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Xero-Integration) |
| Shopify pushing Sale Orders into CartonCloud | [Template in Zapier](https://workflows.zapier.com/?attempt_id=5ff955f1-56d4-4508-9ec6-7aadb7d190d1&template=841627) | [Shopify Integration Requirements](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Shopify-Integration) |

For applications not mentioned above, please see the [Available Integrations](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Available-Integrations) section of the Knowledge Base to check the integration requirements for the specific application.

### How to set up a Zapier Integration with CartonCloud

**Note, this is only applicable to one-way integrations into CartonCloud.**

- Navigate to the CartonCloud Public Zapier (through your Zapier account login).
- Select one of our Templates or search for an app to connect to.

![public-zapier-integrations-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/public-zapier-integrations-0.png?width=670&height=342&name=public-zapier-integrations-0.png)

![public-zapier-integrations-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/public-zapier-integrations-1.png?width=670&height=342&name=public-zapier-integrations-1.png)

- Once you have selected an application or template, Zapier will guide you through the steps to set up the connection.

![public-zapier-integrations-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/public-zapier-integrations-2.png?width=670&height=304&name=public-zapier-integrations-2.png)

![public-zapier-integrations-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/public-zapier-integrations-3.png?width=670&height=342&name=public-zapier-integrations-3.png)

- Most of the information will be pre-filled if you are using a template.
- When you get to the ' Connect to CartonCloud ' step, you need to have the API Clients Credentials (the Client ID and the Client Secret). Please see the API Clients section of this article for more information.

### API Clients for Zapier

To create a Zapier connection with CartonCloud, you require an API Client (created in CartonCloud). Each Zapier integration you create requires its own API Client. For example, if you are creating a Shopify connection for one customer and a Starshipit and Shopify integration for another customer, you would need three different API Clients.

Once you have created the API Client, you will need to use the credentials to connect your CartonCloud account.

**Connecting CartonCloud in Zapier:**

![public-zapier-integrations-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/public-zapier-integrations-4.png?width=571&height=377&name=public-zapier-integrations-4.png)

**CartonCloud API Client credetials:**

![public-zapier-integrations-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/public-zapier-integrations-5.png?width=544&height=208&name=public-zapier-integrations-5.png)
**How to create an API Client in CartonCloud**

When creating the API Client in CartonCloud, it is recommended that you name the Client something that is easily associated with the Zapier connection. For example, if you create a Shopify integration for Coastal Coffee Company, you would call the API Client 'Shopify Zapier Coastal Coffee Company'.
You can find steps on how to create an API Client [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/API-Clients).

**Credentials for the API Client**

When creating the Zapier integration and connecting your CartonCloud account, you must provide the appropriate API Client Credentials. Upon creating the API Client, you are provided with the Credentials; however, if you were to misplace the Credentials, you can follow the below steps to generate the Credentials again.

---

<a id="route-optimisation-software-integration"></a>
## Route Optimisation Software Integration

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/route-optimisation-software-integration_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How it works
- Exporting data from bulk allocations
- Import updates
- Setting up an import update parser
  - Uploading the file
  - Successful import
  - Unsuccessful import
  - Email notifications

### Purpose

From the bulk allocation page, it is possible to export data in a chosen format that can then be imported into route optimisation software such as MarketMotion.

This is ideal for operations who use CartonCloud as their TMS but want to use other software for route optimisation.

If you are interested in setting this integration up. Please contact our [Integrations team](https://support.cartoncloud.com/servicedesk/customer/portal/2/create/27).

### How it Works

CartonCloud exports the data of the consignments you wish to optimise in third party software. This data will be formatted to be compatible with the software.

The file exported from CartonCloud is then imported into the route optimisation software which calculates the best order for your deliveries. This data including the order of deliveries is saved in an updated file.

CartonCloud imports the updated file the order of deliveries, along with other allocation information such as the driver and run sheet is updated within the system.

Deliveries will now take place using CartonCloud in the order that was determined by your route optimisation software.

![route-optimisation-software-integration-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/route-optimisation-software-integration-0.png?width=670&height=297&name=route-optimisation-software-integration-0.png)

### Exporting data from Bulk Allocations

Once our integrations team has configured your account to export data in the required format for the optimisation software you are using the following steps can be taken to export data.

- Navigate to the Bulk Allocations page by selecting **Transport** in the main menu, then **Bulk Allocations** in the drop-down menu.
- Select the consignments you wish to export data on. Then select**Export Selected to CSV**

![route-optimisation-software-integration-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/route-optimisation-software-integration-1.png?width=670&height=417&name=route-optimisation-software-integration-1.png)

- A box will appear allowing you to select the format you wish to export the data in. This will be named as the Route optimisation software you use and will only appear after you have consulted with our integrations team.

![route-optimisation-software-integration-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/route-optimisation-software-integration-2.png?width=566&height=272&name=route-optimisation-software-integration-2.png)

- Select Export and the consignment data will be exported to the desired format, ready to be imported back into your route optimisation software.

### ​​​​​​​Import Updates

The import updates button allows you to parse in a file containing the order deliveries should take place and automatically allocate consignments to the appropriate run sheets and drivers as specified.

This button will only appear on your bulk allocation page if you already have a

parser

of the "Consignment Allocation" class set up.

### Setting up an Import Update Parser

Parsers must be set up to read in the format of the file sent from the specific route optimisation software you are using. Therefore if you wish to have one set up please contact our [integrations team](https://support.cartoncloud.com/servicedesk/customer/portal/2/create/27).

Once the Parser has been set up the button to import updates will appear as shown below.

![route-optimisation-software-integration-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/route-optimisation-software-integration-3.png?width=670&height=363&name=route-optimisation-software-integration-3.png)

Selecting this button will open a box as shown below.

![route-optimisation-software-integration-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/route-optimisation-software-integration-4.png?width=564&height=350&name=route-optimisation-software-integration-4.png)
If you only have one Parser type set up then it will automatically populate the dropdown menu. However, if you have multiple parsers set up you should select the appropriate one from the drop-down menu.

Once ready, you should then select **Choose File** to select the CSV file template containing the update information that you wish to process.

CSV file templates may look slightly different based on the optimisation software you are pulling data from. In the generic example below some of the key fields and the corresponding logic is explained.

**Import Update Fields**

![route-optimisation-software-integration-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/route-optimisation-software-integration-5.png?width=670&height=104&name=route-optimisation-software-integration-5.png)

| **Field** | **Details** |
| --- | --- |
| Consignment ID (required) | A consignment ID **MUST** be provided for the import to be successful. This means that all consignments must already be created before attempting to import updates for them.   Failing a single consignment ID will result in the entire import failing. |
| Run Sheet name **(required)** | You **MUST** provide a run sheet name in the template file.   If you provide a Run sheet name in the template a new run sheet with that name will be created in the system.   That is unless a run sheet with an identical name already exists for the same date.   If a run sheet name is not provided the import will fail and an error message will appear explaining why. |
| Driver Name | If a valid driver name is provided in this field then the file import will automatically allocate the corresponding consignment ID to that driver.   Supplying a non-valid driver name will result in the import failing and an error message will be displayed. |
| Run Sheet Date | If a run sheet date is provided it will be used to set the date of the run sheet being created.   If a run sheet date is not provided then the system will check the [Delivery Run schedule](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Delivery+Schedules+for+Addresses%2C+Delivery+Zones%2C+Delivery+Runs) and attempt to determine a run sheet date.   If no date is provided and a run sheet date cannot be determined from the [Delivery Run schedule](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Delivery+Schedules+for+Addresses%2C+Delivery+Zones%2C+Delivery+Runs) then the import will fail and an error message will appear explaining why. |
| Vehicle Name | If a valid vehicle name is provided the consignment will be allocated to that vehicle   A non-valid vehicle name will cause the import to fail and an error message will appear explaining why. |
| Sort Order | This will be determined by the third party route optimisation software you are using. In order for CartonCloud to accept the value given it must be a numeric value.    In the case where the third party software does not output a numeric value for the order consignments should be delivered in a run then we can manipulate the data given to turn it into a numeric field.   The sort order determined what order consignments should be delivered on a run sheet. For example in the example file on the Gold Coast AM Run: Consignment 1152 (sort order 1), then Consignment 1154 (sort order 2), then Consignment 1151 (sort order 3). |
| Arrive Time | This field is not required and will only appear if requested. This is just one example of the many scenarios that can be set up using [custom fields](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Custom+Fields). If you have other fields that need to be updated from your route optimisation software these can be accommodated. |
| Depart Time | See above. |

**Uploading the file**

Once the file with the appropriate data is ready click **Choose File** then select the file you wish to upload.

![route-optimisation-software-integration-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/route-optimisation-software-integration-6.png?width=596&height=365&name=route-optimisation-software-integration-6.png)
Once selected, click **Import**.

The results of the import will then be displayed.

**Successful Import**

On a successful import, the results of the import will be displayed, showing which consignment ID was allocated and any corresponding details.

![route-optimisation-software-integration-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/route-optimisation-software-integration-7.png?width=670&height=246&name=route-optimisation-software-integration-7.png)
**Unsuccessful Import**

On an unsuccessful import attempt, error messages will be displayed to explain what went wrong. In the example below a consignment ID was missing from the file, causing the entire import to fail.

This will need to be fixed by supplying a consignment ID of the consignment to be allocated then reuploading the file.

![route-optimisation-software-integration-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/route-optimisation-software-integration-8.png?width=670&height=115&name=route-optimisation-software-integration-8.png)

**Email Notifications**

Please note using this function will not trigger any email notifications.

---

<a id="sap-integration"></a>
## SAP Integration

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/sap-integration_

### Table of Contents

- Overview
- What does the integration allow?
- What you need to get started
- Next steps to get connected

### Overview

SAP has various products including SAP ERP (formally R3), SAP Business One (SAP B1) and SAP S/4 HANA and each product depending available functionality and the business requirements can integrate with CartonCloud. This article focuses on XML iDocs. Enterprises that have a preference for / pre-existing Edifact and/or ANSI X1 2 messaging in place via a message broker / middleware can also be supported.

### What does the integration allow?

SAP is generally considered one of the most comprehensive Enterprise Resource Management Systems and therefore supports a comprehensive suite of functions and messaging relating to warehousing and distribution activities. These workflows include:

![sap-integration-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/sap-integration-0.png?width=400&height=200&name=sap-integration-0.png)

- Purchase Order, ASN, Stock Transfer, Returns in SAP creates a new Purchase Order in CartonCloud
- Sale Order, Packing Instructions, Deliveries, Shipments in SAP create a Sale Order in CartonCloud

![sap-integration-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/sap-integration-1.png?width=400&height=200&name=sap-integration-1.png)

- A Purchase Order confirmation in CartonCloud sends a goods receipt confirmation to SAP
- Sale Order pick confirmation in CartonCloud sends a Goods Issue Confirmation to SAP
- Product status change in CartonCloud creates an inventory status change in SAP
- Stock adjustment in CartonCloud creates a Warehouse Inventory Adjustment in SAP
- Stock on Hand Report in CartonCloud creates a Inventory Discrepancy Report in SAP

### What you need to get started

- The customer must be created in CartonCloud
- Your customer requires a SAP account. Generally enterprises using SAP will have an in house SAP specialist or third party SAP consultant to help set up the integration.
- SAP version (SAP ERP, SAP B1, SAP S/4 HANA)
- Message Framework (iDoc (xml), Edifact, X12, Custom xml, csv)

### Next steps to get connected

- Read the [SAP Requirements](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/SAP-Requirements)
- [/articles/Knowledge/SAP-Requirements](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/SAP-Requirements)Send an integration request via a[New Ticket](https://help2.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/new-support-ticket) (with the requirements included - the more information regarding the listed requirements the better!). The team will provide you with a quote upon sending through your request.[https://app.slack.com/team/USLACKBOT](https://app.slack.com/team/USLACKBOT)

---

<a id="sap-requirements"></a>
## SAP Requirements

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/sap-requirements_

### Table of Contents

- Integration requirements
  - Requirements from the CartonCloud Tenant (the 3PL, owner of the CartonCloud account)
  - Requirements from the SAP account holder

### Integration requirements

If you have decided you would like to integrate CartonCloud with SAP, we require you to send through a integration request via [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport) with the below information and one of our Integration Consultants will be in contact to arrange a scoping meeting.

Depending on complexity and availability of all parties, SAP integration projects can take between 2 - 5 months including scoping, analysis and mapping documentation, configuration and build, systems integration testing (SIT), user acceptance testing, Go-Live migration and support.

**Pricing will be provided upon sending through your request and after the initial scoping call.**

### Requirements from the CartonCloud Tenant (the 3PL, owner of the CartonCloud account)

- The customer the integration is for must be set up in CartonCloud
- Product attributes (Dangerous Goods, Temperate Controlled, Foodstuff, Fragile/sensitive, Apparel)
- Product tracking requirements (Batch, Lot, Manufacture, Expiry Date, Serial Numbers)
- Known / current workflows (Receiving, Picking, Returns, Re-works, Kitting, Damages)

### Requirements from the SAP account holder

- Complete and submit a CartonCloud Integrations Request Form .
- SAP version (SAP, ERP, SAP B1, SAP S/4 HANA)
- Message framework (iDoc (xml), Edifact, X12, Custom xml, csv)
- Specifications for messages
- Sample files
- Contact details for the customer's in-house / SAP partner specialist (name, email)

<a id="setting-up-shopify-stock-sync-by-email-or-ftp"></a>
## Setting up Shopify Stock Sync by FTP

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/setting-up-shopify-stock-sync-by-email-or-ftp_

### Table of Contents

- StockSync
- Prerequisites
- Generic guide on how to set up StockSync file feed
  - Step 1 - Feed Manager
  - Step 2 - Matching Column
  - Step 3 - Filter
  - Step 4 - Advanced Settings
  - Step 5 - Turning on StockSync

### StockSync

The StockSync plugin for Shopify allows CartonCloud to send the onhand quantity from CartonCloud to Shopify to update Shopify inventory information.

This is useful if you require stock on hand in CartonCloud to update Shopify inventory.

The Shopify store owner is responsible for configuring the StockSync settings.

### Prerequisites

- Before setting up Stock Sync by FTP you must first ensure you have installed and paid for Shopify StockSync plugin.

### Generic guide on how to set up StockSync File Feed

- Log into your Shopify account.
- Using the top search bar, select Apps and then type in StockSync .
- Select StockSync .

![setting-up-shopify-stock-sync-by-email-or-ftp-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-up-shopify-stock-sync-by-email-or-ftp-0.png?width=670&height=95&name=setting-up-shopify-stock-sync-by-email-or-ftp-0.png)

- On the StockSync page, select +Setup New feed . Or if you have not created a feed before select +Create New.

![setting-up-shopify-stock-sync-by-email-or-ftp-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-up-shopify-stock-sync-by-email-or-ftp-1.png?width=670&height=163&name=setting-up-shopify-stock-sync-by-email-or-ftp-1.png)

- On the Create New Feed screen, from the Choose an action on how to import products from supplier drop down select Update - Existing products .
- On the Confirm to proceed prompt, select Yes .
- Edit the title Update Profile X to CartonCloud StockSync .
- Select the Connections menu and then FTP .
- On the Confirm to proceed prompt, select Yes .
- Edit the title Update FTP - X to CartonCloud StockSync .
- Update the fields specified as below:

- Step 1 - Feed Manager
  - FTP
    - Add username (provided by the CartonCloud Integrations team).
    - Add password (provided by the CartonCloud Integrations team).
    - Add Host: ftp.cartoncloud.com.
    - Add Directory and File name (only add the filename provided by the CartonCloud Integrations team).
    - For After Process - Select Delete My File .
    - Select Test Connection .
    - If everything is correctly set, this response should appear - Connection Successful .
  - File Setting
    - File Format : select CSV .
    - Column Separator : select the comma ,
    - File Encoding : select Auto .
    - First Row is Header : select Yes .
    - Skip feed rows : leave blank.
- Select Next .

- Step 2 - Matching Column
  - Note: if drop down values do not appear, zoom out to 30 - 50%.
  - Product Identifier :
    - Store field : select the field you utilised, most of the time this is SKU (Variant's SKU). In the box underneath the Store field, remove any value that may be here and select ProductCode .
    - Select the settings cog to see if any additional product identifier settings are required.
  - Store Product Fields :
    - Store field : select Availably Quantity. In the box under Store field, select AvailableQuantity .
    - Select the settings cog to see if any additional product identifier settings are required.

-
  - If you have multiple Locations (Location IDs) in your Shopify Store :
    - Navigate back to Shopify, select Settings > Locations and view the number of locations.
    - Under the Store Product Field(s), select the settings cog.
    - Select the Inventory Location for the warehouse
    - See here to check if any additional settings need to be configured for your store and workflow setup.
    - Note, if you believe Product Options (Variations), or Metafield are required to be able to sufficiently identify products, please contact the CartonCloud Integrations team here .

-
  - Select Next . The response, CartonCloud StockSync settings updated , should appear.

- Step 3 - Filter
  - Products can be filtered if you do not wish the StockSync process to update certain inventory values. You can leave the Store Product - Store Field Name as “Published” and leave the default value of “Any”. You can apply any rules to Store Products, or to data in the Incoming Feed.
  - The below settings can be configured to suit the setup you require:
    - Ignore variants with "Track inventory"
    - Ignore variants with zero quantity
    - Ignore variants with quantity

-
  - Select Next . The response, CartonCloud StockSync settings updated , should appear.

- Step 4 - Advanced Settings
  - Auto Publish/Unpublish:
    - Auto publish/ unpublish: can be left as Do not apply .
    - Auto archive products: can be left as Do not apply .
  - Out of Stock Strategy:
    - Note, the below settings have significant impact on your Shopify store set up and integration. Please ensure you follow the Download Export Product advice prior to turning on either of the below settings. It is also highly recommended that you read the following articles - [Out of Stock Strategy (OOS)](https://help.stock-sync.com/en/article/out-of-stock-strategy-oos-m5qjng/) and [Store Product Filter](https://help.stock-sync.com/en/article/store-product-filter-1uqbxfq/).
    - Set out of stock to ZERO quantity: can be turned on with care if the above articles are read, however, this setting is generally not turned on (note, it is highly recommended that the above articles are read before enabling this setting) .
    - Select Finish .

If any of the generic settings above do not meet your requirements, please read the article -[Setting up the feed with Stock Sync](https://support.stock-sync.com/support/solutions/44000814776). If a pop up window appears prompting you with links to articles, it is highly recommended that you read the articles.

- Step 5 - Turning on the StockSync
  - From the Dashboard, select the desired Feed Name to turn on.
  - If the Feed Name reads 'FTP - 1' change this to 'CartonCloud Sync'.
  - Select Turn on under the Schedule.
  - On the Feed Detail screen, select Preview Sync .
  - The Processing screen will appear until the preview is available.
  - Select Feed Details, select Activity Logs List .
    - Select the Details link > select the Change Logs > and click Not in Store links to download the test results.
    - Refresh the screen until you receive a file. If the file does not appear, it may be empty. This would indicate the SKUs in CartonCloud have not been identified via StockSync in Shopify, so an update to resolves this would be needed.
    - Explore the file to confirm:
      - The Before Qty (what is in Shopify) and
      - The After Qty (what will be updated from CartonCloud)
    - And confirm this is the desired outcome for Products and Variants (if applicable).
  - To set a schedule, first check what time zone your Shopify is in and what time zone CartonCloud are using in scheduling file creation.
  - The default time zone should be aligned with the Warehouse time zone.
  - Select Turn on here or Start Process .
  - On the Automate this feed screen , slide the Schedule is enabled toggle to turn it on.
  - Se the Schedule Frequency to Daily .
  - Update the Schedule Time to 11:50 .
  - Select Ok .
  - The Dashboard will now show an active Stock Feed with a frequency and time in the schedule.
  - Monitor to see Inventory On Hand Quantities in CartonCloud are reflected in Shopify as expected.

---

<a id="shippit-integration"></a>
## Shippit Integration

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/shippit-integration_

### Table of Contents[#what-does-the-integration-allow](https://help.cartoncloud.com#what-does-the-integration-allow)

- Overview
- What does the integration allow?
- What you need to get connected
- Next steps to get connected

### Overview

Shippit is a multi-carrier shipping software that has the ability to integrate with CartonCloud.

### What does the integration allow?

**CartonCloud Sale Order creates a shipment within Shippit.**

![shippit-integration-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/shippit-integration-0.png?width=400&height=200&name=shippit-integration-0.png)

- Transfers the delivery address and Sale Order items with the respective weights.
- Custom fields can be mapped to fields within Shippit.
- Choose if the shipment in Shippit is created once the Sale Order is packed or dispatched in CartonCloud.
- Confirm the packaging to match with your preferred carrier and generate the labels from within Shippit.

**Pull tracking information from Shippit to CartonCloud**

![shippit-integration-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/shippit-integration-1.png?width=400&height=200&name=shippit-integration-1.png)

- The Shippit tracking ID or courier tracking number can be pulled from Shippit back into the CartonCloud Sale Order.
- These tracking details can then be sent to other applications such as Shopify when marking orders as fulfilled.

**Printing shipping labels**

![shippit-integration-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/shippit-integration-2.png?width=614&height=307&name=shippit-integration-2.png)

- By default, labels can be printed directly from within the Shippit application or you can choose to configure to print labels directly from within CartonCloud.
- Printing can occur automatically by selecting an order status event such as when an order is marked or packed or by clicking a custom button.
- This is useful as it eliminates the need to continually switch between platforms.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/AHALxanwNdU

### What you need to get connected

- Your customer requires a Shippit account.

### Next steps to get connected

- Read the integration requirements - Shippit Requirements
- Send an integration request via a New Ticket (with the requirements included - the more information regarding the listed requirements the better!). The team will provide you with a quote upon sending through your request.

---

<a id="shippit-requirements"></a>
## Shippit Requirements

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/shippit-requirements_

### Table of Contents

- Integration Requirements
  - Requirements from the CartonCloud Tenant (the 3PL, owner of the CartonCloud account)
  - Requirements from the Shippit account holder

### Integration Requirements

If you have decided you would like to integrate CartonCloud with Shippit, we require you to send through an integration request via [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport) with the below information. Pricing will be provided upon sending through your request.

### Requirements from the CartonCloud Tenant (the 3PL, owner of the CartonCloud account)

- The trigger you would like to set that will determine when an order is sent to Shippit. For example, when a Sale Order reaches the status of Packed or Dispatched.
- If only specific orders should be sent to Shippit, such as orders going to a particular state.

### Requirements from the Shippit account holder

- Complete and submit a CartonCloud Integration Request Form .
- Your Shippit API Key.
- If only specific orders should be sent to Shippit, such as orders going to a particular state.
- Confirm if you would like the Shippit tracking ID or the Courier Tracking Number sent back to CartonCloud.
- Please note if you wish for the Courier Tracking Number to be returned this must be checked using a scheduled processor. When this API call to obtain tracking details occurs Shippit will automatically update the shipment to 'Ready to Ship'. We therefore, recommend only checking for tracking details at chosen times when you know shipments will already be processed.

---

<a id="shipstation-integration"></a>
## ShipStation Integration

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/shipstation-integration_

### Table of Contents

- Overview
- What does the integration allow?
- What you need to get started
- Next steps to get connected

### Overview

ShipStation is a shipping and fulfilment platform that has the ability to integrate with CartonCloud.

### What does the integration allow?

**ShipStation to CartonCloud (Sale Order creation)**

**![shipstation-integration-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/shipstation-integration-0.png?width=400&height=200&name=shipstation-integration-0.png)**

- A new order in ShipStation creates a new Sale Order in CartonCloud.
- Delivery address, sale order items, weight, custom fields (if applicable) from CartonCloud can be transferred to the shipment in ShipStation.
- Select the Sale Order status in CartonCloud to trigger the creation of the shipment in ShipStation, for example packed or dispatched.
- Confirm packaging matches your preferred carrier and generate labels from within ShipStation.

**Confirm tracking from ShipStation to CartonCloud**

![shipstation-integration-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/shipstation-integration-1.png?width=400&height=200&name=shipstation-integration-1.png)

- Tracking number from the shipment in ShipStation can be updated on the respective order in CartonCloud.
- The tracking number can be sent to other applications such as Shopify, when marking orders as fulfilled.

**Print ShipStation labels directly from within CartonCloud**

- ShipStation labels can be printed directly from within CartonCloud.

### What you need to get started

- A ShipStation account owned by the Tenant or Customer.
- Your ShipStation API Key and API Secret. This is unique to your company's ShipStation account and can be found inside of the ShipStation application under Settings > API Settings.
- If the Tenant's account, we need to know the child account name.

### Next steps to get connected

- Read the Integration Requirements - ShipStation Requirements
- Send an integration request via a New Ticket (with the requirements included - the more information regarding the listed requirements the better!). The team will provide you with a quote upon sending through your request.

---

## ShipStation Requirements

If you have decided you would like to integrate CartonCloud with ShipStation, we require you to send through an integration request via[CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport) with the below information. Pricing will be provided upon sending through your request.

### Requirements from the CartonCloud Tenant (the 3PL, owner of the CartonCloud account)

- Please let us know if CartonCloud will receive orders from ShipStation.

<a id="shopify-integration-troubleshooting"></a>
## Shopify Integration Troubleshooting

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/shopify-integration-troubleshooting_

### Table of Contents

- [Issue](https://help.cartoncloud.com#issue)
- [What causes a Shopify order to fail and not be marked as fulfilled](https://help.cartoncloud.com#what-causes-a-shopify-order-to-fail-and-not-be-marked-as-fulfilled)
- [Resolution](https://help.cartoncloud.com#resolution) ​​​​​​​

### Issue

Order in Shopify has not been marked as fulfilled even if the Sale Order has been packed or dispatched from CartonCloud.

### What causes a Shopify order to fail and not be marked as fulfilled

CartonCloud is able to mark a Shopify order as fulfilled once it has been packed or dispatched in CartonCloud. When the order is created in Shopify and is sent to CartonCloud via API, a unique reference number is captured for each individual line of product on a Sale Order. This unique reference number needs to be sent back to Shopify (once the CartonCloud Sale Order is packed or dispatched) in order to fulfill the order in Shopify.

A Shopify order fulfilment will fail if the unique reference number against each product line is not sent back to Shopify correctly. This can occur if:

- You manually enter the order or parse through the order
- If you delete product lines from an order
- If an order is originally rejected (for example, due to not enough stock). Even if you are able to reprocess the order, the order will not be able to be fulfilled in Shopify.
- The product on an order is changed (removed and a new product added)
- The product code on an order is changed (the same product on the order but the code on the product in CartonCloud had been updated)
- The quantity of a product line has either increased or decreased

### Resolution

If the order has not been fulfilled in Shopify due to the above mentioned reasons you will need to manually go into Shopify and fulfill the order.

---

<a id="starshipit-integration"></a>
## Starshipit Integration

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/starshipit-integration_

### Table of Contents

- Overview
- What does the integration allow?
- What you need to get started
- Next steps to get connected

### Overview

Starshipit is a shipping and fulfilment platform that has the ability to integrate with CartonCloud.

### What does the integration allow?

**A Sale Order in CartonCloud creates a shipment in Starshipit**

![starshipit-integration-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/starshipit-integration-0.png?width=670&height=123&name=starshipit-integration-0.png)

- Delivery address, sale order items, weight, custom fields (if applicable) from CartonCloud can be transferred to the shipment in Starshipit
- Select the Sale Order status in CartonCloud to tigger the creation of the shipment in Starshipit , for example packed or dispatched
- Confirm packaging matches your preferred carrier and generate labels from within Starshipit

**Tracking number from Starshipit updated on the respective CartonCloud order**

![starshipit-integration-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/starshipit-integration-1.png?width=670&height=116&name=starshipit-integration-1.png)

- Tracking number from the shipment in Starshipit can be updated on the respective order in CartonCloud
- The tracking number can be sent to other applications such as Shopify, when marking orders as fulfilled.

**Print Starshipit labels directly from within CartonCloud![starshipit-integration-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/starshipit-integration-2.png?width=482&height=241&name=starshipit-integration-2.png)**

- Starshipit labels can be printed directly from within CartonCloud by either a status event trigger, such as an order being marked as packed, or by a custom button .

### What you need to get connected

- Your customer requires a Starshipit account

### Next steps to get connected

- Read the integration requirements Starshipit Requirements
- Send an integration request via a New Ticket (with the requirements included - the more information regarding the listed requirements the better!). The team will provide you with a quote upon sending through your request

---

<a id="starshipit-requirements"></a>
## Starshipit Requirements

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/starshipit-requirements_

### Table of Contents

- Integration requirements
  - Requirements from the CartonCloud Tenant (the 3PL, owner of the CartonCloud account)
  - Requirements from the Starshipit account holder

### Integration requirements

If you have decided you would like to integrate CartonCloud with Starshipit, we require you to send through a integration request via[CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport) with the below information. Pricing will be provided upon sending through your request.

### Requirements from the CartonCloud Tenant (the 3PL, owner of the CartonCloud account)

- The trigger that will determine when a order is sent from CartonCloud to Starshipit. For example, when a Sale Order reaches a status of packed or dispatched.
- If only specific orders should be sent to Starshipit, such as orders going to a particular state.

### Requirements from the Starshipit account holder

- Complete and submit a CartonCloud Integrations Request Form .
- Your Starshipit API Key
- If only specific orders should be sent to Starshipit, such as orders going to a particular state

---

<a id="transvirtual-integration"></a>
## Transvirtual Integration

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/transvirtual-integration_

### Table of Contents

- What does the integration allow?
  - Pushing sale orders or consignments from CartonCloud to Transvirtual
    - How to get connected
  - Pushing proof of delivery (POD) from Transvirtual to CartonCloud consignments
    - How to get connected

### What does the integration allow?

CartonCloud is integrated with Transvirtual allowing jobs picked in the CartonCloud WMS to be automatically transferred into the Transvirtual transport management system.

**Pushing Sale Orders or Consignments from CartonCloud to Transvirtual**

![transvirtual-integration-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/transvirtual-integration-0.png?width=670&height=176&name=transvirtual-integration-0.png)
While there are various ways the integration can be configured, the most common way is to trigger a new transport job to be created within Transvirtual once a Sale Order is packed within CartonCloud.

Once this is done, orders automatically appear in Transvirtual with the status "EDI".

(Note: you can configure it to create the job on Sale Order approval or other statuses too).

In addition, rules can be created to control which orders are sent to Transvirtual. For example, you may use a [Custom Field](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/CKB/Custom+Fields)within your Sale Order to track whether or not the order should be sent to Transvirtual.

**How to get connected**

Connections to Transvirtual need to be configured by the CartonCloud team. Please contact us to get Transvirtual connected.

**Pushing Proof Of Delivery(POD) from Transvirtual to CartonCloud Consignments**

![transvirtual-integration-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/transvirtual-integration-1.png?width=670&height=176&name=transvirtual-integration-1.png)
This integration is available for CartonCloud Consignments; on completion of a delivery in Transvirtual the POD is attached to the CartonCloud consignment and the consignment is marked delivered.

- This POD can then be automatically forwarded to your customer. See how to located [Customer Email Address Settings](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Customer+Email+Address+Settings)

**How to get connected**

Connections to Transvirtual need to be configured by the CartonCloud team. Please contact us to get Transvirtual connected.

---

<a id="workwave-integration-for-allocating"></a>
## Workwave Integration for Allocating

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/workwave-integration-for-allocating_

### Table of Contents

- Overview
- CartonCloud configuration to enable
  - Product type
  - Capacity
  - Custom fields setup to match workwave & using
- How to send to Workwave
- How to retrieve from Workwave
- Important steps / troubleshooting

### Overview

Workwave Integration is a paid feature that requires configuration to set up, for more information please use CartonCloud Help to lodge a case with our support team.

Information related to the Workwave Integration for Push/Pull allocating between CartonCloud and Workwave.

CartonCloud can use Workwave's allocation tools to create Run Sheets within CartonCloud.

CartonCloud as used as the source of consignments, then instead of allocating to drivers within in CartonCloud, pushing (all or selected) consignments to Workwave, using the tools there to allocate and produce Run Sheets in order of deliveries for the drivers. This ordering and allocation are pulled back into CartonCloud producing Runsheets with Drivers already allocated in the exact delivery ordering from Workwave for the consignments.

From there the drivers will have these deliveries on their CartonCloud App ready to deliver and record the PODs.

The integration with Workwave **supports time slots** to be used for allocation purpose, so that start and end of a timeslot can be assigned permanently to addresses or, assigning timeslots specifically to a consignment (which overrides address). This ensures timeslots are adhered to in the allocation process.

Workwave can be used to manually create allocations one by one using the map view, or by putting all consignments into Workwave and using its rules/setup to produce runs for drivers dynamically, i.e. using the capacity of the vehicle and timing of deliveries to create an optimum number of runs and drivers needed to deliver those consignments (auto-creating allocations variable to the consignments). Or a combination of both.

There are two main components within the integration:

- Pushing Consignments to Workwave (from Bulk Allocation screen).
- Pulling approved routes from Workwave (from Bulk Allocation screen).

### CartonCloud configuration to enable

Workwave integration is a paid feature, for details on pricing and information related to implementing this please contact the Service Desk by lodging a case at

CartonCloud Help

.

Turn on Workwave in

Organisation Settings

:

![workwave-integration-for-allocating-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-0.png?width=670&height=447&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-0.png)
This then brings up 2 additional fields - the API Key and Territory mappings. The territory mapping is the operation area in Workwave and typically you are linking a matching territory for the CartonCloud warehouse. This is the territory code from Workwave and the warehouse ID from CartonCloud. Both of these are found from Workwave itself, consult Workwave help on where to find. Enter the Key in and enter the Territory mappings in then hit save. Workwave will now be active.

An example of what the mappings should look like when populated, use as many territory links as needed:

![workwave-integration-for-allocating-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-1.png?width=447&height=209&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-1.png)

**Product Type**

Workwave has fields called Tags (Required), these match with CartonCloud [Product Types](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Product+Types), so configure the names to match for Workwave to successfully sync those fields. e.g. Chilled product type will match a Chilled Required Tag in workwave to segregate vehicles by what they accept etc.

**Capacity**

Workwave has fields in its Vehicles configuration which limits how much a vehicle can carry, these fields can be anything and match the CartonCloud consignment custom fields e.g. Pallet and Carton fields of a consignment. Simply configure both CartonCloud and Workwave names to match for them to sync when passing data back and forth. e.g. Pallet in CartonCloud should match Pallet capacity field in the vehicle setup for Workwave for everything to match. Common examples and use are Pallets, Cartons, Weight and Cubic to allow capacity to work well, either all or 1 or combinations can be utilised.

**Custom Fields Setup to match Workwave & Using**

Custom Fields are required to be configured in CartonCloud to support the integration with Workwave, these fields support the API link, and also allow the use of Service Time for address delivery length and Time Windows for delivery timeslots. 2 different timeslots can be configured per address and 1 overriding timeslot per consignment. The idea is that addresses where needed generally have permanent timeslots if they are not available to be delivered to all day during normal business hours. So by configuring timeslots against the address, these can be used to ensure when Workwave is used for allocation building, the timeslots are factored in and adhered to for the drivers to meet during their delivery schedule.

Navigate to Custom Fields using the Search for anything! box.

Configure in the Address section the 5 fields exactly as per below example, this is important to allow the integration to function correctly:
![workwave-integration-for-allocating-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-2.png?width=670&height=190&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-2.png)

Then configure the Consignment Data section 3 fields exactly as shown:

![workwave-integration-for-allocating-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-3.png?width=670&height=225&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-3.png)
![workwave-integration-for-allocating-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-4.png?width=670&height=422&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-4.png)

The address custom fields can be used by editing the [Addresses & Address Strings](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/createpage.action?spaceKey=KB2&title=Addresses+%26+Address+Strings&linkCreation=true&fromPageId=532546184), populating a Service Time in minutes and the other fields by time start and end. Here is an example of what an address can look like. These fields are optional so populate or don't populate as you desire.
![workwave-integration-for-allocating-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-5.png?width=261&height=328&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-5.png)

The consignment custom fields again are optional, with time windows that if populated override the address time windows. Note the below warning.

**Important Note: Sent to Workwave is a field auto-populated by the system and is used to analyse whether consignments have been sent already or not. Do not use adjust manually unless you have advanced knowledge and need to.**

### How to send to Workwave

Navigate to [Bulk Allocation](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Bulk+Allocation) and using the selection tools there, either select all undelivered consignments requiring allocation or make a custom selection of consignments you would like to allocate in Workwave. Once selected, the use the Send to Workwave button highlighted below to send them to Workwave. If there are any issues an error message will appear, or potentially they may be missing in Workwave, always check you have a matching number of consignments in Workwave as there are in CartonCloud to send.
![workwave-integration-for-allocating-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-6.png?width=608&height=346&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-6.png)

If using type Delivery + Pickup, if 1 or both of the parts are selected, then both Pickup And Delivery consignments will be sent to Workwave for allocation.

A popup will appear with a count of selected Consignments. Clicking the **Send To WorkWave** button in the popup will push the data to WorkWave.

Once the push to WorkWave is complete a success message will show

### How to retrieve from Workwave

Once allocations complete in Workwave, mark the Routes that are finished as Approved Routes in Workwave, then CartonCloud will be able to pull those. It's important the routes are marked as Approved as CartonCloud will not pull any unapproved routes from Workwave, this is to allow pulling only finished allocations.

Navigate to [Bulk Allocation](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Bulk+Allocation) and use the Pull Approved Routes From Workwave button to load the consignments in Workwave back into CartonCloud. Nothing else is needed to be selected on the page, just use the button straight away. The typical use for this is to be used after sending to Workwave, allocating in Workwave, then using the Pull button to copy the allocations from Workwave into CartonCloud as the final step.

Once selected it will ask for the date you would like to retrieve from Workwave for → this is the date that consignments are loaded against in Workwave (i.e. this can be in advance, e.g. tomorrow's date). Click the choose date box:

![workwave-integration-for-allocating-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-7.png?width=670&height=163&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-7.png)
Select date from the calendar then uses the Pull Approved Routes button to pull the Routes from Workwave into CartonCloud.

![workwave-integration-for-allocating-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-8.png?width=670&height=218&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-8.png)
Then you will get a screen which shows how many routes were pulled back, how many Run Sheets were created if there were any fails. It will also show Errors like this example, which indicates a configuration error as no Workwave link is configured here. The other common Error is that there are no approved routes in Workwave for the date requested, either because they're on a different date or they are unapproved. This often happens because Workwave won't approve a Route if it has allocation issues.

![workwave-integration-for-allocating-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/workwave-integration-for-allocating-9.png?width=670&height=231&name=workwave-integration-for-allocating-9.png)
When the Pull process is complete you should have Runsheets (Transport menu→ Runsheets) matching your Workwave routes, with the consignments in the same order as Workwave had them too. If the Driver is also configured, congratulations you're finished. The drivers now have their runs allocated ready for loading and deliveries!

### Important steps / Troubleshooting

- For Workwave to create an approved Route the consignments need to be acceptable to the Vehicles tags such as Product Types of Chilled, Frozen etc, the capacities of Pallets and Cartons to match. WW back > CartonCloud
- Only approved Routes will be available to CC - WW will not approve a route if it has allocation issues
- Ensure the date is correct if you fail to upload any routes
- When pulling Routes back into CartonCloud the Vehicle name from Workwave must be set up in CartonCloud under the [Vehicles](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/createpage.action?spaceKey=KB2&title=Vehicles+-+Track+your+fleets+performance&linkCreation=true&fromPageId=532546184) section as well and match exactly.
- When pulling Routes back with drivers into CartonCloud the driver email details (or login as its used) must be set up in CartonCloud under the [Users](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Users) section as well and match exactly.

Notes:

1. Currently, only Pickup, Delivery and Pickup+Delivery consignment types are supported from CartonCloud when using this Workwave integration, others such as Point to Point are currently unsupported and will not work properly.
2. The Pull phase is currently limited to same-day dates in Workwave, so bear that in mind for the process. (Updated and now supports multiple dates).

---

<a id="zapier-integration-faq"></a>
## Zapier Integration FAQ

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/zapier-integration-faq_

### Frequently asked questions by those using Zapier to Integrate with CartonCloud

**I've received multiple emails for the same rejected order, why?**

When Zapier tries to create an order in CartonCloud, and it cannot, CartonCloud sends an error message to Zapier. For example: 400 - Order already Exists.
As this is seen as an error by Zapier, it uses its "Autoreplay" feature ( [https://zapier.com/help/autoreplay/](https://zapier.com/help/autoreplay/) ) to retry the order, a total of 5 times.

For this reason, you will receive 6 "rejection" messages for the same order.

**Can Zapier be used to send messages back from CartonCloud to my other systems?**

Yes, via our new Webhook system. Let us know what you're trying to achieve so we can assist in getting it up and running.

### **Xero Specific FAQ**

**What is the recommended workflow to get an order from Xero to CartonCloud?**

Orders created in Xero are sent to CartonCloud when Approved. This means you can create orders in a Draft status in Xero, and they will not be sent to CartonCloud until they are Approved. If you create and approve an order in one step, it'll be sent straight to CartonCloud (see the note on the delay below).

**After creating an invoice in Xero, it takes several minutes to show up in CartonCloud, why?**

From our experience, it can take anywhere from 1 to 5 minutes for orders created in Xero to appear in CartonCloud. This is due to the way in which Zapier integrates with Xero. Every 5 minutes Zapier polls Xero for new orders. It fetches the 100 most recent APPROVED invoices (using the "Date" Field to determine most recent), then looks through them looking for any it hasn't seen before and sends those which are new to CartonCloud.

**I Approved an order in Xero which never appeared in CartonCloud**

This can occur due to the way in which the Zapier / Xero connection works. As Zapier polls Xero for the 100 most recent APPROVED invoices (using the "Date" field to determine the most recent), Zapier may miss Invoices from Xero if you create more than 100 with the same Date, or, you approve an invoice whose date is more than 100 invoices old.

![zapier-integration-faq-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/zapier-integration-faq-0.png?width=670&height=340&name=zapier-integration-faq-0.png)

**Workarounds:**

1. If you're creating more than 100 invoices in a single day, we recommend adjusting the date to the next day after you hit 100.
2. If you're approving an old invoice, update the Date to today before approving.

**I created an invoice in Xero, then modified it - but CartonCloud received the modified version?**

Due to the delay in sending outlined above, the invoice is sent to CartonCloud in its current state when the outbound sending is performed.

This means, if you create an invoice, then modify it before Xero has sent it to CartonCloud, it ends up sending CartonCloud the modified version.

However, this doesn't mean you can modify orders in Xero and have the changes reflect in CartonCloud. If the order has already been sent to CartonCloud, changes made in Xero will not be reflected in CartonCloud.

**Can my Invoice PDF be sent to CartonCloud automatically as well?**

Yes. When you have us connect your Xero to CartonCloud, be sure to mention that you want this to be done.

Note that CartonCloud will receive the Invoice PDF in the Branding Theme selected at the time the order is sent to CartonCloud, which may be anywhere from 1-5 mins after the order is actually approved.

We've had a case where a client wanted to send one Branding Theme to CartonCloud, while they wanted another to be printed at their end. They were creating & approving the order in Xero with the branding theme they wanted to have appear in CartonCloud, then immediately editing the invoice to the branding theme they wanted to print. Due to the delay in the Xero API, when Xero sent the invoice to CartonCloud around 1-5 mins later, the Branding Theme selected was the one they wanted to print (not the one they wanted to send).

There are several workarounds for this:

1. Create the invoice with the Branding Theme you want to print, immediately print, then edit the order to the Branding Theme you want to send.
2. After creating the invoice, wait at least 5 minutes (or check that it's appeared in CartonCloud) before editing the Branding Theme
3. Don't automate the Invoice PDFs coming into CartonCloud, and instead email them from Xero into CartonCloud (this can be configured by our staff, just let us know).

---

# Integrations > Parsers

<a id="configuring-parsers-for-customers"></a>
## Configuring Parsers for Customers

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/configuring-parsers-for-customers_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Steps to configure Parsers for customers
  - Add Customer to the Parser
  - Download the Parser template
  - Provide the To Email Address to your customer

### Purpose

Parsers allow your customers to upload or email files into CartonCloud and the order be automatically created. In order for your customers to be able to do this, you will need to configure the Parser to the customer.

### Steps to configure Parsers for Customers

1. Add Customer to the Parser
2. Download the Parser template
3. Provide the To Email Address to your customer

### Add Customer to the Parser

- Navigate to the Parsers page by typing **Parsers** to the Search for Anything bar.
- Click **View** against the Parser you wish to connect to the Customer.

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-0.png?width=670&height=229&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-0.png)

- Click **Add Customer to Parser**.

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-1.png?width=670&height=284&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-1.png)

- Select the relevant Customer from the dropdown **Customer** menu.
- Click **Add**.

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-2.png?width=670&height=147&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-2.png)

Connecting the Customers Email Addresses to the Parser and setting up error email notification

You will now need to add the Customer's email address to the Parser. You can also optionally select if you wish the email address to receive an email if the Parser fails to upload.

It is recommended that you enable the Parser failure email notification. This will help ensure you are notified if an order fails to upload and are able to rectify it as soon as possible.

- Navigate to the Customers page, **Contacts**>**Customers**.
- Select the relevant Customer and click **Edit**.
- Select the **Email** tab.
- Type the email address into the**New email** field.
- Click **Add new email**.

If you don't wish to use a single email address and instead allow multiple email addresses with the same domain, such as '@cartoncloud.com.au', please see the [Wildcard Email Addresses](https://help.cartoncloud.com/#wildcard) section.

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-3.png?width=670&height=156&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-3.png)

- Enter the name for who will be using the email address into the **Name** field.

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-4.png?width=670&height=257&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-4.png)

- Tick the box next to the Parser you wish to associate to the email address. You can also tick Send Failure To if you wish the email address to receive an email if the Parser fails to upload.
- Click **Save**.

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-5.png?width=670&height=222&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-5.png)

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-6.png?width=670&height=113&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-6.png)

Never link two parsers to a single email address which have the same To Address.

This will cause problems because, upon import, the system will not know which Parser to use and will go with whichever Parser it finds first. This leads to unpredictability in how the data is parsed.

**Wildcard Email Addresses**

Wildcard email addresses allow you to configure a particular domain to work with a given parser.

For example, for the above customer Flower Fitness, several people in the business may need to send through the Parser from their email address. In this case, you could set up a wildcard email address for the domain @flowerfitness.com. With this setup, anyone from Flower Fitness with the domain @[flowerfitness.com](http://flowerfitness.com/) in their email address will be able to send through the Parser.

Wildcard email addresses are added by using a * character before the @ symbol. For example:

*@flowerfitness.com

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-7.png?width=670&height=199&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-7.png)

### Download the Parser template

Once you have set up the Parser for the customer, you will need to download and provide your customer with the Parser template. Your customer must use this template to be able to successfully parse an order into CartonCloud.

- Navigate to the Parser page by typing **Parsers** into the **Search for anything**bar.
- Click **View** against the relevant Parser.

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-8.png?width=670&height=225&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-8.png)

- Click the **Download** button.

### Provide the To email address to your Customer

Once you have provided the Parser template to your customers you will also need to provide them with the To Address and ensure their email address is listed in the Allowed From Address(es). Your customer must use the To Address to send through their parser file.

- Navigate to the relevant Customer, **Contacts**>**Customers**.
- Click **Edit**.
- Select the **Parser** tab.
- Within the relevant Parser, you will see the **Allowed From Address(es)**and **To Address**.
  - **Allowed From Address(es)**: the addresses that are allowed to send the Parser in.
  - **To Address**: the email address the user must send the Parser to.

![configuring-parsers-for-customers-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/configuring-parsers-for-customers-10.png?width=670&height=109&name=configuring-parsers-for-customers-10.png)

Understanding To Email Address Formats within Parsers:

Typically, email addresses for parsers are in the format:

{**type**}+{**slug**}@[in.cartoncloud.com.au](http://in.cartoncloud.com.au/)

i.e., sale_orders+bruce_transport@in.cartoncloud.com.au

type:

This is the type of email being loaded in. The type can be anything; however, we generally make it clear as to what the Parser is designed to import because this helps when you have multiple parsers, for example:

sale_orders+bruce_transport@in.cartoncloud.com.au
purchase_orders+bruce_transport@in.cartoncloud.com.au
manifests+bruce_transport@in.cartoncloud.com.au
invoices+bruce_transport@in.cartoncloud.com.au

For this reason, using a type such as "orders" would be a poor choice because when you have clients sending you both Sale Orders and Purchase Orders it is not apparent which email address to send to.

slug:

The slug is the tenant's name as specified in the URL at the top of the page. Email addresses are case-insensitive, so you can use whatever case you want in the email address.

In this example, the slug in the URL is Bruce_Transport. Therefore, the slug in the email address can be bruce_transport, Bruce_Transport, BRUCE_TRANSPORT or any other case variation.

---

<a id="email-setup-for-incoming-emails-parsers-auto-forward-emails"></a>
## Email Setup for Incoming Emails / Parsers / Auto Forward Emails

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/email-setup-for-incoming-emails-parsers-auto-forward-emails_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Setup instructions
- Email account which catches all outgoing emails
- Email account which gets sent a copy of all incoming emails

### Purpose

To set up your CartonCloud account to be able to receive emails with Parsers from Customers.

For example, a Purchase Order Parser can be emailed to the dedicated email address and the file will be imported into CartonCloud and the relevant Purchase Orders will be created in CartonCloud.

### Setup Instructions

Email Setup for Incoming Email (ie: Manifests, Purchase Orders, Sale Orders, Invoices)

You have two options when configuring the email for incoming emails.

1. The email can be sent to an **@[in.cartoncloud.com.au](http://in.cartoncloud.com.au/)** domain.
  - The configuration for emails needs to be anything then a + symbol then the name of the tenancy in CartonCloud, i.e the name top left of the screen then the CartonCloud domain. e.g. manifests+xyzlogistics@in.cartoncloud.com.au
  - Now customers can email files to that address and if setup on [Parsers](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Parsers) the files will be imported using the parser.
2. The email can be sent to your own domain e.g. [xyzlogistics.com.au](http://xyzlogistics.com.au/)

-
  - If you want to use your own domain, ie: manifests@xyzlogistics.com.au, then you'll need to contact your IT department and have them set up an auto-forward keeping the **original sender's details intact.**manifests+xyzlogistics@in.cartoncloud.com.au
  - You will also need to add the email domain under Organisation Settings → Email → Receive incoming mail addressed to the domain ([xyzlogistics.com.au](http://xyzlogistics.com.au/))
  - Now files can be sent to the email of [xyzlogistics.com.au](http://xyzlogistics.com.au/) and they will forward to the CartonCloud email address in the background and be imported using the relevant [Parsers](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Parsers)

Note, you will need to [add the Customer to the Parser and configure the Customer's email address to the Parser](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Configuring-Parsers-for-Customers)for the Customer to be able to email the Parser file into CartonCloud.

**We recommend creating the following email addresses:**

| **Local Email Address** | **Forward to Email Address** |
| --- | --- |
| manifests@xyzlogistics.com.au | manifests+xyzlogistics@in.cartoncloud.com.au |
| sale_orders@xyzlogistics.com.au | sale_orders+xyzlogistics@[in.cartoncloud.com.au](http://in.cartoncloud.com.au/) |
| purchase_orders@xyzlogistics.com.au | purchase_orders+xyzlogistics@[in.cartoncloud.com.au](http://in.cartoncloud.com.au/) |
| invoices@xyzlogistics.com.au | invoices+xyzlogistics@[in.cartoncloud.com.au](http://in.cartoncloud.com.au/) |

- Modify the existing parsers in the system to have the correct "to" address, as per the previous line.

### Email Account which Catches all outgoing emails:

When emails are going out of CartonCloud (to tenants-customers, for things like Invoices, Proof Of Delivery Notifications etc), the tenant themselves has no record of those emails, or nowhere they can go to see them. For this reason, an email address can be set up to receive a copy of all outgoing emails (via BCC). Normally a Gmail account should be used for this, it's easy to just set up something like xyzlogistics_cartoncloud@gmail.com. Staff from xyzlogistics can then log in and search/see the emails.

### Email Account which gets sent a copy of all incoming email:

If the tenant is using the @[in.cartoncloud.com.au](http://in.cartoncloud.com.au/) domain, then they have no way of seeing all incoming emails. For this reason, incoming emails can be forwarded to another email account, normally Gmail as per above. Note: only emails that CartonCloud can correctly identify are for the tenant will be forwarded.

If the tenant is forwarding from their own domain, ie: manifests@xyzlogistics.com.au → manifests+xyzlogistics@in.cartoncloud.com.au, then it's recommended they simply handle the forwarding within their own mail server to send it onto some other Gmail account (thereby forwarding to two different email addresses).

---

<a id="how-to-run-a-report-in-myob-that-lists-so-s-for-a-parser-and-includes-ship-to-address"></a>
## How to Run a Report in MYOB

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-run-a-report-in-myob-that-lists-so-s-for-a-parser-and-includes-ship-to-address_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to steps

### Purpose

This is a guide to extracting Sale from MYOB for import to CartonCloud through a Parser.

### How to steps

We can easily import your sales orders from MYOB through a parser.  To do this you can run an MYOB report, add additional columns to the report, then email it to the nominated parser email address.

Make sure you run the report 'Sales Item Detail Report'.

Click on the Insert/Modify Tab, then click on the Show/Hide button.

This will allow you to add the appropriate fields to the report.

Refresh report.

Then email the report to the nominated email address  e.g. Sales_Order_Clearwater+Tenant_Name@in.cartoncloud.com.au

![how-to-run-a-report-in-myob-that-lists-so-s-for-a-parser-and-includes-ship-to-address-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-run-a-report-in-myob-that-lists-so-s-for-a-parser-and-includes-ship-to-address-0.png?width=670&height=333&name=how-to-run-a-report-in-myob-that-lists-so-s-for-a-parser-and-includes-ship-to-address-0.png)

## List of Parsers

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/list-of-parsers_

### Table of Contents

- List of Parsers
- Standard Parsers ​​​​​​​

### List of Parsers

List of Standard and Specific Parsers including templates and example files.

### Standard Parsers

| **Parser Name** | **Parser Type** | **Description** |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bulk Product Importer | Product | Allows a file type to be read into CartonCloud, which will [create a Product](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/add-a-product) in the system. |  |
| Consignment Items Import Parser *Please note this Parser DOES not support Return and Pickup + Delivery Consignments | Consignment With Items | Create multiple Consignments, over multiple Customers, using zero or many Manifests.  Update existing Consignments for changes that have been made after the original Consignment was imported. See[Updating Consignments via Parser](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Updating-Consignments-via-Parser) for more information.  Import the Consignments into CartonCloud with the allocation information from another  system (if using an allocation system) CartonCloud will then create the Run Sheets and allocation to match |  |
| CSV Manifest with Consignment Items | Manifest With Consignment Items | Import Manifests into CartonCloud. This parser is a basic template which includes all possible fields. It is recommended if you would prefer a user friendly format for your customers that you use the Standard Manifest Booking Form with Drop Downs. |  |
| CSV Purchase Order Parser | Purchase Order | Import Purchase Orders into CartonCloud |  |
| CSV Sale Order Parser | Sale Order | Import Sale Orders into CartonCloud |  |
| POD Parser - QR Code | Consignment Invoice | To receive signed PODs from onforwarders/carriers provided the signed documents contain a QR code overlay. See [Using QR codes to automatically attach a POD to a Consignment](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Using-QR-codes-to-automatically-attach-a-POD-to-a-Consignment) for more information. |  |
| Standard Consignment Items Parser XLS | Consignment With Items | Import Consignments into CartonCloud. This parser allows for different pick up and delivery addresses for each consignment |  |
| Standard Manifest Booking Form with Drop Downs | Manifest With Consignment Items | Import Manifests with Consignment Items into CartonCloud. This Parser uses a user friendly template with drop down options making the Consignment booking process easier for your customers.  Note: this template only allows for one pickup location for all Consignments on the Manifest. |  |

For more information please see [Exporting and Importing Products](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Exporting-and-Importing-Products).  Note: if you are a customer of a CartonCloud account owner please reach out to your CartonCloud account owner for a template. |
| Consignment Items Import Parser | csv | Download [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hubfs/Parser%20Files/Consignment_Items_Import_Parser_Sample_File%20(1).csv) |
