![calculating-warehouse-expenses-plcalculating-warehouse-expenses-4pl-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/calculating-warehouse-expenses-plcalculating-warehouse-expenses-4pl-1.png?width=490&height=293&name=calculating-warehouse-expenses-plcalculating-warehouse-expenses-4pl-1.png)

- Finally, select Link and it will be saved as displayed in the example below.

![calculating-warehouse-expenses-plcalculating-warehouse-expenses-4pl-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/calculating-warehouse-expenses-plcalculating-warehouse-expenses-4pl-2.png?width=609&height=347&name=calculating-warehouse-expenses-plcalculating-warehouse-expenses-4pl-2.png)

- Provided you have linked the Supplier to its correct rates in the Basics tab you are now finished and ready to begin using the Supplier within CartonCloud.
- The Supplier will begin to generate Bills for work it completes so you are able to track expense and income.

### Loading Expenses against Sale Orders, Purchase Orders and Storage Periods

- The rates loaded into the Supplier's Charge card for picking, packing and storage are used when calculating the expenses.
- In addition, Item Charge Data and Adhoc Charges can be modified independently for both Income and Expense:

![calculating-warehouse-expenses-plcalculating-warehouse-expenses-4pl-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/calculating-warehouse-expenses-plcalculating-warehouse-expenses-4pl-3.png?width=501&height=262&name=calculating-warehouse-expenses-plcalculating-warehouse-expenses-4pl-3.png)

---

<a id="charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges"></a>
## Charging a fixed amount for Storage over a Period

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Adding fixed charge
- Charging storage products to be zero charged
  - Change product to be $0 charges
  - Change warehouse location to be $0 charge

### Purpose

Storage Charges can be added for fixed periods. This is to cover situations like charging an area of the warehouse at a fixed rate each week, for example, 500sqm @ $2000.00 per week. Or charging containers at a fixed rate. The goal is to have either this only, or a situation where stock can be stored in these areas without influencing the storage period; i.e. different storage levels, same charges.

### Adding Fixed Charge

Start with adding a New [Product Types](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=530056207), we suggest calling it Fixed Storage.

![charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-0.png?width=670&height=399&name=charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-0.png)

Then make sure the warehouse locations will accept that stock, for example, the warehouse locations that these products are going into.

Also as part of this change, change the Location Product Type to be Fixed Storage as well.

![charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-1.png?width=670&height=463&name=charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-1.png)
Add a new Product called Fixed Storage (or whatever name will be appropriate so on the billing your customer can reconcile the charge).

Use the new Product Type created previously called Fixed Storage. If you understand the concept well here, instead of changing the customer charges for storage later, you could simply change the storage charge here to Per Item and enter the weekly / monthly charge here instead.

![charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-2.png?width=670&height=499&name=charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-2.png)

Now go to the Rate Cards and in the Storage Section assign your rate per storage period. For example, $2000 per week. This would be for 1 x the amount, there is the opportunity to charge per sqm for example, so $2000 / 500, you would have $4.00 instead.

Also in Rate Cards go to the area called Charge Product Type and change from Product to Location. This means that the charging will look at the Warehouse Locations Product Type instead of the products themselves that are loaded in that warehouse location.

This is a major charging change and is best applied when the customer has a consistent charging scheme. i.e. if changing to this charging for a bulk location and other products for the same customer require mixed product type charging not relevant to the warehouse location, i.e. the products are mixed throughout the warehouse location, then this will not work well. Suggestion is to split the customer into 2 different customers for handling/charging purposes or pursue alternative methods.

![charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-3.png?width=670&height=461&name=charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-3.png)
Now go to the Warehouse Location and add the stock directly to it e.g. 1 amount for the full $2000 per storage period charge. This can be handled like normal stock, by removing the stock or editing the amounts to achieve the pricing changes required. Through [Stock Adjustments](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Stock+Adjustments) for example.

![charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-4.png?width=670&height=272&name=charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-4.png)
![charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-5.png?width=670&height=389&name=charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-5.png)

This has created an adjustment Purchase Order for the storage to start and you're away. There will now be a fixed storage charge getting applied to each invoice.

### Changing Storage Products to be Zero Charged

The reason for this is to support the situation where you want the area to have a fixed charge regardless of storage volumes each storage period. There are 2 main ways to achieve this; changing the product to not charge any storage, or changing the location to not charge any storage. Both use the Product Types control the charging.

#### **Change Product to be $0 charges**

Using either the standard Product Types loaded on a product or by creating a unique one for the products being assigned to the locations that have fixed charges, go to the Rate Cards and for the Product Types load a $0.00 charge for their storage.

So you should end up with the new Product Type having the fixed storage amount as described above, and then the actual customer products having no charge.

#### **Change Warehouse Location to be $0 charge**

Similar to the other process, the difference is that the Product Type to be $0.00 charged on the customer charges is the Warehouse Location Product Type instead. Still change in the customer charges, but also swap the charging methodology from the Product to the location instead.

![charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-6.png?width=670&height=461&name=charging-a-fixed-amount-for-storage-over-a-period-monthly-weekly-storage-charges-6.png)

**Using Warehouse Locations Groups**

Another method is to use the groups option to combine multiple operational warehouse locations into less for charging purposes.

---

<a id="charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments"></a>
## Charging for Futile / Cancelled / Redelivered Consignments

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Duplicate the consignment
- Manually add additional charge(s)
- Add an adhoc charge for attempted delivery

### Purpose

If you attempt to perform a delivery which is unsuccessful, and you wish to charge the client for the delivery, plus a potential redelivery (so the charges are applied twice), there are three ways to achieve this:

### Duplicate the Consignment

By duplicating the Consignment, you create two jobs which are billable. We recommend duplicating the consignment if you're performing a delivery and need to charge both the original job and the new job.

Duplicating a Consignment can be done from the Duplicate Button within the View Consignment screen:

![charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-0.png?width=420&height=153&name=charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-0.png)
The, mark the original consignment as "Reissued".

![charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-1.png?width=480&height=263&name=charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-1.png)
The new consignment you have created can now be allocated to a driver as per normal, and both jobs will be charged.

### Manually Add Additional Charge(s)

You can also manually add charges to a consignment from the Charges Tab within the Consignment.

![charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-2.png?width=507&height=361&name=charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-2.png)

### Add an Adhoc Charge for Attempted Delivery

If you have a standard fee you apply for attempting a delivery, you can simply add a new

Adhoc Charge

, and give it a descriptive name, such as "Futile Delivery Fee", you can then simply reallocate the original consignment. This adhoc charge will then be applied

in addition

to the standard Consignment Rates.

![charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-3.png?width=670&height=315&name=charging-for-futile-cancelled-redelivered-consignments-3.png)

---

<a id="charging-methods"></a>
## Charging Methods

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/charging-methods_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Charging Methods

### Purpose

When adding charges to your Transport Rate you will have a number of different charging methods to choose from. The below steps through each charging method with examples to assist you when selecting a charging method and configuring the charge.

### Charging Methods

**Flat Fee**

A flat fee allows you to charge a single fixed fee regardless of usage. This means that irrespective of how far or how large the delivery is, the fee will always be charged. This is useful if you have a delivery basic charge that you charge for all deliveries regardless of the type, location, or size of the consignment.

**Flat Fee example:**Basic Charge of $12.

![charging-methods-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-0.png?width=670&height=126&name=charging-methods-0.png)
**Per Charge**

Per charge charging method will charge per the set variable you select. For example, if you have a delivery fee of $10.00 per pallet the $10.00 value will be applied by the number of pallets on the consignment. You can charge per item (cubic, pallets, quantity, space, weight), invoice value, distance traveled, hours taken, or COD value.

**Per Charge Distance Travelled example:**A delivery fee of $0.80 per distance traveled.

![charging-methods-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-1.png?width=670&height=138&name=charging-methods-1.png)
**Per Charge Invoice Value example:**A delivery fee that is 4% of the invoice value.

![charging-methods-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-2.png?width=670&height=126&name=charging-methods-2.png)

**Conditional**

Conditional charging is a more complex yet highly configurable charging method. It allows you to configure rates based upon specified conditions. For example, for a hand unload charge, you can set the charge to be applied when the word 'hand' is included in the delivery instructions.

To configure the conditional charge:

- Select a conditional variable from the drop-down menu. Select the one that is most appropriate for the condition you are configuring.
- Select the logical operator which should be applied (for example, equal to or greater than). The type of logical operators you have to choose from will depend on what conditional variable you selected in the previous field.
- Select the second condition variable to complete the condition equation.

**Conditional Charge example:** Hand Unload Charge of $25.00 per pallet that is applied when the instructions contain the word 'Hand'.

![charging-methods-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-3.png?width=670&height=175&name=charging-methods-3.png)
**Sliding Scale**

A sliding scale charging method allows you to apply varying rates depending on the quantity of a particular item. For example, you may wish to charge more for the first pallet and then progressively charge less for each additional number of pallets you deliver.

To configure the sliding scale:

- Select the variable the sliding scale will apply to (for example, pallets).
- Select **Add Scale Range.**
- Set the ranges in which your scale will be divided by filling in the **Up to** field. The **Greater than** field will auto-populate with the last number from your previous range.
- Fill in the rate for each scale range and how you will charge each range (per in range charge, per charge, or flat fee).

![charging-methods-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-4.png?width=670&height=293&name=charging-methods-4.png)
**Per In Range Charge**

By selecting the per in range charge type, CartonCloud will only charge the fees associated with the range that the quantity applies to and not include charges specified in any other ranges.

In the below example, if you have 14 cartons the charge applied will come from the scale range of 10 - 25, however, only 3 cartons will be charged as only 3 cartons sit within that scale range (14 - 10 = 3). Therefore, the charge will be 3 cartons at $3.00 per carton plus the $25 flat fee included within that scale range. Charges from the previous scale will not be included. Similarly, if you have 55 cartons the charge will be 5 (55 - 50=5) cartons at $3.00 per carton and the $50 flat within that scale range.

![charging-methods-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-5.png?width=567&height=354&name=charging-methods-5.png)
 **Non-Cumulative vs Culmative Sliding Scale**

There are two ways in which a Sliding Scale can be configured, it can be either **cumulative** or **non-cumulative**. When creating a sliding scale, the default is non-cumulative. If you wish to use a cumulative scale you need to click the black arrow (the arrow will turn white with a black circle around it) within the applicable range to set the range as cumulative.
![charging-methods-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-6.png?width=670&height=143&name=charging-methods-6.png)
**Non-Cumulative**

Non-cumulative means that a single price will be applied to all pallets.

For example, you may charge freight in the following way:

| **Pallets** | **Price** |
| --- | --- |
| 1-2 | $50 / per pallet |
| 3-5 | $45 / per pallet |
| 6-10 | $40 / per pallet |
| 11+ | $38 / per pallet |

If you had 6 pallets, the charge would be 6 x $40 = $240.

![charging-methods-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-7.png?width=670&height=364&name=charging-methods-7.png)

**Cumulative**

Cumulative means that various prices will be applied to the pallets within the scale.

For example, you may charge freight in the following way:

| **Pallets** | **Price** |
| --- | --- |
| 1-2 | $50 / per pallet |
| 3-5 | $45 / per pallet for every pallet after the 2nd |
| 6-10 | $40 / per pallet for every pallet after the 5th |
| 11+ | $38 / per pallet for every pallet after the 10th |

If you had 6 pallets the charge would be: (2 x $50) + (3 x $45) + (1 x $40) = $275.

![charging-methods-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-8.png?width=670&height=363&name=charging-methods-8.png)
**Item Code**

Item code charging is only available if you are configuring a **Consignment Item Charge Group.**

Item Code charging will charge each individual consignment item line on the Consignment.

**To configure an Item Code:**

- Select **Item Code.**

![charging-methods-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-9.png?width=670&height=456&name=charging-methods-9.png)

- Select the applicable consignment item from the drop-down menu.
- Then select **Add Charge for Item Codes**. A drop-down will appear and you will have four different charge methods to choose from (use the above descriptions when selecting the appropriate charge configuration).

![charging-methods-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-10.png?width=670&height=343&name=charging-methods-10.png)

- In the below example, a different charge has been set up for each different consignment item (beverage carton, beverage pallet, and oversized pallet).

![charging-methods-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/charging-methods-11.png?width=670&height=418&name=charging-methods-11.png)

---

<a id="charging-storage-for-consumables"></a>
## Charging Storage for Consumables

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/charging-storage-for-consumables_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Customers storage charges for consumables
- Your own storage charges for consumables or office files

### Purpose

Storage Charges can be added for storage of customers Consumables or unusual products such as Office Files etc. Also, a similar procedure can be used to load storage in and not charge it, so it can show locations accurately and allow you to locate stock such as your own office files but not charge storage for them.

### Customers Storage Charges for Consumables

To charge consumables they need to be entered as a Product, have their own Product Type assigned if they are charged differently to the rest of the Customer's Products and then have a different storage charge for the new Product Type if it's different as well.

1. If the storage rates are different from existing products then first create a new Product Types called Consumables for example. Skip this step if the rates are to be the same as existing products for the customer. To charge Consumables at different rates i.e. different sized pallets or different types requiring different pricing, create more Product Types to cater to this e.g. Consumables, Consumables Large etc.
2. Load a Product called Consumables (load more names if there are multiple types of them you want to easily identify as well), here is how to load Products . Make the Base Unit of Measurement a Pallet → this is to make management easier, so you can just put 1 pallet in each warehouse location to be charged. However, you can still use Cartons/Units etc if desired to track in more detail. Use the Product Type of Consumables created before, create more Products and use extra Product Types if multiple pricing is required. Generally use the Storage Charge type of Per Location (and bulk location pallets) for the product.
3. Go to the Rate Cards and then the Storage Tab to load a price for the storage of each pallet of consumables in the locations. Load the charge against the Consumables Product Type created earlier, and load different pricing for the different types as needed. Note to track but not charge the customer, simply enter $0 as the charge for these.
4. Create a Purchase Order and load in the Quantity of each Product created before, allocating a quantity of 1 pallet to each location that needs charging. Complete fully to allocated. To not charge your customers for this if required, go to the Charges tab of the Purchase Order and remove the automatic charging, then select Finalize Charges button to stop the system trying to calculate charges, on top of this a Misc Charge with Description of No Charge and value of $0.00 can be added too. An alternative to this is loading stock through the Stock Adjustments , through the Warehouse Location Adding Stock to a Warehouse Location through Stock Adjustments .

From here you can add more Purchase Orders and do [Stock Quantity Adjustments](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Stock+Adjustments) or [Sale Orders](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Sale+Orders) to increase and decrease the locations and pallets being charged each week as needed.

### Your own Storage Charges for Consumables or Office Files

Similar to above with an extra step of creating an internal [Customer](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Setting+up+a+Customer) so none of your customers get charged. For Example, Demo Logistics if your company was called Demo Logistics and then follow steps as above for tracking and location filling purposes without affecting customers. This gives you the benefit of accurate warehouse capacity statistics and checking empty locations for missing revenue for example.

---

<a id="charging-storage-for-different-sized-items"></a>
## Charging Storage for different sized items

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/charging-storage-for-different-sized-items_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Background
- How to steps

### Purpose

To charge for products of different sizes.

### Background

If you have products which take up more than one physical pallet space, you'll likely want to charge more for their storage.

Each product type in CartonCloud can be charged different storage rates, so by creating a new product type, you can then define a new storage rate specifically for this type of product - it could be less or more than your normal storage rates.

### How to Steps

1. Add new Product Types for the different sized items. For example, Standard Pallet and Oversize Pallet.
2. Add a new Product (which is of the new Product Types)
3. Enable Warehouse Locations to allow the new Product Type to be stored there.
4. Create new storage charges for the new Product Types

---

<a id="consignment-adhoc-charges"></a>
## Consignment Adhoc Charges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/consignment-adhoc-charges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Loading consignment adhoc charges
- Where to submit consignment adhoc charges
  - Adhoc charges view
  - POD + adhoc charges
  - Error + adhoc charges
  - Running adhoc charge timers

### Purpose

Within the CartonCloud mobile apps, drivers have the option to submit Adhoc Charges for a Consignment, either as part of a POD or Error or from the separate Adhoc Charges view. Watch the video to see how it works!

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/juxMBhrVgm8

### Loading Consignment Adhoc Charges

Consignment Adhoc Charge details get loaded when the user refreshes the Consignment list. Current values for Adhoc Charges will not be deleted on refreshing Consignments unless a more recent Adhoc Charge value is available in the CartonCloud system. Likewise, running timers are never deleted when refreshing from the server.

This makes it possible to preload Adhoc Charges for Consignments in the web app that will appear on the driver's mobile app on refreshing Consignments and entering a Consignment Detail view.

For more information on how to Adhoc Charges in the CartonCloud web app, check out these articles: [Adhoc Charge Setup](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Adhoc+Charge+Setup), [View Rate Card](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/View+Rate+Card).

### Where to Submit Consignment Adhoc Charges

Adhoc Charges can also be submitted after grouping several consignments within the mobile app and proceeding to the detail view. If this is the case, then the recorded Adhoc Charges will be applied to all consignments within the group.

#### **Adhoc Charges View**

On viewing a consignment's details, if adhoc charges are available for the tenant, an additional menu tab will be available. Swipe from right to left on the tab bar and see the **Adhoc Charges** option. Click the **Adhoc Charges**button to see all adhoc charges. You can enter the adhoc charges from this screen and click **Submit All**.

![consignment-adhoc-charges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/consignment-adhoc-charges-0.png?width=223&height=455&name=consignment-adhoc-charges-0.png)

#### **POD + Adhoc Charges**

When recording a POD for a consignment and if there are adhoc charges available for the tenant, an optional tick box will be displayed as part of the **Record POD** view.

The tick box is automatically selected if values have been previously entered for any of the adhoc charges.

The values for the adhoc charges can be edited by tapping the value fields (or, in the case of a timer, also starting / stopping / resetting the timer).

The adhoc charges will only be submitted as part of the POD if the tick box is activated.

![consignment-adhoc-charges-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/consignment-adhoc-charges-1.png?width=267&height=547&name=consignment-adhoc-charges-1.png)

#### **Error + Adhoc Charges**

When lodging an Error for a consignment and if there are adhoc charges available for the tenant, an optional tick box will be displayed as part of the **Lodge Error** view.

The tick box is automatically selected if values have been previously entered for any of the adhoc charges.

The values for the adhoc charges can be edited by tapping the value fields (or, in the case of a timer, also starting / stopping / resetting the timer).

If the tick box is activated, the adhoc charges will only be submitted as part of the Error.

![consignment-adhoc-charges-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/consignment-adhoc-charges-2.png?width=265&height=542&name=consignment-adhoc-charges-2.png)

#### **Editing & Saving Adhoc Charges**

Adhoc Charges can either be Text Fields, Sliding Scales or Timers.

![consignment-adhoc-charges-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/consignment-adhoc-charges-3.png?width=292&height=583&name=consignment-adhoc-charges-3.png)
Text Fields and Sliding Scales are represented by a Text Field that can be edited by typing into it, while a Timer has a value display plus several action buttons to start/stop/reset buttons. To upload all adhoc charges for the job, select **Save All**.

To manually edit a timer value, tap the timer value field and it will bring up an edit view that allows you to select a time between 1 minute & 24 hours. On selecting "Save," this value will be shown in the timer's value field.

![consignment-adhoc-charges-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/consignment-adhoc-charges-4.png?width=275&height=489&name=consignment-adhoc-charges-4.png)

#### **Running Adhoc Charge Timers**

An adhoc charge timer can be kept running while navigating away from a consignment detail view. It will keep running until stopped or until the consignment is removed from the job list.

When navigating away from a consignment detail view with an active timer, an alert is displayed to warn of any running timers and offer several options on how to proceed:

- **Keep Timers Running:**All running timers will continue running.
- **Discard Timer Values:**All running timers will be stopped and their values discarded, i.e. they will be reset to 0.
- **Stop Timers & Save Value:** All running timers will be stopped and their current value submitted to the CartonCloud system.

Selecting any of these options will only affect running timers. Any timers that have been previously stopped will retain their values and not change their current state.

![consignment-adhoc-charges-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/consignment-adhoc-charges-5.png?width=297&height=528&name=consignment-adhoc-charges-5.png)
If there is one or multiple running timers for a consignment, a timer icon will be shown in the consignment list for the affected consignment.

![consignment-adhoc-charges-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/consignment-adhoc-charges-6.png?width=294&height=521&name=consignment-adhoc-charges-6.png)

---

<a id="consignment-charges"></a>
## Consignment Charges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/consignment-charges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Description for the Consignment Charges tab

### Purpose

The Consignment Charges will display what charges are associated with the order and provide greater visibility and a breakdown of the rates applied. In addition, you can add and amend charges related to the Consignment.  CartonCloud will attempt to calculate the charges as soon as the Consignment is created. This allows you to see the charges straight away.

### Description for the consignment charges tab

![consignment-charges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/consignment-charges-0.png?width=670&height=309&name=consignment-charges-0.png)

| **Field name** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| **Details** |  |
| Finalise Charges | If you wish to finalise the charges and prevent changes from being made to the charges on the Consignment, then tick this box. This will be the final amount shown on the invoice. If you do need to make changes, simply untick the box. |
| Assigned to Invoice | If the invoice has been assigned to an invoice, the invoice number will show here, and you will be able to click through to it. There will also be link text of (remove from invoice) which, by clicking, will remove the Consignment from the invoice. If it has not yet been assigned to an invoice, it will read 'Not yet assigned to an invoice'. |
| Rate Card (Income) | The Rate Card that the rates applied to the Consignment are being pulled from. |
| Charge Errors | If the Consignment has charge errors or not. This field will read as 'No' if there is no charge error. If there is a charge error, it will specify the error such as 'No matching rate found'. It will also show the appropriate from and to rate zones and income zones alongside the error.  You will need to resolve the charge error before the consignment charges can be finalised. |
| Income | The total income value. |
| Income Rate Zones | If the income rate zones have been automatically determined, this will read 'Automatically Determine - > Automatically Determine'. If there has been a manual adjustment and the income zone has been overridden, then the name of the income zones will show here. For example, 'Gold Coast Depot - > SC'. If the zones are automatically determined, they are applying the logic configured when[associating location entries to income zones](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/creating-location-entries-transport-zone-set). If the Consignment is using a [cascading Rate Card](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/0QlwIg), then the zones shown will be from the cascading Rate Card. |
| **Charges** |  |
| +Add Income Charge | To add a manual charge to the Consignment. These are outside the automatically applied charges from the Rate Card. For more information on how to add an income charge, please click [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/swM3I). |
| Automatically Calculate Income Charges | By clicking this button, charges will be re-calculated. |
| Type | The type of charge, Income or Expense. |
| Automatic | If the charge was calculated automatically (from the Rate Card), there will be a tick in this column. |
| Fee Category | The [Fee Category](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/Mo-cI) associated with the charge. |
| Description | A description or breakdown of the charges. For example, 2 Cartons @ $9.00 total ($18.00). |
| Collect Zone | It will show the Rate Zone (the zone determined from the transport rate being utilised) and Income Zone (the zone determined from the 'from' address) being utilised for the collection address. It will be displayed in the Rate Zone (Income Zone) format. For example, Gold Coast (Gold Coast South). |
| Deliver Zone | It will show the Rate Zone (the zone determined from the transport rate being utilised) and Income Zone (the zone determined from the 'to' or delivery address) being utilised for the delivery address. It will be displayed in the Rate Zone (Income Zone) format. For example, Gold Coast (Gold Coast South). |
| Zone Set | The Zone Set name being used to determine the income zones for the Consignment. This helps to identify how the charge is being matched. |
| Income | The income dollar value for the charge. |
| Apply Fuel Levy | If the fuel levy has been applied or not. |
| Fuel Levy | The fuel levy calculation (if a fuel levy is applicable to the charge). The breakdown of the fuel levy will show with the percentage and cost of the fuel levy. |
| **Adhoc Charge Data** |  |
| Name | The name of the Adhoc charge |
| Qty | The quantity associated with the adhoc charge (how many times will the charge be applied) |
| Unit Price | The unit price of the charge (what applying this charge once will equal) |
| Total | The total dollar value against the charge (for example, if the charge had a quantity of 2 then the unit price will be multiplied by 2 to get the total price) |
| Action | If updating the quantity against the charge you will need to click the green save button in the action column that will appear when you make the adjustment. |

---

<a id="creating-a-storage-periodcreating-a-storage-period"></a>
## Creating a Storage Period

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/creating-a-storage-periodcreating-a-storage-period_

Based on your selection of Invoicing and Storage Charge settings, CartonCloud will generate the Storage Periods and add these automatically to Customer Invoiced. Sometimes though, there may be instances when you need to manually create a Storage Period which will later go on an invoice.

- To begin, type Storage Periods into Search for Anything!
- Once on the page, select +Add Storage Period
- Enter the details for the Customer and Date.

![creating-a-storage-periodcreating-a-storage-period-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-a-storage-periodcreating-a-storage-period-0.png?width=361&height=305&name=creating-a-storage-periodcreating-a-storage-period-0.png)

- Then select Create Storage Period and the storage period will instantly be created within the system.

![creating-a-storage-periodcreating-a-storage-period-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-a-storage-periodcreating-a-storage-period-1.png?width=670&height=204&name=creating-a-storage-periodcreating-a-storage-period-1.png)

- Now that the storage period is created it will automatically go onto the next generated invoice.
- However, you can also choose to manually add it to an invoice if you wish.

---

<a id="error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly"></a>
## Error! You may be under-charging this consignment

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly_

### Table of Contents

- Issue
- Why the issue has occurred
- Example scenario
- How to fix the charge error

### Issue

By default, CartonCloud will raise a charge error whenever an item code is included on a consignment

but does not have a charge associated with it

.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-0.png?width=670&height=43&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-0.png)

### Why the issue has occurred

There are two common reasons why this error could occur:

1. When a Transport Product is added to a Consignment, but the Transport Product's quantity is entered incorrectly (against the incorrect column within the Consignment Items tab or if the value is left off completely).
2. If the Transport Product on the Consignment does not have an associated rate in the relevant rate card (when using the Item Code charging method).

### Example Scenario

In this example scenario, a consignment has been booked going from the Gold Coast to Brisbane.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-1.png?width=670&height=345&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-1.png)

A transport rate has been set up in the Customer's Rate Card to charge $80 per Item Code of "Pallet" or "Skid", with the quantity (per) calculated by the value entered in the "Pallet" column of a consignment.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-2.png?width=670&height=225&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-2.png)

However, this example consignment has the number of transport products entered incorrectly, with the number of skids being entered against the "Qty" column, rather than the "Pal" column.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-3.png?width=670&height=142&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-3.png)

As there is no rate set up for the Transport Code "Skid" to define a charge for

Skids x Qty

the system detects this and produces a consignment charge error as displayed below.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-4.png?width=670&height=43&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-4.png)

The same charge error message can also be found within the Consignment Charges tab, in the details section.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-5.png?width=670&height=296&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-5.png)

### How to fix the charge error

There are three ways to resolve a charge error.

**1) Clear the charge error**

If you are certain that no charges are missing then you can clear the charge error by selecting **(clear)** on the Consignment charges tab, within the details section.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-6.png?width=670&height=210&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-6.png)
**2) Check that Consignment Items are entered correctly**

The most common cause of the error is that the number of items has been entered within the wrong column in the Consignment Items tab.

For example, if it's a pallet causing the error, then you will need to check that the number of pallets is entered under the "Pal" column (assume that is how rates have been configured).

If you are unsure of how rates have been configured, then follow the steps below to check.

**Check the rate for the transport product**

To check how a transport product is set up to be charged in CartonCloud:

- Navigate to the associated transport rate by selecting the Rate ID link.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-7.png?width=670&height=296&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-7.png)

- Scroll down until you find the charges related to the item codes on the consignment.
- Check which column on the consignment item tab the transport produce is set up to rate by checking the "Per" field.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-8.png?width=670&height=232&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-8.png)

- In this example, we can see that a charge has been set up to calculate the transport codes "Pallets" and "Skids" based on the number entered in the "Pal" column.
- Now, amend the values entered against the consignment item columns as required so that the values are recorded against the appropriate column.
- Then, on the Charges tab of the Consignment, select **Automatically Calculate Income Charges.**

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-9.png?width=670&height=108&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-9.png)

- The consignment's charges should now calculate successfully and can be saved by selecting **Save**.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-10.png?width=670&height=321&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-10.png)

- The charge error should now disappear from within the Consignment's Charges tab and charges will display.

**3) Check there is an associated rate for all Consignment Items**

- To check the Rate Card for an associated Consignment Item rate:
- Navigate to the associated transport rate by selecting the Rate ID link.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-11.png?width=670&height=296&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-11.png)

- Check the rate to see if all relevant Transport Products on the consignment have an Item Code Charge.
- If a Transport Product on your Consignment does not have an Item Code Charge, add a new Item Code Charge for the relevant Transport Product.
- Navigate back to the Consignment and on the Charges tab of the Consignment, select **Automatically Calculate Income Charges.**

**![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-12.png?width=670&height=108&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-12.png)**

- The consignment's charges should now calculate successfully and can be saved by selecting **Save**.

**3) Enable 'Allow Zero Charge'**

If you have Item Codes or Charge Groups that you do not always charge for then they can be configured to 'Allow Zero Charge' from within the transport rate. By allowing zero charges, CartonCloud will not classify that particular Item Code, Charge Group, or rate (depending on how you configure the zero charges) as an error when the charge is $0.

There are three different locations on the transport rate where you can allow zero charging.

![error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-13](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-13.png?width=670&height=413&name=error-you-may-be-under-charging-this-consignment-please-check-all-items-have-been-entered-correctly-13.png)

1. **Rate based 'Allow Zero Charges'** -
  - Enabled: no error will be raised if no charges are calculated (unless certain Charge Groups or Item Codes are turned off then they will not be included).
  - Turned off: an error message will appear if there is no charge calculated.
2. **Charge Group based 'Allow Zero Charges'** - there are two types of Charge Groups, Consignment Item, and Consignment Totals, and 'Allow Zero Charges' works differently for the two.
  1. **Charge Group based on Consignment Item:**
    - Enabled: no error will be raised if no charges are calculated for the charges within that Charge Group (unless certain Item Codes within that Charge Group are turned off then they will not be included) and any items on the Consignment that do not have a charge will be skipped.
    - Turned off: an error message will appear if there are no charges for the Consignment Items within the Charge Group.
  2. **Charge Group based on Consignment Total:**
    - Enabled: no error will be raised if no charges are calculated for the charges within that Charge Group.
    - Turned off: an error message will appear if there is no charge calculated for that Charge Group.
3. **Item Code based 'Allow Zero Charges'** - this option is only available for Charge Group Consignment Item charges.
  - Enabled: if there is no charge with that particular Consignment Item then no error message will appear.
  - Turned off: an error message will appear if there is no charge associated with that Consignment Item.

To configure the Transport Rate to allow zero charging:

- Navigate to the associated Transport Rate by selecting the Rate ID link.

- Select if you wish to configure zero charges by the entire rate, Charge Groups, and/or Item Codes.
- Select the 3 vertical dot icon on the relevant charge, then enable the **Allow zero charge** toggle.

---

<a id="flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order"></a>
## Flexible Handling Charges - Sale Order & Purchase Order

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order_

When you pick a Sales Order or put away a Purchase Order, CartonCloud will automatically calculate what it thinks was handled. For example, if you were told to pick 50 Cartons of Cherries, and the product has been loaded such that 50 Cartons = 1 Pallet, CartonCloud will automatically detect that you've performed a pallet pick and charge handling accordingly.

There are situations, however, where it's much harder to figure out what was really done.

For example, you have a pallet that contains 50 boxes of Cherries, and you're told to pick 35 cartons. You could either pick 35 cartons off the pallet onto a new pallet (and charge for handling 35 cartons), or you might choose to shift the whole pallet, and then off-load 15 cartons back into the location (and charge for handling 1 pallet + 15 cartons).

For this reason, you're able to manually specify the number of Cartons, Pallets (or any other measurements) handled from within the Sale Order, and this then updates the charging.

![flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order-0.png?width=670&height=418&name=flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order-0.png)

To make the change to 1 Pallet and 15 Cartons simply change the quantities:

![flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order-1.png?width=398&height=429&name=flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order-1.png)
**Why can't I change the Unit Price?**

The Unit Price shows, but cannot be modified because this would break the presentation of the Invoice Summary, in which a table is built that contains both the default prices and the total quantities picked within the period:

![flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order-2.png?width=670&height=319&name=flexible-handling-charges-sale-order-purchase-order-2.png)
If you do need to change the price, go into the Customer Charges and make changes to the handling fees there, then simply click the Reset button and the default rates will be reloaded from the Customer's Rate Card.

---

<a id="fuel-levy"></a>
## Fuel Levy

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/fuel-levy_

### Table of Contents

- Overview
- Fuel Levy Application Level
- How to create a Base Fuel Levy (Fuel Price History page)
- How to apply Rate Card and Transport Rates Fuel Levy
- How to activate the Fuel Levy
- Fuel Levy calculation

### Overview

A fuel levy is a surcharge applied to your transport rates. In CartonCloud there are three levels at which the fuel levy can be adjusted. Control over whether a fuel levy is applied at all is controlled by the Transport Rate.

### Fuel Levy Application Level

| **Level** | **Application** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Base Fuel Levy (Master %) | Applied to all transport rates within the account, unless overridden by the Rate Card or Transport Rate fuel levy. | The base fuel levy can be updated from the Fuel Price History page. From this page you can also view the history of fuel price changes. Read [Fuel Price History page](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Fuel-Levy#how-to-create-a-base-fuel-levy) for more information |
| Rate Card Fuel Levy Offset | Applied to all Transport Rates within the Rate Card, unless overridden by the Transport Rate fuel levy. | Navigate to the Rate Card page by typing **Rate Card** in the Search for anything bar. If editing an existing Rate Card, select the applicable rate card and **Edit** at the bottom of the screen. You will then be able to select if you wish to ignore the base fuel levy and create a new levy or add an offset to the base fuel levy. |
| Transport Rate Fuel Levy / Offset | Appliedd to all charges within that Transport Rate. | Navigate to the Rate Card page by typing '**Rate Card**' in the Search for anything bar. Select the applicable Rate Card and then the applicable Transport Rate (from within the Transport Rates tab of the Rate Card) or create a new one.  You can either configure the fuel levy to Rate (this will override all other set levies with the rate you input) or Offset (this will add an offset on top of the existing fuel levy (based on the above two levels)). |

### How to create a Base Fuel Levy (Fuel Price History page)

You create the Base Fuel Levy from the Fuel Price History page. To do this;

- Type in Fuel Levy into the Search for anything bar.
- Here you will see the current and past Fuel Levy. To create a new one click the blue Change button in the top left corner.

![fuel-levy-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/fuel-levy-0.png?width=670&height=284&name=fuel-levy-0.png)

- Enter the Fuel Levy (Percentage) .

![fuel-levy-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/fuel-levy-1.png?width=670&height=167&name=fuel-levy-1.png)

- If you wish to set a Start Date and/or End Date. Tick the 'Set Start/End Date of Fuel Price' option. Enter the relevant date. If you do not enter a date the Start Date will default to now and the End Date will default to never end.

![fuel-levy-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/fuel-levy-2.png?width=670&height=233&name=fuel-levy-2.png)

- Click Save .

**Points to consider:**

- Any Fuel Levy that was created prior to end of November 2023 will not show a start or end date.
- If you enter a Fuel Levy and set a future date it will override any existing fuel levy with the same future date.

### How to apply Rate Card and Transport Rates Fuel Levy

To make changes to the Fuel Levy at the Rate Card and Transport Rates level you will need to do so from within the Rate Card and the Transport Rate itself.

| **Rate** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Fuel Levy Offset (Rate Card & Transport Rate) | The amount that should be applied to the base fuel levy. For example, if the base fuel levy is 12% but you wish a customer to always be 2% higher, you would apply a Fuel Levy Offset of 2% to their associated Rate Card. On the other hand, if you require the customer to track 2% below the base fuel levy, you would apply an offset of -2%.  **Fuel Levy offsets can be set on both the Rate Card and Transport Rate and will be added together if set on both.** |
| Ignore Base Setting (Rate Card) | If you just want to fix the fuel levy for a customer, then setting "Ignore Base Setting" to Yes will cause the Fuel Levy Offset to be applied to a base fuel levy of 0.   For example, if you want to charge a 7% fuel levy regardless of what your overall Fuel Levy is, then you would set "Ignore Base Setting" to Yes, and Fuel Levy Offset to 7%. |
| Rate (Transport Rate) | From the dropdown menu, you can select either Offset or Rate. By selecting rate, you can apply a completely new fuel levy from the Rate Card and Base fuel levy. |

### How to activate the Fuel Levy

To activate the fuel levy simply select the fuel icon (it will turn green) next to the applicable charge. You will need to do this for every applicable charge within the Transport Rate. The fuel icon will be green when it is being applied and will stay grey when it is not being applied.

**Fuel levy not active (no fuel levy will be applied to this charge):**

![fuel-levy-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/fuel-levy-3.png?width=670&height=75&name=fuel-levy-3.png)

### Fuel Levy calculation

The calculation of the fuel levy will be shown on the Consignment charges tab against the Consignment. The fuel levy percentage and dollar amount being charged will show in the calculations.

The below video covers how to apply a fuel levy.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/Ba8fFU79pik

---

<a id="how-to-aggregate-handling-charges"></a>
## How to aggregate Handling Charges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-aggregate-handling-charges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Aggregation options
- Use case
- How to set up aggregation for handling charges
  - Step 1: enable aggregation form the rate card settings
  - Step 2: add handling charge

### Purpose

To aggregate Handling Charges by SKU by location or across the entire warehouse.

### Aggregation Options

| **Picking Charge Aggregation Options** | **Explanation** | **Import/Export Option Name** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| No aggregation (default) | Products will not aggregate together. | NONE |
| Aggregate by SKU by Location | CartonCloud will aggregate all products for a Sale Order in the same location, regardless of whether they are from different POP records. | LOCATION |
| Aggregate by SKU across the entire warehouse | Regardless of what location the products for a Sale Order are in, they will be aggregated. | WAREHOUSE |

**An example where 6 bottles = 1 case**

| **Location** | **Pick / Put-away Quantity** | **No aggregation (default)** | **Aggregate by SKU by Location** | **Aggregate by SKU across the entire warehouse** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AA-01-01 | 5 | Charge 5 Bottles | Combine 7 bottles together (5+2) which converts to 1 Bottle + 1 Case Charge 1 Case + 1 Bottle | Combine 12 bottles together (5+2+5), which converts to 2 cases. Charge 2 Cases |
| AA-01-01 | 2 | Charge 2 Bottles |  |  |
| AA-01-04 | 5 | Charge 5 Bottles | Charge 5 Bottles |  |
| **Total Charge** |  | **12 Bottles** | **6 Bottles + 1 Case** | **2 Cases** |

### Use Case

Scenario: You are a wine distributor that sells to your customers down to the bottle level.

If your customer orders a case of wine, and there is less than a case left against one POP record, CartonCloud will charge for bottle picks rather than a full case because it is splitting the charges down to bottles. You would then need to manually amend these charges to reflect what was ordered and actually picked. With aggregation turned on, CartonCloud will combine the number of bottles against the Sale Order (either by location or the entire warehouse) and look at it collectively rather than individually and charge in that manner. This means your charging better reflects what was actually picked.

### How to set up aggregation for Handling Charges

#### **Step 1: Enable aggregation from the Rate Card settings**

Before creating your Handling Charges you will need to enable aggregation from the Rate Card of the relevant Customer. To do so:

- Navigate to the Rate Card page, **More**>**Rate Cards**.
- Select the relevant Rate Card.

Ensure the Customer you are wishing to set up aggregation for is associated with the Rate Card

- Select the blue **Edit** button at the bottom of the Rate Card.
- Within the **Purchase Order** box, select **Default (Scale to Highest Unit Of Measure)**from the **Putaway Charge Option** dropdown.
- Select the aggregation option from the**Putaway Charge Aggregation** dropdown.

![how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-0.png?width=670&height=112&name=how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-0.png)

- Scroll down to the **Sale Order**box, select **Default (Scale to Highest Unit Of Measure)**from the **Putaway Charge Option** dropdown.
- Select the aggregation option from the**Putaway Charge Aggregation** dropdown.
- Scroll down to the bottom and click the green **Save** button.

#### **Step 2: Add Handling Charge**

- Navigate to the relevant Rate Card and select the **Handling Charges** tab.
- Select **+Add Handling Charge**.

![how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-1.png?width=670&height=132&name=how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-1.png)

- Select the**Product Unit of Measure.**
- Select the **Charge Behaviour, Charge this Unit Of Measure.**

![how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-2.png?width=670&height=208&name=how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-2.png)

If you wish to select a different Charge Behaviour and round up or down when charging please see [How to charge of Unit of measure for all Handling Charges](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges).

- Select the**Charge Structure.**
- Enter the**Rate.**
- Repeat the same for **Outbound Charge.**
- Repeat this process for the relevant product unit of measure conversions that your customer will be utilising.
- Scroll down and select**Save.**

![how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-3.png?width=670&height=131&name=how-to-aggregate-handling-charges-3.png)

---

<a id="how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges"></a>
## How to charge one Unit of Measure for all Handling Charges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Use cases
- How to charge one unit of measure for all handling charges
  - Step 1 - enable the correct picking charge option
  - Step 2 - set up the handling charge

### Purpose

To define one Unit of Measure for charging regardless of whether the Unit of measure is picked or put away.

### Use Cases

You are a wine distributor, and 6 bottles make up a case. Your customers can order in quantities of individual bottles; however, you always want to charge a case picking rate regardless of the units picked. In this scenario, you would configure the bottle charge to consistently scale up to the nearest case and charge in cases.

In this example, assume we have **Bottles** pick fee configured to **Round up to next (larger) Unit of Measure**(cases)**.**

1 case picking fee = $0.50

| **Location** | **Order/pick quantity** | **No aggregation (default)** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AA-01-01 | 5 bottles | Round up 5 bottles to 1 case Charge 1 Case |
| AA-01-01 | 2 bottles | Round up 2 bottles to 1 case Charge 1 Case |
| AA-01-04 5 | 5 bottles | Round up 5 bottles to 1 case Charge 1 Case |
| **Total Charge** |  | **3 Cases = $1.50** |

![how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-0.png?width=670&height=153&name=how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-0.png)

### How to charge one Unit of Measure for all Handling Charges

*Following the same example, the below steps will show how to set up the same Unit of Measure for all Sale Order handling fees.*

To set up this charging method, you need to:

1. Select the Picking Charge Option of, **Default (Scale to Highest Unit of Measure)** within the Rate Card Settings.
2. Create the Handling Charge and set up the Charge Behaviour to round up or down to the correct Product Unit of Measure.

#### **Step 1 - Enable the correct Picking Charge Option**

- Navigate to the Rate Card page, **More**>**Rate Cards**.
- Select the relevant Rate Card.
- Click the **Edit** button at the bottom of the screen.
- Scroll down to the Sale Order settings and select **Default (Scale to Highest Unit of Measure)** from the **Picking Charge Option** drop-down menu.

![how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-1.png?width=670&height=112&name=how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-1.png)

- Select the Aggregation option you wish to use. Please click here for more information on aggregation.

**Aggregation Options:**

| **Picking Charge Aggregation Option** | **Explanation** |
| --- | --- |
| No aggregation (default) | Products will not aggregate together. |
| Aggregate by SKU by Location | CartonCloud will aggregate all products for a Sale Order in the same location, regardless of whether they are from different POP records. |
| Aggregate by SKU across the entire warehouse | Regardless of what location the products for a Sale Order are in, they will be aggregated. |

**Example**

Following the same previous example, assume we have**Bottles**pick fee configured to**Round up to next (larger) Unit of Measure (cases).**

**(6 bottles = 1 case)**

| **Location** | **Pick/Putaway Quantity** | **No aggregation** | **Aggregate by SKU by Location** | **Aggregate by SKU across the entire** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AA-01-01 | 5 bottles | Round 5 bottles up to 1 case  Charge 1 case | Combine 7 bottles together (5+2) which converts to 1 Bottle + 1 Case Round 1 bottle + 1 case up to 2 cases Charge 2 cases | Combine 12 bottles together (5+2+5), which converts to 2 cases. Charge 2 Cases |
| AA-01-01 | 2 bottles | Round 2 bottles up to 1 case Charge 1 case |  |  |
| AA-01-04 | 5 bottles | Round 5 bottle up to 1 case Charge 1 case | Round 5 bottles up to 1 case  Charge 1 case |  |
| **Total Charge** |  | **3 Cases** | **3 cases** | **2 Cases** |

#### **Step 2 - Set up the Handling Charge**

Following the same example, we will set up a Case picking fee for all Units of Measure (bottles, cases and pallets)

- Navigate to the Rate Card page, **More**>**Rate Cards**.
- Select the relevant Rate Card.
- Select the Handling Charges tab.
- Click **Add Handling Charge**.
- Select the **Product Unit of Measure** you wish to charge all picks by from the drop-down menu (In this example, Cases).
- Select **Charge this Unit of Measure** from the **Charge Behaviour** drop-down menu.
- Enter the rate for Cases picking charge.

![how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-2.png?width=670&height=211&name=how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-2.png)

- Select **Save**.
- Click **Add Handling Charge**.
- Select the higher Product Unit of Measure (in this example, Pallets) to that of the Unit of Measure you wish to be charging by from the drop-down menu.
- Select **Scale down to previous (smaller) Unit of Measure**.

![how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-3.png?width=443&height=222&name=how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-3.png)

- Select **Save**.
- Click **Add Handling Charge**.
- Select the lower Product Unit of Measure (in this example, Bottles) to that of the Unit of Measure you wish to be charging by from the drop-down menu.
- Select **Round up to next (larger) Unit of Measure**.

![how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-4.png?width=467&height=234&name=how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-4.png)

- Select **Save**.

This now means you will have three charges set up that will all point towards a Case charge.

![how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-5.png?width=670&height=153&name=how-to-charge-one-unit-of-measure-for-all-handling-charges-5.png)

---

<a id="how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse"></a>
## How to charge storage until a Sale Order order leaves the warehouse

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to steps
- Manual control
- How do we know when the consignment has left the warehouse?

### Purpose

By default, when a Sale Order is picked, CartonCloud stops charging storage as the stock on hand is decremented.

Normally this is fine, however in some cases, you may want to continue to charge Storage after the Sale Order has been packed until it is actually removed from the warehouse (as the stock pay be picked and packed, but then stored in the warehouse until it goes onto a truck or gets collected by the customer).

### How to steps

In CartonCloud, there are multiple ways to 'dispatch' the Sale Order and prevent further storage charges:

- Automatically Dispatching the Sale Order when it finishes being Packed (Default)
- Automatically Dispatching the Sale Order when the Consignment leaves the Warehouse (recommended for more accurate storage charges)
- Manually marking the Sale Order Dispatched or setting a Future Dispatch Date

To do this, you first need to change a few settings within the customer which will prevent the stock from being decremented the moment the order is packed.

- From the customer settings, (Contacts -> Customers -> Edit the Customer -> Warehouse Management -> Sale Orders), then scroll down to the section "Status Updates"

![how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-0.png?width=604&height=165&name=how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-0.png)

- Firstly, you need to setup CartonCloud to decrement the Onhand Stock (and therefore, stop charging storage), when the order is marked Dispatched.

![how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-1.png?width=670&height=168&name=how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-1.png)

- After doing that, you can configure when the Sale Order will be marked Dispatched

![how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-2.png?width=670&height=144&name=how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-2.png)

### Manual Control

If you don't want CartonCloud to automatically handle the dispatch of the sale order, when you've finished packing a Sale Order you have the option to either Dispatch Now, or set a date for the dispatch to happen automatically. Also note, that if you have the Sale Order setup to Automatically Dispatch when the Consignment leaves the Warehouse, CartonCloud will dispatch when either happens (ie: if the Future Date you set arises before the Consignment leaves, it'll dispatch. Conversely, if the consignment leaves before the Future Date, it'll dispatch the sale order).

Important: If you don't set a dispatch date, the system will continue to charge storage until you manually mark the order dispatched.

![how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-3.png?width=426&height=253&name=how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-3.png)
Once you have set the future date, you can see when the order is due to be dispatched, and make changes if needed:

![how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-4.png?width=497&height=281&name=how-to-charge-storage-until-a-sale-order-order-leaves-the-warehouse-4.png)

### How do we know when the Consignment has left the warehouse?

If the Consignment Status is updated beyond 'In Warehouse', this will trigger the dispatch. So if you mark "Delivered", "Collected", "With on Forwarder" or "In Transit (Warehouse->Delivery)", the Sale Order will dispatch.

---

<a id="how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge"></a>
## How to check the charge history for an individual charge

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Benefits
- Scope
- How to check the charge history for an individual charge

### Purpose

To check the history of an individual charge on a Consignment, Manifest, Run Sheet, Sale Order, or Purchase Order.

### Benefits

- Provides visibility to any changes that have occurred on the charge and when the charge was created.
- Charge history is useful if the customer has raised an invoice inquiry and you need to investigate the timeline/details of the charge.

### Scope

Charge history is available for any type of charge that is applied an individual:

- Consignment
- Manifest
- Run Sheet
- Sale Order
- Purchase Order

### How to check the charge history for an individual charge

In the steps below, a Sale Order will be utilised as an example, however, these steps can be applied to any of the mentioned scope items above.

- Navigate to the relevant page (either Consignment, Manifest, Run Sheet, Sale Order or Purchase Order) by typing in the page name to the Search for anything bar.
- Select the relevant Sale Order and then select the Charges tab.

![how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-0.png?width=670&height=281&name=how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-0.png)

- Navigate to the bottom of the page and select the relevant charge you wish to check the history of.

Note, you can only view the charge history for one individual charge at a time. In other words, you can not view all charges and their history for a particular Sale Order at the same time.

![how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-1.png?width=670&height=95&name=how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-1.png)

- Select the History tab.

Note, if you wish to edit the charge select the edit button at the bottom of the screen. If you edit the charge this modification will be reflected in the charge history.

- The History page will show all associated history for that charge including when it was created, when modifications were made and the user who made them.

![how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-2.png?width=670&height=256&name=how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-2.png)

If you are making a modification to the charge you can add a comment to the history detailing why the change was made and what the change was. To do so, select the Add history button at the bottom of the page.

![how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-3.png?width=670&height=256&name=how-to-check-the-charge-history-for-an-individual-charge-3.png)

---

<a id="how-to-resolve-consignment-charge-errors"></a>
## How to resolve Consignment Charge Errors

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-resolve-consignment-charge-errors_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Consignment charge error report

### Purpose

There are a number of things which can cause CartonCloud to create a Charge Error for your Consignment. This guide provides a process for working through and clearing the Income Charge Errors in a way that corrects the system so the same charge error will not occur next time.

### ![how-to-resolve-consignment-charge-errors-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-resolve-consignment-charge-errors-0.png?width=470&height=478&name=how-to-resolve-consignment-charge-errors-0.png)
Consignment Charge Error Report

This report provides bulk information about which Consignments have Charge Errors. It can be run from within the Invoices screen.

---

<a id="how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments"></a>
## How to stop charging for cancelled, returned or lost Consignments

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to steps

### Purpose

By default CartonCloud charges for Consignments regardless of their delivery status. However, if you want to set up a rule so that when consignments are set to a particular status such as cancelled then they do not charge, then this article will show you how.

Additionally, you can also name these cancelled statuses to be called whatever you want.

### How to steps

- To begin, select Transport in the main menu, followed by Consignments in the drop-down menu.
- Then on the Consignments page, select Related , followed by Consignment Delivery Cancelled Statuses from the drop-down menu.

![how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments-0.png?width=400&height=158&name=how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments-0.png)

- Now on the Consignment Delivery Cancelled Statuses page you can see that all for all statuses Is Charged is currently set to Yes. To make the system not charge for a particular Status then this setting needs to be set to no.

![how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments-1.png?width=610&height=296&name=how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments-1.png)

- For example, to make the Cancelled Status stop charging for a Consignment select Edit

![how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments-2.png?width=670&height=326&name=how-to-stop-charging-for-cancelled-returned-or-lost-consignments-2.png)

- Then untick the Is Charged box.
- Optionally, you can also rename the Status (perhaps your operation calls a cancelled job something slightly different).
- Finally, select Save .
- Now the next time a cancelled Consignment has its charges automatically calculated by the system (either when it gets put on an invoice, or the Automatically calculate income charges button is clicked on the consignment charge tab).

---

<a id="how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order"></a>
## How to zero out charges on an order

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Steps to zero out charges against an order

### Purpose

There may be some cases where you need to zero out charges on an order and have $0 charges against it.

### Steps to zero out charges against an order

**The charges you will need to zero out on the order include:**

1. Item Charge (not for Consignments)
2. Adhoc Charge
3. Charges

![how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-0.png?width=670&height=270&name=how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-0.png)
**To zero out the charges:**

- Navigate to the order you need to zero out charges on.
- Select the Charges tab.
- Zero out any charges for the Item Charge Data and select Save. Repeat this process for the Adhoc Charge Data table.

![how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-1.png?width=670&height=79&name=how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-1.png)

- Scroll down to the Charges table and select Automatically Calculate Income Charges .

- Click on the individual charge.

![how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-2.png?width=670&height=118&name=how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-2.png)

- Click the red Delete button.
