![how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-3.png?width=670&height=220&name=how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-3.png)

- Repeat this process for all charges.
- Click Mark Charges Finalized. This will add the order to the Invoice with no charges against the order.

![how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-4.png?width=670&height=216&name=how-to-zero-out-charges-on-an-order-4.png)

---

<a id="maximum-charge"></a>
## Maximum Charge

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/maximum-charge_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Use Cases
- How To Steps

### Purpose

The Maximum Charge function allows you to apply a maximum value to certain charges within a Transport Rate to ensure the charge never exceeds a defined value. If the charge exceeds the defined value then the maximum charge will be applied. On the other hand, if the charge is less than the maximum value, then the charge calculation will remain as is.

Note, if the Transport Rate has a surcharge, this will not be included in the calculation when determining if the maximum charge will be applied.

### Use Cases

The Maximum Charge functionality ensures that a charge never exceeds an unreasonable dollar amount. This is extremely useful if you charge by a value percentage, such as the invoice value of a Consignment. Depending on the value of the product, the charge can fluctuate significantly between different Consignments, creating the need for a cap to be applied to the charge.

For example, if you have two Consignments for a customer and one has an invoice value of $50, and the other is $3000, there will be a significant charge discrepancy between the two products.

Percentage Invoice Value Charge = 20% of the Invoice Value

Consignment 1: Invoice Value = $50

Consignment 2: Invoice Value = $3000

Consignment 1 Invoice Value Charge = 50 x 0.20 = $10

Consignment 2 Invoice Value Charge = 3000 x 0.20 = $600

**If a Maximum Charge of $100 is utilised for the above example:**

**Consignment 1 Invoice Value Charge** = 50 x 0.20 = $10

**Consignment 2 Invoice Value Charge** = $100 (Charge is greater than $100, so the Maximum Charge is applied)

### How To Steps

- Navigate to the Rate Card page by typing Rate Cards into the Search for anything bar.
- Select the relevant Rate Card.
- Select the Transport Rates tab.

![maximum-charge-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/maximum-charge-0.png?width=670&height=200&name=maximum-charge-0.png)

- Either select an existing Transport Rate or select +Add Rate .
- Select the three vertical dot icon in the second panel.

![maximum-charge-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/maximum-charge-1.png?width=670&height=335&name=maximum-charge-1.png)

- Enable Maximum Charge by selecting the slide toggle. It will turn green once it is enabled.

![maximum-charge-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/maximum-charge-2.png?width=670&height=401&name=maximum-charge-2.png)

- The Maximum Charge box will appear, and you will be able to configure the charge.

![maximum-charge-3-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/maximum-charge-3-1.png?width=670&height=114&name=maximum-charge-3-1.png)

- Select the Fee Category's from the drop-down menu that you wish to apply the Maximum Charge to.
- Select a Maximum Charge Description from the drop-down menu.

You can customise the Fee Category and Maximum Charge Description by creating a Fee Category. See the [Fee Category](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/charge-subclasses-fee-category) article for more information.

- Insert the Maximum Charge value and apply the fuel levy by clicking the fuel icon.

![maximum-charge-4-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/maximum-charge-4-1.png?width=670&height=125&name=maximum-charge-4-1.png)

---

<a id="prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message"></a>
## Prevent Undercharged Storage - You're potentially undercharging storage! message

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message_

### Table of Contents

- Warehouse location capacities
- Storage charges
- How to prevent undercharges storage
- What to do if you already have stock which is being undercharged
- Bulk splitting

### Warehouse location capacities

- Single Pallet Used typically for racked locations and locations which contain a single pallet or less.
- Multiple Pallets For bulk areas such as loading bays, or large unracked areas where many pallets of the same SKU will be stored.

### Storage charges

CartonCloud is able to handle a wide variety of storage situations;

- Single SKUs on a Single Pallet location (charges the location once)
- Multiple SKUs on a Single Pallet location (charges the location once)
- Multiple Pallets within a Multiple Pallet location (charges for each pallet)
- Multiple Pallets each containing multiple SKUs within a Multiple Pallet location (charges for each pallet)

When working with Single Pallet Locations, CartonCloud determines storage charges by looking at the Warehouse Location of Purchase Order Products (stock).

When CartonCloud sees multiple Purchase Order Products on a single Warehouse Location, it will only charge the customer once for using that location.

However, in the case of Multiple Pallet Locations, CartonCloud uses another identifier, the Pallet Id, to determine how many pallets are stored within a specific Multiple Pallet Location.

This means that if multiple Purchase Order Product records are allocated to the same Pallet Id, then they'll only generate charges for one pallet of storage.

**Important:**This also means that if a single, large, Purchase Order Product record is allocated to just one Pallet Id, it'll only be charged as one space.

For example, if you have a product that is measured in Pallets, and you load 5 Pallets to a single Pallet Id, then you'll only charge storage for a Single Pallet space.

### How to Prevent Undercharged Storage

When verifying a Purchase Order into stock, CartonCloud will warn you when it thinks you're going to load more than a pallet worth of stock into a single location (and thereby, undercharge the storage).

![prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message-0.png?width=670&height=167&name=prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message-0.png)

CartonCloud will warn you when it thinks you're loading too much stock into a single location and therefore undercharging storage

When this occurs, it is often easiest to use the *Bulk Split* functionality to break the stock into multiple records (each with their own Pallet Id). You can do this from the Verification page by clicking More->Bulk Split.

### What to do if you already have stock which is being undercharged

You can use the Bulk Split method to break the stock record up into multiple records. Simply view the stock record and then click **Bulk Split** down the bottom.

![prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message-1.png?width=670&height=204&name=prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message-1.png)

### Bulk Splitting

To perform a bulk split follow the below steps:

- Select**More** on the product displaying the warning.
- Select **Bulk Split**.
- Confirm how many units or cartons are on a pallet in the Qty/ Pallet field.
- Click **Bulk Split**.

In the above example, a single Purchase Order Product line contained 8 units of steel toolboxes. When Bulk splitting the toolboxes we confirm that only 4 fit onto a pallet, meaning two pallets are arriving. Once the bulk split is performed the product is split from one to two purchase order Product lines (the same number as the number of pallets), this now allows us to allocate them to separate Warehouse Locations.

The same process can be followed to Split products that may need to be separated due to their Status. For example, if 3 cartons at the top of the pallet are damaged you can perform a *split*, then allocate them to a different part of the warehouse.
![prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message-2.png?width=670&height=329&name=prevent-undercharged-storage-you-re-potentially-undercharging-storage-message-2.png)

---

<a id="purchase-order-adhoc-charges"></a>
## Purchase Order Adhoc Charges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/purchase-order-adhoc-charges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Loading purchase order adhoc charges
- Where to submit purchase order adhoc charges
- Editing & Saving Adhoc Charges
- Running adhoc charge timers

### Purpose

Within the CartonCloud mobile apps, pickers can choose to submit Adhoc Charges against Purchase Orders, assuming that Adhoc Charges for the Purchase Order have been configured in the web app.

### Loading purchase order adhoc charges

Purchase Order Adhoc Charge details get loaded when the user selects a Purchase Order from the list of Purchase Orders. Existing values for Adhoc Charges will not be deleted on re-loading a Purchase Order unless a more recent Adhoc Charge value is available in the CartonCloud system. Running timers are never deleted when refreshing from the server.This makes it possible to preload Adhoc Charges for Purchase Orders in the web app that will appear on the picker's mobile app on loading a Purchase Order and entering the Adhoc Charge view for a Purchase Order.

For more information on how to Adhoc Charges in the CartonCloud web app, check out these articles: [Adhoc Charge Setup](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Adhoc+Charge+Setup), [View Rate Card](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/View+Rate+Card).

### Where to Submit Purchase Order Adhoc Charges

**Purchase Order Detail View**

On viewing a Purchase Order's details, if there are adhoc charges available, an additional menu tab will be available at the bottom of the screen. Simply select the Charges tab at the bottom of the screen to enter the adhoc charges.

![purchase-order-adhoc-charges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-adhoc-charges-0.png?width=250&height=513&name=purchase-order-adhoc-charges-0.png)

![purchase-order-adhoc-charges-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-adhoc-charges-1.png?width=262&height=536&name=purchase-order-adhoc-charges-1.png)

If Adhoc Charges are available for the Purchase Order, the Adhoc Charges tab will be available both for Purchase Orders that have not yet been verified and those that have already been verified and are awaiting allocation of products to a location.

**On Verifying**

On selecting **Verify**from the bottom bar when viewing a Purchase Order, if there are Adhoc Charges available for this Purchase Order, an additional Adhoc Charge view will be shown to allow the user to adjust and save Adhoc Charges for the verified Purchase Order.

For more information on the Purchase Order Putaway Process check out this article: [Receiving on the mobile app (scan putaway)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=530058312)

![purchase-order-adhoc-charges-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-adhoc-charges-2.png?width=292&height=598&name=purchase-order-adhoc-charges-2.png)

If you do not wish to see the adhoc charges view upon verifying a Purchase Order you can disable this function in Organisation Settings by following the below steps:

- Navigate to Organisation Settings, More>Organisation Settings.
- Select the Warehouse tab.
- Within the Purchase Order Configurations and the subheading Mobile App Configuration, untick the Enforce Adhoc Charges before completing Receiving setting.

### Editing & Saving Adhoc Charges

Check out this article with more information on the different types of adhoc charges and how to edit & save them:  [Consignment Adhoc Charges - Editing & Saving Adhoc Charges](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+Adhoc+Charges#ConsignmentAdhocCharges-Editing&SavingAdhocCharges)

### Running Adhoc Charge Timers

Check out this article with more information on how running adhoc charge timers are being displayed and what actions might be required on leaving a Purchase Order detail view with a running timer: [Consignment Adhoc Charges - Running Adhoc Charge Timers](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+Adhoc+Charges#ConsignmentAdhocCharges-RunningAdhocChargeTimers)

Note: The above-mentioned article is talking about Adhoc Charges for Consignments but the handling of running Adhoc Charge timers is equivalent to the Purchase Order behaviour.

---

<a id="putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options"></a>
## Putaway (Purchase Orders) and Picking (Sale Orders) Charge Options

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to select the putaway and picking charge option
- Putaway (Purchase Order) handling fee algorithm
- Picking (Sale Order) handling fee algorithms

### Purpose

There are different ways to calculate Putaway (Purchase Orders) and Picking (Sale Orders) Handling Fees. The below explains the different Handling Fee charging methods and how they would be applied in your operation. This will help you determine which method you should select.

Upon selecting a Putaway and Picking Charge option, this method will then apply when you are adding Handling Charges to your Rate Card.

### How to select the putaway and picking charge option

The Putaway and Picking charging method utilised is controlled at the Rate Card level. To change the method:

- Navigate to the Rate Cards page, **More**>**Rate Cards**.
- Select the relevant Rate Card.
- Select the **Edit** button at the bottom of the screen.

![putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options-0.png?width=670&height=258&name=putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options-0.png)

- Scroll down to the Sale Order and Purchase Order settings.
- Select the charging method from the drop-down menu under **Putaway Charge Option** and **Picking Charge Option**.

![putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options-1.png?width=670&height=199&name=putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options-1.png)

![putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options-2.png?width=670&height=199&name=putaway-purchase-orders-and-picking-sale-orders-charge-options-2.png)

If you select Default (Scale to Highest Unit of Measure), you will need to choose a charge aggregation option. See [Charge Aggregation options](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/View-Rate-Card-Handling-Charges#charge-behaviour-and-aggregation) for more information.

- Scroll down to the bottom of the page and select Save .

### ​​​​​​​ Putaway (Purchase Order) handling fee algorithm

There are two different ways in which Putaway fees can be automatically calculated.

**Default (Scale to Highest Unit Of Measure):** CartonCloud will scale the products to the highest unit of measure and charge that way.

Note that if you select Default (Scale to Highest Unit of Measure), you will need to select a charge aggregation option. See [Charge Aggregation options](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/View-Rate-Card-Handling-Charges#charge-behaviour-and-aggregation) for more information.

**Charge Base Unit Of Measures:** CartonCloud will always charge by the base unit of measure.

The following table explains the difference between each of the two methods:

| **Situation** | **Default (Scale to Highest Unit of Measure) with no aggregation** | **Charge Base Unit of Measure** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Put 1 carton into a location | Charge a carton put-away | Charge a carton put-away |
| Put 1 pallet into a location (i.e., A product that is measured in pallets) | Charge a pallet put-away | Charge a pallet put-away |
| Put 10 cartons into one location, with no conversion to pallets.      Put 10 cartons into one location, which are 2 different products.       Put 10 cartons into one location, which are the same product, but 2 different batches. | Charge 10 carton put-aways | Charge 10 carton put-aways |
| Put 48 cartons into one location, and there's a conversion where 48 cartons = 1 pallet. | Charge a pallet put-away | Charge 48 carton put-aways |
| Put 47 cartons into one location, and there's a conversion where 48 cartons = 1 pallet. | Charge 47 carton put-aways | Charge 47 carton put-aways |
| Put 49 cartons into one location, and there's a conversion where 48 cartons = 1 pallet. | Charge 1 pallet put-away + 1 carton put-away | Charge 49 carton put-aways |
| Put 49 cartons into one location, and there's no conversion from cartons to pallets. | Charge 49 carton put-aways | Charge 49 carton put-aways |

### Picking (Sale Order) handling fee algorithms

There are several different ways in which Picking fees can be automatically calculated.

**Default (Scale to Highest Unit Of Measure):** CartonCloud will scale the products to the highest unit of measure and charge that way.

Note that if you select Default (Scale to Highest Unit of Measure), you will need to select a charge aggregation option. See [Charge Aggregation options](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Putaway+%28Purchase+Orders%29+and+Picking+%28Sale+Orders%29+Charge+Options#Putaway(PurchaseOrders)andPicking(SaleOrders)ChargeOptions-aggregation) for more information.

**Charge Base Unit of Measures:**CartonCloud will always charge by the base unit of measure.

**Intelligently Charge Pallet Picks Where Possible:** if the location went empty when you picked the order, it would charge a pallet pick if it is cheaper. When a quantity is picked that is more than the unit to pallet conversion, CartonCloud will charge 1 pallet pick + the extra number of units left that don't make up a pallet, unless the unit picks cost more than a pallet pick.

**Charge Pallet Picking Per Location:**It will always charge a pallet pick regardless of what quantities you have picked.

The following table explains the difference between each of the three methods:

| **Situation** | **Default (Scale to Highest Unit of Measure) - with no aggregation** | **Charge Base Unit of Measure** | **Intelligently Charge Pallet picks where possible** | **Charge Pallet Picking Per Location** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Picking 1 carton from one location | Charge a carton pick | Charge a carton pick | Charge a carton pick | Charge a pallet pick |
| Picking 1 pallet from one location (i.e., A product that is measured in pallets) | Charge a pallet pick | Charge a pallet pick | Charge a pallet pick | Charge a pallet pick |
| Picking 10 cartons from one location, with no conversion to Pallets      Picking 10 cartons from one location, which are 2 different products.      Picking 10 cartons from one location, which are the same product, but 2 different batches. | Charge 10 carton picks | Charge 10 carton picks | Will anything be left in the location after picking?       No - the whole pallet can be moved for picking (Charge whichever is less expensive, either 10 carton picks or 1 pallet pick.)      Yes - the whole pallet cannot be moved for picking; individual carton picks must occur. (Charge 10 carton picks) | Charge a pallet pick |
| Picking 48 cartons from one location, and there's a conversion where 48 cartons = 1 pallet. | Charge a pallet pick | Charge 48 carton picks | Charge a pallet pick | Charge a pallet pick |
| Picking 47 cartons from one location, and there's a conversion where 48 cartons = 1 pallet. | Charge 47 carton picks | Charge 47 carton picks | Will anything be left in the location after picking?       No - the whole pallet can be moved for picking (Charge whichever is less expensive, either 47 carton picks or 1 pallet pick.)      Yes - the whole pallet cannot be moved for picking; individual carton picks must occur. (Charge 47 carton picks) | Charge a pallet pick |
| Picking 60 cartons from one location, and there's a conversion where 48 cartons = 1 pallet. | Charge 1 pallet pick + 12 carton pick | Charge 49 carton picks | Charge 1 pallet pick + 12 carton pick, unless if 12 carton picks cost more than 1 pallet pick then it would be Charge 2 pallet picks. | Charge a pallet pick |
| Picking 49 cartons from one location, and there's no conversion from cartons to pallets. | Charge 49 carton picks | Charge 49 carton picks | Will anything be left in the location after picking?       No - the whole pallet can be moved for picking (Charge whichever is less expensive, either 49 carton picks or 1 pallet pick.)      Yes - the whole pallet cannot be moved for picking; individual carton picks must occur. (Charge 49 carton picks) | Charge a pallet pick |

Location-based is based on the locations for the whole order - i.e. will consolidate order lines to per location per order only.

For Bulk Locations (Multiple Pallets), the charging and allocation are based on the Pallet IDs created when allocating purchase order stock to bulk locations.

---

<a id="recalculating-consignment-charges"></a>
## Recalculating Consignment Charges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/recalculating-consignment-charges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- What determines when Consignment charges are recalculated

### Purpose

If you edit a Consignment that is already attached to an invoice that is

not yet Approved

and the Consignment charges are

not yet finalised

, then some updates to the Consignment can cause the charges to be recalculated.

### What determines when Consignment charges are recalculated

In the case that you are editing a Consignment that is already attached to an invoice that is not yet Approved and the Consignment charges are not yet finalised, then updating certain fields will trigger a charge recalculation in the background. In this case, the new charges will be displayed against the Consignment and on the invoice. However, if the charge recalculation causes a charge error, then the charge error will be displayed.

If you edit the following fields, a charge recalculation will be triggered:

- Customer
- Collect Address
- Delivery Address
- Service Type (only applicable for V2 of CartonCloud)
- Customer Reference (only applicable for V2 of CartonCloud)
- Consignment Data Custom Fields (only applicable for V2 of CartonCloud)
- Consignment Items and associated Custom Fields (only applicable for V2 of CartonCloud)

**Please note ad-hoc and manual charges are not impacted and are retained on the Consignment.**

---

<a id="sale-order-adhoc-charges"></a>
## Sale Order Adhoc Charges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/sale-order-adhoc-charges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Loading sale order adhoc charges
- Where to submit sale order adhoc charges
  - Sale order detail view
  - On finishing packing
- Editing and saving adhoc charges
- Running adhoc charge timers

### Purpose

Within the CartonCloud mobile apps, pickers can choose to submit Adhoc Charges against Sale Orders, assuming that Adhoc Charges for the Sale Order have been configured in the web app.

### Loading Sale Order Adhoc Charges

Sale Order Adhoc Charge details get loaded when the user selects a Sale Order from the list of Sale Orders. Existing values for Adhoc Charges will not be deleted on re-loading a Sale Order unless a newer Adhoc Charge value is available in the CartonCloud system. Running timers are never deleted when refreshing from the server.

This makes it possible to preload Adhoc Charges for Sale Orders in the web app that will appear on the picker's mobile app on loading a Sale Order and entering the Adhoc Charge view for a Sale Order.

For more information on how to Adhoc Charges in the CartonCloud web app, check out these articles: [Adhoc Charge Setup](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Adhoc+Charge+Setup), [View Rate Card](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/View+Rate+Card).

### Where to Submit Sale Order Adhoc Charges

#### **Sale Order Detail View**

On viewing a Sale Order's details, if there are adhoc charges available, an additional menu tab will be available. Simply swipe from right to left on the tab bar and you will see the **Adhoc Charges** option. Tap this button and all relevant adhoc charges will be displayed.

![sale-order-adhoc-charges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/sale-order-adhoc-charges-0.png?width=268&height=552&name=sale-order-adhoc-charges-0.png)

#### **On Finishing Packing**

On selecting **Finish Packing**from the bottom bar when viewing a Sale Order, if there are Adhoc Charges available for this Sale Order, an additional Adhoc Charge view will be shown to allow the user to adjust and save Adhoc Charges for the completed Sale Order.

For more information on the Sale Order, Packing Process check out this article: [Scan Picking Process](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Scan+Picking+Process)

![sale-order-adhoc-charges-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/sale-order-adhoc-charges-1.png?width=301&height=615&name=sale-order-adhoc-charges-1.png)

If the Sale Order has been set up to automatically generate a Consignment for the Sale Order upon finishing packing, the Adhoc Charges view will be displayed before the Consignment label printing view or the Consignment items vide (depending on settings configured) is displayed. On closing the Adhoc Charges view, the label printing view will be displayed.

If you do not wish to see the adhoc charges view upon completing a pick you can disable this function in Organisation Settings by following the below steps;

- Navigate to Organisation Settings, **More**>**Organisation Settings**.
- Select the **Warehouse** tab
- Under the **Sale Order Configurations**heading and then the sub-heading **Mobile App Configuration**, untick the **Enforce Adhoc Charges** **before completing Pick** box to disable the function.

### Editing & Saving Adhoc Charges

Check out this article with more information on the different types of adhoc charges and how to edit & save them:

Consignment Adhoc Charges - Editing & Saving Adhoc Charges

### Running adhoc charge timers

Check out this article with more information on how running adhoc charge timers are being displayed and what actions might be required on leaving a Sale Order detail view with a running timer: [Consignment Adhoc Charges - Running Adhoc Charge Timers](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+Adhoc+Charges#ConsignmentAdhocCharges-RunningAdhocChargeTimers)

Note: The above-mentioned article is talking about Adhoc Charges for Consignments but the handling of running Adhoc Charge timers is equivalent to the Sale Order behaviour.

---

<a id="setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging"></a>
## Setting more than 2 decimal places for Charging

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Configuration
- Decimal entry

### Purpose

If you charge your Customers at rates with greater than two decimal places, follow the below instructions to set the precision that suits your needs up to a maximum of 6 decimal places. Currently, CartonCloud supports adjusting the decimal places for handling and storage charges only.

**Once you have added more decimal places you can't reduce them, so be sure to set them to the value you need.**

### Configuration

To configure the decimal place setting, from the menu, select **More**, then select **Organisation Settings**.

Navigate to the Invoice tab and then scroll down until you find the Rates section as below. Enter in the number of decimal places you need, then scroll to the bottom of the page and select **Save**.

![setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-0.png?width=670&height=170&name=setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-0.png)

### Decimal entry

Once you have saved your setting, then you will be able to see and enter the additional decimal values against your handling inbound and outbound charges or storage charges. These can be found in Rate Cards. Below is a view of the Handling Charge edit screen.

![setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-1.png?width=670&height=387&name=setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-1.png)
Below you can see the summary of handling charges, displaying the rates to the decimal values set.

![setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-2.png?width=670&height=225&name=setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-2.png)
When viewing a Sales Order you can also see the unit rate to the number of decimal places you require. Please note that the calculation will round at the line level.

![setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-3.png?width=670&height=331&name=setting-more-than-2-decimal-places-for-charging-3.png)

<a id="setting-the-number-of-free-days-within-a-storage-period"></a>
## Setting the number of free days within a Storage Period

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/setting-the-number-of-free-days-within-a-storage-period_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Example
- How to steps

### Purpose

The ability to provide Customers with a certain number of free storage days prior to storage charges applying. Applying free storage days is often utilised when stock is received into a warehouse and will be collected quickly. In addition, it is an incentive for the customer to arrange a quick collection of their stock. However, if the stock is not collected within that free storage days period then the customer will be charged storage in the usual manner.

Free storage days is set up against the Rate Card. Therefore, all customers that belong to that Rate Card will be provided those storage free days.

### Example

As an example, if a customer has 3 storage free days and the storage week commences on a Sunday, then if ALL the products were received on a Monday and ALL collected by Wednesday then they would not be charged storage, however, if the customer only collect some or All on the Thursday, then the customer would be charged for the weeks storage as per normal. A different example would be if the goods were received on a Friday and collected on the following Monday, they would not get charged for the first week as the storage free days carried them over to the next week.

You can find the setting for Storage Period Free Days on the **Details** tab of a Rate Card, under the storage heading as below.

![setting-the-number-of-free-days-within-a-storage-period-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-the-number-of-free-days-within-a-storage-period-0.png?width=670&height=292&name=setting-the-number-of-free-days-within-a-storage-period-0.png)

### How to steps

- To enable Storage period Free days, navigate to Rate Cards, find the Rate Card you want to modify and select Edit .
- Scroll down until you get to the Storage section and at the bottom of the list you will find the Storage Period Free Days setting. The default for this setting is  0 (no storage period free days) so adjust it to the number of free days you wish to apply to your storage periods for all Customers on that particular Rate Card.

Note: Storage Free Days only apply to Per Location, Per Location & Bulk Pallets and Per Item Storage Charge methods.

![setting-the-number-of-free-days-within-a-storage-period-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-the-number-of-free-days-within-a-storage-period-1.png?width=670&height=308&name=setting-the-number-of-free-days-within-a-storage-period-1.png)

---

<a id="setting-up-cartoncloud-to-automatically-rate-consignments"></a>
## Setting up CartonCloud to automatically rate Consignments

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/setting-up-cartoncloud-to-automatically-rate-consignments_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to steps

### Purpose

Please note that if your CartonCloud account was created prior to 1 July 2019 the below functionality may not be available at this stage. If you would like to discuss migrating to the newer version of the CartonCloud TMS, please use [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/new-support-ticket) and lodge a case with our Service Desk.

CartonCloud automatically calculates your consignment charges by using the[Income Zone Sets](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/setting-up-income-zone-sets), [Rate Card](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/rate-cards-overview) and [Transport Rates](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/creating-transport-rates).

### How to steps

To set up CartonCloud to automatically calculate consignment charges, please follow the below steps:

1. Create Income Zone Sets
2. Associate the Income Zone Set with the relevant Rate Card
3. Create Transport Rates

The below diagram helps explain how CartonCloud uses Income Zone Sets, Rate Cards, and Transport Rates to automatically calculate consignment charges.

![setting-up-cartoncloud-to-automatically-rate-consignments-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-up-cartoncloud-to-automatically-rate-consignments-0.png?width=670&height=503&name=setting-up-cartoncloud-to-automatically-rate-consignments-0.png)

**Income Zone Sets:**Income Zone Sets contain the zones your operation uses for charging.

**Rate Card:** In CartonCloud the rates you charge your customers are controlled by a Rate Card. You can set Rate Cards to be used by just a single Customer, multiple Customers, or all your Customers, depending on how you want to charge for your services. To enable the automatic calculation of consignment charges the Customer's Rate Card must be associated with the applicable Income Zone Set.

**Transport Rates:**Transport Rates are the rates your customers will be charged for deliveries and are created within a Rate Card. CartonCloud applies these rates when automatically calculating consignment charges.

**First Step: [Setting up Income Zone Sets](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/setting-up-income-zone-sets)**

---

<a id="storage-charge-methods"></a>
## Storage Charge Methods

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/storage-charge-methods_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Storage Charge Methods
  - Per Location (default)
  - Per Location (and bulk location pallets)
  - Volume Based
  - Weight Based
  - Per Item
  - Per Pallet (based on quantity)
  - Per Unit of Measure

### Purpose

When configuring your Storage Charges in the Rate Card there are a number of different storage charge methods you can utilise. Each charge method has a different use case and set up configuration.

### Storage Charge Methods

Please note that before selecting a Storage Charging Method, charges are calculated on either the Product Type of the Warehouse Location or the Product Type of the Product. This can be configured within the [Rate Card](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Rate-Card) under the Charge Product Type Option.

**Product**: charges storage based on the Products Product Type. For example, if you have an ambient product temporarily stored in a chilled location but want to charge still the ambient rate, you would use this setting.

**Warehouse Location**: charges storage based on the Warehouse Locations Product Type. For example, you would use this setting if you have an ambient warehouse but charge differently for racked locations vs block-stacking locations; you would use this setting. Otherwise, all products will be charged as ambient regardless of their location.

#### Per Location (default)

Storage charges are calculated based on the number of Warehouse Locations that are used. In the case where multiple products are stored on a single pallet, the system will just charge the location once. Bulk locations may be used to store multiple 'pallets' in a single location and therefore charge multiple pallets of storage.

Per-Location charges do not take into account the quantity of product in a location, simply the number of unique locations used.

Note, to ensure all pallets and products are charged correctly; it is recommended that large quantities of products are split into individual pallets (therefore, into different locations).

**Configuration required to enable the charging method:**No

**Where to add the storage charge:**Rate Card

**Use Case:** This method is recommended when most of the warehouse is racked, and multiple products are stored within individual locations.

**Charge Product Type Option Impact:**Yes - will charge by the Product Type option selected.

**How it appears on the Invoice Summary:**

**![storage-charge-methods-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-0.png?width=670&height=198&name=storage-charge-methods-0.png)**

#### ![storage-charge-methods-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-1.png?width=670&height=125&name=storage-charge-methods-1.png)

---

#### Per Location (and bulk location pallets)

Calculated similarly to Per-location (default), however, the product quantity is taken into account when calculating storage charges. This means large quantities of stock can be loaded into locations without being split into individual pallets.

**Notes:**

- Both Single Pallet and Multiple Pallet locations take quantity into account, meaning if you load 2 Pallets worth of stock into a Single Pallet Location (which isn't possible, but the software allows this to happen), then it'll charge 2 x pallets for that location.
- The determination of the Number of Pallets comes from the "[Oversize Unit](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Products-Units-of-Measure) ".

**Single Pallet Locations:**

If you are using this charging method for single pallet locations, CartonCloud will default by the number of pallets in the location (even if this exceeds one). However, if you wish only to allow one charge for each location, you can change this from the [Organisation Settings](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Organisation+Settings) .

The option is called: *"If any Per Location Storage Charge Methods are used - Should all stock on Single Pallet Locations be combined into a single charge?"*

- **No (Default)** - The location can be charged once for each SKU stored. For example, a client storing 4 different products on a Single Pallet Location will be charged for the location 4 times. If stock quantities greater than 1 pallet are loaded onto the location, (ie: a product is configured as 40 boxes per pallet, and 45 boxes are loaded into the location), the location will be charged for the number of pallets stored, ie: 2 pallets.
- **Yes** - All stock on a Single Pallet location is combined into a single charge. For example, a client storing 4 different products on a Single Pallet location will only be charged for the location once. If stock quantities greater than 1 pallet are loaded onto the location, (ie: a product is configured as 40 boxes per pallet, and 45 boxes are loaded into the location), the location will still only be charged once.

**Configuration required to enable the charging method:**Yes

To utilise the Per Location (and bulk location pallets) storage charge method, you must first configure the Product settings. Then, depending on your warehouse and storage set-up, you may need to change the Organisation Settings.

**Product Settings**

- Navigate to the Products page, **Warehouse**>**Products**.
- Select the applicable Product.
- Select the green **Edit** button at the top of the screen.
- Scroll down to **Storage Charge Method** and select **Per Location (and bulk location pallets)**.
- Select the green **Save** button.

**And further configuration from the Organisation Settings**

If you would like to prevent single pallet warehouse locations from being charged more than once:

- Navigate to Organisation Settings, **More**>**Organisation Settings**.
- Select the **Invoice** tab.
- Scroll down to the **Storage** section.
- Select **Yes** from the drop-down option under **If "Per Location (and bulk location pallets)" Storage Charges are used - Should all stock on Single Pallet Locations be combined into a single charge?**

**Where to add the storage charge:**Rate Card

**Use Case:**This method is recommended in warehouses where bulk products are stored and multiple products do not share locations.

**Charge Product Type Option Impact: Y**es - will charge by the Product Type option selected.

---

#### Volume Based

It is calculated based on the total amount of product volume in the warehouse during the given time period.

The number of warehouse locations used does not impact the storage charges.

**Configuration required to enable the charging method:**Yes

To utilise Volume Based storage charge method, you will first need to configure the Product settings:

- Navigate to the Products page, **Warehouse**>**Products**.
- Select the applicable Product.
- Scroll down to **Storage Charge Method** and select **Volume Based.**
- Within the **Product Volume (in Cubic Meters)** box, enter the volume.
- Select the green **Save** button.

**Where to add the storage charge:**Rate Card

**Use Case:**This method is most commonly used as it can accurately track the actual storage space being utilised.

**Charge Product Type Option Impact:**Yes - will charge by the Product Type option selected.

**How it appears on the Invoice Summary:**

**![storage-charge-methods-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-2.png?width=670&height=31&name=storage-charge-methods-2.png)**

**![storage-charge-methods-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-3.png?width=670&height=146&name=storage-charge-methods-3.png)**

---

#### Weight Based

It is calculated based on the total amount of product weight in the warehouse during the given time period.

**Configuration required to enable the charging method:**Yes

To utilised Weight Based storage charge method, you will first need to configure the Product Settings:

- Navigate to the Products page, **Warehouse**>**Products**.
- Select the applicable Product.
- Scroll down to **Storage Charge Method** and select **Weight Based.**
- Within the Product Weight (in Kilograms) box, enter the weight

**Where to add the storage charge:**Rate Card

**Charge Product Type Option Impact:**Yes - charge by the Product Type option selected

**How it appears on the Invoice Summary:**

**![storage-charge-methods-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-4.png?width=670&height=38&name=storage-charge-methods-4.png)**

![storage-charge-methods-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-5.png?width=670&height=142&name=storage-charge-methods-5.png)

---

#### Per Item

This method calculates individual storage rates for products at their base unit of measure.

**Configuration required to enable the charging method:**Yes

To utilise the Per Item storage charge method, you will first need to configure the Product Settings:

- Navigate to the Products page, **Warehouse**>**Products**.
- Select the relevant Product.
- Click the green **Edit** button at the top of the screen.
- Navigate to **Storage Charge Method**.
- Select **Per Item**from the drop-down menu.
- The **Per Item Storage Charge** box will appear. Enter the rate.
- Scroll down and select the green **Save** button.

**Where to add the storage charge:**Against the Product

**Use Case:**This method is helpful if you store odd-shaped items (meaning that pallet storage calculation methods are inaccurate) and stock needs to be separated from one another (meaning that volume-based storage calculation methods are incorrect). For example, a fridge may have a separate storage charge of $4.50 per fridge per week, while a microwave may have a storage charge of $1.20 per microwave per week.

**Charge Product Type Option Impact:**No impact - always charges based on item fees loaded against the Product.

**How it appears on the Invoice Summary:**

**![storage-charge-methods-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-6.png?width=670&height=43&name=storage-charge-methods-6.png)**

---

#### Per Pallet (based on quantity)

The total quantity of each Product is aggregated across all warehouse locations, and then the number of pallets are calculated and rounded up to the nearest whole number. Finally, the unit of measure conversion for the pallet quantity is utilised to determine the number of pallets. For example, if 6 cartons = 1 pallet and you have 8 cartons of one product for a storage period, CartonCloud will round up and charge 2 pallets for that storage period.

**Configuration required to enable the charging method:**Yes

To utilise the Per Pallet (based on quantity) storage charge method, you must first set up the Product Unit of Measure for pallet for each applicable Product using the charging method.

**Where to add the storage charge:**Rate Card

**Use Case:**This method is most commonly used as it can accurately track the actual storage space being utilised.

**Charge Product Type Option Impact:**No impact - the charge will always be based on the Product Type of the Product.

**How it appears on the Invoice Summary:**

**![storage-charge-methods-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-7.png?width=670&height=42&name=storage-charge-methods-7.png)**

![storage-charge-methods-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-8.png?width=670&height=142&name=storage-charge-methods-8.png)

---

#### Per Unit of Measure

This storage charge method calculates storage rates for products based on the Unit of Measure specified. This is similar to Per Item storage charging; however, you can have different charges for each different Unit of Measure. Additionally, you can choose to aggregate the stock by location or warehouse and round up or down to the closest Unit of Measure.

When selecting this charging method, you will need to choose a Unit of Measure Aggregation option:

The below will depend upon what Rate Type is selected (if the Unit of Measure is rounding up or down)

The below examples assume we have a product in which 6 bottles = 1 case. Therefore, there is no Unit of measure rounding up or down in the below example.

| Location | Storage Quantity | Aggregate by SKU by location | Aggregate by SKU across entire the warehouse |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AA-01-01 | 5 bottles | Combine 7 bottles together (5+2) which converts to 1 Bottle + 1 Case Charge 1 Case + 1 Bottle | Combine 12 bottles together (5+2+5), which converts to 2 cases. Charge 2 Cases |
| AA-01-01 | 2 bottles |  |  |
| AA-01-04 | 5 bottles | Charge 5 Bottles |  |
| **Total Charge** |  | **6 bottles + 1 case** | **2 cases** |

**Configuration required to enable the charging method:**Yes

To utilise the Per Unit of Measure storage charge method, you will first need to configure the:

**Product Settings:**

- Navigate to the Products page, **Warehouse**>**Products**.
- Select the relevant Product.
- Click the green **Edit** button at the top of the screen.
- Navigate to **Storage Charge Method**.
- Select **Per Unit of Measure**from the drop-down menu.
- You will also need to select the Unit of Measure Aggregation option. This will override the setting you configure on the Rate Card.
  - **As per rate card default setting:** this will apply the aggregation set up against the Rate Card.
  - **Per SKU, Per Location (and Bulk Location Pallets):** Aggregate all products on an SKU in one location.
  - **Per SKU, Across the Entire Warehouse:** Aggregate all products on an SKU across the entire warehouse.
- Scroll down and select the green **Save** button.

If you are updating this in bulk via Product export/import you will need to update the file with either of the below:

**Unit Of Measure Aggregation:**

| Unit of Measure Aggregation Option | Product Export Value |
| --- | --- |
| **As per rate card default setting** | Default |
| **Per SKU, Per Location (and Bulk Location Pallets)** | Location |
| **Per SKU, Across the Entire Warehouse** | Warehouse |

**Rate Card Settings:**

To set a default aggregation option for all Products on the Rate Card you can do so from the Rate Card settings.

- Navigate to the relevant Rate Card, **More**>**Rate Cards**.
- Select the blue **Edit** button at the bottom of the screen.
- Scroll down to the **Default Unit Of Measure Storage Aggregation** option.
- Select the relevant aggregation option.

**Where to add the storage charge:**Rate Card

**Charge Product Type Option Impact:**No impact - the charge will always be based on the Product Type of the Product.

**How it appears on the Invoice Summary:**

![storage-charge-methods-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-9.png?width=670&height=50&name=storage-charge-methods-9.png)
![storage-charge-methods-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-charge-methods-10.png?width=670&height=159&name=storage-charge-methods-10.png)

---

<a id="storage-periods-and-storage-charging"></a>
## Storage Periods and Storage Charging

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/storage-periods-and-storage-charging_

### Table of Contents

- Storage periods
- Configuring storage period settings
- Manually creating storage periods
- Storage charges
- Creating storage charges
- Split month storage billing
- How CartonCloud calculated storage charges

### Storage periods

Storage Periods are the number of days at a time that you wish to charge storage for.

Storage Periods contain a start and end date and are created automatically within CartonCloud as soon as products are loaded into warehouse locations.

### Configuring Storage Period Settings

You can configure the Storage Period start and end date by editing the relevant Rate Card. See [Add/Edit Rate Card](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Rate-Card) for more information.

### Manually Creating Storage Periods

In some cases, you may want to manually create a Storage Period and then manually add it to an invoice. If you do, please read [Creating a Storage Period](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Creating-a-Storage-PeriodCreating-a-Storage-Period)and [Adding Storage Periods to an Invoice](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Adding-Storage-Periods-to-an-Invoice) for more information.

### Storage charges

Storage Charges are applied to stock that is in the warehouse and allocated to a warehouse location. Storage Charges are automatically calculated and applied to invoices.

### Creating Storage Charges

There are a variety of different methods which can be used to calculate storage charges. See [Rate Card - Storage Charges](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/View-Rate-Card-Storage-Charges)for more information on how to create a Storage Charge and the different charging methods that can be utilised.

Storage Charges are always incurred for the full Storage Period for items. For example, if the defined Storage Period is 1 week and a pallet is only in the warehouse for 5 minutes it will still be charged 1 week of storage. Storage is never reduced for 'partial' periods.

### Split Month Storage Billing

If you charge your customers monthly for storage you have the option of enabling split monthly storage billing. This allows you to offer a discounted rate for stock that comes in later in the first month of the stock being in the warehouse. You have the ability to choose where the month is split and how much of a discount you wish to offer your customer. For example, if you choose to split the month on the 15th of every month and a discount of 15%, for any stock that comes into a warehouse location after the 15th will receive a discounted rate of 15% off their storage rates.

These settings are set up at the Organisation Settings and Rate Card level. If configured from the Organisation Settings, the settings will be applied to any new Rate Card that is created. If configured at the Rate Card level, the settings will only be applicable for that specific Rate Card.

### How CartonCloud calculated storage charges

CartonCloud will combine existing storage and new storage to calculate the Invoice Periods Storage Charges.

Existing storage: is the stock that is already on hand at the start of the storage period (the date allocated is prior to the storage period start date).

New storage: all the inbound stock that falls within the storage period (date allocated is within the storage period range).

The following diagram shows two storage periods, Storage Period 1 and Storage Period 2.
Assuming we're calculating storage charges on **Storage Period 2**, the Purchase Order Products which were allocated to locations prior to Storage Period 2 are charged as Existing Storage (as they were already in the warehouse when the Storage Period began), and the Purchase Order Products allocated during Storage Period 2 are charged as New Storage.

![storage-periods-and-storage-charging-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/storage-periods-and-storage-charging-0.png?width=562&height=141&name=storage-periods-and-storage-charging-0.png)

---

<a id="submitting-adhoc-charges"></a>
## Submitting Adhoc Charges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/submitting-adhoc-charges_

Within the CartonCloud mobile app users have the option to submit Adhoc Charges for either a Consignment, Sale Order, Purchase Order or a Run Sheet.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/juxMBhrVgm8

---

<a id="supplier-charges"></a>
## Supplier Charges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/supplier-charges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Expense charge calculation
- How the customer charges are selected
- Supplier rates for specific customers

### Purpose

Please note that if your CartonCloud account was created after 1 July 2019, you will not have access to Carriers and Drivers Expenses / Suppliers. Please reach out to our Service Desk team via [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/new-support-ticket) if you have any questions.

Income charges in CartonCloud, are calculated using the "Customer Charges" associated with the Customer. However, if Receiver Pays Freight & A2IC Mapping is enabled, the Customer Charge card used will come from the "Charge Customer".

Expense rates, however, have another layer of control, whereby the Customer Charges can be specified within the Supplier. In addition, customer-supplier specific rates can be configured.
For example Customer A can have different expense rates to Customer B when a specific Supplier is used.

### Expense charge calculation

While Calculating the Expense charge for consignment, the charges are calculated on the basis of **Driver/Carrier/Warehouse** Mapping.

**Driver Mapping**: If Driver is associated to the consignment then, the **supplier** which is linked to the driver gets associated to the consignment along with its charges.

**Carrier Mapping**: If no driver is associated to the consignment then, the charges are based on the carrier linked to the consignment.

**Warehouse Mapping**: If no driver/carrier mapping is associated then, the charges are based on the warehouse.

**Note:**When no Rate Cards are found appropriate error message "**Expense: No rates found for Supplier: '**xxxx**' with Rate Zone: '**xxxx'**(clear)**" is displayed.

How the "Customer Charges" are selected

When CartonCloud is calculating the Expense Rates for a job, the following logic applies:

![supplier-charges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/supplier-charges-0.png?width=255&height=374&name=supplier-charges-0.png)

![supplier-charges-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/supplier-charges-1.png?width=670&height=199&name=supplier-charges-1.png)

### Supplier rates for specific customers

Suppliers have the ability to charge different rates depending on the customer they're doing the work for. Say for example you have 3 Parties,  Customer A, Customer B and Supplier X. For delivering Customer A's consignments, Supplier X charges $10 per pallet. However, for delivering Customer B's consginments, Supplier X charges $15 per pallet. This can be easily configured by creating individual charge cards where required, and linking them to the Supplier.

By default, Supplier charges will apply to all customers. However, these rates can be overwritten for specific customers.

From the Edit Supplier Screen, simply click "Add Customer to link to Specific Supplier Charge".

![supplier-charges-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/supplier-charges-2.png?width=670&height=198&name=supplier-charges-2.png)
Then select the customer who you want to charge differently through billing:

![supplier-charges-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/supplier-charges-3.png?width=583&height=289&name=supplier-charges-3.png)
Then, select the appropriate Supplier Charge to use, and click "Save".

![supplier-charges-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/supplier-charges-4.png?width=670&height=255&name=supplier-charges-4.png)
Now, all jobs for **Steam Chef Store**will be charged out using the **4 Degrees** rate card.

---

<a id="surcharges"></a>
## Surcharges

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/surcharges_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to access surcharges
- Adding a new surcharge
- Applying the surcharge to a transport rate on a rate card
- Example of a surcharge applied to a rate
- Applying a surcharge to the minimum charge

### Purpose

Please note that if your CartonCloud account was created prior to 1 July 2019 the below functionality may not be available at this stage. If you would like to discuss further, please use [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com) and lodge a case with the Service Desk.

The Surcharges page provides the ability to create customisable and conditional Surcharges which can then be applied to transport rates on Rate Cards.

For example, a Surcharge can be created which is only applied on the condition that the required delivery date is a Saturday or Sunday.

### How to access surcharges

- To access the Surcharges page navigate to More in the main menu, followed by Surcharges in the drop-down menu.

![surcharges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/surcharges-0.png?width=670&height=328&name=surcharges-0.png)
From here you will be able to view, search and edit already created Surcharges.

### Adding a new surcharge

- To add a new Surcharge, begin by selecting +Add Surcharge .

![surcharges-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/surcharges-1.png?width=670&height=328&name=surcharges-1.png)

- Give the Surcharge a name which allows you to identify it easily.

![surcharges-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/surcharges-2.png?width=670&height=313&name=surcharges-2.png)

### Applying the Surcharge to a Transport Rate on a Rate Card

- To apply the Surcharge begin by navigating to Rate Cards by selecting More in the main menu, followed by Rate Cards in the drop-down menu.
- Select the Rate Card which contains the transport rate you wish to apply the surcharge to.
- Navigate to the Transport Rates tab.
- Select the Transport Rate you wish to apply the surcharge to.
- Once viewing the Transport rate, select the 3 verticle dots as shown in the screenshot below, then enable Surcharges.
- Now, select the Fee Category you would like the Surcharge to apply to within the Transport Rate you are viewing, and finally, the particular surcharge to apply (which was created in the last part of this article).

Please note that multiple surcharges can be applied to multiple fee categories at the same time.

- Lastly, scroll to the bottom of the page and select Save.

In the example provided, we are charging a Weekend Surcharge on all fee categories within this Transport Rate, which is set up for Hourly Hire rates.

![surcharges-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/surcharges-3.png?width=539&height=431&name=surcharges-3.png)
 Now whenever the rate is applied to a consignment and the Surcharges conditions are met the Surcharge will be applied.

### Example of a Surcharge applied to a Rate

In the example below an hourly hire consignment was booked charging for 4 hours of Semi hire at a rate of $125.00 on a Saturday. As the condition of it being weekend delivery was met the system has applied the surcharge of 100% on top of the hourly hire rate.

An extra hour of demurrage time was also charged on the day, but as the Surcharge is not applied to this ad hoc rate it does not affect it.

![surcharges-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/surcharges-4.png?width=670&height=106&name=surcharges-4.png)
Applying a Surcharge to the Minimum Charge

If you want to apply a Surcharge to a Minimum charge then you will need to ensure you have selected the fee category (minimum charge description) of the minimum charge for it to apply.

As shown below the fee category must either be set to "All" or otherwise match the Minimum Charge Description provided as shown below.

![surcharges-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/surcharges-5.png?width=581&height=470&name=surcharges-5.png)

### **Example of a Surcharge applied to a Minimum Charge**

In the example below an hourly hire consignment was booked charging for 1 hour of Semi hire at a rate of $125.00 on a Saturday. As a minimum charge of $200 was set up to be applied based on the rate shown above the system instead applied the minimum charge. As the condition of a weekend delivery was met the system has applied the surcharge of 100% on top of the minimum charge.

An extra hour of demurrage time was also charged on the day, but as the Surcharge is not applied to this ad hoc rate as ad hoc rates are not affected by surcharges.

![surcharges-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/surcharges-6.png?width=670&height=99&name=surcharges-6.png)

---

<a id="troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors"></a>
## Troubleshooting Storage Charge Errors

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to identify storage charge errors
- How to resolve storage
  - Check 1 - check the storage charge method set up against the product is used in the rate card
  - Check 2 - check the charge product type option
  - Check 3 - check that there is a charge set up for the product type
- Common storage charge errors
- Other Tools

### Purpose

Storage Charge errors will appear on your invoice when CartonCloud is unable to calculate charges for the Storage Period.

To troubleshoot Storage Charge Errors, there are certain checks you can complete to find the issue and resolve it.

### How to identify Storage Charge Errors

- Navigate to the Invoices page, **More**>**Invoices**.
- Identify the Invoice with Charge Errors and select it.
- Select the Storage Periods tab (it should have a numbered circle indicating an error exists).
- Click **View** against the Storage Period.
- Select the **Charges** tab.
- Read the Charge Error and resolve the error using the below checks and common charge error types.

### How to resolve Storage Charge Errors

Most Storage Charge errors occur when there is no charge within the **[Customer's Rate Card](https://3883312.hs-sites.com/rate-cards)**for that Product Type or Storage Charge Method specified within the **[Product](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/products).**

#### **Check 1 - Check the Storage Charge Method set up against the Product is used in the Rate Card**

- Navigate to the Products page, **Warehouse**>**Products**.
- Select **More**>**Export Products.**

**![troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-0.png?width=670&height=142&name=troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-0.png)**

- Open the downloaded file and check **column H** for the **Storage Charge Method**.

![troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-1.png?width=670&height=332&name=troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-1.png)

- Check the Rate Card has Storage Charges using the Storage Charge Methods selected for the Products.

![troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-2.png?width=670&height=144&name=troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-2.png)

- If there are no Storage Charges for that Storage Charge Method, you will need to add the m. Click here for steps on how to add Storage Charges.

If you select Per Pallet Storage Charge Method, ensure you set up the correct pallet conversions.

#### **Check 2 - Check the Charge Product Type Option**

- Navigate to the relevant Customer's Rate Card.
- Select the **Details** tab.
- Scroll down to the **Storage**section and check the **Charge Product Type Option**.

![troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-3.png?width=670&height=238&name=troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-3.png)

- There are two options;
  - **Product:** charges storage based on the Products Product Type.
  - **Warehouse Location:**charges storage based on the Warehouse Locations Product Type.

#### **Check 3 - Check that there is a charge set up for the Product Type**

Depending on the results from Check 2, you will need to check the Product's Product Type or the Warehouse Location's Product Type to ensure there is a charge configured for the Product Type.

Product's Product Type:

- Navigate to the Product (that had the Storage Charge error).
- Select the **Details** tab.
- Check the **Product Type** and the **Storage Charge Method**.

![troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-4.png?width=670&height=265&name=troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-4.png)

**Warehouse Location Product Type:**

- Navigate to the relevant **Warehouse Location**.
- Check the **Product Type**.

![troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-5.png?width=670&height=335&name=troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-5.png)

Check there is a charge set up for that Product Type

- Navigate to the Customer's Rate Card.
- Select the **Storage Charges** tab.
- Check there is a charge set up for the relevant Product Type.

![troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-6.png?width=670&height=145&name=troubleshooting-storage-charge-errors-6.png)

If there are no charges set up for the Product Type, follow the instructions [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/eAH8Hw) to set up a new storage charge for that Product Type.

Common Storage Charge Errors

To resolve an error, you will have to go to the [Customer's Rate Card](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/eAH8Hw) or [Product settings](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/5AGYHw).

| **Error Type** | **Resolution** |
| --- | --- |
| No General Per Pallet Qty rates loaded | **Resolved at: the Rate Card** Add General + Per Pallet Qty rates to the Storage Charges tab of the Customer's Rate Card. |
| No Frozen Per Pallet Qty rates loaded | **Resolved at: the Rate Card** Add General + Per Pallet Qty to the Customer Storage Charges Tab |
| No Per-Location Charges exist | **Resolved at: the Rate Card.**   Refer to Check 1   Look at the Charge Product Type Option; it will be Product or Location; add a Storage Charge for Per Location or Product. |
| No Per Item Storage Charge | **Resolved at: Product settings** Products have been set up using the "Per Item" charge method, but no charge has been set. To resolve:   [Export the Product file](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/MQ03I)for the customer   Check the "Storage Charge Method" column.   If this is correctly set up and you want to use this charge method, add the charge in the column headed "Per Item Storage Charge". Import the file with the charges back into the system to update your products.    If the charge is incorrect, update the "Storage Charge Method" option to the correct charge type and import the file to update the system. |
| No Standard Pallet Volume rates loaded | **Resolved at: the Rate Card.** Add Standard Pallet rates to the Customer's Rate Card. |
| Error calculating charge for product id (1077). Oversize unit not defined | **Resolved at: Product settings** To identify the product ID in question, head to the Products page. View any product. In the webpage address bar, you can now enter the Product ID from the charge error and view: Check the Product for the Storage Charge Method (Check 1).  **Per Location** Check your Product Settings to allow for "Oversize" items. This setting, when enabled, will notify you when you may be over-allocating to a single pallet. For example, oversize warnings should always be enabled for Pallet Units of Measure. This will prevent you from allocating more than one pallet worth of stock to a single location. In addition, CartonCloud will provide you with a warning message that you may be under-charging for storage.  To enable this setting:   Navigate to the Product Unit of Measure page by typing Product Unit of Measure into the Search for anything bar.   Select edit against the relevant Product Unit of Measure.   Change the Oversize Warning to yes.    **Per Pallet (based on qty) Charge Method** If Storage Charges are set to "Per Pallet (based on qty)", check that you have [set up the conversion](https://help.cartoncloud.com/x/EAO_Hw) for the base unit of measure to a pallet. If you want your Product to charge per pallet qty, you must state how many units are on a pallet, i.e. 256 per pallet or 126 per pallet. |
| Storage & Invoice periods are not aligned. | **Resolved at: Organisation Settings**   Navigate to **Organisation Settings**>**Invoice**.    Scroll down to Invoice Defaults and Storage.    Check the '**Default Next Invoice Date'** and **'Default First Day Of Week**' match the **'Storage Starts On'** date. |
| My pallet location is charging twice even though I have selected 'Per Location' charging. | **Resolved at: the Rate Card** By default, when stock is received, it is always charged as 'new' storage even if it is allocated to a location already containing stock. If you do not want to charge new storage, you need to set Double Charge Storage to No (this setting can be found in the Rate Card under the Details tab). |
| Storage has not been charged for backdated Purchase Orders. | The system will always generate the Storage Period charge based on the "Date Added" purchase order by default. This means that if you have backdated the purchase order to be "Date Allocated" in the week previous, the Storage Period will need to be manually created and added to your invoice. However, suppose the invoice has already been approved and sent to the customer. In that case, you can create Storage Periods manually and add these to a new invoice to calculate the extra revenue. |
| No Product Type Charge for Non Dangerous Goods | **Resolved at: the Rate Card** Add a charge to the Rate Card for the Product Type Non-Dangerous Goods. |
| No Product Type Charge for (General, Bottle, Keg etc.) which is currently being stored in the Warehouse | **Resolved at: the Rate Card** Add a charge to the Rate Card for the Product Type specified.  **If this product type should not be in use or if you want to check which products are using this product type, you can review the products by [Exporting and Importing Products.](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/exporting-and-importing-products)** |
| Changing Storage Settings within Invoice Period | Suppose you have changed the Storage Settings for a customer within a Storage Period. In that case, you must create [a Storage Period](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Creating-a-Storage-PeriodCreating-a-Storage-Period) and re-calculate your invoice totals to reflect the change. For example, you now wish to charge your customer for Daily Storage, where previously they were on a weekly storage invoice. As this is during the current invoice period, the system will no longer automatically generate the invoice for the previous weekly charge and, in this case, hasn't yet reached the schedule to start the daily storage creation. So instead, follow the steps above to create the missing storage periods for each day (Daily Charging), and then go into your invoices and add these periods in. Once you have completed this, you must re-calculate the totals to reflect the additional days. |
| Daily Charged Storage is not generating for each day of the week, only for the first day. | **Resolved at: Organisation Settings and  Rate Card** In CartonCloud, two settings determine when and how the Storage Charges are calculated.       **[Organisation Settings - Invoice](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Organisation+Settings+-+Invoice)**   At an organisational level, the Default Storage Days drive the system to generate Storage Periods. So if you wish to have customers on a daily storage charge, for example, you would have the Default Storage Days set to "1."  (Some tenants of CartonCloud may have this set to "7" by default. If you do not wish to charge storage periods less than weekly, you do not need to change this) **Rate Card Settings** If you wish to override the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level, you can modify the settings at the Rate Card level within the Rate Card settings. |
| Storage Periods are not attaching themselves to the invoice (They have to be manually added) | **Resolved at: Organisation Settings** This is because the Storage Periods and the Invoice Periods are not aligned.   Navigate to **Organisation Settings**>**Invoice**.    Then, scroll down to Invoice Defaults and Storage.    Check the '**Default Next Invoice Date'** and **'Default First Day Of Week**' match the **'Storage Starts On'** date.   **Once you have aligned the dates, you will need to then either:**   The most recent Storage Period for this customer needs to be updated to finish the same date/time as the invoice, then, it'll start from there going forward, and everything will be synchronised OR   You don't invoice storage for a week, and then next week, the invoice will pick up the old storage. |
| The product does not allow zero storage charge, however, the storage charge has come out as $0. For example, 'Per pallet rates for Ambient zone are zero, but product: Coffee beans does not allow zero storage charges'. | **Resolved at: Product Settings**  Use the [Exporting and Importing Products.](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/exporting-and-importing-products) Update the value for the mentioned Product under the header *Allow Zero Storage Charge* to Yes,  Re-import the file to update. |
| Error calculating charge for product id [x]: Multiple oversize units of measure cannot exist | Resolved at: Product Unit of Measure Settings This error is due to the mentioned Product ID having Multiple Oversize Units assigned to the location which is not allowed when charging Per Pallet Based on QTY storage charge method.  You will need to either remove one of the products with an oversize unit from the location or you will need to to turn off the Oversize Warning field. To turn off the Oversize Warning you will need to:  Navigate to the Product Unit of Measure page by typing in Product Unit of Measure into the Search for anything bar.  Select the relevant Product Unit of Measure which is oversized. Set the **Oversize Warning** field to '**No**'. |

### Other Tools

In addition to the above you can also use the Stock Movement Report to reconcile storage charges by select the report movement type of Storage Charges. See [Stock Movement Report](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Stock-Movement-Report) for more information.

---

<a id="understanding-sale-order-item-charge-data-handling-charges"></a>
## Understanding Sale Order Item Charge Data (Handling Charges)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/understanding-sale-order-item-charge-data-handling-charges_

When dealing with large Sale Orders that contain many item charges it can sometimes become difficult to work out the break down of charges for specific units of measurement and the quantities associated.

We've built a tool to help make this much easier, the  **Explain** button.

![understanding-sale-order-item-charge-data-handling-charges-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/understanding-sale-order-item-charge-data-handling-charges-0.png?width=670&height=544&name=understanding-sale-order-item-charge-data-handling-charges-0.png)
Located on the Charges tab when Viewing a Sale Order, selecting the Explain button will provide you with a breakdown of how Handling Charges are being generated from the Product stock selected.

![understanding-sale-order-item-charge-data-handling-charges-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/understanding-sale-order-item-charge-data-handling-charges-1.png?width=670&height=333&name=understanding-sale-order-item-charge-data-handling-charges-1.png)

This will allow you to easily troubleshoot when the handling charges generated on a Sale Order aren't what you might have expected.

It's possible after reviewing the break down you may need assistance

**Video Overview**

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/Hw4Cs2nZZcM

---

<a id="use-highest-charge-and-charge-group"></a>
## Use Highest Charge and Charge Group

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/use-highest-charge-and-charge-group_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Benefits
- Use Case Example
- How To Steps

### Purpose

With '**Use Highest Charge**' enabled, only the highest charge returned within a charge group will be applied to the Consignment.

With '**Use Highest Charge Group**' enabled, the value of each Charge Group will be compared against each other and the Charge Group with the highest value will be applied to the Consignment.

### Benefits

- You will not need to make manual calculations to determine which charging method will return the highest charge.
- Allows flexibility in your charging methods, as you do not have to select only one charging method.

### Use Case Example

**Use Highest Charge:**

For example, if you want to charge delivery on either the spaces or pallets, depending on what cost is greater you can enable 'Use Highest Charge'. This means, depending on what charge type brings back the highest charge that method will be utilised.

![use-highest-charge-and-charge-group-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/use-highest-charge-and-charge-group-0.png?width=572&height=420&name=use-highest-charge-and-charge-group-0.png)
