Assuming we're calculating storage charges on **Storage Period 2**, the Purchase Order Products which were allocated to locations before Storage Period 2 are charged as Existing Storage (as they were already in the warehouse when the Storage Period began), and the Purchase Order Products allocated during Storage Period 2 are charged as New Storage.

![view-rate-card-storage-charges-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/view-rate-card-storage-charges-2.png?width=522&height=131&name=view-rate-card-storage-charges-2.png)

### How modifying in and out dates impacts Storage Charges

CartonCloud allows you to make retrospective changes to the Purchase Order Allocated Dates and Sale Order Packed / Dispatched Dates.

Doing so will affect storage charges for Storage Periods that fall within the dates changed. Typically, this will only affect Storage Periods which have not yet been invoiced. However, Storage Periods which have already been invoiced can be manually "Recalculated" for the updated charges to be calculated.

**Example:**

We have weekly Storage Periods which run Monday → Sunday.

Invoicing is done on a Monday morning for the previous week.

On Friday, a Purchase Order is received and verified; however, for some reason, CartonCloud is not updated at the time. Come Monday, and the Purchase Order is verified within CartonCloud; however, to capture the Storage Charges from the previous week, the Allocated Date is updated to the previous Friday.

In this situation, there is now a Storage Period that has been affected (the previous week); however, this Storage Period has already been invoiced. Therefore, for it to be updated to reflect the new charges, it needs to be recalculated.
![view-rate-card-storage-charges-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/view-rate-card-storage-charges-3.png?width=670&height=198&name=view-rate-card-storage-charges-3.png)
Once this has been done, the Storage Period will reflect the new charges based on the stock that has arrived on Friday.

### Split Month Storage Billing

If you charge your customers monthly for storage you have the option of enabling split month storage billing. This allows you to offer a discounted rate for stock that comes in later in the first month of the stock being in the warehouse or for stock that leaves the warehouse early in its last month of storage. You have the ability to choose where the month is split and how much of a discount you wish to offer your customer.

For example, if you choose to split the month on the 15th of every month and a discount of 15%, for any stock that comes into a warehouse location late after the 15th will receive a discounted rate of 15% off their storage rates. Or if you choose to split the month on the 10th of every month and a discount of 5% for any stock that leaves early in its last month of storage, any stock that leaves before the 10th of its last month of storage will receive a discounted rate of 5% off their storage rates.

This can be configured from within the Rate Card and at the Organisation Settings level. If configured at the Organisation Settings, the settings will be automatically applied to any new rate card created. If the settings are configured at the rate card level, they will only apply to that particular rate card.

- Navigate to the relevant Rate Card, More > Rate Card .
- Select Edit against the Rate Card.
- Scroll down to Storage Period Generation .
- For Storage Period Generation select Monthly .
- Fill in the following fields:
- Day of month to charge on: day of the month to charge on
- Split Month Discount: select enabled
- Days before split occurs: the number of days into the storage period after which the split should occur. This will define when stock is considered in the split period and the discount is applied. For example, if you set the days to 20, any stock that comes in after 20 days of the month will be considered in the second split period and receive the percentage discount off the storage rates.
- Discount applied for:
  - First month (allocation) - this will be applied for stock that arrives late in the first month. **(only applicable if using per pallet or per location storage charge method)**
  - Last month (depletion) - this will be applied for stock that leaves early in its last month of storage *(only applicable if using per location storage charge method. This option is currently in beta release only. If you would like access to this beta release please reach out to the team via [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport) ​​​​​​).*
  - Both First month (allocation) and last month (depletion) - both discounts will be applied. This means a discount will be given to stock that arrives late in the first month and to stock that leaves early in its last month of storage **(only applicable if using per location storage charge method. This option is currently in beta release only. If you would like access to this beta release please reach out to the team via [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport) ​​​​​​).**
- Percentage discount: the percentage discounted from the storage rates. The percentage is only applied to stock that is allocated to a warehouse location post the day the split occurs.
- Scroll down and select Save .

![view-rate-card-storage-charges-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/view-rate-card-storage-charges-4.png?width=422&height=372&name=view-rate-card-storage-charges-4.png)

---

<a id="view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates"></a>
## View Rate Card - Transport Rates

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Steps to create a Transport Rate
- Charge group types
  - Consignment total charges
  - Consignment item charges

### Purpose

Please note that if your CartonCloud account was created prior to 1 July 2019 the below functionality may not be available at this stage. If you would like to discuss migrating to the newer version of the CartonCloud TMS, please use [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/new-support-ticket) and lodge a case with our Service Desk.

Before starting this article, you must have already completed [Setting up CartonCloud to automatically allocate Consignments](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Setting-up-CartonCloud-to-automatically-allocate-Consignments), [Setting up CartonCloud to automatically rate Consignments](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Setting-up-CartonCloud-to-automatically-rate-Consignments), [Setting up Service Types](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Setting+up+Service+Types), and [Setting up Transport Products](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Setting+up+Transport+Products).

Transport Rates are the rates your customers will be charged for deliveries and are created within a Rate Card.

Within a Transport Rate, you will have either a single or multiple Charge Groups and within that Charge Group, you will create your charges using different charging methods. For more information on what a Charge Group is, please see the [Charge Group](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/View+Rate+Card#ViewRateCard-chargegroup) section of this article.

### Steps to create a Transport Rate

Follow the below steps to create a Transport Rate.

**1. Add a new Transport Rate** - from within the Rate Card, add a new Transport Rate.

- Type **Rate Card** into the **Search for anything** bar.
- Select the relevant Rate Card and click **Transport Rates** from within the Rate Card.
- Click **Add Rate** in the top left corner.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Jun-01-2026-09-32-49-2468-PM.png?width=3819&height=927&name=image-png-Jun-01-2026-09-32-49-2468-PM.png)

- Upon adding a new Transport Rate the below screen will appear.

![view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-1.png?width=670&height=323&name=view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-1.png)

**2. Set up the Transport Rate** - fill in the applicable fields for both the header and default information sections.

![view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-2.png?width=670&height=323&name=view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-2.png)

- The below field descriptions will assist you when filling in this information.
- **Remember to activate the rate once completed by sliding the toggle in the top right.**

![view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-3.png?width=670&height=237&name=view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-3.png)

| **Section** | **Field** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Header Information | Name | The name of the Transport Rate. It is important that you use a name that allows you to easily identify the rate. We suggest using a descriptive name, for example, GC Depot - North & South. |
|  | Effective Date | The date the rates will become effective and be used to calculate consignment charges. This is helpful if you do not wish to use the rates immediately and are instead building the rates for a future date. To use the Transport Rate immediately, use today's date. |
|  | Expiry Date | Date when the rates are no longer valid and CartonCloud will no longer use the rates to charge consignments. This is useful when planning to transition from one Transport Rate set up to a new one (one would expire on a set date, and the new one would become active on the same date). |
|  | Review Date | The date you would like to review the rate. This field is not required and can be left blank. |
|  | Service Type | What type of service the rate is for e.g General Service, Hourly Service, Express Service. |
|  | From Zone | The Zone from which the consignment is originating from. |
|  | To Zone | The Zone the consignment is going to. |
| Default Information | No Charge | If you do not wish to charge for the movement. This is useful if you do not charge customers to bring goods back to your warehouse. |
|  | Fuel Levy | You can apply a different fuel levy from the rate card by either overriding the existing fuel levy (selecting Rate from the drop-down menu) or offsetting it (selecting Offset from the drop-down menu and the off-set percentage amount). For more information see [Fuel Levy](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Fuel-Levy). |
|  | Inactive/Active slide bar | This sliding bar will active the rate. |
|  | Add Charge Group | Select to start adding charges to the Transport Rate. |

**3. Configure charges**- use the **Add Charge Group** to select the Consignment Charge Group Types you would like to add to your Transport Rate and the appropriate charging methods to configure your charges.

Before configuring your charges it is recommended that you read the [Charge Group Types](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/View+Rate+Card#ViewRateCard-chargegroup)section of this article.

- Select **Add Charge Group.**
- Select the relevant **Consignment Charge Group** from the drop-down menu.

![view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-4.png?width=670&height=289&name=view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-4.png)

- Select the relevant [Fee Category.](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Charge-Subclasses-Fee-Category)
- Click **Add Consignment Totals/Item Charge** to begin configuring your charge. You will have four or five (depending on what type of Charge Group you selected) [charging methods](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/charging-methods) to choose from. For Consignment Item Charges you will have an additional option of Item Code which will enable you to charge per item.
- Fill in the charge amount and what it will be charged by (depending on what [Charging Method](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Charging-Methods) you have selected).

![view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-5.png?width=670&height=228&name=view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-5.png)

- Ensure to apply the**[fuel levy](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Fuel-Levy)** by clicking the fuel icon next to the applicable charge.

It is suggested you apply a fuel levy to all charges that use fuel. For example, a hand-unload fee would not incur a fuel levy as you do not use fuel to perform the task. See [Fuel Levy](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Fuel-Levy) for more information.

- Once you have added your initial charge, you can continue to add new charges to the Transport Rate by selecting the **Add Charge Group** button at the bottom of the screen.
- There is no limit to the number of charges you can have in the one Charge Group or Transport Rate.

| **Field/button** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Charge Group based on Consignment Totals | The type of charge group you have selected will appear at the top of the charge. |
| Delete button | Delete the charge. This will not delete the Transport Rate, only the charge configuration. |
| Three dot icon | Extra features that can be enabled when configuring the charge   **Allow zero charge:** once enabled this will prevent CartonCloud from producing an error message when there are no charges for a particular rate or consignment item. For more information see the [Allow Zero Charge](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Allow-Zero-Charge).    **Charge Weight:**allows you to charge per weight (adds this option to the per charge drop-down menu) andprovides a cubic configuration at the top of the charge group.    **Charge Pallets:** allows you to charge per pallet (adds this option to the per charge drop-down menu) and provides a pallet configuration at the top of the charge group.    **Sum by item code**(only available for Charge Group based on Consignment Items): sums the total item codes instead of charging each line individually. |
| Fee Category | A way to identify and describe the charge e.g 'Delivery'. This title will appear on the customer's invoice in reference to the charge. |
| Add Consignment Totals Charge | Add the charge configuration. This is how the charge will be calculated. There are four different charge configurations to choose from. |
| Add Charge Group | Select this if you wish to add a completely new charge (different from the one you just added). You can either select the same Charge Group as your previous charge or select a different one. |

### Charge Group Types

A Charge Group provides the template and structure to create charges and allows you to group different charges within a Transport Rate. The two types of Consignment Charge Groups you can choose from include, a Consignment Total Charge or a Consignment Item Charge. Depending on how you wish to charge your consignments will dictate what type of Charge Group you select.

#### **Consignment Total Charges**

Consignment Total Charges will charge by the total number of items in a consignment. For example, if you have a consignment with the consignment items of one pallet and two cartons the charge will be applied to all three items.

#### **Consignment Item Charges**

Consignment Item Charges will charge by a single line item in a consignment. This means you can apply different rates to different consignment items within one consignment. For example, for a consignment with one pallet and two cartons, you can apply one rate for the pallet and another rate for the cartons.

Note, you can have both types of Charge Groups within one Transport Rate.

**Example Transport Rate with both types of Charge Groups**

![view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-6.png?width=670&height=472&name=view-rate-card-transport-ratesview-rate-card-transport-rates-6.png)

# Mobile App

<a id="android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications"></a>
## Android Mobile Sound and Vibration Notifications

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Benefits
- Prerequisites
- How to enable vibration notifications
- How to enable sound notifications

### Purpose

On your Android Mobile application, you have the option to enable sound and vibration notifications when error messages are presented.

Please note, at this stage, the sound and vibration notifications are only available for Android devices and not iOS.

### Benefits

- Reduced risk of the user missing the error message.
- The user does not need to be viewing the mobile device screen to know an error has occurred.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/dM75htcvgvs

### Prerequisites

- Ensure you have enabled sound and vibration on your device settings. If you have not turned them on, you will be prompted when using the CartonCloud application.

![android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-0.png?width=264&height=587&name=android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-0.png)

- Ensure the volume is turned up and that silent mode is not turned on.

### How to enable Vibration Notifications

- Navigate to **Settings**.

![android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-1.png?width=280&height=622&name=android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-1.png)

- Under Error Notifications, check the Enable Vibration Notifications check box.

![android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-2.png?width=271&height=602&name=android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-2.png)

### How to enable Sound Notifications

- Navigate to **Settings**.

![android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-3.png?width=277&height=616&name=android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-3.png)

- Scroll to the bottom and click **Sound Settings**.

![android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-4.png?width=269&height=598&name=android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-4.png)

- Click on a sound option to hear a preview of the sound.

![android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-5.png?width=280&height=622&name=android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-5.png)

- Select the sound you wish to use as the error message and click the back arrow in the top left.

![android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-6.png?width=273&height=612&name=android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-6.png)

Select the No Sound option if you want to turn the sound notification off.

**Example error message**

![android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-7.png?width=279&height=620&name=android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-7.png)
**Examples of when an error sound and vibration will play:**

Please note, the below is only examples and not an extensive list. The error sound and vibration will play each time an error message is received.

- Quantity confirmation screen - scan/input a non-existent barcode of Unit of Measure.
- Quantity confirmation screen - scan incorrect SSCC/Custom Field barcode.
- Location filter/product filter - scan/input a non-existent barcode of the Product
- Search screen - scan/input a non-existent POP or warehouse location.

Swipe to dismiss the error message

You can dismiss the error message by swiping it across to the left.

![android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-8.png?width=261&height=583&name=android-mobile-sound-and-vibration-notifications-8.png)

---

<a id="app-permissions"></a>
## App Permissions

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/app-permissions_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Permissions
  - Required permissions
  - Changing permissions

### Purpose

Both Android and iOS require the user to give permissions to the CartonCloud app to access certain information and device functionality.

### Permissions

**Required Permissions**

| **Description** | **Android** | **iOS** |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Location** | Location is required to submit a driver's location while using the app and on completing actions in the app, e.g. submitting a POD. | Initial access granted automatically on install. (Android versions prior to 6.0)   Requested once, the first time access to location is required - on initial launch of app. (Android 6.0 and above) | Requested once, on initial launch of the app. |
| **Camera** | CartonCloud requires access to the device's camera to upload Invoice Photos and include photos for Errors and PODs. | Initial access granted automatically on install. (Android versions prior to 6.0)   Requested once, the first time access to camera is required. (Android 6.0 and above) | Requested once, the first time access to camera is required. |
| **Photos / Device Storage** | In addition to taking photos with the device's camera, the user can also choose to use a photo already on their device. | Initial access granted automatically on install. (Android versions prior to 6.0)   Requested once, the first time access to photos is required. (Android 6.0 and above) | Requested once, the first time access to photos is required. |
| **Notifications** | Notifications need to be enabled for the CartonCloud app to receive Push Notifications from the web app. | Initial access granted automatically on install. | Requested once, on initial launch of the app. |

**Android-Only**

**![app-permissions-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/app-permissions-0.png?width=253&height=450&name=app-permissions-0.png)**

| **Description** | **Android** |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Send SMS** | Provided as an option to contact customers in Consignment Detail View if contact information has been provided. | Initial access granted automatically on install. |
| **Call Phone** | Provided as an option to contact customers in Consignment Detail View if contact information has been provided. | Initial access granted automatically on install. |

**iOS-Only**

![app-permissions-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/app-permissions-1.png?width=282&height=501&name=app-permissions-1.png)

| **Description** | **iOS** |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Background App Refresh** | The CartonCloud app continues certain operations while in the background e.g. checking the user's "Clock On" status with the server to ensure Push Notifications are sent correctly. | Initial access granted automatically on install. |

**Changing Permissions**

Users may change permissions given to the CartonCloud app at any stage, by modifying the app settings on their device.

**Android**

Permissions can be modified in ***Settings > Apps > CartonCloud > Permissions***on devices that allow users to manage permissions. This is only possibly on devices running Android 6.0 (Marshmallow) and up.

Notification settings are handled separately in some newer versions of Android though and can be tuned in more fine-grain detail in ***Settings > Notifications > App Notifications > CartonCloud***, as well as just switching them on or off in the app permission settings*.
![app-permissions-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/app-permissions-2.png?width=399&height=343&name=app-permissions-2.png)***iOS**

All CartonCloud app related permissions can be modified in ***Settings > CartonCloud.***

![app-permissions-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/app-permissions-3.png?width=243&height=432&name=app-permissions-3.png)

---

<a id="app-regions"></a>
## App Regions

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/app-regions_

### Mobile App Region Switching

CartonCloud is hosted in two different regions, Asia Pacific (Sydney) and North America (Ohio). When CartonCloud tenancies are created, they are created in either Asia Pacific or North America depending on the geographic location.

### Accessing the various regions

### Webapp

Accessing the different regions via the webapp is achieved through two different URLs

Asia Pacific: [app.cartoncloud.com](http://app.cartoncloud.com)North America: [app.na.cartoncloud.com](http://app.na.cartoncloud.com)

### Mobile App

On the mobile app, the region is always visible on the login screen.

![app-regions-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/app-regions-0.png?width=269&height=556&name=app-regions-0.png)

On first install, the app automatically selects a region based on the phone’s system region and remembers the selected region for future logins:

- Phones set to the Americas: default to North America
- All other regions: default to Asia Pacific

To log in, select the desired region, enter the username and password, and proceed.

![app-regions-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/app-regions-1.png?width=287&height=593&name=app-regions-1.png)

To switch regions, log out of the app and select the other region on the login screen before logging in again.

<a id="custom-fields-on-mobile"></a>
## Custom Fields on Mobile

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/custom-fields-on-mobile_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Where custom fields are displayed in the mobile app
  - Consignment data custom fields
  - Consignment item custom fields
  - Consignment detail screen
  - Sale order consignment creation screen
- Purchase order product custom fields
- Sale order custom fields

Purpose

CartonCloud allows users to create their own "custom" fields within the web app. This provides users with a way to capture additional data, that isn't captured by the default fields.

The most obvious example of this is the opening and closing time of an address on a consignment. By default, this data is not captured within CartonCloud, but by creating a custom field it can be captured at the time of creating the address, [then automatically linked to the consignment data](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Custom+Field+Linking).

When [custom fields are created and configured](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Custom+Fields), the user is presented with an option to choose if the custom field will appear on the mobile application or not.

### Where custom fields are displayed in the mobile app

Custom fields can be displayed in multiple locations throughout the mobile application, as illustrated below. Additionally, they can be configured to show on the detailed view, or detailed view and list view.

The field names given are examples only and can be customised as required based on an operation's requirements.

**Consignment Data Custom Fields**

![custom-fields-on-mobile-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-0.png?width=250&height=445&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-0.png)

 ![custom-fields-on-mobile-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-1.png?width=239&height=425&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-1.png)

In the above example, a custom field has been set up for the Opening and Closing times of a delivery. The closing time example has been configured to display on the list view (as this is useful information for the driver), while the opening time is only displayed on the detailed view.

It is also worth noting that when no data is entered into a custom field that it will not display at all (such is the case with Bobs Grocery Burleigh Heads).

**Consignment Item Custom Fields**

![custom-fields-on-mobile-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-2.png?width=249&height=443&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-2.png)

![custom-fields-on-mobile-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-3.png?width=482&height=271&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-3.png)

**Consignment Detail Screen**

Custom fields saved against a consignment item can be viewed first accessing the details view of a consignment, then selecting the **View Items** button.

**Sale Order Consignment Creation Screen**

![custom-fields-on-mobile-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-4.png?width=244&height=433&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-4.png)

![custom-fields-on-mobile-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-5.png?width=240&height=427&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-5.png)
Once a Sale Order has been picked if you have enabled automatic consignment creation from Sale Orders then the custom fields will display during the Consignment creation stage.

### Purchase Order Product Custom Fields

**![custom-fields-on-mobile-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-6.png?width=272&height=484&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-6.png)**

![custom-fields-on-mobile-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-7.png?width=263&height=468&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-7.png)

![custom-fields-on-mobile-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-8.png?width=257&height=457&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-8.png)
It is important to note that unlike other custom fields, Purchase Order custom fields are [set up within the edit customer page](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Setting+up+Custom+Fields+for+Purchase+Order+Products).

These fields will display on both the pick list view and on the quantity confirmation view (provided you select to expand custom fields).

In the above example, a whole coffee bean distributor uses Purchase Order Custom fields to capture the roast date for their stock.

### Sale Order Custom Fields

**Sale Order Details View**

Sale order custom fields will display when selecting a Sale Order and navigating to the details view.

![custom-fields-on-mobile-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/custom-fields-on-mobile-9.png?width=277&height=492&name=custom-fields-on-mobile-9.png)

---

<a id="how-to-check-which-version-of-the-cartoncloud-app-you-are-running"></a>
## How to check which version of the CartonCloud app you are running

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-check-which-version-of-the-cartoncloud-app-you-are-running_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Check which version in iOS
- Check which version in Android

### Purpose

How to check which version of the app you're running.With CartonCloud, new features, bug fixes, and general improvements are being released all the time. Our web application is updating constantly without you needing to do anything, however the mobile phone apps need to be updated (often manually).

It's important to keep updated on the Mobile Devices so you get access to the latest features and fixes.

### Check which version

To check which version you're running, open the CartonCloud App and then open the Menu on the left and select "Settings". You will find the version information in the "About" section.

![how-to-check-which-version-of-the-cartoncloud-app-you-are-running-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-check-which-version-of-the-cartoncloud-app-you-are-running-0.jpeg?width=203&height=409&name=how-to-check-which-version-of-the-cartoncloud-app-you-are-running-0.jpeg)

![how-to-check-which-version-of-the-cartoncloud-app-you-are-running-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-check-which-version-of-the-cartoncloud-app-you-are-running-1.jpeg?width=217&height=446&name=how-to-check-which-version-of-the-cartoncloud-app-you-are-running-1.jpeg)

---

<a id="how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app"></a>
## How to update the CartonCloud mobile app

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Benefits of updating the mobile app
- How to update automatically
  - iOS
  - Android
- How to update manually
  - iOS
  - Android

### Purpose

In order to optimise the full benefits of the CartonCloud mobile app, the latest version of the app must be installed on your device. To ensure your app is up to date, you can configure your device to update it automatically or manually update it.

### Benefits of updating the mobile app

- Latest features - our latest features and improvements are made available by updating the app, therefore, you need the latest version of the app to utilise the latest features.
- Improved performance - faster loads times and reduced risk of the app crashing when you are using the latest version of the app.
- Bug fixes - bugs can impact app performance and usability. Updates will fix any bugs that may be in the application.
- Security - outdated apps can pose a higher risk of cyberattacks.

### How to update automatically

**iOS**

- Navigate to **Settings** on the device.
- Click **App Store**.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-0.png?width=252&height=448&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-0.png)

- Under the **Automatic Downloads** section, slide the toggle against **App Updates**.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-1.png?width=275&height=489&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-1.png)
The apps on your device will now automatically update as updates become available.

**Android**

- Navigate to the Google Play Store.
- Select the User button in the top right corner of the screen.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-2.png?width=266&height=591&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-2.png)

- Select **Manage apps and device**.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-3.png?width=271&height=602&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-3.png)

- Select the **Manage** tab.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-4.png?width=276&height=613&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-4.png)

- Select **CartonCloud** from the available apps.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-5.png?width=278&height=618&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-5.png)

- Click the three-dot icon in the top right corner.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-6.png?width=298&height=662&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-6.png)

- Select the tick box next to the **Enable auto-update.**

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-7.png?width=280&height=622&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-7.png)

Your device will now automatically update the CartonCloud application as new updates are released.

### How to update manually

**iOS**

- Navigate to the **App store**.
- Select the **User** icon in the top right corner.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-8.png?width=311&height=553&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-8.png)

- Scroll through the list of apps until you locate CartonCloud.
- Select **Update**.

**Android**

- Navigate to the Google Play Store.
- Select the User button in the top right corner of the screen.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-9.png?width=298&height=662&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-9.png)

- Select **Manage apps and device**.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-10.png?width=312&height=693&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-10.png)

- Select the **Manage** tab.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-11.png?width=281&height=624&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-11.png)

- Select **CartonCloud** from the available apps.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-12.png?width=285&height=633&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-12.png)

- Select the green **Update** button.

![how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-13](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-13.png?width=273&height=607&name=how-to-update-the-cartoncloud-mobile-app-13.png)

<a id="keyboard-not-showing-ios"></a>
## Keyboard not Showing (iOS)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/keyboard-not-showing-ios_

### Table of Contents

- Issue
- How to resolve the issue

### Issue

When a Bluetooth scanner is connected to your mobile device, the keyboard becomes disabled (it won't show when you tap a text field).

More information on how to connect a Bluetooth scanner: [Connect a Bluetooth Barcode Scanner](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Connect+a+Bluetooth+Barcode+Scanner)

Recommended scanning devices: [Scanning Devices](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Scanning-Devices)

### How to resolve the issue

Double press the trigger button on your scanner to bring up the keyboard on your mobile device.

<a id="push-notifications"></a>
## Push Notifications

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/push-notifications_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How push notifications are displayed
- Trigger events
- Enabling push notifications
- Disabling push notifications
- Sharing devices
- Multiples devices

### Purpose

Push Notifications allow drivers to be automatically and immediately informed of changes affecting their jobs, which have been made in the CartonCloud web app (e.g. by an Admin adding a new job onto a driver).

### How push notifications are displayed

The way push notifications are displayed on the mobile devices depends on the state of the CartonCloud app at the time of receiving the notification.

The app is**open, showing the job** list
A drop-down info bar will be displayed upon receiving a notification and the job list will be refreshed automatically.

If you are using an Android device and do not see a drop-down info bar upon receiving a push notification (only a CartonCloud icon in the notification bar), you may have to enable "Heads up" notifications in your device's Notification settings (see *"Enabling Push Notifications"* below for more information).

- The app is open, not showing the job list (e.g. recording a POD) On iOS devices, a drop-down info bar will be displayed upon receiving a notification (whereas on Android devices the CartonCloud logo will appear in the notification bar) but the job list will NOT be refreshed automatically. This is to prevent interferences while using the app to record PODs, lodge errors etc. Upon returning to the job list, it will automatically be refreshed.
- The app is running in the background The device will show a standard notification informing the user that the CartonCloud app has received a notification. Upon tapping on the notification (or swiping if in lock screen on iOS), the CartonCloud app will be opened and the job list automatically refreshed.
- The app is closed The device will show a standard notification informing the user that the CartonCloud app has received a notification. Upon tapping on the notification (or swiping if in the lock screen), the CartonCloud app will be opened and the job list automatically refreshed.

Once notifications have been received, they can be viewed in the **Notifications View**, which can be accessed via slide-in menu within the app (button in the top left corner). This view gives the driver some more information about affected jobs and allows direct access to the Consignment Detail View if the job is available in the job list.

![push-notifications-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/push-notifications-0.png?width=250&height=445&name=push-notifications-0.png)
![push-notifications-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/push-notifications-1.png?width=255&height=454&name=push-notifications-1.png)

### Trigger events

Not all actions performed in the web app trigger notifications to avoid spamming the drivers and to keep pushed information relevant.

Changes that trigger a notification being sent to a driver include:

- Adding a job : The driver the job has been allocated to will be notified. If the job was previously assigned to a different driver, this driver will also be notified about the job having been removed from their list.
- Removing a job: The driver the job has been removed from will be notified. If the job has been re-assigned to a different driver in the process, this driver will also be notified about the job having been added to their list.
- Modifying a job: A notification will be sent to the assigned driver.
  - Addresses: If an address that affects a job does get modified in the system. For example, the delivery address of a delivery job, the pickup address of a pickup job or either the pickup or delivery address of a point-to-point job.
  - Quantities: A change in quantities, e.g. the number of pallets or cartons in a Consignment.
  - COD Value: Modified Cash On Delivery value.
  - Special instructions: Modified special instructions text.

Only changes on jobs that are in the driver's current job list (or will be part of it once added) trigger notifications. This means for example that drivers will not be informed about changes to completed deliveries.

Moreover, if a change affects multiple Consignments (e.g. when changing the driver assigned to a run sheet), notifications will be bundled as to not overwhelm the driver with notifications. All details of bundled notifications can still be viewed in the Notifications view within the app.

### Enabling push notifications

A driver can choose to receive notifications or (temporarily) disable them. This prevents drivers from receiving notifications while not working, for example as adjustments get made overnight.

3 conditions need to be fulfilled for a driver to receive notifications:

1. **Notifications enabled on the mobile device**
**iOS:**On first starting up the app after installing it or after an update from a version prior to 1.2.7, the app will automatically request access to Notifications on the device. This setting can be modified at any stage, either by seeking out the CartonCloud app settings in the device settings (*Settings > Notifications > CartonCloud*) or by using the shortcut button provided in the Settings view within the CartonCloud app.
If notifications are disabled for the CartonCloud app no notifications will be received, irrespective of whether the user is logged in and clocked in.
**Android:**On first starting up the app after installing it or after an update from a version prior to 4.1.3, the app will automatically be given access to Notifications. This setting can be modified at any stage, either by seeking out the CartonCloud app settings in the device settings (*Settings > Apps  > CartonCloud*) or by using the shortcut button provided in the Settings view within the CartonCloud app. For more fine-grain adjustments to Notifications, go to *Settings > Notifications > CartonCloud*on your device. Additionally, if you are not seeing drop-down info bars (*"Heads Up"* Notifications) when receiving a Push Notification while using the app, ensure that this setting is enabled in *Settings > Notifications.*
If notifications are disabled for the CartonCloud app no notifications will be received, irrespective of whether the user is logged in and clocked in.
![push-notifications-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/push-notifications-2.png?width=293&height=521&name=push-notifications-2.png)

![push-notifications-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/push-notifications-3.png?width=283&height=503&name=push-notifications-3.png)

![push-notifications-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/push-notifications-4.png?width=324&height=577&name=push-notifications-4.png)

2. **Logged in**
The driver needs to be logged in to receive notifications. On logout, no more notifications will be sent to the device until all conditions are fulfilled again.

3. **"Clocked on"**- going online / offline **IMPORTANT: MAKE SURE YOU ARE CLOCKED ON TO RECEIVE NOTIFICATIONS - THEY DON'T WORK IF YOU'RE CLOCKED OFF.**
A driver may choose to log into the app to check their job list but decide not to receive notifications at this particular time. To allow for this case, a driver needs to specifically choose to receive notifications while using the app by activating the "Clock On" button in the top right corner of the main view. On clocking off, no more notifications will be sent to the device until all the conditions are fulfilled again.
![push-notifications-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/push-notifications-5.png?width=305&height=543&name=push-notifications-5.png)

![push-notifications-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/push-notifications-6.png?width=344&height=612&name=push-notifications-6.png)

### Disabling Push Notifications

If any of the conditions described under *Enabling Push Notifications* are not fulfilled, no notifications will be sent out to the driver's device. This means Push Notification can simply be disabled by selecting the "Clock Off" button in the top right corner, as described above.

### Sharing Devices

If a mobile device is shared between several drivers, only notifications that affect the driver that is currently logged into the CartonCloud app (and fulfils all the conditions under *Enabling Push Notifications*) will be sent to the device.

### Multiple Devices

If a driver is logged into multiple devices at the same time, notifications will be sent to all their devices if the conditions under *Enabling Push Notifications* are fulfilled.

---

<a id="sending-diagnostics"></a>
## Sending Diagnostics

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/sending-diagnostics_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to send diagnostics

### Purpose

The CartonCloud Mobile Apps offer users the option to upload diagnostics data. This is to allow the CartonCloud team to identify any issues that a mobile app user might experience while using the app.

### How to send diagnostics

- Tap the menu selection and click Settings .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Dec-11-2025-08-06-31-9723-AM.jpeg?width=244&height=492&name=image-png-Dec-11-2025-08-06-31-9723-AM.jpeg)

- Tap SEND DIAGNOSTICS .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Dec-11-2025-08-08-10-3500-AM.jpeg?width=262&height=530&name=image-png-Dec-11-2025-08-08-10-3500-AM.jpeg)

Selecting this option uploads the app's diagnostics data to the CartonCloud web app where it can be accessed by the CartonCloud team for issue investigation purposes.

---

<a id="settings-view"></a>
## Settings view

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/settings-view_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Where to find the settings view
- App version
- Organisation
- Warehouse
- Push notifications
- Additional address information
- Rapid sale order packing
- Enable hardware keyboard (android only)
- Default map (iOS only)
- Support - send diagnostics

### Purpose

Within the CartonCloud mobile apps, the Settings View allows the user to customise the default settings to better suit their preferences on what to display and how to display it in the app. Also, for drivers allocated to several organisations, the Settings View allows them to switch organisation to be shown the corresponding consignment list.

### Where to find the settings view

**Please ensure you have selected the correct mode for the settings you wish to access before clicking Settings.**

The Settings View can be accessed by first selecting the menu button in the top left corner of the navigation bar and then choosing the "*Settings*" option in the slide-in menu.

![settings-view-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/settings-view-0.png?width=294&height=523&name=settings-view-0.png)

A number of adjustments can be made within the Settings View. Further information on each setting is provided below.

![settings-view-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/settings-view-1.png?width=315&height=560&name=settings-view-1.png)

### App version

This shows the user the version of the CartonCloud app they are currently using.

### Organisation

A user can be associated with multiple organisations and on tapping the currently selected organisation's name will be presented with a window showing all organisation options. If a user is only associated with one organisation, tapping the organisation name will not bring up any options.
On choosing a new organisation and saving the settings via the tick button in the top right corner of the view, other pages such as the consignment list will be switched over to the jobs corresponding to the newly selected organisation.

![settings-view-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/settings-view-2.png?width=285&height=617&name=settings-view-2.png)

### Warehouse

As part of the "Picking" user mode available to users with a Packer role, users are able to select which warehouse they want to see Sale Orders for. For this option to be displayed, the user is required to have a Packer role for the currently selected Organisation and be in "Picking" user mode. If you are in "Driving" mode but choose to change your Organisation to one where you only have a Packer role, the warehouse details will be displayed and upon saving the adjusted settings the user mode will be automatically changed to "Picking".

![settings-view-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/settings-view-3.png?width=250&height=434&name=settings-view-3.png)

### Push notifications

You may not want to receive Push Notifications from the CartonCloud app, so the Settings View allows you to enable or disable this feature. On selecting this setting, you will be taken to the App Settings on your device where you may adjust your preferences.

For more information on Push Notifications please check out this article: [Push Notifications](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Push+Notifications)

### Additional address information

A driver can choose to be shown additional address information or decide to hide it. The default is to hide additional address information as it may not be relevant to all users. On changing this preference, remember to save your settings by selecting the tick button in the top right corner of the Settings View.

For more information on Additional Address Info please check out this article: [Showing Additional Pickup And Delivery Info](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Showing+Additional+Pickup+and+Delivery+Info)

### Rapid sale order packing

For more information on Rapid Sale Order Packing please check out this article: [Rapid Sale Order Packing](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Rapid+Sale+Order+Packing)

### Enable hardware keyboard (android only)

If you are using an Android device with a physical keyboard this option allows you to toggle between input types (physical or touch keyboard) on the quantity confirmation and verification view during the [scan picking process.](https://help.cartoncloud.com/kb2/mobile-app-support/warehouse-pickers-packers-incoming/scan-picking/scan-picking-process#ScanPickingProcess-PackConfirmationView)

### Default map (iOS only)

As you can navigate to an address from within the CartonCloud apps, you may want to select which map app should be opened to continue your navigation - Apple Maps or Google Maps (if installed on the device). For Android devices, this options is not available. Simply highlight your chosen map option by tapping on it and save your settings by selecting the tick button in the top right corner of the Settings View.

Warehouse (iOS only)

As part of the "Picking" user mode available to users with a Packer role, users are able to select which warehouse they want to see Sale Orders for. For this option to be displayed, the user is required to have a Packer role for the currently selected Organisation and be in "Picking" user mode. If you are in "Driving" mode but choose to change your Organisation to one where you only have a Packer role, the warehouse details will be displayed and upon saving the adjusted settings the user mode will be automatically changed to "Picking".

For more information on Scan Picking please check out this article: [Scan Picking](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Scan+Picking)

For more information on User Modes please check out this article: [User Modes](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/User+Modes)

### Support - send diagnostics

Selecting this button will upload diagnostic data to the server. This is to allow the CartonCloud team to identify any issues that a mobile app user might experience while using the app.

If you are experiencing problems with the mobile app. [Please contact our support team for assistance](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Contact+Support).

**Settings not appearing?**

Some settings will only appear based on the [User Mode](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/User+Modes) that is currently selected. For example when in delivery mode, settings related to Sale Orders will not appear (like Rapid Sale Order packing).

To make the setting appear so it can be changed, first change the user mode to the most appropriate option related to that setting (e.g. packing for a Sale Order setting).

---

<a id="troubleshooting-barcode-scanning-mobile-app"></a>
## Troubleshooting Barcode Scanning (Mobile App)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/troubleshooting-barcode-scanning-mobile-app_

### Table of Contents

- iOS
- Android
- Troubleshooting with the Barcode Scan Tester

iOS

- No keyboard displaying when trying to manual enter data: [Keyboard Not Showing (iOS)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=530057541)
- A short delay of 0.1 sec is enabled between barcode reads when using a barcode scanner in the CartonCloud app. Please be aware that scanning barcodes any faster than this may cause barcodes to be read incorrectly.

### Android

- No keyboard displaying when in manual entry alert: This normally happens when your device favours a hardware input method, e.g. barcode scanner, over soft keyboard.
  - Normally a click on the input field will bring up the keyboard. If not, try to double click or long click on the input field. The keyboard may come up or a notification will pop up on the top of the screen, prompting you to “Select keyboard input” or “Choose input method”. Click on that: disable the hardware input method or choose the soft keyboard one.
  - After clicking on the input field, check whether a keyboard symbol shows up in the bottom menu area. Click on that and choose the right keyboard.
  - An alternative is to temporarily disable the barcode scanner and then click on the input field. Some barcode scanners allow to be temporarily disabled with simple steps.
  - If nothing works, unpair the scanner to remove this hardware input method.
- Scanned barcode does not appear: Ensure your Bluetooth barcode scanner is connected to your mobile device. Alternatively, try manual entry.

### Troubleshooting with the Barcode Scan Tester

The **Barcode Scan Tester** is a diagnostic tool in the CartonCloud mobile app that allows users to view exactly what characters are received when scanning barcodes. It’s designed to simplify barcode troubleshooting, particularly where device-specific scan profiles might affect the data received.

- When scanning a barcode, all characters received are displayed in the ASCII section in their Hex codes, including any non-printable characters.
- ASCII (American Standard Code for Information Interchange) code is the numerical representation of characters such as 'a', as computers can only understand numbers.
- Any characters that are highlighted red are non-printable.
- The transfer speed is tested and the result posted under 'Data Transfer Rate'.
  - If the transfer speed is fast it will show as OK.
  - If the transfer speed is slow, it will show as Too Slow. The threshold for “Too Slow” is 0.08 sec/character

**To use the Barcode Scan Tester:**

- On the CartonCloud mobile app select the menu navigation bar and then Settings.
- Scroll down to Support and select BARCODE SCAN TESTER.
- From here, you can test the barcode by scanning it.

![troubleshooting-barcode-scanning-mobile-app-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/troubleshooting-barcode-scanning-mobile-app-0.png?width=238&height=469&name=troubleshooting-barcode-scanning-mobile-app-0.png)

---

# Mobile App > Mobile App Transport

<a id="add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app"></a>
## Add/edit Consignment Items for Drivers on the Mobile App

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Benefits
- Use cases
- Workflow
- Prerequisites
- User / driver settings
- Customer settings
- How to steps

### Purpose

Please note that if your CartonCloud account was created prior to 1 July 2019 the below functionality may not be available at this stage. If you would like to discuss migrating to the newer version of the CartonCloud TMS, please use [CartonCloud Help](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/new-support-ticket) and lodge a case with our Service Desk.

Drivers can edit or add Consignment Items on a Consignment via the mobile app.

In addition, you can choose to trigger a Consignment Error on the web app when the Driver makes a change to the Consignment. This way, you are aware of the change, which allows you to proactively manage communications with the customer and action any relevant admin activities related to the change.

### Benefits

- Flexibility to adjust the Consignment Item upon collection and before delivery to ensure the quantities delivered matches the record in CartonCloud.
- Greater data accuracy in real-time.
- Improved flexibility and autonomy for the Driver delivering the freight.
- Reduced manual admin, as changes can be made at the time rather than backtracking later and making the adjustments manually.
- With Consignment Errors enabled, you will be aware of any discrepancies and changes to deliveries, allowing you to stay on top of customer communications and related admin activities.

### Use Cases

- If a driver collects freight and the quantities vary from what was booked, they can adjust the quantities on the mobile app to match when they deliver the goods.
- For a Pick Up Consignment - the Consignment Items listed on the Consignment doesn't match what the Driver is collecting, so they update the quantities on the mobile app at the point of pickup.
- For a Delivery Consignment - the items packaged for delivery differ when the Driver is loading the vehicle to what the Consignment lists. For example, 2 x 6 bottle cases of wine are taped together, so instead of being two cartons, it is now one carton being delivered.
- The Driver is delivering bulk freight and needs to add the weight of the load after it is loaded on as they go over the weighbridge before delivering the load.

### Workflow

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-0.png?width=670&height=135&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-0.png)

### Prerequisites

To enable your Driver to edit and add Consignment Items on a Consignment, you need to configure the below settings:

Note, you can complete all configurations at the Organisation Settings; however, if you wish to apply different settings for a particular Driver or Customer, you need to do so at the User Settings and Customer Settings

| **Settings** | **Configuration for add/edit Consignments** |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | **Allow drivers to add/edit Consignments** | **Create Consignment Errors for Driver Edits to Consignment Items** |
| Organisation Settings | Yes - it will apply to all Drivers | Yes - it will apply to all Customers & Drivers |
| Customer Settings | N/A - not controlled at the Customer Settings | Yes - it will only apply to that particular Customer |
| User Settings | Yes - it will only apply to that specific Driver | N/A - not controlled at the User Settings |

### Organisation Settings

Note, you can control all drivers' settings from the Organisation Settings; however, if you need to override the setting for a particular driver, you can do so from the User settings.

- Navigate to the Organisation Settings, **More**>**Organisation Settings**.
- Select the **Mobile App** tab.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-1.png?width=670&height=276&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-1.png)

- Scroll down to the Consignment Items box.
- Tick **Allow drivers to add/edit consignment items** (you can override this setting for particular drivers from the User Settings).

To ensure you are on top of any changes made, you can turn on Consignment Error notifications when the Driver edits the Consignment Items.

- Tick**Create Consignment Errors for Driver Edits to Consignment Items** (you can override this setting for particular Customers from the Customer Settings. You can NOT override this setting for specific drivers.).
- Select **Save**.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-2.png?width=503&height=138&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-2.png)

### User/Driver Settings

You can edit the driver settings when you wish to configure settings differently for a particular driver. For example, if you have temporary drivers, you may not want them to have the ability to edit and add Consignment Items.

- Navigate to the Users page, **Contacts**>**Users**.
- Select the Driver you wish to change the settings for.
- Select the blue **Edit** button.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-2.png?width=532&height=146&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-2.png)

- Scroll down to the Roles section, and against the Driver role, select the setting you wish to apply from the **Enable this Driver to Add or Edit Consignments Items via the Mobile Device** drop-down menu.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-3.png?width=569&height=299&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-3.png)

- Select **Save**.

| **Setting** | **Explanation** |
| --- | --- |
| Use Organisation Settings (default settings) | The settings configured at the Organisation Settings will be applied to the individual user. |
| Yes | Regardless of the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level, the Driver will be able to add or edit Consignment Items on their mobile device for consignments allocated to them. |
| No | Regardless of the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level, the Driver will NOT be able to add to edit Consignment Items on their mobile device for consignments allocated to them. |

### Customer Settings

You can edit the Customer Settings when you wish to override the Consignment Error notification settings configured at the Organisation Settings level for a particular Customer.

- Navigate to the Customers page, **Contacts**>**Customers**.
- Select the Customer you wish to change the settings for and click **Edit**.
- Select the **Transport Management**tab and then the **Consignment Errors** tab.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-4.png?width=670&height=338&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-4.png)

- Select the setting you wish to apply from the **Create Consignment Errors for Driver Edits to Consignment Items** drop-down menu.

| **Setting** | **Explanation** |
| --- | --- |
| Yes | Regardless of the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level, a Consignment Error will be triggered for Driver Edits to Consignment Items for the Customer. |
| No | Regardless of the settings configured at the Organisation Settings level, NO Consignment Error will be triggered for Driver Edits to Consignment Items for the Customer. |
| Use Organisation Settings | The settings configured at the Organisation Settings will be applied to the Customer. |

### How to Steps

Making changes on the mobile application (for the Driver)

- Navigate to the relevant Consignment on the mobile application.
- On the **Details** tab and under the **Delivery Details,**select **EDIT ITEMS**.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-5.png?width=670&height=164&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-5.png)

- Select the plus icon in the top right to **add a Consignment Item**.
- Select the Service Type drop-down menu **to change the Service Type**.
- Select the pencil icon to **edit the Consignment Item.**

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-6.png?width=275&height=595&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-6.png)

**Add a Consignment Item**

- Select the plus icon in the top right corner.
- Select the Transport Product, description and quantities.
- Click **Add**.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-7.png?width=295&height=613&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-7.png)

**Change the Service Type**

- Select the Service Type drop-down menu.
- Select the **Service Type** you wish to apply.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-8.png?width=306&height=680&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-8.png)

**Edit the Consignment Item**

- Select the pencil icon.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-9.png?width=284&height=631&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-9.png)

- Enter the new quantity.
- Select **Save**.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-11.png?width=271&height=553&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-11.png)

- Select **Submit**.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-12.png?width=271&height=558&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-12.png)

#### Actioning/resolving the Consignment Errors on the web application

You will only receive Consignment Errors when drivers edit the Consignment Item if you have the option enabled at the [Organisation](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=577767258#Add/editConsignmentItemsforDriversontheMobileApp-organisation) or [Customer Settings](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=577767258#Add/editConsignmentItemsforDriversontheMobileApp-customer).

The Consignment Error will appear in the Consignment Error tab on the Consignment. You can action the Consignment Error from the **Consignment itself** or the **Consignment Errors page**.

To update your Customer on the change to the Consignment Items (Consignment Error), you can email them the change; however, this can only be actioned from the [Consignment Errors page](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=577767258#Add/editConsignmentItemsforDriversontheMobileApp-page).

**From the individual Consignment**

- Navigate to the Consignments page, **Transport**>**Consignments**.
- Select the relevant Consignment or search for the reference.
- Select the **Errors** tab.
- You will see the changes made to the Consignment Items in the **Comments** section.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-13](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-13.png?width=670&height=201&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-13.png)

- Select the tick box against the Consignment Errors you wish to resolve.
- Select **Mark Resolved**.
- Enter the reason why the error is being resolved and click **Resolve**.

**From the Consignment Errors page**

- Navigate to the Consignment Errors page by typing in **Consignment Errors** to the Search for anything bar, or from the Dashboard, click **Unresolved Consignment Errors**.
- Click on the individual Consignment Error to view each error and see what changes were made to the Consignment Items.
- To view the error, see the **Comments** section.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-14](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-14.png?width=670&height=246&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-14.png)

- Select the blue Email button.
- Enter the **To email** and click **Send**.
- To resolve the issue, click **Mark Resolved**.

![add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-15](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-15.png?width=670&height=246&name=add-edit-consignment-items-for-drivers-on-the-mobile-app-15.png)

- To mark **Consignment Errors as resolved in bulk**, navigate back to the **Consignment Errors page**.
- Use the tick box to select the Consignment Errors you wish to resolve.
- Select **Mark Resolved**.
- Enter comments for why the error is being resolved.
- Click **Resolve**.

---

<a id="cash-on-delivery"></a>
## Cash On Delivery (Mobile)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/cash-on-delivery_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Identifying COD Consignments
- Collecting COD

### Purpose

The CartonCloud mobile app allows Drivers to collect Cash On Delivery (COD) as part of the POD process.

### Identifying COD Consignments

COD consignments are marked in the consignment list with a "COD" label just above the consignment type label.

![cash-on-delivery-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/cash-on-delivery-0.jpeg?width=271&height=540&name=cash-on-delivery-0.jpeg)

In addition, on selecting the COD consignment from the list more COD information is available in the consignment Details tab.

![cash-on-delivery-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/cash-on-delivery-1.jpeg?width=283&height=574&name=cash-on-delivery-1.jpeg)

### Collecting COD

CODs are collected as part of the POD process. As for a standard consignment simply select the **"Record POD"** tab in the Consignment Detail view.

![cash-on-delivery-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/cash-on-delivery-2.jpeg?width=277&height=561&name=cash-on-delivery-2.jpeg)
If COD is required,**Record Cash On Delivery**will be automatically ticked and more input fields related to the COD collection displayed. Edit these fields as needed and follow the instructions provided, e.g. "Only leave goods if customer pays COD".

If the COD has been marked as "required" in the CartonCloud system, not ticking the **Record Cash On Delivery** box and selecting **Save POD**will bring up an alert asking for the reason of not collecting COD before allowing you to proceed.

---

<a id="end-of-day-checks"></a>
## End of Day Checks

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/end-of-day-checks_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Check outbox for failed uploads
- Check for Remaining Jobs
- Upload any invoices not part of a consignment

### Purpose

At the end of a driver's working day, it is best to perform a number of checks to ensure all necessary data for the day's jobs have been submitted.

- Empty Outbox
- Empty Consignment List (or no consignments left for delivery/pickup today)
- Invoice Photos Uploaded

### Check outbox for failed uploads

At the end of your working day, ensure that no uploads are remaining in your outbox. Should you find that there are failed uploads available, then check your devices internet connection as this will be the cause of the issue. You might have collected PODs throughout the day while out and about in areas that did not provide Internet access, so it is important to check that your Outbox is clear of any failed uploads at the end of the day. These uploads may include not only PODs but also other important items like lodged errors or invoice photos.

If there are still items in the outbox after the driver has confirmed the devices internet connection is working, then the driver should contact the depot, who can then [contact CartonCloud for further support](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/new-support-ticket), as this should not occur.

![end-of-day-checks-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/end-of-day-checks-0.png?width=296&height=525&name=end-of-day-checks-0.png)

### Check for Remaining Jobs

Ensure no jobs due for delivery/pickup today have been left in the job list. The only jobs still appearing in the consignment list might be those that have a pending POD upload in the outbox and are highlighted in yellow, or those that have had an error lodged against them and are highlighted in purple.

- In the case of consignments outstanding, ensure they have been signed off correctly by the receiver
- In the case of error consignments, check whether you have any stock left to return to the warehouse.

No Consignments outstanding

![end-of-day-checks-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/end-of-day-checks-1.png?width=249&height=443&name=end-of-day-checks-1.png)

![end-of-day-checks-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/end-of-day-checks-2.png?width=245&height=436&name=end-of-day-checks-2.png)

### Upload Any Invoices Not Part of a Consignment

Should you be left with any Invoices / PODs that are not associated to any consignment in your job list, ensure that these have been uploaded as an "*Invoice Photo*" from within the CartonCloud app to allow office staff to assign them to the corresponding consignment using the CartonCloud web app.

- To upload an invoice photo, select the Camera icon at the top of the screen.

![end-of-day-checks-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/end-of-day-checks-3.png?width=257&height=456&name=end-of-day-checks-3.png)

- Then choose to either take the Photo now or upload a previously taken photo from the mobile's library

![end-of-day-checks-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/end-of-day-checks-4.png?width=277&height=492&name=end-of-day-checks-4.png)

- Once the photo has been uploaded it will appear to office staff on the CartonCloud Dashboard as an "Unassigned Consignment Invoice / POD".
- The office staff will also be able to view which User has uploaded the photo and when. This is to assist in matching it up to the correct consignment record.

---

<a id="eta-text-messages"></a>
## ETA Text Messages

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/eta-text-messages_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Sending an ETA text message
- When ETA text messages are not available
- How the ETA is calculate

### Purpose

The CartonCloud mobile apps allow drivers to contact customers by call or text message (assuming a telephone number has been provided for the customer's address via the web app). One of the contact options is an ETA text message. While the standard text message option will have an empty body, an ETA text message is pre-filled with various information about the consignment as well as an estimated arrival time.

### Sending an ETA text message

To send a text message containing consignment information and an estimated time of arrival, proceed to the relevant consignment's detail view within the app. There, given a telephone number has been provided for the address and the mobile device allows phone calls or text messages, you will find a contact button next to the address header. On selecting this button, various options are presented: call, text, text ETA. Choose the ETA option, wait while the estimated time of arrival is being calculated and then find yourself in the device's message sending app. There you will notice that both the telephone number and the text message body have been pre-filled for you.

### When ETA text messages are not available

Under certain circumstances, the ETA text message button may be disabled. This is the case if any of the following situations apply:

- No Internet: an Internet connection is required for the calculation of an estimated time of arrival.
- No Geo Location: if the customer's address does not have a valid geo location associated with it, an estimated time of arrival cannot be calculated.
- No Current Location: if access to the mobile device's location has been previously denied or not yet granted, an estimated time of arrival cannot be calculated.

Within the mobile apps, the appropriate explanation for the disabling of the button will be given when attempting to contact a customer.

### How the ETA is calculated

The estimated time of arrival is calculated based on the coordinates of two locations:

- Start location: Current location of device
- End location: Geo location associated to the selected address

CartonCloud sends both of these addresses (as coordinates) in a request to the Google Distance Matrix API, which will then return a response containing the estimated travel time. Google calculates the travel time using a "*Best Guess*", based on both historical traffic conditions and live traffic. The travel mode is always presumed to be "*driving*" with the departure time set to the current time.

---
