<a id="grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod"></a>
## Grouping Consignments (Mobile) to record a single POD

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to group Consignments
  - Grouping mode
  - Selecting an address group
  - Grouping consignments
  - Cancelling a grouping
  - Allowed consignment groupings
  - Collecting a POD
  - Recording adhoc charges
  - Failed POD upload

### Purpose

Consignment Grouping on the mobile app allows a driver to collect one Proof Of Delivery for several consignments at once.

### How to group Consignments

Consignments can be easily grouped by selecting the "Grouping" menu button in the navigation bar.

![grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-0.png?width=206&height=414&name=grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-0.png)

**Grouping Mode**

While in the "*Grouping Mode*", all jobs in the consignments list will be displayed in the same way as in the standard list view. On selecting consignments to be added to the group, these consignments will be highlighted in blue.

In the example below, 3 consignments have been selected for grouping.

![grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-1.png?width=228&height=458&name=grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-1.png)
Two buttons are shown at the bottom of the view:

- **Group**: Select this to group the selected consignments.
- **Cancel**: Select this to cancel the grouping.

**Selecting An Address Group**

If you select an address group as part of your consignment grouping, all the consignments it contains will automatically added to the grouping as well.

In the example below, an individual consignment and an address group have been selected. Therefore, on selecting the "*Group*" button, a total of 4 consignments will be grouped together - the 1 individual consignment plus the 3 consignments contained within the address group.

![grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-2.png?width=261&height=525&name=grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-2.png)

### Grouping Consignments

Upon selecting the "*Group*" button, you will be forwarded straight to the consignment detail view, which now contains all selected consignments, one after another. Simply scroll down to see all the consignments in the group.

![grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-3.png?width=415&height=402&name=grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-3.png)

If you select "Group" without having selected at least two consignments, a warning will be displayed and you won't be forwarded to the consignment detail view.

![grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-4.png?width=208&height=418&name=grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-4.png)

**Cancelling a Grouping**

A consignment grouping can be cancelled by either selecting the "*Cancel*" button while in the "*Grouping Mode*", or by simply selecting the back button (arrow on the top left of the view) once you are in the grouped consignment detail view.

Once cancelled, the grouping will be dissolved.

**Allowed Consignment Groupings**

As you select consignments and then attempt to group them, a warning may appear informing you that your selection is not valid. This can happen in several cases:

- Trying to group a Point To Point consignment: it is not possible to group a Point To Point consignment
- Trying to group consignments of various different types: it is not possible to for example group a Delivery consignment with a Pickup consignment
- Trying to group consignments requiring Cash On Delivery: it is not possible to group COD consignment

**Collecting a POD**

Once you have grouped consignments, collecting a joint POD for all of them is straightforward. Simply select the "*Record POD*" tab and continue as normal - e.g. collecting number of cartons, signature and recipient details as required. Then select "*Save POD*" and the POD will be submitted to CartonCloud, consequently marking all consignments of the group as delivered / picked up.

![grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-5.png?width=226&height=454&name=grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-5.png)
You will notice that the number of for example cartons displayed at the top of the "*Record POD*" view is the combined value of all grouped consignments. In the example above, the POD was collected for 2 grouped consignments, one with 2 cartons to deliver and the second one with 10 cartons to deliver, therefore displaying 12 cartons as the total number for the POD.

**Recipient Signature and / or Invoice Photos**

For a standard delivery Consignments (i.e. not *Authority To Leave* Consignments), either a signature or an invoice photo needs to be collected from the recipient. One or both of these actions need to be completed to be able to submit the POD.

**Signature**

A signature collected for a group of consignments is automatically recorded as part of the proof of delivery against **all** consignments in the group.

**Invoice Photos**

In addition to a signature (or as an alternative), invoice photos can be collected as part of the group POD. First, tap on any of the photo buttons ( **+** ), then choose either a single consignment from the group to attach the invoice to or **Select All**to attach the same invoice photo to all consignments in the group. If a single consignment has been chosen, the selection needs to be confirmed. Then, a photo can be taken or chosen from the device library.

![grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-6.png?width=602&height=408&name=grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-6.png)
On saving a POD for a group of consignment **WITHOUT** a signature but **WITH**invoice photos, the app validates that at least one invoice photo has been recorded for each consignment. If an attempt is made to save a POD without an invoice photo against all consignments (or a signature), an error message will be shown, advising about next steps.

![grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-7.png?width=488&height=492&name=grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-7.png)

**Recording Adhoc Charges**

When recording Adhoc Charges for a grouped consignment an alert will be displayed to notify you that the adhoc charges will be applied to all consignments in the group. To submit adhoc charges for individual consignments in the grouping you will need to select the applicable consignment from the consignment list and add the adhoc charges from there.

![grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-8.png?width=270&height=481&name=grouping-consignments-mobile-to-record-a-single-pod-8.png)

**Failed POD Upload**

Should the upload of the POD fail, you will notice only one upload item for the whole group in the outbox - the combined POD. The failed consignments though are highlighted as failed individuals in the consignment list. On successful retry all consignments that were part of the group will be removed from the consignment list.

**Lodging an Error**

No error can be lodged against grouped consignments.

---

<a id="list-view-search"></a>
## List View Search

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/list-view-search_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Search criteria
- Grouped consignments
- Available functionality
  - Unavailable
  - Available

### Purpose

Within the CartonCloud mobile app, the search integrated into the consignment list view enables user to quickly locate consignments based on certain search criteria.

The search bar is located above the consignment list and can be activated by tapping on any part of the white text field.

![list-view-search-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/list-view-search-0.jpeg?width=262&height=530&name=list-view-search-0.jpeg)

As you start typing your search term into the now active text field, the consignment list will automatically be filtered to only show those consignments that fulfil your search criteria. Additionally, the matching text will be highlighted for each consignment in the list view if applicable. Please note that the search is case-insensitive.

- To cancel a search, simply press the “Cancel” button to the right of the search text field
- To clear a search term, select the “X” within the text field, on the right-hand side. This “X” will only appear once you have started entering a text into the search field.

### Search Criteria

Currently, the list search allows you to search for matching text in:

- Consignment Customer Name
- Consignment Address / Suburb
- Consignment Id
- Consignment Reference

### Grouped Consignments

Consignments that are normally automatically grouped by address group will be displayed as individual search results while performing a search.

### Available Functionality

While in search mode, some list view actions will not be available, while others operate as normal.

Please see [Consignment List View](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+List+View) for more information on all available list actions.

Unavailable

- **Drag and Drop**: Consignments cannot be dragged & dropped while in search mode
- **Lock / Unlock**: Consignments cannot be locked into their position / unlocked form their position while in search mode.

Available

- **Select Consignment:**On selecting a consignment, the corresponding consignment detail view will be shown, as per normal list view behaviour.
- **Record POD**: Available on swiping on a consignment list row as normal.
- **Grouping:**Consignments can be grouped as normal, by selecting the grouping button from the top bar.

---

<a id="lodging-an-error"></a>
## Lodging an Error

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/lodging-an-error_

### Table of Contents

- Lodging a consignment error when the consignment is undeliverable
- When the consignment is partially deliverable
- When the consignment is deliverable
- Resolving lodged errors

### Lodging a consignment error when the consignment is undeliverable

- On the CartonCloud mobile app, Delivering mode, select the relevant Consignment.

Note, errors cannot be lodged for grouped Consignments, only single Consignments.

- If the Consignment is completely undeliverable:
  - Select the Lodges Error tab.
  - Select No when prompted if you are delivering anyting from the Consignment.

![lodging-an-error-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/lodging-an-error-0.jpeg?width=255&height=515&name=lodging-an-error-0.jpeg)

-
  - Select the relevant error reason.

![lodging-an-error-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/lodging-an-error-1.jpeg?width=264&height=527&name=lodging-an-error-1.jpeg)

-
  - Fill out the relevant information relating to the error and select Save Error.
-

The error will then be visible to admin users within your CartonCloud account.

### When the consignment is partially deliverable

- If the Consignment is partially deliverable, select the Record POD tab.
- Update the quantity delivered.

![lodging-an-error-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/lodging-an-error-4.jpeg?width=268&height=536&name=lodging-an-error-4.jpeg)

- Select the Save POD button to submit your POD.
- You will be prompted to record an error for the short delivery.

![lodging-an-error-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/lodging-an-error-5.jpeg?width=265&height=529&name=lodging-an-error-5.jpeg)

### When the Consignment is Deliverable

- Select the Record POD tab
- Update the delivery details.
- Tick the I need to record an error box at the bottom of the screen.

![lodging-an-error-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/lodging-an-error-6.jpeg?width=271&height=553&name=lodging-an-error-6.jpeg)

### Resolving lodged errors

- To resolve Consignment Errors lodged via the driver app, navigate to the Consignment in the CartonCloud web app and select the Errors tab.

![lodging-an-error-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/lodging-an-error-7.png?width=670&height=289&name=lodging-an-error-7.png)

- Within this tab, any lodged error linked to this Consignment will be displayed and can be marked as resolved or displayed in more detail.

**![lodging-an-error-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/lodging-an-error-8.png?width=670&height=240&name=lodging-an-error-8.png)**

---

<a id="mobile-map-view"></a>
## Mobile Map View

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/mobile-map-view_

### Table of Contents

- Scope
- Pins
- Pin locations
- Pin colours
- Pin numbers
- Consignment information & link through
- Consignments without geo location
- Current location
- Route optimisation

### Scope

The map view within the CartonCloud mobile app is a powerful tool to not only provide the location and type/status of consignments in one easy glance but also to optimize a driver's route.

### Pins

Consignments within the driver's consignment list are represented by pins on the map view.

![mobile-map-view-0-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/mobile-map-view-0-1.png?width=226&height=402&name=mobile-map-view-0-1.png)

### Pin Locations

A pin's location is determined by a consignment's geolocation (coordinates) as defined by its delivery or pickup address within the CartonCloud web app.

### Pin Colours

The colour of a pin on the map varies depending on the type of consignment and the current status of the consignment.

![mobile-map-view-1-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/mobile-map-view-1-1.png?width=287&height=511&name=mobile-map-view-1-1.png)

| **Colour** | **Status** |
| --- | --- |
| Dark Orange | Normal |
| Light Orange | Pickup Job |
| Red | Error Lodged |
| Green (with "split" split pins showing both a Pickup and a Delivery stage) | Point to Point Job |

Please check out this article with detailed information on how consignment types and statuses are represented by pin colours: [Consignment Status Colours](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Consignment-Status-Colours)[https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Consignment-Status-Colours](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Consignment-Status-Colours)

### Pin Numbers

The numbers displayed on the consignments pin represent the order of the consignments in the sorted list view, with the number 1 corresponding to the first position in the list view. On Point to Point consignments, the second number (shown in brackets) highlights which stage of the Point to Point consignment the pin represents.

### Consignment information & link through

On tapping a pin, a info "bubble" with more information about the consignment will be displayed. This bubble can be dismissed by tapping anywhere else on the screen. Should you want to continue to the consignment's Detail View, simply tap on the info bubble and you will be directed to this view.

![mobile-map-view-2-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/mobile-map-view-2-1.png?width=341&height=607&name=mobile-map-view-2-1.png)

### Consignments without geo location

If no valid geolocation has been provided for a consignment, no pin can be displayed for it on the map. To highlight the fact that one of the consignments is not represented by a pin, an icon (crossed out pin) at the bottom of the map view will be displayed, including a count of how many consignments are affected.

![mobile-map-view-3-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/mobile-map-view-3-1.png?width=349&height=621&name=mobile-map-view-3-1.png)

Should you want to focus the map on your current location, select the corresponding button at the bottom of the map view.

### Current location

Should you want to focus the map on your current location, select the corresponding button at the bottom of the map view.

![mobile-map-view-4-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/mobile-map-view-4-1.png?width=374&height=665&name=mobile-map-view-4-1.png)

### Route Optimization

Please check out this article with detailed information on how route optimization works in the CartonCloud mobile apps: [Route Optimisation](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Route-Optimisation)

---

<a id="pod-fields-customisation"></a>
## POD Fields Customisation

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/pod-fields-customisation_

### Table of Contents

- [Purpose](https://help.cartoncloud.com#purpose)
- [Prerequisites](https://help.cartoncloud.com#prerequisites)
- [Benefits](https://help.cartoncloud.com#benefits)
- [Use Cases](https://help.cartoncloud.com#use-cases)
- [How-To Steps](https://help.cartoncloud.com#how-to-steps)

### Purpose

This article explains how to customise the fields displayed in the Recipient Details section of the Record POD screen within the CartonCloud Mobile App. By default, this section only includes First Name and Last Name fields. You can modify this to capture additional details like Time In, Time Out, or Temperature, depending on your delivery requirements.

### Prerequisites

- Admin access to the CartonCloud Web App
- Driver must be using the CartonCloud Mobile App
- The driver must log out and back in for changes to apply, unless the app is updated

### Benefits

- Capture relevant delivery or recipient data for compliance, customer service, or audit purposes
- Ensure accurate record-keeping at the point of delivery
- Adapt POD fields to meet industry-specific requirements

### Use Cases

- Cold chain logistics where **temperature** must be recorded
- High-security deliveries where **recipient identification** is critical
- Time-tracking for **Time In** and **Time Out** to monitor delivery efficiency

### How-To Steps

To customise the fields shown on the **Record POD** screen in the CartonCloud Mobile App:

- Navigate to Organisation Settings by typing Organisation Settings into the **Search for Anything** field.
- In the Organisation Settings page, click the **Mobile App** tab.
- Locate the section for **Consignment Custom Fields**.

![image-png-Nov-26-2025-05-49-22-2071-AM](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-26-2025-05-49-22-2071-AM.png?width=670&height=292&name=image-png-Nov-26-2025-05-49-22-2071-AM.png)

- Select + Add Field and fill in the below information:
  - **Name** — This is the label shown in the Mobile App (e.g. *First Name*, *Temperature*).
  - **Required** — Tick this if drivers must enter a value before completing POD.
  - **Enabled** — Untick this to hide the field without deleting it.

- After making your changes, click **Save**.

Ask your drivers to **log out and back in** to the CartonCloud Mobile App so the changes take effect.

- Once applied, your customised fields will appear on the Record POD screen during delivery confirmations in the Mobile App.

---

<a id="proof-of-delivery-v2-beta"></a>
## Proof of Delivery V2 (Beta)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/proof-of-delivery-v2-beta_

### Table of Contents

- [Purpose](https://help.cartoncloud.com#purpose)
- [Prerequisites](https://help.cartoncloud.com#prerequisites)
- [Benefits](https://help.cartoncloud.com#benefits)
- [Use Cases](https://help.cartoncloud.com#use-cases)
- [The difference between Documents Essentials and Advanced](https://help.cartoncloud.com#the-difference-between-documents-essentials-and-advanced)
- [Limitations](https://help.cartoncloud.com#limitations)
- [How to access Proof of Delivery V2 Beta](https://help.cartoncloud.com#how-to-access-proof-of-delivery-v2-beta)
- [How to steps](https://help.cartoncloud.com#how-to-steps)

### Purpose

Proof of Delivery V2 (Beta) allows drivers to capture more detailed delivery evidence using CartonCloud’s Documents functionality. It provides flexibility by enabling photo and video uploads directly from the mobile app during or before delivery.

This feature is designed to give your team better tools for confirming deliveries, particularly when handling fragile or high-value goods, and ensures clear visual records are available for both internal teams and customers.

### Prerequisites

- You must be using the CartonCloud mobile app version 6.35.0 or higher.
- POD V2 must be enabled in your Organisation Settings. See [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com#how-to-access-proof-of-delivery-v2-beta) for steps.
- Documents are split into two tiers: See detailed differences between the two [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com#the-difference-between-documents-essentials-and-advanced).
  - **Essentials**: available to all accounts
  - **Advanced**: available to Premium accounts. (All accounts are able to trial Advanced Documents for free until April 2026. After this, only accounts with [WMS Premium](https://cartoncloudorg.lightning.force.com/articles/Knowledge/WMS-Premium) will maintain Advanced Documents and all other accounts will switch to Essentials Documents.)
- **Optional:** Select a default Document visibility option from: **Organisation Settings** > **Transport Documents**.

### Benefits

- Capture up to 100 full-resolution photos and videos (Advanced Documents)
- Faster image capture (Multi-photo mode, Advanced Documents)
- Option to require at least one image per POD
- Ability to submit photos and videos without submitting a POD, useful when loading vehicles
- Improved offline functionality for drivers
- Better visibility and audit trail for both customers and admins

### Use Cases

- Deliveries involving fragile or high-value items.
- Capturing damage evidence before delivery is finalised.
- Recording proof of vehicle loading for transport compliance.
- Supporting customer service with visual confirmation.

### The difference between Documents Essentials and Advanced

Advanced Documents provides additional capability for higher resolution images, significantly increases the number of attachments per document, provides additional storage capacity and adds other timesaving enhancements.

Advanced Documents is a paid add-on available with [WMS Premium](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/WMS-Premium). For more information on WMS Premium and to upgrade your account, please reach out to our Service Desk team [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport).

The detailed differences between the two are outlined below:

| **Essentials Documents** | **Advanced Documents** |
| --- | --- |
| Max 6 images per Document | Max 100 images per Document |
| Images are always compressed (POD resolution ~700 kb/image) | Default to full-resolution Compression options available via settings |
| Max attachment size 1mb | Max attachment size 500mb |
|  | Video support  Multi-photo mode |
| Deleted Documents and attachments cannot be restored | Deleted Documents and attachments can be viewed and restored by non-customer roles |
| 10gb included storage | 100gb included storage |
|  | Image Metadata - date, time and location Information (where available) |

### Limitations

Please note, this feature is currently in Beta as there are a couple of limitations. These will be addressed in future updates.

1. Photos taken using POD V2 are not attached to delivery emails, and are not included in the POD PDF. Customers can access the photos & videos through the web portal in a new tab, “Documents (new)”.
2. Delivery notifications may be triggered while photos and videos are still being uploaded.
3. When enabled, the Delivery mode of the mobile app contains 2 Outboxes. The original outbox continues to show pending POD uploads, and there is a new Outbox (POD V2) accessible from the menu which contains pending photo/video uploads. This new outbox also contains documents pending upload from Sale Orders and Purchase Orders.

### How to access Proof of Delivery V2 Beta

- Navigate to **More** > **Organisation Settings** > **Mobile App**.
- Enable **Use POD V2 (Beta)**.
- Drivers on app versions 6.35.0 and higher will then see the new POD interface.

### How to Steps

You can upload images or videos without submitting a POD, such as during vehicle loading.

#### To record Documents without the POD:

- On the mobile app, select **Delivering** mode.
- Select the relevant Consignment.
- Tap the **Record POD tab**.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png.jpeg?width=231&height=465&name=image-png.jpeg)

- Tap the plus icon under **Add Invoice Photos**.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-1.jpeg?width=210&height=425&name=image-png-1.jpeg)

- Select a **Name** for the Document. This does not change the attributes of the document and is for labelling purposes only.
- Enter a **Description**.
- Select the **Visibility** option.

-
  - **Internal only:** no customer access. Admins and packers can edit.
  - **Shared with customer**: customer can view. Admins and packers can edit.
- Upload the attachment using either:
  - **Single capture:** take one photo using the camera on the device.
  - **Multi-capture:**rapidly take multiple photos without having to close and reopen the camera.
  - **Video**: record a video to upload using the camera on the device. By default, sound is turned off when recording the video however, you can turn this on when recording the video.
  - **Library**: access attachments saved to your device to upload to the Document.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-2.jpeg?width=239&height=477&name=image-png-2.jpeg)

- If you wish to add more attachments, select the plus icon next to the attachment you just uploaded.
- Tap **Save** when finished.

You will now be able to see the Documents and the associated attachments against the Consignment on the web app under the Documents tab.

#### **To record Documents with the POD**

- On the mobile app, select **Delivering** mode.
- Select the relevant Consignment.
- Tap the **Record POD**tab.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-3.jpeg?width=262&height=527&name=image-png-3.jpeg)

- Tick the items that were delivered.
- Tap to record a signature.
- Tap the plus icon under **Add Invoice Photos**.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-4.jpeg?width=262&height=527&name=image-png-4.jpeg)

- Select a Name for the Document. This does not change the attributes of the document and is for labelling purposes only.
- Enter a **Description**.
- Select the **Visibility** option.

-
  - **Internal only:** no customer access. Admins and packers can edit.
  - **Shared with customer:** customer can view. Admins and packers can edit.
- Upload the attachment using either:
  - **Single capture:** take one photo using the camera on the device.
  - **Multi-capture:** rapidly take multiple photos without having to close and reopen the camera.
  - **Video**: record a video to upload using the camera on the device. By default, sound is turned off when recording the video however, you can turn this on when recording the video.
  - **Library**: access attachments saved to your device to upload to the Document.
- If you wish to add more attachments, select the plus icon next to the attachment you just uploaded.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-17-2025-05-22-03-6726-AM.jpeg?width=251&height=501&name=image-png-Nov-17-2025-05-22-03-6726-AM.jpeg)

- Tap **Save** once you have finished uploading the attachments.

You will now be able to see the Documents and the associated attachments against the Consignment on the web app under the Documents tab.

- Note: the POD outbox is in a different location:
  - Select the menu navigation.
  - Select **Outbox (POD V2).**

**![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-17-2025-05-23-23-4397-AM.jpeg?width=258&height=510&name=image-png-Nov-17-2025-05-23-23-4397-AM.jpeg)**

---

<a id="recording-a-pod"></a>
## Recording a POD

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/recording-a-pod_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Video overview
- Record POD shortcut
- Consignment detail view
- Record information
- Delivered / picked up quantities
- Recipient signature and/or invoice photos
- Pickup details
- Leave without signature
- Recipient details
- Adhoc charges
- Cash on delivery
- Errors
- Submit POD
- POD Photo Requirement

### Purpose

When a CartonCloud user is in the **Delivering** mode, they are able to record PODs for any jobs assigned to them in the CartonCloud system.

For more information on the various user modes in the CartonCloud mobile apps, check out this article: [User Modes](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/User+Modes)

### View Overview

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/MjoU8g2b5kk

### Record POD Shortcut

From the Consignment List, swiping on an individual Consignment or a group of Consignments shows the driver the option of recording a POD. Selecting this means the driver is redirected directly to the  **Record POD**tab in the Consignment Detail View and can start recording a POD.
![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-26-2025-05-29-57-7637-AM.jpeg?width=235&height=275&name=image-png-Nov-26-2025-05-29-57-7637-AM.jpeg)
For more information on how to use the  **Record POD** shortcut from the Consignment List, check out this article:  [Consignment List View](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+List+View)

### Consignment detail view

On selecting a row in the list view, you will be forwarded to the Consignment Detail View which presents you with more information about the Consignment. In the case of grouped Consignments, tapping on the group's main row (the one containing the counter button) will again forward you to the Consignment Detail View. This time though the details of all the consignments contained within the group will be displayed, one after another, the same way they would be presented if you grouped consignments manually through the "*Group Consignments*" menu option.

Within the Consignment Detail View, choose the **Record POD** tab at the top of the view to enter the POD view.

![recording-a-pod-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-1.png?width=307&height=547&name=recording-a-pod-1.png)

### Record information

The information that needs to be recorded as part of a POD depends on various factors, including type of Consignment (e.g. Pickup, Delivery), organisation settings etc.

For more information on recording **PODs for consignment groups**, check out this article: [Consignment Grouping Via Mobile - Collecting a POD](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Grouping+Consignments+%28Mobile%29+to+record+a+single+POD#GroupingConsignments(Mobile)torecordasinglePOD-CollectingaPOD)

### Delivered / Picked Up Quantities

Depending on the type of Consignment (whether items are to be picked up or delivered), the header on this section either says "*Delivered*" or "*Picked Up*".

The expected quantities as per the Consignment details are preloaded but can be adjusted by the driver if needed - i.e. delivering more or fewer items than expected, or different items (e.g. pallets rather than cartons). This could be required when a consignment is entered with incorrect data, or the collection point did not have all of the stock available.

![recording-a-pod-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-2.png?width=255&height=454&name=recording-a-pod-2.png)

If a consignment contained many types of Consignment items such as the example below they will be listed on separate lines within this view.

To mark a single line as delivered, select its box, for example, to mark Cartons as delivered in the example below the box surrounded in red would be selected.

![recording-a-pod-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-3.png?width=322&height=572&name=recording-a-pod-3.png)

To mark all items as delivered, select the "Delivered in full" box.

If the quantities recorded as part of the POD vary from the expected quantities, an alert will pop up when submitting the POD, confirming why the adjustment happened. For more information on this behaviour check out this article: [Lodging an Error - When the Consignment is Partially Deliverable](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Lodging+an+Error#LodginganError-WhentheConsignmentisPartiallyDeliverable)

### Recipient Signature and/or Invoice Photos

For a standard delivery Consignment (i.e. not an *Authority To Leave* Consignment), you need to either collect a signature from the recipient or take a photo of the paper invoice (with the recipient's signature on it, if required). One or both of these actions need to be completed to be able to submit the POD.

Multiple invoice photos can be submitted if desired (up to 5).

### Sale Order Product Details

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/zwSFw9vsjzE

If the consignment you are recording a POD for has been generated from a Warehouse Sale Order then it is possible to view the products as they were listed on that Sale Order. This is useful for when a customer wants to check the contents of a consignment but the actual consignment is only recorded based on the packaging (e.g. cartons).

![recording-a-pod-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-4.png?width=579&height=326&name=recording-a-pod-4.png)

### Pickup Details

For a Pickup Consignment, recipient details are optional and the POD can be submitted without collecting them. To collect recipient details as part of the POD, select the **Record pickup details**checkbox and enter the required details.

![recording-a-pod-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-5.png?width=312&height=556&name=recording-a-pod-5.png)![recording-a-pod-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-6.png?width=305&height=543&name=recording-a-pod-6.png)

### Leave Without Signature

If "*Authority to Leave"*has been enabled for the Consignment, an additional checkbox will be displayed on the Record POD view. On selecting this checkbox, recipient signature (or invoice photo) and recipient details are no longer required when submitting the POD. Now, information about where the stock was left is required - by adding the leave location text and taking a photo of the location. Both of these are required before submitting an Authority To Leave POD.

![recording-a-pod-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-7.png?width=299&height=532&name=recording-a-pod-7.png)

### Recipient Details

The required (and optional) recipient details fields can be defined in the CartonCloud web app under *Organisation Settings > Mobile App*. The fields defined here will then be displayed on the mobile app for the driver to fill in when submitting a POD. Some fields may be required while others can be set as optional.

![recording-a-pod-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-8.png?width=670&height=281&name=recording-a-pod-8.png)

PODs cannot be submitted with missing required fields.

### Adhoc Charges

When recording a POD for a Consignment and if there are adhoc charges available, an optional tick box will be displayed as part of thePOD view.

The tick box is automatically selected if values have been previously entered for any of the adhoc charges. The values for the adhoc charges can be edited by tapping the value fields (or in the case of a timer also starting / stopping / resetting the timer).

![recording-a-pod-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-9.jpeg?width=257&height=518&name=recording-a-pod-9.jpeg)

For more information on Consignment Adhoc Charges, check out this article: [Consignment Adhoc Charges - POD + Adhoc Charges](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+Adhoc+Charges#ConsignmentAdhocCharges-POD+AdhocCharges)

### Cash On Delivery

If the consignment requires [Cash On Delivery](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Cash+On+Delivery) or has the option of collecting Cash On Delivery, the POD view will display an additional checkbox called **Record Cash On Delivery**. If COD is required, the POD cannot be submitted without the driver confirming that the required COD has been collected.

For more information on how to collect Cash On Delivery on the CartonCloud mobile app check out this article: [Cash On Delivery](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Cash+On+Delivery)

### Errors

If an error needs to be lodged against a consignment, this can be done by selecting *"I need to record an error"* and then selecting the **Save POD** button. You will then be taken through the process of lodging an error.

![recording-a-pod-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-10.jpeg?width=230&height=469&name=recording-a-pod-10.jpeg)

For more information on how to lodge an error as part of a POD, check out this article: [Lodging an Error](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Lodging+an+Error)

### Submit POD

To submit a POD, select the **Save POD** button at the bottom of the Record POD view.

![recording-a-pod-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-11.jpeg?width=255&height=520&name=recording-a-pod-11.jpeg)

The POD upload will consequently be displayed in the **Outbox**tab under the **Pending Items** section header until the upload has either been successfully completed or failed.
![recording-a-pod-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-12.png?width=281&height=500&name=recording-a-pod-12.png)

**Successful POD Upload**

If a POD upload is successful, it will no longer show in the Outbox list and the corresponding consignment will be removed from the Consignment list unless the "Show Completed" option has been selected in the Consignment List filter. If this filter has been activated, completed Consignments will be highlighted with a green background.

![recording-a-pod-13](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-13.png?width=274&height=488&name=recording-a-pod-13.png)

**Failed POD Upload**

If a POD upload has failed, the **Outbox** tab will display a number representing the number of failed uploads. Additionally, all failed POD uploads will be listed in the Outbox list under the **Proof of Delivery**section header.

![recording-a-pod-14](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-14.png?width=295&height=525&name=recording-a-pod-14.png)

To obtain more information on why the upload failed, swipe left on the POD upload you are interested in, and choose **Show Error**.

![recording-a-pod-15](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-a-pod-15.png?width=339&height=603&name=recording-a-pod-15.png)

From the swipe menu, you can also choose to delete the POD or retry the POD upload. Similarly, tapping **Retry All** on top of the view will retry uploading all failed uploads in the Outbox list.

Choosing Delete will permanently delete the POD, including any signatures or photos collected as part of the POD.

Consignments with failed uploads will remain in the Consignment List and are highlighted with a yellow background.

For more information on Consignment statuses and their highlighting, check out this article: [Consignment Status Colours](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+Status+Colours)[https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+Status+Colours](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+Status+Colours)

### POD Photo Requirement

If you wish photo capture to be required on PODs, this can be enabled from the Organisation Settings and the Customer Settings.

**Organisation Settings:** this will enable it for all customers

- Navigate to Organisation Settings, More > Organisation Settings .
- Select the Mobile App tab and the Proof of Delivery section.
- Require Proof of Delivery Photo s set to Yes .
- Scroll down and click Save .

**Customer Settings:**will override Organisation Settings for the particular Customer

- Navigate to Customer Settings, Contacts > Customers > Click Edit against the relevant Customer.
- Select the Transport Management > POD tab.
- Require Proof of Delivery Photos set to Yes .
- Scroll down and click Save .

---

<a id="recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered"></a>
## Recording the GPS location of where a Consignment was delivered

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Turning location recording on
- Example POD
- In the history tab of the consignment
- On the POD

### Purpose

In CartonCloud it is possible to record the GPS location of the driver at the time of delivery. This is setting is turned off by default and must be turned on for each customer that you wish to record the coordinates for. Please note turning this on will make the location appear on the POD sent from the system.

The GPS coordinates are taken directly from the mobiles data, therefore the driver's location services must be turned on within the mobile's settings for the feature to work. The accuracy of the location is also dependant on the mobile's GPS, not CartonCloud.

This can be a useful tool to check if a consignment was dropped off at the correct location, especially if a Customer is reporting not received it.

Please note, the location data is accurate enough to check if a Driver has delivered to the correct street, but not necessarily accurate enough to determine the exact number within that street (might show #9 instead of #11). Again, this is dependant on the mobile's GPS data, not CartonCloud.

Driver's using iOS devices must enable Precise location permissions for CartonCloud when recording the POD. If **precise location** is not turned on then the device will report an approximate location only, resulting in this location being recorded on the POD.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/fpQWcuetbMI

### Turning Location Recording on

To turn on the recording of GPS coordinates for a Customer you will need to do the following,

- Select **Contacts**, then **Customers** from the drop-down in the main menu.
- Select the Customer you wish to turn the setting on for.
- Select **Edit**.
- Select the **Transport Management > POD** tab.
- Enable Show delivery GPS Coordinates in Consignment History.
- Click Save.

All POD's for that customer will now include the GPS location data of the driver at the time the driver submits the POD signature.

### Example POD

An example of how the POD will look for the following delivery.

![recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered-0.png?width=670&height=226&name=recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered-0.png)

### In the History tab of the Consignment

![recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered-1.png?width=670&height=119&name=recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered-1.png)

Clicking **View on Map** will then take you that location on Google Maps using the coordinates.

![recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered-2.png?width=497&height=363&name=recording-the-gps-location-of-where-a-consignment-was-delivered-2.png)

### On the POD

The GPS coordinates and link to view on Google will also appear on the PDF POD file.

---

<a id="showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info"></a>
## Showing Additional Pickup and Delivery Info

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Activating additional pickup / delivery information
- Displayed information

### Purpose

When viewing the details of a consignment in the CartonCloud mobile apps the relevant addresses will be displayed. This means that for a delivery consignment the address the delivery is destined for is shown. Similarly, a pickup consignment will show the address the consignment will be picked up from.

In addition to these essential addresses, CartonCloud offers the option to show additional Pickup or Delivery Info for consignments.

The optional additional Pickup / Delivery info is de-activated by default. To activate, please follow the instructions below.

### Activating additional pickup / delivery information

- Use the menu navigation and select Settings .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-27-2025-01-28-39-2784-AM.jpeg?width=249&height=500&name=image-png-Nov-27-2025-01-28-39-2784-AM.jpeg)

- Enable Show Consignment Origin .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-27-2025-01-29-01-1479-AM.jpeg?width=253&height=512&name=image-png-Nov-27-2025-01-29-01-1479-AM.jpeg)

### Displayed information

The additional address information displayed varies depending on the consignment type.

**Pickup:**You can now also see where a Pickup consignment's final destination is, not just where the Pickup is.
![showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-2.png?width=243&height=432&name=showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-2.png)

![showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-3.png?width=251&height=447&name=showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-3.png)
**Delivery:**You can now see where a Delivery consignment originated from (e.g. which one of your warehouses), not just where it is getting delivered to.
![showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-4.png?width=245&height=435&name=showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-4.png)

![showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-5.png?width=262&height=466&name=showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-5.png)

**Onforwarder:**You can now also see where an Onforwarder consignment originated from (e.g. which one of your warehouses), not just the Onforwarder's address and the final delivery address.

![showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-6.png?width=269&height=479&name=showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-6.png)

![showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-7.png?width=243&height=432&name=showing-additional-pickup-and-delivery-info-7.png)

Please note that Point to Point consignments are not affected by the activation of the Additional Pickup / Delivery Info option. Point to Point consignments will continue to display both a Pickup and a Delivery address.

# Mobile App > Mobile App Warehouse

<a id="access-scan-move-functionality"></a>
## Access Scan Move Functionality

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/access-scan-move-functionality_

### Table of Contents

- How to access Scan Move
  - As a packer or as a driver & packer
  - As a driver

Please note that the Scan Move functionality is only available to mobile app users that have been assigned a *Packer* role through the CartonCloud web app. Users with only *Driver* level permission will not be able to access this functionality.

For more information on User Modes please check out this article: [User Modes](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/User+Modes)

If you're not a Packer but would like to access the Scan Move functionality, use the Demo Mode available on the app login screen to get the chance to trial this feature.

More information about Demo Mode: [Demo Account](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Demo+Account)

### How to access Scan Move

**As a Packer Or As A Driver & Packer**

Assuming that you have been assigned either a "Packer" or both a "Packer" & a "Driver" role through the CartonCloud web app, the Scan Move functionality can be accessed easily by either choosing to enter the **Scan Move** user mode on login or selecting the **Switch User Mode** option in the main-slide in menu, which is accessed through the menu button in the top bar within the app.

- User Mode selection on login

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Screenshot%202026-03-25%20111439-png.png?width=278&height=592&name=Screenshot%202026-03-25%20111439-png.png)

- Switch Mode Menu option

![access-scan-move-functionality-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/access-scan-move-functionality-1.png?width=258&height=460&name=access-scan-move-functionality-1.png)

- User Mode switching

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Mar-26-2026-07-12-32-2824-PM.png?width=278&height=537&name=image-png-Mar-26-2026-07-12-32-2824-PM.png)

**As a Driver**

Assuming that you have only been assigned a "Driver" role through the CartonCloud web app, the Scan Picking functionality will not be available to you, as you will only be able to operate in **Delivering** mode.

---

## Access Wave Picking Functionality

- As a driver

### As a packer or as a driver & packer

Assuming that you have been assigned either just a "Packer" or both a "Packer" & a "Driver" role through the CartonCloud web app, the Wave Picking functionality can be accessed easily by either choosing to enter the **Wave Picking** user mode on login or selecting the **Switch User Mode** option in the main-slide in menu, which is accessed through the menu button in the top bar within the app.

**User Mode Selection on Login**

Assuming that you have only been assigned a "Driver" role through the CartonCloud web app, the Wave Picking functionality will not be available to you, as you will only be able to operate in **Delivering** mode.
