<a id="add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view"></a>
## Add product code to mobile app view

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Prerequisites

### Purpose

To add the product code to the mobile app view (for Sale Orders and Purchase Orders). Currently, you can only view the base Unit of Measure description.

**Current View (product name only)**

![add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-0.png?width=287&height=510&name=add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-0.png)
**Product Code View (product code and name)**

![add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-1.png?width=291&height=518&name=add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-1.png)

### Prerequisites

You must already have the relevant Products set up in CartonCloud. See

Add a Product

for more information on how to set up Products in CartonCloud.

How to add product code to the mobile app view

To add product code to the mobile app view you will need to export your Product list, edit it and then re-import it into CartonCloud.

**Export Product List**

- Navigate to the Products page, **Warehouse**>**Products**.
- Select the blue **More** button.
- Select **Export Products**.

**Edit the Product List file**

- Open the downloaded XSL file.
- Navigate to column **AP** called **Code**. It will be highlighted in blue.

![add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-2.png?width=670&height=293&name=add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-2.png)

- Insert a new column to the right of column AP.
- In the new column in row 2, enter the CONCATENATE formula: **=CONCATENATE(AP2," ",AO2)**
- Repeat this process for all the Products you wish to show the code for on the mobile app view. If you wish to do this for all Products, double click the bottom corner of the cell to copy the formula down the column.
- Copy the data from the **AQ** column.
- Paste the data into the **AO** column (Description column). Ensure you select the paste option, '**Paste as Values**'.
- Delete the original formula column (column AQ).
- Save the file.

**Import the edited Product List file**

- Navigate back to the CartonCloud Products page.
- Select the blue **More** button.
- Click **Import Products**.
- Select the relevant file.
- Click **Upload File**.
- You will then be directed to the Product Import Results page and will receive a success message.
- On your mobile view, you will now be able to see the Product code in front of the product name.

![add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-3.png?width=311&height=553&name=add-product-code-to-mobile-app-view-3.png)

---

<a id="adding-purchase-order-products"></a>
## Adding Purchase Order Products

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/adding-purchase-order-products_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- To add a product

### Purpose

Purchase Order Products can be added to a Purchase Order that is either **Not Yet Received** or **Received**.

### To add a product

- Select the plus icon in the top right of the Purchase Order Detail view.

![adding-purchase-order-products-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/adding-purchase-order-products-0.png?width=252&height=508&name=adding-purchase-order-products-0.png)

- On selecting this button, the Product Add view will open.
- Select the kind of product you want to add from the Product Search view. Tap the " Tap to choose product " text.

![adding-purchase-order-products-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/adding-purchase-order-products-1.png?width=273&height=551&name=adding-purchase-order-products-1.png)

- To choose the kind of product to add, either search for a product by typing into the search field, or scan a product unit of measure barcode.
- After selecting a product from the search results, tap " Change" to confirm your selection.
- On scanning a product barcode, the Product Search view will be automatically dismissed.
- The Product Add view will now automatically populate with all the fields available to modify the product, e.g. expiry date picker, product packaging etc.
- Once you have edited all desired fields, choose " Save " (either from the top left or the bottom of the view) to add the product to the Purchase Order. The product list will then automatically be reloaded to reflect the newly added product.

Please note that the **Quantity** field is a required field when adding a product.

---

<a id="creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app"></a>
## Creating a Wave Pick from the Mobile App

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Pre-requisites
- Steps

### Purpose

To create Wave Picks from the mobile app.

### Pre-requisites

- The user must have a Packer role.
- To create the Wave Pick a Wave Pick Template needs to be created first.

### Steps

Before being able to create a Wave Pick on the mobile app you will need to first create a [Wave Pick Template](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Wave-Pick-Templates) to select from.

- On the mobile app from the switch mode screen select Wave Picking .

![creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-0.png?width=239&height=479&name=creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-0.png)

- Select the plus icon in the top right to add a Wave Pick.

![creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-1.png?width=239&height=481&name=creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-1.png)

- Select a Prefill Template from the drop-down menu. You will be able to see all of the Wave Pick Templates you have created.

![creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-2.png?width=245&height=496&name=creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-2.png)

![creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-3.png?width=246&height=496&name=creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-3.png)

- Type in the Max# of Sale Orders .
- Select Create .

![creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-4.png?width=250&height=506&name=creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-4.png)

- You will then be taken straight into the Wave Pick to start picking.

![creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-5.png?width=256&height=514&name=creating-a-wave-pick-from-the-mobile-app-5.png)

---

<a id="details-page-and-items-list"></a>
## Details page and items list

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/details-page-and-items-list_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Items
- Details
- Navigating the details and items list page
- Details page
- Items list
- Items list searching
- Show Items Details
- Items list grouping
- Items list sorting

### Purpose

The details and items page provides more detail relating to the Sale Order.

### Items

The items list displays all the products within the order being picked/packed.

### Details

The details page shows the Sale Order detail information such as dates and special instructions.![details-page-and-items-list-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/details-page-and-items-list-1.png?width=267&height=533&name=details-page-and-items-list-1.png)

### Navigating the details and items list page

- From the Task List, click on the relevant Task.
- Slide the top toggle bar from the Items list to the Details page to access either page.

### Details page

The details page will provide more information related to the Sale Order. This information includes:

- The Customer
- Sale Order ID
- Customer Reference
- The delivery address
- Supplier (if applicable)
- Required Delivery Date
- Required Ship Date
- Expected Ship Date
- Packing Instructions
- Customer Invoice Status
- Expected Delivery Run
- Expected Carrier
- Transport Service Type (if applicable)
- Custom Fields
- If labels have been printed or not

### Items list

The items list screen is used to display all the products within the order being picked/packed, the quantity associated with each and the warehouse location.

![details-page-and-items-list-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/details-page-and-items-list-2.png?width=274&height=552&name=details-page-and-items-list-2.png)

**Item grouping (grouping for serial number (unique reference) verify/capture)**

Note, if an order contains multiple items with similar attributes, they will be grouped together in the items list in order to allow for more efficient picking. The criteria which items must meet in order to be grouped together is as follows:

- Same product
- Same location(Unallocated included)
- Same Expiry date (Blank included)
- A single POP Custom Field set up with Serial number Capture or Verify as a required step
- Same non-unique custom field values
  - Items with a value for a custom field must all have the same value in order to be grouped together.
  - Items without a value for a custom field must all have the no value in order to be grouped together.
- No additional custom fields that require a value to be captured

### Items list searching

Using the search functionality on the items list, you can search by product name, custom field or any matchable detail within the items list.

- Select the search icon in the top right corner of the items list.

![details-page-and-items-list-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/details-page-and-items-list-3.png?width=228&height=460&name=details-page-and-items-list-3.png)

- Use the search bar to type in the search term.

![details-page-and-items-list-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/details-page-and-items-list-4.png?width=243&height=482&name=details-page-and-items-list-4.png)

Note, the search will clear once the search is completed and you leave the search screen.

### Show Items Details

By selecting the 'Show Items Details' option you can view key items details from the Pick Pack screen. This includes Pallet ID, Expiry Date and Custom Fields. This option is enabled by default.

You can now view key item details—such as Pallet ID, Expiry Date, and Custom Fields—directly from the Item List screen in Picking mode.

![details-page-and-items-list-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/details-page-and-items-list-5.png?width=258&height=523&name=details-page-and-items-list-5.png)

![details-page-and-items-list-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/details-page-and-items-list-6.png?width=262&height=525&name=details-page-and-items-list-6.png)

### Items list grouping

Note, any filters will remain even if you exit the app. Filters will only clear if you manually clear them.

### How to group items:

- Select the Filter icon in the top right.
- Under Grouping, select either None , Location or Product .

![details-page-and-items-list-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/details-page-and-items-list-7.png?width=310&height=622&name=details-page-and-items-list-7.png)

- The item list display will change depending on the grouping selected.

**Location:**

![details-page-and-items-list-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/details-page-and-items-list-8.png?width=305&height=615&name=details-page-and-items-list-8.png)
**Product:**

![details-page-and-items-list-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/details-page-and-items-list-9.png?width=296&height=593&name=details-page-and-items-list-9.png)

### Items list sorting

Using the sorting functionality on the items list, you can sort the items list by warehouse location A-Z or Z-A or Pick Order ASC (ascending) or Pick Order DESC (descending).

- Select the Filter icon in the top right.
- Under the Sorting heading, select either Location A-Z , Location Z-A, Pick Order ASC or Pick Order DESC
- If using Pick Order ASC or Pick Order DESC, will list the products in the order of their pick order (set against the warehouse locations). To use this sorting method you will need to enable the below setting and set up the warehouse location sort order (pick path sequence) :
  - Navigate to Organisation Settings , More > Organisation Settings .
  - Select the Warehouse tab.
  - Tick Sort Pick/Pack List by Warehouse Locations Pick Order.
  - Scroll down and click Save .
  - Set up the warehouse locations with a sort order (pick path sequence). See here for detailed steps.

---

<a id="editing-purchase-order-products"></a>
## Editing Purchase Order Products

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/editing-purchase-order-products_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Edit
- Allocate
- Assign Pallet
- More

### Purpose

After selecting a Purchase Order Product from the product list within the **Verify** tab, the Confirmation View will be displayed. From here you can confirm or save product quantities and edit Purchase Order Products.

### Edit

By selecting the edit button in the top bar of the confirmation view, essential details of the Purchase Order Product can be edited: product, location, expiry date and status. Simply tap the product or location field to bring up the Product Search and Location Allocation view, respectively.

![editing-purchase-order-products-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/editing-purchase-order-products-0.png?width=244&height=493&name=editing-purchase-order-products-0.png)

- From here you can tap the Product field to change the product or click the location field to change the warehouse location.

![editing-purchase-order-products-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/editing-purchase-order-products-1.png?width=235&height=473&name=editing-purchase-order-products-1.png)

### Allocate

In addition to being able to allocate a product to a location from the **Allocate** tab, products can also be allocated from the Confirmation View by selecting the location button in the top bar. The Location Allocation view will then open and allow you to add/edit the location for the product.

![editing-purchase-order-products-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/editing-purchase-order-products-2.png?width=260&height=525&name=editing-purchase-order-products-2.png)

![editing-purchase-order-products-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/editing-purchase-order-products-3.png?width=268&height=541&name=editing-purchase-order-products-3.png)

### Assign Pallet

From the confirmation view you can assign a pallet barcode to the order or generate one. See [Pallet Handling](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Pallet-Handling) for more information.

### More

Selecting the **"More"**offers you a number of ways of editing the Purchase Order Product.

After selecting a Purchase Order Product from the product list within the **Verify** tab, the Confirmation View will be displayed. Apart from allowing the user to confirm or save product quantities, it also offers multiple ways of editing the Purchase Order Product.

For more information on how the Confirmation View works, check out this article: [Receiving on the mobile app (scan putaway)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=530058312)

---

<a id="how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app"></a>
## How to view Sale Order Products details on a Consignment within the Mobile App

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to enable sale order details on consignments POD recording screen

### Purpose

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/zwSFw9vsjzE

If the consignment you are recording a POD for has been generated from a Warehouse Sale Order then it is possible to view the products as they were listed on that Sale Order. This is useful for when a customer wants to check the contents of a consignment but the actual consignment is only recorded based on the packaging (e.g. cartons).

![how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-0.png?width=590&height=332&name=how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-0.png)
This feature requires a setting to be enabled for the Customer who owns the consignment:

### How to enable Sale Order details on Consignments POD Recording screen

**Enabling Sale Order details for a specific Customer**

- In the main menu of the Web app go to **Contacts** > **Customers**
- Search for and select the Customer you wish to enable this feature for
- Now on the View Customer page, select **Edit** at the bottom left of the page
- Select the **Transport Management** tab, followed by the **POD** sub-tab
- Scroll down to the setting **Show Sale Order details for Consignment on Mobile**, then set it to "Yes"
- Select **Save** at the bottom of the page for the change to be applied
- The button will now appear on the Consignments record POD screen, provided the consignment has been generated from a Sale Order.

**Enabling Sale Order details for all Customers (By default Customers are set to use the Organisation setting)**

- In the main menu of the Web app go to **More** > **Organisation Settings**
- Select the **Transport** tab, then scroll down to the **Other Transport Settings** section
- Scroll down to the setting **Show Sale Order details for Consignment on Mobile**, then select it to be ticked
- Select **Save** at the bottom of the page for the change to be applied
- The button will now appear on the Consignments record POD screen, provided the consignment has been generated from a Sale Order.

**Example:**

On the Record POD screen of this consignment, the Driver is given the necessary transport information that this consignment contains 9 Cartons.

![how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-1.png?width=333&height=591&name=how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-1.png)

However, the receiver needs to know what is inside the Cartons before signing, and the driver does not have any additional paperwork on them to verify the contents.

Luckily, the Display Sale Order Products feature has been enabled for this customer, so when the driver selects Tap to record signature the following screen displays.

![how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-2.png?width=526&height=296&name=how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-2.png)

The driver can then select the **View Product Details** button and the contents of the Cartons will appear on the screen, based on the information entered on the Sale Order from which the consignment was generated.

![how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-3.png?width=538&height=303&name=how-to-view-sale-order-products-details-on-a-consignment-within-the-mobile-app-3.png)

---

<a id="tms-pack-screen-workflow"></a>
## Mobile Pack Screen Workflow

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/tms-pack-screen-workflow_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Benefits
- Set up steps
- Workflow

### Purpose

Within CartonCloud you have the ability of using the warehouse management and transport management system in conjunction to create an optimised packing and shipping process. Utilising the Mobile Pack Screen from the transport management system allows warehouse staff on the floor to be able to enter the shipping quantity, weights and dimensions into the mobile app at the time of pick and pack. In addition, they can automatically print the CartonCloud transport label (and if using an external carrier, print their label) and place on the carton at the time of packing.

### Benefits

- Saves time
- Improves dispatch accuracy by removing manual processes
- Streamlines the dispatch process
- Makes the integration into external carriers more efficient

### Set up steps

To set up the Mobile Pack Screen for your CartonCloud account, please follow the below steps:

1 - Enable the correct customer settings

- For steps on how to enable the correct customer settings, please see Generating a Consignment from a Sale Order

2 - Map product unit of measure to to the consignment item field ***(optional)***

- For steps on how to map the product unit of measure and consignment item please see Mapping Product Unit of Measures to Consignment Fields/Transport Products

### Workflow

*Play video from time stamp 5:45*

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/qtOsoep1MdM

- Once you have finished packing the Sale Order you will be directed to the Mobile Pack Screen.
- From here you can update and change the quantity, weights and dimensions by clicking the edit pencil icon against the item.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0RF000000AoTN&feoid=00N5j00000K0J4b&refid=0EM5j000006n1dP)

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0RF000000AoTN&feoid=00N5j00000K0J4b&refid=0EM5j000006n1dU)

- You can also update the Service Type against the Service Type drop down menu (see Setting up Service Types for more information).

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0RF000000AoTN&feoid=00N5j00000K0J4b&refid=0EM5j000006n1fG)

- To print shipping labels click the green Print button.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0RF000000AoTN&feoid=00N5j00000K0J4b&refid=0EM5j000006n1da)

- Enter the number of labels you wish to print and then click Print .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0RF000000AoTN&feoid=00N5j00000K0J4b&refid=0EM5j000006n1gY)

- You can also choose to add an item by clicking the plus icon in the top right hand corner.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0RF000000AoTN&feoid=00N5j00000K0J4b&refid=0EM5j000006n1gd)

- Fill in the item information and click Add .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/servlet/rtaImage?eid=ka0RF000000AoTN&feoid=00N5j00000K0J4b&refid=0EM5j000006n1gi)

- Once you have finished updating and adding any relevant information on the pack screen click Close .

---

<a id="multi-capture"></a>
## Multi capture unique reference scanning

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/multi-capture_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Benefits
- Limitations
- Use cases
- Set up requirements
- Multi-unique reference capture process

### Purpose

The ability to scan a 2D barcode and record multiple unique references against lines of product on a Purchase Order.

In some cases, products may be supplied with a 2D barcode that stores a string of values related to the product, for example, the serial numbers for 10 items of a product. If your customers utilise these 2D barcodes for their products you can scan this barcode and record the unique references against multiple lines of product.

### Benefits

- Creates a more efficient process of recording unique references
- Saves time
- Better utilisation of the information provided to you by your customers

### Limitations

Currently, this process does not support setups that have more than one custom field using unique references per customer.

### Use cases

This process is only possible if there is a 2D barcode provided to you by your Customer. In this case, the 2D barcode will contain a string of values separated with a separation character. Once scanned the values contained in the barcode will be recorded against the individual lines of product on the order. For example, if you store Solar Panels and on the order paperwork there is a list of the serial numbers contained in the order and a 2D barcode that contains all of the individual products' serial numbers you can scan this barcode rather than scanning each individual serial barcode.

**Example of a 2D barcode:**

![multi-capture-0-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/multi-capture-0-1.png?width=451&height=330&name=multi-capture-0-1.png)

### Set up requirements

Inbound unique reference scanning is set up from a customer level and at the purchase order product custom field. To enable inbound unique reference scanning:

- Navigate to the relevant Customer, Contacts > Customers .
- Click Edit.
- Select the Warehouse Management > Purchase Order Products tab.
- From the relevant Custom Field, enable the below settings:
  - Enforce unique values
  - Split to lowest Unit of Measure
  - Make this a required step
- OR you can select Serial Number captured on Inbound and Verified on Outbound from the Configuration Type. Choosing this option will ensure all of the correct settings have been selected.
- Scroll down and select Save .

Please note, Is required can be set to optional at the customer purchase order product custom field but must be required at the product level (for the product you wish to enable inbound unique reference scanning for). To make this change at the product level you will need to edit the relevant product and scroll to the bottom under Modify Purchase Order Product Fields.

### Multi-unique reference capture process

See Purchase Order (inbound unique reference scanning for more information on unique reference scanning set-up.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/7nnGudpHJps

- On the mobile app select the Putaway mode.
- Select the relevant Purchase Order you wish to receive in.
- Click Receive.

![multi-capture-1-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/multi-capture-1-1.png?width=245&height=496&name=multi-capture-1-1.png)

- Select a line of product to start recording the unique references against the stock.
- In the bottom left corner a barcode icon with the pop up message 'Add Required Values' will appear for the fields that are marked as required. ie: Serial Number. Tap the pop up message or the barcode symbol below it.

![multi-capture-2-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/multi-capture-2-1.png?width=246&height=500&name=multi-capture-2-1.png)

- Scan the 2D barcode.
- You will be prompted to confirm if you wish to scan multiple codes. Select Use Multiple Capture .

![multi-capture-3-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/multi-capture-3-1.png?width=332&height=187&name=multi-capture-3-1.png)

- Alternatively, before you scan the barcode you can slide the toggle called Enable Multi Capture.

![multi-capture-4-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/multi-capture-4-1.png?width=239&height=483&name=multi-capture-4-1.png)

- Each individual unique reference will be recorded against each line of stock.

---

<a id="packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture"></a>
## Packing a Sale Order (Quantity Confirmation Page & verify and capture)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to pack a Sale Order on the mobile app
- Verifying unique references with 'Prevent changes' enabled
- How to revert a task that is currently in progress of packing
- Video Guides

### Purpose

To pick and pack a Sale Order on the mobile app. Through this process, you will need to confirm quantities, verify fields such as SSCC or batch numbers and capture fields if required.

### How to pack a Sale Order on the mobile app

- From the Task list view, select the Task you need to action (The sale Order you wish to pack).
- Start the Task by clicking Start Task .

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-0.png?width=260&height=518&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-0.png)

- Click on the Product you wish to start packing first.

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-1.png?width=260&height=518&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-1.png)

- You will then reach the verification page. Your Purchase Order Product Custom Fields configurations for that customer will dictate what steps appear here.

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-2.png?width=273&height=546&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-2.png)

- Click on the first step to verify the field. You can either verify by scanning or manually typing the field in. To scan, click the scan icon in the bottom right.

**Scan to verify or verify using the keyboard:**

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-3.png?width=280&height=567&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-3.png)

- Continue the same process for each verification step.

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-4.png?width=290&height=574&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-4.png)

Note: if the Purchase Order Product Custom Field is marked as required, you will not be able to skip the verification step.

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-5.png?width=288&height=584&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-5.png)

- You will now need to confirm the quantity. The quantity confirmation screen will appear automatically once you have finished the verification steps.
- Before confirming the quantities, you can slide the bar down to reveal the recommended unit to pick in, the option to perform a Quick Confirm and additional information related to the line of product (product code, base unit of measure, expiry date if applicable).

**To perform Quick Confirm:**

- Slide the bar down.
- Click Quick Confirm. The quantities will all be confirmed and will return you to the other lines that need to be picked for the order.
- You can also click Complete Task. This will perform a Quick Confirm for each line of product on the order.

**To confirm quantities using the plus and minus icons:**

You can use the plus and minus icons to increment the quantity by the unit of measure options available. A recommended pick will appear which does the unit of measure conversions for you and shows you what is the most optimal pick.

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-6.png?width=290&height=545&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-6.png)

**To confirm quantities using the calculator view:**

You can confirm quantities using the calculator view, which allows you to enter the quantity manually.

- Select the pencil icon next to the units you need to pick or the middle button for each respective unit of measure.

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-7.png?width=291&height=586&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-7.png)

- Use the calculator to enter the quantity you wish to pick and click Confirm Quantity.

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-8.png?width=294&height=591&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-8.png)

**To reset once the item has been completely picked**

Once you have completely picked an item on a order you have the option to reset it by clicking the Reset button. This will take the order back to 0 items picked. This is useful if you make a mistake when picking the order and provides greater control in the picking process.

- Click Reset at the bottom of the screen (this will only appear once the item is completely picked).

**![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-9.png?width=294&height=593&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-9.png)**

- Click OK when prompted.

**![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-10.png?width=299&height=599&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-10.png)**

**![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-11.png?width=296&height=590&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-11.png)**

- The item now has 0 items picked.

**To perform Complete Task (when units are still not yet confirmed)**

If you do not need to update and scan each individual product line of an order you can complete Quick Confirm at a Task level using the Complete Task button at the bottom of the order screen.

**Criteria to use Quick Confirm Complete Task:**

- The product is **not** weight based
- The product **does not**have required custom fields

**Use cases for Quick Confirm Complete Task:**

- User has several items on an order and they do not want to individually confirm the picked quantity for each item, they are able to tap the complete task button and the order will be completed.
- User is using a printed pick list to pick the items in the warehouse and then wanting to make the order as **packed** on the mobile app, they can just tap complete task rather than working through the list of items.

Complete task will complete all items that don't require further information such as specific weights or custom field information.  Tapping complete task will complete all other items and then filter the list to show just items that need further user input.

**Scanning to Quick Confirm**

Scanning an item barcode or unit of measure barcode will increment the picked quantity by that amount, if that meets the required quantity on the order the item will automatically be marked as picked.

### Verifying unique references with ‘Prevent changes’ enabled

If you have ‘Prevent changes’ enabled, the stock selection criteria will remain, including custom fields (unique references). With this option selected you will have to verify and pick the exact reference recorded against the line of stock. However, on the other hand, if you do not have this option enabled then you can utilise the Reallocation feature.

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-12.png?width=272&height=547&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-12.png)

### How to revert a task that is currently in progress of packing

Users are able to revert orders back to Awaiting Pick and Pack if the order is in Packing in Progress status and they are assigned to the order.

When a user has started a task and the task is assigned to them they can revert the order by:

- Select the back button.
- You will be prompted with a 'Save Packing Progress' pop up. You can select to:
  - Save Packing Progress: saves the packing progress and the order remains in a 'Packing in Progress' status.
  - Cancel Packing: the order reverts to 'Awaiting Pick and Pack' status. The user is prompted to choose to stay assigned to the task or be unassigned.
  - Cancel: cancel the option to go back and continue working on the order.

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-13](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-13.png?width=277&height=558&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-13.png)

![packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-14](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-14.png?width=269&height=532&name=packing-a-sale-order-quantity-confirmation-page-verify-and-capture-14.png)

### Video Guides

**Picking a Sale Order using Picking**

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/0BKsdYd7bpo

**Picking a Sale Order using Task Mode with unique references**

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/FGZcw2depyQ

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/7wczVWYfQXM

<a id="photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app"></a>
## Photos and Documents on Purchase Orders (mobile app)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Prerequisites
- Use Cases / Benefits
- Process
  - Adding Documents to Purchase Orders on Inbound
- Uploading photos and documents in the background

### Purpose

The process for adding and capturing photos and Documents on Purchase Orders during the inbound process on the mobile app.

### Prerequisites

- Use a mobile device with a built-in camera. If not, take photos on another device, then send them to your mobile device to upload via the Library option or use the web app for upload.
- Allow the CartonCloud app access to your Camera and Photos on your mobile device. You will be prompted to do this when you first use the Document feature on your mobile device.
- If you would like your Customer to be notified when Documents are uploaded against a Purchase Order ensure they are set up with Purchase Order Verified and Allocated notifications. These notifications will include Documents if they have been uploaded and are a Shared with Customer visibility level. See [Customer Settings - Email (Notifications)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Customer-Settings-Email-Notifications) for more information.

### Use Cases / Benefits

- Take photos of stock as it is received in the warehouse.
- Record damages at the time stock is received.
- Provide greater transparency and updates to your customers on their stock.
- Provide greater transparency internally with photo visibility supplied at the time of receipt or damage and document history for who uploaded the attachment.
- A more advanced inbound process providing greater service and value to your customers.

### Process

From the mobile app, you can add documents to Purchase Orders until the Purchase Order is allocated.

### Adding Documents to Purchase Orders on Inbound

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/RvKECtVE8pk

- Navigate to the Putaway mode on the mobile app.
- Select the Purchase Order you wish to receive.
- Documents can be added at any stage throughout the inbound process until the Purchase Order is in a status of ‘Allocated’.
- To add a Document, select the Photos button at the bottom of the screen.

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-0.png?width=247&height=498&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-0.png)

- Select the **Name** for the Document from the drop-down. *Note that names are not customisable, however, they will be in the future.*
  - **Receival**: use this name when receiving stock that is not damaged.
  - **Receival (Damages):** use this name when receiving damaged freight.
  - A default visibility option can be selected from the Organisation Settings. For more information click here .
- Enter information related to the Document within the **Description** text box. This will be visible to anyone who has access to the Document.
- Select the visibility option: (a default visibility option can be selected from the Organisation Settings. More information [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Organisation-Settings-Warehouse-Order-Documents))
  - **Internal only**: only internal users of your CartonCloud account will be able to see the Document. This includes all User types apart from Customers. Only Admin and Packers can edit the Document.
  - **Shared with Customer:**all user types in your CartonCloud account including Customers can see the Document. Only Admin and Packers can edit the Document.
- Upload the attachment using either:
  - **Single capture**: take one photo using the camera on the device.
  - **Multi-capture:**rapidly take multiple photos without having to close and reopen the camera.
  - **Video:** record a video to upload using the camera on the device. By default, sound is turned off when recording the video however, you can turn this on when recording the video.
  - **Library**: access attachments saved to your device to upload to the Document.
- As the attachments upload to the Document, do not leave the screen as they will not save.

Note, that you do not have to upload an attachment when creating a Document. You can choose to add this at a later stage from the web app.

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-1.png?width=251&height=501&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-1.png)

- If you wish to add more attachments select the plus icon next to the attachment you just uploaded.
- Select **Save** once you have finished uploading the attachments.

**![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-2.png?width=253&height=510&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-2.png)**

- You will now be able to see the Documents and the associated attachments against the Purchase Order on the web app.

### Adding Documents from Purchase Order Products view

You can add Documents to the Purchase Order from the Purchase Order Product view.

- From within the Purchase Order, click on a product line (the Purchase Order Product).

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-3.png?width=276&height=555&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-3.png)

- Select the edit pencil icon.

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-4.png?width=284&height=575&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-4.png)

- Select the camera icon in the top right corner. Alternatively, if you update the status of the Purchase Order Product you will be prompted to confirm if you would like to add a Document.

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-5.png?width=272&height=546&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-5.png)

- Select the **Name** for the Document from the drop-down. *Note that names are not customisable, however, they will be in the future.*
  - **Receival**: use this name when receiving stock that is not damaged.
  - **Receival (Damages):** use this name when receiving damaged freight.
- Enter information related to the Document within the **Description** text box. This will be visible to anyone who has access to the Document. The description will be pre-filled with the product code for the Purchase Order Product you have selected.
- Select the visibility option:
  - **Internal only**: only internal users of your CartonCloud account will be able to see the Document. This includes all User types apart from Customers. Only Admin and Packers can edit the Document.
  - **Shared with Customer:**all user types in your CartonCloud account including Customers can see the Document. Only Admin and Packers can edit the Document.
- Upload the attachment using either:
  - **Single capture**: take one photo using the camera on the device.
  - **Multi-capture:**rapidly take multiple photos without having to close and reopen the camera.
  - **Video:** record a video to upload using the camera on the device. By default, sound is turned off when recording the video however, you can turn this on when recording the video.
  - **Library**: access attachments saved to your device to upload to the Document.
- As the attachments upload to the Document, do not leave the screen as they will not save.
- Select Save once you have finished uploading the attachments.

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-6.png?width=289&height=587&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-6.png)

- You will now be able to see the Documents and the associated attachments against the Purchase Order on the web app.

### Uploading photos and documents in the background

When uploading photos and documents to Purchase Orders the files can be uploaded in the background whilst you continue processing the order.

You will be notified that the file is uploading in the background:

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-7.png?width=274&height=551&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-7.png)

To check progress on the upload or to retry failed uploads you can use the Outbox feature. To access this feature be sure you are in the Wave Picking mode, select the side navigation and then select Outbox.

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-8.png?width=277&height=551&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-8.png)

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-9.png?width=273&height=545&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-9.png)

---

<a id="photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app"></a>
## Photos and Documents on Sale Orders (mobile app)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Prerequisites
- Use Cases / Benefits
- Process
  - Before picking an order
  - Upon picking completion
  - On the Pack screen
- Uploading Photos and Documents in the Background

### Purpose

The process for adding and capturing Photos and Documents on Sale Orders during the outbound process on the mobile app.

### Prerequisites

- Use a mobile device with a built-in camera. If not, take photos on another device, then send them to your mobile device to upload via the Library option or use the web app for upload.
- Allow the CartonCloud app access to your Camera and Photos on your mobile device. You will be prompted to do this when you first use the Document feature on your mobile device.
- If you would like your Customer to be notified when Documents are uploaded against a Sale Order ensure they are set up with Sale Order Packed notification. These notifications will include Documents if they have been uploaded and are a Shared with Customer visibility level. See [Customer Settings - Email (Notifications)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Customer-Settings-Email-Notifications) for more information.

### Use Cases / Benefits

- Provide photo evidence to your customers of what has been packed in the Sale Order. This is useful if the person receiving the order claims certain products were not included.
- To verify packing requirements with photo evidence. This is helpful if your customer requires orders packed a certain way.
- Provide greater transparency and updates to your customers on their orders.

### Process

You can add a Photo/Document to a Sale Order before picking the order or upon picking completion.

#### **Before picking an order:**

- Select Picking mode.

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-0.png?width=271&height=546&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-0.png)

- Select the order you wish to add the photo/document to.

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-1.png?width=271&height=548&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-1.png)

- Tap the three dot icon in the top right corner.

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-2.png?width=296&height=601&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-2.png)

- Select **Add Document**.

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-3.png?width=278&height=560&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-3.png)

- Select the **Name** for the Document from the drop-down. Note that names are not customisable, however, they will be in the future.
  - **Packing Confirmation:** use this Name when confirming the packed order. For example, taking a photo of the packaging to confirm the order has been packed to the customer’s specifications.
  - **Other:**for anything other than Photos and Documents relating to packing confirmation.
- Enter information related to the Document within the **Description** text box. This will be visible to anyone who has access to the Document.
- Select the visibility option: (a default visibility option can be selected from the Organisation Settings. More information [here](https://cartoncloudorg.lightning.force.com/articles/Knowledge/Organisation-Settings-Warehouse-Order-Documents))
  - **Internal only:** only internal users of your CartonCloud account will be able to see the Document. This includes all User types apart from Customers. Only Admin and Packers can edit the Document (Documents can only be edited via the web app).
  - **Shared with Customer:**all user types in your CartonCloud account including Customers can see the Document. Only Admin and Packers can edit the Document (Documents can only be edited via the web app).
- Upload the attachment using either:
  - **Single capture**: take one photo using the camera on the device.
  - **Multi-capture:**rapidly take multiple photos without having to close and reopen the camera.
  - **Video:** record a video to upload using the camera on the device. By default, sound is turned off when recording the video however, you can turn this on when recording the video.
  - **Library**: access attachments saved to your device to upload to the Document.

![photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-4.png?width=272&height=550&name=photos-and-documents-on-purchase-orders-mobile-app-4.png)

- Select **Save** once you have finished uploading the attachments.
- You will now be able to see the Documents and the associated attachments against the Purchase Order on the web app.

#### **Upon picking completion**

- Once all products have been picked for the order and before completing the Task, select Photos on the bottom right of the screen.

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-5.png?width=294&height=591&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-5.png)

- Select the **Name** for the Document from the drop-down. Note that names are not customisable, however, they will be in the future.
  - **Packing Confirmation:** use this Name when confirming the packed order. For example, taking a photo of the packaging to confirm the order has been packed to the customer’s specifications.
  - **Other:**for anything other than Photos and Documents relating to packing confirmation.
- Enter information related to the Document within the **Description** text box. This will be visible to anyone who has access to the Document.
- Select the visibility option: (a default visibility option can be selected from the Organisation Settings. More information [here](https://cartoncloudorg.lightning.force.com/articles/Knowledge/Organisation-Settings-Warehouse-Order-Documents))
  - **Internal only:** only internal users of your CartonCloud account will be able to see the Document. This includes all User types apart from Customers. Only Admin and Packers can edit the Document (Documents can only be edited via the web app).
  - **Shared with Customer:**all user types in your CartonCloud account including Customers can see the Document. Only Admin and Packers can edit the Document (Documents can only be edited via the web app).
- Upload the attachment using either:
  - **Single capture**: take one photo using the camera on the device.
  - **Multi-capture:**rapidly take multiple photos without having to close and reopen the camera.
  - **Video:** record a video to upload using the camera on the device. By default, sound is turned off when recording the video however, you can turn this on when recording the video.
  - **Library**: access attachments saved to your device to upload to the Document.

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-6.png?width=327&height=660&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-6.png)

- Select **Save** once you have finished uploading the attachments.
- You will now be able to see the Documents and the associated attachments against the Purchase Order on the web app.

**On the Pack Screen**

If you are [creating Consignments from the Sale Order](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Generating-a-Consignment-from-a-Sale-Order), you can also add Photos and Documents from the [TMS pack screen](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/TMS-Pack-Screen-Workflow) when confirming Consignment Items.

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-7.png?width=315&height=584&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-7.png)

### Uploading Photos and Documents in the Background

When uploading photos and documents to Purchase Orders the files can be uploaded in the background whilst you continue processing the order.

You will be notified that the file is uploading in the background:

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-8.png?width=299&height=601&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-8.png)

To check progress on the upload or to retry failed uploads you can use the Outbox feature. To access this feature be sure you are in the Wave Picking mode, select the side navigation and then select Outbox.

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-9.png?width=273&height=543&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-9.png)

![photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-10.png?width=266&height=531&name=photos-and-documents-on-sale-orders-mobile-app-10.png)

---

<a id="pick-to-tote"></a>
## Pick to Tote

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/pick-to-tote_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Workflow Overview
- Workflow Use Cases
- Benefits
- Prerequisites
- Considerations
- How to set up tote barcodes
- Completing a Pick to Tote Wave Pick on the Mobile App
- How to Video

### Purpose

Pick to Tote streamlines warehouse efficiency by enabling pickers to fulfil multiple orders simultaneously, sorting items directly into totes as they navigate the warehouse.

Pick to Tote is included in all warehouse plans; however, [Wave Pick Auto Generation (Advanced Automation,](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Advanced-Automation-Wave-Picks)which enables automated wave pick generation is only included within [WMS Premium](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/WMS-Premium). Please reach out to our team [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/contactsupport) if you would like more information on WMS Premium.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/3r66YydGM4c

### Workflow Overview

Pick to Tote is a picking workflow designed to reduce the time spent picking. This is achieved by allowing pickers to pick multiple orders (usually 10-30) simultaneously, sorting them into totes as they go. This reduces the total walking time through the warehouse and the speed of the picking process. After picking, the trolley containing the totes is wheeled back to a packing station where the packing is completed. The [Pack Screen](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Pack-Screen) can be utilised to complete the packing step.

![pick-to-tote-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-0.png?width=670&height=215&name=pick-to-tote-0.png)

### Workflow Use Cases

This workflow is designed for e-commerce operations that have high-volume orders with a small number of SKUs on each order.

It is recommended that Pick to Tote is used in conjunction with the [Pack Screen](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Pack-Screen) for a more optimised and efficient workflow.

### Benefits

- Increased order accuracy, by attributing the product to the correct order as soon as the product is picked from the shelf, reducing the risk of products being packed with the wrong order.
- Pick to Tote enables a more efficient picking process by allowing warehouse workers to pick multiple orders at once, while moving through the warehouse in a single trip, avoiding multiple trips and retracing steps to fulfil orders.
- Increased flexibility within Wave Picking as Pick to Tote supports an additional picking workflow to support different warehouse and business requirements.
- Ensures faster fulfilment and increased productivity.

### Prerequisites

- A Pick to Tote Wave Pick must first be created before it can be actioned and completed on the mobile or web app. See [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Creating-a-Wave-Pick-for-multiple-Customers) for steps on how to create a Wave Pick.
- Totes on a trolley with barcodes to be scanned during picking.
- Tote barcodes are set up correctly. See [How to set up Tote barcodes](https://help.cartoncloud.com#how-to-set-up-tote-barcodes) for more information.
- Scanners are required for Pick to Tote.

### Considerations

- The mobile app must be used when picking a Pick to Tote Wave Pick, however, either the web app or mobile app may be used for the packing process. If packing from the web app, it is recommended to use the [Scan Order / Tote](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Scan-Order-Tote)screen. Scanning the tote barcodes will jump you straight into the web app pack screen for the order.
- It is recommended that the totes utilised are all the same size. Currently, in CartonCloud there is no way to direct the order to a certain tote based on the order size.

### How to set up tote barcodes

Pick to Tote requires barcoded totes to be scanned during the picking process. In order to determine what barcode is a tote barcode, there is an Organisation Setting ‘Reqex for Tote Barcode Validation” which controls the validation for what is a valid tote barcode.

By default, CartonCloud supports tote barcodes in the following format:

[Trolley #] [Tote Colour] [Tote #]

Eg:

1 Yellow 1

1 Blue 3

For example, following that format, assume you have a trolley with 5 levels and 5 totes per level, with each level containing different coloured totes, this is the suggested naming convention:

![pick-to-tote-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-1.png?width=670&height=391&name=pick-to-tote-1.png)

Barcodes following this naming convention have been created [here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1X1R8SCmPeEiPTaTKq7AH8htJE2018JxEidQNfM9tpSE/edit?tab=t.0). However, should you wish to create your own, barcodes can then be generated using tools such as [https://barcode.tec-it.com/en/,](https://barcode.tec-it.com/en/)

If you wish to use a different format to the one mentioned above you can change the regex by:

- Navigate to Organisation Settings, **More**>**Organisation Settings**.
- Select the **Warehouse** tab.
- Scroll down to **Mobile App Configuration** and **Reqex for Tote Barcode Validation**.
- Select **Edit**.
- Add a name and the regular expression (regex).
- Select **Save**.
- Create barcodes for the format you have created.

### Completing a Pick to Tote Wave Pick on the Mobile App

- From the Switch Mode screen, select **Wave Picking**.
- Select the relevant Pick to Tote Wave Pick. Note, that the Wave Pick type will be shown in red above the Wave Pick name.

![pick-to-tote-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-2.png?width=283&height=571&name=pick-to-tote-2.png)

- Select **Pick**.

![pick-to-tote-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-3.jpeg?width=309&height=623&name=pick-to-tote-3.jpeg)

- The Pick to Tote screen has three different views:
  - **Items:**the products on the Wave Pick

![pick-to-tote-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-4.png?width=328&height=656&name=pick-to-tote-4.png)

- **Orders:** the orders on the Wave Pick

![pick-to-tote-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-5.png?width=309&height=622&name=pick-to-tote-5.png)

- **Details**: the details of the Wave Pick

![pick-to-tote-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-6.png?width=306&height=618&name=pick-to-tote-6.png)

- To start picking the products you can scan either the product or warehouse location barcode. Alternatively, you can tap into the product.
- This will then show the product and the orders it is required for. Scan either the product or tote barcode to assign the product to the tote. You can scan the product or the tote barcode first, however, both have to be confirmed to confirm the pick into the tote. The product can either be scanned or confirmed using the quantity confirmation page by tapping into the product. However, the tote must be confirmed by scanning the tote barcode.
- If you wish to pick an alternative order first, simply select it from the list and the list will reorder.

![pick-to-tote-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-7.png?width=289&height=578&name=pick-to-tote-7.png)

- Until a Tote is assigned to that order the order will show as Awaiting Tote and if the product’s order already has had a tote assigned from a previous product the tote will show and not allow you to assign a different tote.

![pick-to-tote-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-8.png?width=281&height=567&name=pick-to-tote-8.png)

- Continue with all products on the Wave Pick.
- Once all products have been picked into their respective totes, select **Complete Pick**.

![pick-to-tote-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/pick-to-tote-9.png?width=284&height=572&name=pick-to-tote-9.png)

- You will be prompted to continue with the pack step on the mobile or back to the Wave Pick List where the pack step can either be completed at a later stage on the mobile or to be completed on the web app. It is recommended that the pack step be completed on the web app via the [Pack Screen](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Pack-Screen).
- If completing the pack on the mobile, select the order to pack and follow the steps [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Wave-Pick-Pack). If completing the pack on the web app, use the [Pack Screen](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Pack-Screen) and follow the steps outlined [here](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Pack-Screen).

### How to Video

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/1XFUS-67XSA

---

<a id="printing-sale-order-labels-on-the-mobile-app"></a>
## Printing Sale Order labels (on the mobile app)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/printing-sale-order-labels-on-the-mobile-app_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to print a Sale Order

### Purpose

To print Sale Order labels through the picking and packing process.

### How to print a Sale Order

You can print a Sale Order label at any time during the picking and packing process. To do so:

- Select the three-dot

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Feb-26-2026-08-06-44-1134-PM.png?width=331&height=676&name=image-png-Feb-26-2026-08-06-44-1134-PM.png)

- Select the Print button.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Feb-26-2026-08-10-54-1026-PM.png?width=332&height=676&name=image-png-Feb-26-2026-08-10-54-1026-PM.png)

- Enter the number of labels you wish to print and then click Print .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Feb-26-2026-08-12-35-4859-PM.png?width=331&height=676&name=image-png-Feb-26-2026-08-12-35-4859-PM.png)

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Feb-26-2026-08-13-20-9482-PM.png?width=332&height=676&name=image-png-Feb-26-2026-08-13-20-9482-PM.png)
