<a id="product-bulk-allocation"></a>
## Product Bulk Allocation

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/product-bulk-allocation_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Accessing bulk allocation functionality
- Locating
- Bulk allocation in strict mode

Purpose

Bulk allocation of products within a Purchase Order allows users to quickly locate multiple (or all) products into a single warehouse location.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/JzE6R2j7Ik4

### Accessing Bulk Allocation Functionality

To access the Bulk Allocation functionality, simply enter a Purchase Order, navigate to the **Locate** tab and select the grouping button in the top bar.

![product-bulk-allocation-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-bulk-allocation-0.png?width=251&height=433&name=product-bulk-allocation-0.png)

Once the grouping mode has been activated, product lines can either be manually selected one by one or all at once by using the **Select All** button at the bottom of the screen. In the active grouping mode the grouping button will be shown in red, and there will be an additional bar displaying a **Locate**button at the bottom of the screen.

**Manual Selection**

Once the grouping mode is activated, products can be added to the selection by simple tapping them. They will then be highlighted in blue and showing an orange tick box to indicate that they are part of the selection.

**Select All**

The round button at the bottom of the screen (part of the **Locate** bar), represents the **Select All**functionality.

![product-bulk-allocation-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-bulk-allocation-1.png?width=283&height=488&name=product-bulk-allocation-1.png)
On selecting this button one of three scenarios takes place:

**Button was already selected**

If the button was already selected (shown by being a full orange circle rather than just a circle outline): All selected products will be unselected and the button again shown as unselected (outline only).

**No products with location**

If none of the products in the list have a location yet (i.e. all of them are **Awaiting Location**): All products will be selected.

**Some products with location**

If some of the products in the list already have a location: An alert will be displayed checking whether or not to include those products that already have a location in the product selection.

![product-bulk-allocation-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-bulk-allocation-2.png?width=289&height=499&name=product-bulk-allocation-2.png)

On selecting **Include**, all products will be selected.

![product-bulk-allocation-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-bulk-allocation-3.png?width=308&height=531&name=product-bulk-allocation-3.png)

On selecting **Do Not Include**, only products without a location will be selected.

### Locating

Once the product selection represents the item that you want to locate into the same location, select **Locate** at the bottom of the screen and follow the instructions to choose a location by either manually searching or scanning a warehouse location barcode.
![product-bulk-allocation-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-bulk-allocation-4.png?width=314&height=542&name=product-bulk-allocation-4.png)

![product-bulk-allocation-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-bulk-allocation-5.png?width=310&height=532&name=product-bulk-allocation-5.png)

![product-bulk-allocation-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-bulk-allocation-6.png?width=321&height=571&name=product-bulk-allocation-6.png)

The products will then be updated server-side to show their new location.

### Bulk Allocation in Strict Mode

Bulk Allocation is not available if strict processes for putaway have been enabled.
For more information on strict processes, check out this article: [Enforce Strict Processes For Packing And Putaway](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Enforce+Strict+Processes+For+Packing+And+Putaway)

---

<a id="product-viewing-options-for-mobile"></a>
## Product Viewing Options for Mobile

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/product-viewing-options-for-mobile_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to edit the product name that appears on the mobile app

### Purpose

By default, when viewing a Purchase Order or Sale Order on the mobile app, the conversion Unit of Measurement description will be shown. This can be customised by using the Product import/export feature.
![product-viewing-options-for-mobile-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-viewing-options-for-mobile-0.png?width=258&height=517&name=product-viewing-options-for-mobile-0.png)

### How to edit the product name that appears on the mobile app

To edit the product name on the mobile app view you will need to export your Product list, edit it and then re-import it into CartonCloud.

- Navigate to the Products page, Warehouse > Products .
- Select the blue More button.
- Select Export Products .

![product-viewing-options-for-mobile-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-viewing-options-for-mobile-1.png?width=670&height=167&name=product-viewing-options-for-mobile-1.png)

- Open the downloaded XSL file.
- Scroll across the file to the right until you reach the coloured section of the Conversion Measurement fields.

![product-viewing-options-for-mobile-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-viewing-options-for-mobile-2.png?width=670&height=67&name=product-viewing-options-for-mobile-2.png)

- Now you can choose how you would like the Product details to be displayed on the mobile app (Description and Code, Description only or Code only). To do this you need to update the "Description" field for the Conversion Measurement. See Add product code to mobile app view for more information on how to include both the product code and name in the mobile view.
- In the example, the Description and the Code (this is the code as it appears in Column E of the file) have been selected for viewing.

![product-viewing-options-for-mobile-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-viewing-options-for-mobile-3.png?width=670&height=66&name=product-viewing-options-for-mobile-3.png)

- Navigate back to the CartonCloud Products page.
- Select the blue More button.
- Click Import Products .

![product-viewing-options-for-mobile-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-viewing-options-for-mobile-4.png?width=670&height=178&name=product-viewing-options-for-mobile-4.png)

- Select the relevant file.
- Click Upload File .

![product-viewing-options-for-mobile-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-viewing-options-for-mobile-5.png?width=564&height=149&name=product-viewing-options-for-mobile-5.png)

- You will then be directed to the Product Import Results page and will receive a success message.

![product-viewing-options-for-mobile-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-viewing-options-for-mobile-6.png?width=670&height=88&name=product-viewing-options-for-mobile-6.png)

Now you can refresh your screen in the mobile app and view the updated Product details

![product-viewing-options-for-mobile-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/product-viewing-options-for-mobile-7.png?width=271&height=536&name=product-viewing-options-for-mobile-7.png)

---

<a id="purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning"></a>
## Purchase Order (inbound) unique reference scanning

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Benefits
- Use cases
- Set up requirements
- How to record unique references on inbound

### Purpose

Allow users to record unique references (ie: serial number), either by scanning or inputting the data against stock coming into the warehouse (via a Purchase Order).

### Benefits

- Allows you to record unique references against the base unit of measure (smallest unit of measure) of a product
- Improves tracking accuracy of items
- Allows for data captured on the inbound process to be validated on the outgoing process (as items leave the warehouse)
- More accurate traceability of all items (not just the purchase order as a whole)
- Assist with any recall process or warranty claims
- Provide greater visibility to your customers on their inventory
- Better meet customer requirements for recording custom and unique data against stock

### Use Cases

- A pallet comes into a warehouse with 30 individual items and each item needs a serial number scanned against it.
- Wanting to group individual items together on a pallet and use a single pallet barcode to move the items into a warehouse location
- Unloading a container to multiple pallets and locating each pallet to a location

### Set up requirements

Inbound unique reference scanning is set up from a customer level and at the purchase order product custom field. To enable inbound unique reference scanning:

- Navigate to the relevant Customer, Contacts > Customers .
- Click Edit.
- Select the Warehouse Management > Purchase Order Products tab.
- From the relevant Custom Field, enable the below settings:
  - Enforce unique values
  - Split to lowest Unit of Measure
  - Make this a required step
- OR you can select Serial Number captured on Inbound and Verified on Outbound from the Configuration Type. Choosing this option will ensure all of the correct settings have been selected.
- Scroll down and select Save .

Please note, Is required can be set to optional at the customer purchase order product custom field but must be required at the product level (for the product you wish to enable inbound unique reference scanning for). To make this change at the product level you will need to edit the relevant product and scroll to the bottom under Modify Purchase Order Product Fields.

### How to record unique references on inbound

**Video instructions**

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/9tvF2gTfmj8

**Written instructions**

- Select the Putaway user mode.

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-0.png?width=255&height=505&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-0.png)

- Select the relevant Purchase Order you wish to receive in.
- Click Receive .

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-1.png?width=269&height=544&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-1.png)

- Tap the product. If the quantity is greater than the product's pallet quantity (defined against the product) you can split the product into pallet quantities. It is suggested that you do split the quantity into pallet quantities as this will make it easier when you need to move and locate the pallet.
- Click the three dot action icon in the top right and click Bulk Split .

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-2.png?width=296&height=597&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-2.png)

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-3.png?width=296&height=597&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-3.png)

- The pallet conversion quantity will show, this is the quantity it will be split by. If you wish to change it, edit the number. Tap Bulk Split .

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-4.png?width=290&height=579&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-4.png)

- You will now see individual lines of product for each pallet. Select a pallet to verify

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-5.png?width=308&height=626&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-5.png)

- Select a line of product to start recording the unique references against the stock.
- From this screen, you can then choose to assign a pallet barcode and/or print a pallet label by clicking the pallet icon. For more information please see Pallet Handling .

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-6.png?width=319&height=649&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-6.png)

- In the bottom left corner a barcode icon with the pop up message 'Add Required Values' will appear for the fields that are marked as required. ie: Serial Number. Tap the pop up message or the barcode symbol below it.

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-7.png?width=334&height=679&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-7.png)

- Scan or type in the unique reference field, in this case the Serial Number and click Submit .

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-8.png?width=324&height=656&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-8.png)![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-9.png?width=336&height=677&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-9.png)

- You will be prompted to scan or input the reference for each individual item. You will be able to see the reference scanned for each item under the Scan Confirmation heading.

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-10.png?width=325&height=653&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-10.png)

- If you select the Scan History heading you will be able to see each successful and unsuccessful scan and if the field has been edited.

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-11.png?width=339&height=685&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-11.png)

- If you need to edit a field that has already been scanned or remove the scanned value from the group, select the pencil icon against the reference in the Scan Confirmation heading.

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-12.png?width=327&height=661&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-12.png)

- Make any required changes and select Submit .
- From this screen you can also split the item from the current group after the Unique Reference has been captured. By clicking Split, the selected item will be removed from the original group and have it's pallet barcode removed. This allows you to update the status of individual item, for example if it is found to be damaged or needs to be assigned to an alternative pallet to the original group.

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-13](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-13.png?width=326&height=652&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-13.png)

- If you record a reference that is not unique you will receive an error message and you will need to enter a unique reference.

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-14](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-14.png?width=340&height=673&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-14.png)

- Once all items have a reference recorded against it, select Done in the top right corner of the screen.

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-15](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-15.png?width=327&height=657&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-15.png)

- Click Confirm .

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-16](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-16.png?width=334&height=670&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-16.png)

- Repeat this process for the other pallets.
- Once you have recorded the data for each item, click **Verify**.

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-17](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-17.png?width=350&height=706&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-17.png)

- In the above scenario, each individual item on the pallet has a different unique reference recorded against it, however, they are still grouped together by the same pallet ID.
- You can now locate the pallet, select a pallet to locate.
- Scan the warehouse location (or type the location) and click Locate .

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-18](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-18.png?width=282&height=567&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-18.png)

![purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-19](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-19.png?width=281&height=566&name=purchase-order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning-19.png)

---

<a id="purchase-order-list"></a>
## Purchase Order List

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/purchase-order-list_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Purchase order information
- Refresh Purchase Orders
- Search Criteria

### Purpose

The Purchase Order list displays all Purchase Orders for the current warehouse (see [Switch Warehouse](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Switch+Warehouse) for information on how to change the current warehouse) that are either **Not Yet Received, Received**or **Verified**.

![purchase-order-list-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-list-0.png?width=257&height=443&name=purchase-order-list-0.png)

### Purchase Order Information

![purchase-order-list-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-list-1.png?width=386&height=99&name=purchase-order-list-1.png)

**The information displayed includes:**

- ******Purchase**** Order Status:**this label will display either **NR**(Not Yet Received), **R**(Received) or **V** (Verified) and reflects the current status of the Purchase Order. Please note, that you may also briefly see a Purchase Order Status of **A**(Allocated) while a finished Purchase Order is being uploaded to the server.
- **Customer Name:**name of the customer the Purchase Order belongs to.
- **Arrival Time:**the time the Purchase Order arrived.
- ******Purchase**** Order Id**
- Reference: the Purchase Order reference

### Refresh Purchase Orders

There are two ways to refresh the Purchase Order List from the server. Either swipe down while viewing the list (iOS only) or use the **Refresh Purchase Orders** button in the menu.

**Swipe Down to Refresh (iOS only)**

![purchase-order-list-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-list-2.png?width=255&height=440&name=purchase-order-list-2.png)

**Menu Option to Refresh**

![purchase-order-list-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-list-3.png?width=284&height=487&name=purchase-order-list-3.png)

**Search Purchase Orders**
To search the Purchase Order List, tap onto the search bar on top of the list and start typing your search term such as a Purchase Order's reference. The list will be immediately filtered to match your search text and matching text within the list rows will be highlighted in red.

![purchase-order-list-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-list-4.png?width=291&height=502&name=purchase-order-list-4.png)

### Search Criteria

Currently, the list search allows you to search for matching text in:

- Purchase Order Customer Name
- Purchase Order Id
- Purchase Order Reference

**Separate By Customer Name**

Should you prefer the Purchase Order List to be separated into sections by customer name, simply select the Filter button in the top navigation bar and activate the "Split by customer names" option and select the "Apply" button to confirm your selection. You will notice the list has now been updated to reflect your setting.

**Without Splitting By Customer Name**

![purchase-order-list-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-list-5.png?width=294&height=504&name=purchase-order-list-5.png)

**Filter View**
![purchase-order-list-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-list-6.png?width=300&height=517&name=purchase-order-list-6.png)
**With Splitting By Customer**

![purchase-order-list-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-list-7.png?width=278&height=480&name=purchase-order-list-7.png)

---

<a id="purchase-order-product-allocate-list"></a>
## Purchase Order Product Allocate List

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/purchase-order-product-allocate-list_

### Table of Contents

- iOS
- Android
- Product information
- Filter product list
- Allocate product to location
- Show / hide allocated products

For information on how to connect a Bluetooth barcode scanner to your iOS device please check out this article:

Connect a Bluetooth Barcode Scanner

### iOS

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-0.png?width=282&height=502&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-0.png)

### Android

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-1.png?width=290&height=644&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-1.png)

### Product Information

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-2.png?width=368&height=259&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-2.png)

The information displayed includes:

- **Allocation Status:**If the product has not yet been allocated to a location, a red marker featuring a question mark will be displayed here, otherwise a green tick to confirm the product has been allocated to a location.
- **Warehouse Location:**Location of the product within the warehouse (if available, "Unallocated" otherwise).
- **Product Name**
- **Product Quantity**
- **Expiry Date & Custom Fields:**Based on the tenant settings, plus expiry date for the product. More information on setting up Custom Fields: [Enabling Custom Product Fields (Batch, Serial etc)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Setting+up+Custom+Fields+for+Purchase+Order+Products)

### Filter Product List

Please see [Purchase Order Product Verify List - Filter Product List](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Purchase+Order+Product+Verify+List#PurchaseOrderProductVerifyList-FilterProductList) for more information on filtering the product list.

The product list can be filtered by either warehouse location or product barcode. By default, the filter labels on top of the product list will display **All Locations**and **All Products**.

If the Purchase Order is **Received**or **Verified**and filters are applied that lead to only one product fitting the filter criteria, the Location Allocation view will be automatically displayed (see "*Allocate Product To Location*" below for more information).

### Allocate Product To Location

Scanning a location barcode will automatically filter the Allocate list for products matching the code. In the case where only the product matches the filter, the Location Allocation will be automatically displayed.

Alternatively, selecting any product from the Allocate list will open the Location Allocation view and allow you to allocate the product to a location.

Within the Location Allocation view, you can either search for a location by typing into the search field or scan a location barcode. After selecting a location from the search results choose "Locate". If you scanned a location barcode, the view will automatically dismiss and allocate the product to this location. The product list will be updated accordingly and display the new location value for the product.

**Searching for location in Location Allocation View (iOS)**

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-3.png?width=290&height=516&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-3.png)
**Selected Search Result (iOS)**

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-4.png?width=312&height=555&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-4.png)

**Searching for location in Location Allocation View (Android)**

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-5.png?width=331&height=736&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-5.png)

**Selected search result (Android)**

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-6.png?width=296&height=658&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-6.png)

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-7.png?width=302&height=537&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-7.png)

### Show / Hide Allocated Products

By default allocated products are being displayed in the product list. You may prefer to hide them from the list. To do so, select the filter button (filter icon) from the top navigation bar and de-select 'Show completed products' so that the sliding scale is no longer green.

**Showing Allocated Products (iOS)**

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-8.png?width=344&height=612&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-8.png)
**How to Hide Allocated Products (iOS)**

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-9.png?width=315&height=560&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-9.png)
**Hiding Allocated Products (iOS)**

![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-10.png?width=321&height=713&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-10.png)
**Showing Allocated Products (Android)**
![purchase-order-product-allocate-list-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-allocate-list-11.png?width=331&height=736&name=purchase-order-product-allocate-list-11.png)

<a id="purchase-order-product-list"></a>
## Purchase Order Product List

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/purchase-order-product-list_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Verify tab
- Locate tab

### Purpose

To view a Purchase Order's list of products simply select the Purchase Order from the Purchase Orders list. You will be automatically redirected to the **Verify** list of Purchase Order Products if the Purchase Order is in **Not Yet Received**or **Received**state, or the **Locate**tab if the Purchase Order is in **Verified** state.

The **Locate** tab will become unavailable once the Purchase Order has been verified.

### Verify Tab

![purchase-order-product-list-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-list-0.png?width=248&height=503&name=purchase-order-product-list-0.png)

### Locate Tab

![purchase-order-product-list-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-list-1.png?width=274&height=551&name=purchase-order-product-list-1.png)

<a id="purchase-order-product-verify-list"></a>
## Purchase Order Product Verify List

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/purchase-order-product-verify-list_

### Table of Contents

- Product information
- Confirmed product and unconfirmed product quantities
- Filter product list
- Scan-add product quantities
- Confirm unsaved product quantities
- Show / hide completed products
- Showing completed products
- Hiding completed products

For information on how to connect a Bluetooth barcode scanner to your iOS device please check out this article: [Connect a Bluetooth Barcode Scanner](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Connect+a+Bluetooth+Barcode+Scanner)

### Product information

![purchase-order-product-verify-list-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-0.png?width=330&height=289&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-0.png)

The information displayed includes:

- **Saved Quantity / Expected Quantity:**The number of currently saved products and the number of total expected products are displayed here. Once the expected quantity has been reached for a product, a green tick will be displayed.
- Warehouse Location: Location of the product within the warehouse (if available, "Unallocated" otherwise)
- Product Name ​​​

### Confirmed Product and Unconfirmed Product Quantities

| **Confirmed Product** | **Unconfirmed Product Quantities** |
| --- | --- |
| Once a product has been confirmed it will be highlighted in green (see *"Confirm Unsaved Product Quantities"*below, and *"Confirmation View"*in [Receiving on the mobile app (scan putaway)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=530058312) for more information on how to confirm a product). Additionally, as the quantity has been confirmed and therefore now equals the expected quantity, a green tick will be displayed instead of the saved/expected progress circle. ![purchase-order-product-verify-list-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-1.png?width=670&height=213&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-1.png) | In the case where product quantities have been added via Scan Adding (see "*Scan-Add Product Quantities*" below for more information), a red exclamation mark will be displayed for the affected product. Tapping this will offer the option to confirm the product (see "*Confirm Unsaved Product Quantities"*below for more information).         ![purchase-order-product-verify-list-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-2.png?width=670&height=222&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-2.png) |

### Filter Product List

The product list can be filtered by either warehouse location or product barcode. By default, the filter labels on top of the product list will display **All Locations**and **All Products**.

![purchase-order-product-verify-list-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-3.png?width=323&height=557&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-3.png)
To start filtering, either scan a product or location barcode using a Bluetooth barcode scanner connected to the iOS device you're running the CartonCloud mobile app on or add a barcode manually, by tapping either the "All Locations" or "All Products" field. The app will then automatically search for matching products and warehouse locations and filter the list accordingly.

**Manual Barcode Entry**

![purchase-order-product-verify-list-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-4.png?width=323&height=558&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-4.png)
**List Filtered by Warehouse Locations (iOS)**

![purchase-order-product-verify-list-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-5.png?width=321&height=554&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-5.png)

If the Purchase Order is already Received and filters are applied that lead to only one product fitting the filter criteria, Scan-Adding will be automatically applied (see "*Scan-Add Product Quantitie*s" below for more information).

### Scan-Add Product Quantities

Scanning a Product Unit Of Measure barcode will automatically filter the Verify list for products matching the code. In the case where only product matches the filter, the scanned Unit Of Measure will be automatically added to the product quantity. As this quantity will not automatically be confirmed, a red exclamation mark is displayed until the quantity is confirmed (see "*Confirm Unsaved Product Quantities"*below for more information).

If there are multiple products matching the scanned barcode, an information bar will be displayed below the filters, giving more details about what Unit Of Measure has been scanned. This Unit Of Measure can then be added to any of the filtered products by tapping that product in the list.

**Only 1 matching product**

![purchase-order-product-verify-list-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-6.png?width=295&height=656&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-6.png)
**Multiple matching products**

![purchase-order-product-verify-list-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-7.png?width=291&height=646&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-7.png)
**Product quantity added after tapping a product**

![purchase-order-product-verify-list-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-8.png?width=302&height=671&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-8.png)

### Confirm Unsaved Product Quantities

Unsaved product quantities, highlighted in the list with a red exclamation mark for the affected product, can be confirmed by tapping the exclamation mark and selecting "**Confirm**" in the displayed alert. Consequently, the exclamation mark will no longer be shown and the product will be highlighted green as it has now been confirmed.

![purchase-order-product-verify-list-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-9.png?width=309&height=687&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-9.png)

### Show / Hide Completed Products

By default, completed products are displayed in the product list. If you would prefer to hide the completed products you can use the filter function (filter icon) in the top right corner of the navigation bar. Select the filter icon and then click the sliding bar called 'Show completed products' so that it is no longer green (for iOs) or blue (for Android). This will then remove all completed products from the product list.

### Showing Completed Products

### ![purchase-order-product-verify-list-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-10.png?width=296&height=658&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-10.png)

### ![purchase-order-product-verify-list-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-11.png?width=304&height=675&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-11.png)

**Hiding Completed Products**

![purchase-order-product-verify-list-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-12.png?width=319&height=709&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-12.png)
![purchase-order-product-verify-list-13](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/purchase-order-product-verify-list-13.png?width=299&height=664&name=purchase-order-product-verify-list-13.png)

- **Product Quantity**
- **Expiry Date & Custom Fields:**Based on the tenant settings, plus expiry date for the product. More information on setting up Custom Fields: [Enabling Custom Purchase Order Product Fields (Batch, Serial etc)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Setting+up+Custom+Fields+for+Purchase+Order+Products)

<a id="putaway-process"></a>
## Putaway process

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/putaway-process_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Putaway process
- Video tutorial

### Purpose

How to use the Putaway mode on the mobile app to receive, verify and locate a Purchase Order.

### Putaway process

- Navigate to the Putaway mode (see Accessing Putaway Mode for more information).

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Feb-27-2026-03-56-42-7336-AM.png?width=321&height=655&name=image-png-Feb-27-2026-03-56-42-7336-AM.png)

- Select the relevant Purchase Order you wish to receive in.
- Click Receive .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-48-06-8983-PM.png?width=283&height=601&name=image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-48-06-8983-PM.png)

- Tap the product.
- If the quantity is greater than the product's pallet quantity (defined against the product) you can split the product into pallet quantities. It is suggested that you do split the quantity into pallet quantities as this will make it easier when you need to move and locate the pallet.
- Click the three dot action icon in the top right and click Bulk Split .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-49-51-7254-PM.png?width=283&height=606&name=image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-49-51-7254-PM.png)

- The pallet conversion quantity will show, this is the quantity it will be split by. If you wish to change it, edit the number. Tap Bulk Split . You will now see individual lines of product for each pallet.
- Select a product to verify.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-52-28-0716-PM.png?width=283&height=600&name=image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-52-28-0716-PM.png)

- If you have Custom Fields set up and need to record these references upon receiving the stock, see [Purchase Order (inbound) unique reference scanning](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Purchase-Order-inbound-unique-reference-scanning) for how to steps.
- From this screen, you can then choose to assign a pallet barcode and/or print a pallet label by clicking the pallet icon. For more information please see Pallet Handling .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-53-57-5109-PM.png?width=283&height=603&name=image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-53-57-5109-PM.png)

- Confirm the product quantities by scanning the Product Unit of Measure barcode or typing in the confirmed product quantities.
- Once product quantities are confirmed, tap Confirm .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-55-06-7796-PM.png?width=283&height=615&name=image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-55-06-7796-PM.png)

- Repeat this process for the other lines of stock.
- Once you have completed each line, select Verify.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-57-29-0058-PM.png?width=283&height=613&name=image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-57-29-0058-PM.png)

- You can now locate the stock, select a line to locate.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-58-40-8991-PM.png?width=283&height=610&name=image-png-Apr-17-2026-04-58-40-8991-PM.png)

- Scan the warehouse location (or type the location) and click Locate.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Apr-17-2026-05-01-21-5796-PM.png?width=283&height=605&name=image-png-Apr-17-2026-05-01-21-5796-PM.png)

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Apr-17-2026-05-02-40-9663-PM.png?width=283&height=594&name=image-png-Apr-17-2026-05-02-40-9663-PM.png)

### Video Tutorial

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/XBQ8pokhtL4?list=PLxs2KBNumIq7mCiWplTg2emZumXyHN2jD

<a id="rapid-sale-order-packing"></a>
## Rapid Sale Order Packing

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/rapid-sale-order-packing_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- What is Rapid Sale Order Packing?
- Considerations when using Rapid Sale Order Packing
- How to enable/disable Rapid Sale Order Packing

### Purpose

The CartonCloud mobile app allows the user to enable **Rapid Sale Order Packing**to increase the speed of confirming products while packing products.

### What is Rapid Sale Order Packing?

With Rapid Sale Order Packing enabled, on confirming a product line the changes are not immediately submitted to the server but initially stored locally. Consequently, the Sale Order is not being reloaded every time a product line is being confirmed, reducing the wait time between confirmations. This allows packers to dramatically increase the speed of packing.

The Sale Order progress only gets submitted to the server when either selecting **Finish Packing**or exiting the Sale Order through the back button and selecting **Save Packing Progress**.

### Considerations when using Rapid Sale Order Packing

Because the packing progress is only submitted to the server when Finishing Packing (or Saving Packing Progress on exiting the Sale Order view) when Rapid Packing is enabled, issues may arise when multiple packers are working on the same order simultaneously. In this circumstance, to avoid double-picking and synchronisation issues, we recommend disabling Rapid Packing.

**Comparison between regular Sale Order Packing and Rapid Sale Order Packing:**

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/2PrVatJ7kDs

### How to enable/disable Rapid Sale Order Packing

**Please ensure you are in the Picking mode before accessing the settings.**

Rapid Packing is enabled by default but can easily be disabled in the Settings.

- Navigate to Settings, select the menu navigation icon and select Settings .

![rapid-sale-order-packing-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/rapid-sale-order-packing-0.png?width=273&height=556&name=rapid-sale-order-packing-0.png)

- Slide the Enable Rapid Sale Order Packing toggle to green to enable it and the other way to disable it.

![rapid-sale-order-packing-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/rapid-sale-order-packing-1.png?width=291&height=581&name=rapid-sale-order-packing-1.png)

---

<a id="reallocation"></a>
## Reallocation

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/reallocation_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Prerequisites
- How to turn on sale order reallocation
- Use cases
- Benefits
- Limitations
- Criteria for reallocation
- How to use Sale Order Reallocation
- Video Guides

### Purpose

Reallocation allows the picker to pick and scan an alternative POP label or unique POP Custom Field, such as a serial number, and CartonCloud will automatically switch which stock is allocated to the order. Reallocation can be configured at both the Customer and Product level.

### Prerequisites

- Reallocation works best when utilising a scan picking
- You will need to turn on Sale Order Reallocation in the Customer and/or Product settings.

### How to turn on Sale Order reallocation

Please note, the below settings can be updated in bulk using export [customer](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/WMS-Settings-Bulk-Export-Import) and [product](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Exporting-and-Importing-Products) settings.

**Customer Settings (this can be overridden at the product level via the product settings)**

- Navigate to the Customer Settings, Contacts>Customers.
- Click on the relevant Customer and click Edit.
- Select the Warehouse Management > Product tab.
- Update the Enable Product Reallocation field to one of the below Yes options to enable Reallocation:
  - Yes - Optional Confirmation
  - Yes - Confirmation Required : Packers must complete the reallocation step before continuing in the mobile picking workflow.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Mar-16-2026-04-25-54-3186-AM.png?width=3392&height=1646&name=image-png-Mar-16-2026-04-25-54-3186-AM.png)

- You will then need to select a scannable field. This is the field you will scan to confirm what stock you have picked for the order. To have a field appear in the drop-down it needs to be marked as unique against the Purchase Order Product Custom Field.

![reallocation-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/reallocation-1.png?width=438&height=213&name=reallocation-1.png)

**Product Settings*(if you wish to override the settings configured at the Customer level)***

- Navigate to the Product settings, Warehouse > Products .
- Select the relevant Product and click Edit .
- Scroll down to the bottom of the screen to Reallocation and select one of the following options:
  - As per customer default setting : will apply the same setting applied at the customer settings level
  - No : reallocation will not be enabled for this product
  - Yes - Optional Confirmation: reallocation will be enabled for this product
  - Yes - Confirmation Required : packers must complete the reallocation step before continuing in the mobile picking workflow.
- Click Save .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Mar-16-2026-04-32-29-8384-AM.png?width=627&height=294&name=image-png-Mar-16-2026-04-32-29-8384-AM.png)

### Use Cases

- If you have a line of pallets stacked behind each other in multiple pallet bulk locations, they are all the same product code and custom fields, and CartonCloud allocates a POP ID which is located in the 5th line; you will want to instruct CartonCloud that you would like to pick from the front of the pallet. By reallocating the POP ID to one that is at the front of the pallet allows you to more easily and efficiently pick the order without sacrificing the data integrity.
- If you have a carton of mobile phones, all the same product code with unique serial numbers for each unit of stock, and you wish to pick the first unit from the carton, you can do so by scanning the serial number of the first item you pick and the correct POP ID will be reallocated to the order.

### Benefits

- Creates more flexibility in the picking process dependent on where the stock is located
- Increase efficiency in the picking process by allowing pickers to pick the most efficient item from the location
- Maintains data integrity by being able to update data relating to what unit was picked
- Allows stock with serials (or similar unique custom fields) to be tracked from arrival to dispatch without forcing pickers to specifically pick an assigned serial.

### Limitations

**Situations where reallocation will not be possible:**

- Incorrect barcode scanned (not a valid serial or POP barcode)
- POP label scanned is already allocated to another order and there is insufficient suitable stock to replace the POP being used
- Scanned POP not available due to the status of the Purchase Order
- Scanned POP does not meet the selection criteria of the Sales order item (i.e. Expiry date is not in the valid range)
- Scanned POP not available due to the status of the POPs not matching (i.e a POP with the status "Quarantine" can not replace a POP with the status "OK")
- Stock allocated to the order has “Prevent Changes” enabled, effectively locking the designated stock to the order.
- Sale Orders that are assigned to a Wave Pick

### Criteria for Reallocation

- Same Product
- Same Sales Order product criteria (e.g expiry date)

### How to use Sale Order Reallocation

If reallocation is available for the order you are picking (meets the above criteria) a Reallocation available text will appear on the Verify Products page. Depending on your Customer Settings and what your scannable field is, the reallocation arrows will appear against that field and you will need to scan that field on the stock you wish to pick.

**You can also toggle on ‘Scan Pallet or Location’ during reallocation.** This is especially helpful when the POP being scanned does not match every SOP’s criteria. Rather than requiring multiple attempts, CartonCloud can reallocate the scanned POP to one of the compatible SOPs in the list. This significantly improves efficiency in complex stock scenarios.

![reallocation-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/reallocation-3.png?width=293&height=593&name=reallocation-3.png)

#### **Reallocation process:**

- Select the item you wish to pick.

![reallocation-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/reallocation-4.png?width=304&height=613&name=reallocation-4.png)

Grouping for serial number (unique reference) verify/capture

Note, if an order contains multiple items with similar attributes, they will be grouped together in the items list in order to allow for more efficient picking. The criteria which items must meet in order to be grouped together is as follows:

- Same product
- Same location(Unallocated included)
- Same Expiry date (Blank included)
- A single POP Custom Field set up with Serial number Capture or Verify as a required step
- Same non-unique custom field values
  - Items with a value for a custom field must all have the same value in order to be grouped together.
  - Items without a value for a custom field must all have the no value in order to be grouped together.
- No additional custom fields that require a value to be captured
- If you can see the Reallocation available text you will have the option to perform reallocation.  This means you don't have to scan the serial number listed against the Sale Order. If another item is closer / easier to access and meets the product requirements you can scan that serial number instead and it will update the serial number against the order.

![reallocation-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/reallocation-5.png?width=284&height=573&name=reallocation-5.png)

- In this example, the serial number that was scanned was not the serial number listed against the Sale Order, however, because it met the product requirements it was verified and the serial number was updated against the Sale Order.
- Once you select the stock to be picked for the order CartonCloud will record the item that was picked for the order and it will no longer be available for other orders.

Note, if you wish to scan a pallet barcode or location barcode slide the Scan Pallet or Location Barcode toggle. This is useful when the pallet / location has the stock you wish to verify and allows you to verify items in bulk. For example, instead of having to scan both serial numbers, you could scan the pallet and both lines of of stock will be marked as verified.

- Once all lines of product have a serial number recorded against it click **Confirm.**

![reallocation-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/reallocation-6.png?width=294&height=589&name=reallocation-6.png)

- Click Complete Task.

![reallocation-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/reallocation-7.png?width=305&height=609&name=reallocation-7.png)

- Record any Adhoc Charges if required.

![reallocation-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/reallocation-8.png?width=313&height=627&name=reallocation-8.png)

### Video Guides

**Reallocation Explained**

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/4s0OmgB7UeM

**Reallocation with serialised product**

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/WhHAP-YLz6U

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/FVhXJQBcxuw

**Reallocation with non serialised product**

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/UkfWk6FWsT0

<a id="recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app"></a>
## Recording Sale Order Adhoc Charges (on the mobile app)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to record Adhoc Charges

### Purpose

To record Adhoc Charges against the Sale Order when completing the picking and packing of an order.

### How to record Adhoc Charges

You can record Adhoc Charges at any time throughout the picking and packing process. You will also be prompted upon completing a Sale Order pack to record Adhoc Charges if required.

To record Adhoc Charges:

- Select the three dot icon in the top right corner.

![recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app-0.png?width=314&height=633&name=recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app-0.png)

- Select Ad hoc Charges .

![recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app-1.png?width=296&height=595&name=recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app-1.png)

- Enter the charge value against the relevant Ad hoc Charges.
- Click Save .

![recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app-2.png?width=291&height=585&name=recording-sale-order-adhoc-charges-on-the-mobile-app-2.png)
