<a id="scan-allocate-move"></a>
## Scan Allocate / Move

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/scan-allocate-move_

### Table of Contents

- Moving stock with the web app and a scanner
- Moving stock with the web app and no scanner

### Moving stock with the web app and a scanner

Navigate to the Scan-Move Purchase Order Products page by selecting Warehouse in the main menu followed by Scan Allocate / Move.

Ensure the scanner you are using is paired to the computer and turned on.

Now select the only field on the page to enter data into. Scan the location you are moving from or the pallet label.

Next, scan the location you are moving the stock to.

A success status will appear provided the move occurred without fault.

![scan-allocate-move-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Knowledge%20Base%20Import/scan-allocate-move-0-2.png?width=670&height=329&name=scan-allocate-move-0-2.png)

### Moving stock with the web app and no scanner

This is the same process as above when moving stock with the Web App and a Scanner. However, instead, you will need to:

- Manually type the Purchase Order Product, Warehouse Location or Pallet Label to move from into the box and press enter
- Then type in the target locations and press enter

A success status should appear provided the move occurred without fault.

---

<a id="scan-allocation"></a>
## Scan Allocation

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/scan-allocation_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Scan modes
  - Allocate - simple
  - Allocate - advanced
  - Unallocate from run sheet
- How to perform scan allocation
- What is updated when you scan allocate using the allocated modes
- Troubleshooting scanning issues

### Purpose

Scan allocation enables allocation of consignments to drivers by simply scanning a Consignment Barcode, Con-Note QR code, or [QR Code Overlay for Customer Invoices](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/QR+Code+Overlay+for+Consignment+Invoices). You can also use Consignment Reference numbers for allocation which is useful if you have Invoice paperwork from your clients that contains a barcode - simply scan the barcode on their invoice and the job will be allocated.

Barcode numbers and reference numbers can also be keyed into the system using the keyboard - simply press enter after each reference.

### Scan modes

The scan mode you select will define what action occurs within CartonCloud upon a consignment being scanned. Each scan mode is explained in further detail below.

#### **Allocate - Simple**

Allows you to allocate the consignment to a driver and delivery run on a selected date. This will cause a run sheet to be created OR it will allocate the consignment to an already created run sheet if one exists for that driver, on that run, for that date.

If a driver is already allocated prior to scanning, then that allocation will be overridden.

#### **Allocate - Advanced**

Allows you to allocate the consignment to a run sheet and driver, and either leave it on the already selected delivery run (based on consignment address) or override the delivery run. Optionally, the carrier can also be selected overridden.

If a driver is already allocated prior to scanning, then that allocation will be overridden.

#### **Unallocate from Run Sheet**

Allows you to unallocate the scanned consignment from a run sheet or will notify you if the consignment is already not on a run sheet. Mark into Warehouse. Allows you to the scanned consignment from the status of "Awaiting Pickup" to "In Warehouse".

### How to perform Scan-Allocation

- To access scan allocation, select **Transport** in the main menu > **Scan Allocation**.
- Once on scan allocation page, begin by selecting one of the scan modes explained above.
- Depending on the scan mode you are using, fill the required fields, such as Driver, Delivery Run, Date

No fields are required to be filled for the scan modes "Unallocate from Run sheet" or "Mark into Warehouse".

- Select the box under Scan Consignment Barcode, then scan the consignment you wish to allocate.
- The allocation process will be performed immediately, with the result of the allocation displaying as a line on the screen.
- The status column will provide details on what was updated within the consignment.

### What's updated when you Scan Allocate using the Allocate modes

**Consignment**

| **Scenario** | **Changes** |
| --- | --- |
| The consignment was already delivered | No changes are made and a status message will appear to explain why. |
| The consignment was in "In Warehouse" status | Consignment is updated to "In Transit (Warehouse → Delivery)" status. |

**Run Sheet**

| **Scenario** | **Changes** |
| --- | --- |
| If an existing Run Sheet for the Driver / Delivery Run / Date can be found | Consignment is allocated to the existing run sheet. |
| If no existing Run Sheet for the Driver / Delivery Run / Date | A new Run Sheet is created. |

### Troubleshooting Scanning Issues

To debug issues with scanning, please see: [Troubleshooting Barcode Scanner (Web Application)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/troubleshooting-barcode-scanner-web-application)

---

<a id="scan-move"></a>
## Scan Move

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/scan-move_

The Scan Move functionality within the CartonCloud iOS and Android application allow users to move items into a new warehouse location by either manually entering the product label and warehouse location barcodes, or scanning them using a barcode scanner.

**Scan Move 2.0**

Scan Move 2.0 is the latest update for Scan Move and includes additional Scan Move features, such as the [Move Cart](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Scan-Move-2-0-Move-Cart).

Scan Move 2.0 allows you to scan or manually enter a warehouse location barcode and select specific stock within that location to be moved (as opposed to moving all stock within the location). You can also scan or manually enter a Purchase Order Product (POP) barcode and move only a select quantity of that product (rather than the entire POP record).

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/a89yyZYWiiM

For more information see [**Scan Move 2.0 (Basic Move)**](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Scan-Move-2-0-Basic-Move)and**[Scan Move 2.0 (Move Cart)](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Scan-Move-2-0-Move-Cart).**

---

<a id="scan-move-2-0-basic-move"></a>
## Scan Move 2.0 (Basic Move)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/scan-move-2-0-basic-move_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Use cases/benefits
- Prerequisites
- How to use scan move
- Scan move history ​​​​​​​

### Purpose

Scan Move 2.0 allows you to easily move stock from one location to another by either scanning or manually entering a label barcode. It provides details of every record being included in the move and also allows you to view all records that are currently recorded against a particular location or pallet.

In addition, there is a confirmation step when scanning the new location by either double scanning the new location barcode or selecting move on the screen to confirm the move.

### Use cases/benefits

- Provides visibility and details on what is being moved.
- Can be used to check and confirm what stock is associated with the scanned or entered label barcode.

Increased accuracy when using the confirmation step (double scanning the new location barcode or selecting Move on the screen to confirm the move).

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/a89yyZYWiiM

### ​​​​​​​Prerequisites

- You will need to be using a Mobile device (either a Mobile Phone (iOS or Android) or a  [Mobile Computer](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Scanning-Devices)) to access Scan Move.
- If you are using a Bluetooth Barcode Scanner , ensure it is connected and working correctly. See Connect a Bluetooth Barcode Scanner for more information.

### ​​​​​​​How to use scan move

There are two ways you can utilise Scan Move, either by using a **barcode scanner** or **manually** using the device's keyboard.

- Navigate to the Scan Move mode on your mobile device.
- The primary Scan Move screen will be displayed.

![scan-move-2-0-basic-move-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-basic-move-0.png?width=269&height=466&name=scan-move-2-0-basic-move-0.png)

- Scan or manually enter the location, Purchase Order Product (POP) ID, unique reference, Unit of Measure (UOM) barcode or pallet ID barcode into the Scan POP/Location Barcode field at the top of the application.
- You will now be able to view all stock associated with that barcode.

**Scanning a warehouse location barcode:**

![scan-move-2-0-basic-move-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-basic-move-1.png?width=307&height=530&name=scan-move-2-0-basic-move-1.png)

**Scanning a POP ID barcode:**

![scan-move-2-0-basic-move-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-basic-move-2.png?width=327&height=565&name=scan-move-2-0-basic-move-2.png)

- Scan or manually enter the new location barcode into the To Location field at the bottom of the application.

To cancel the location, click the **X** icon in the location field.

- Select Move or scan the location barcode a second time to confirm the move. All Purchase Order Product IDs that are displayed will be moved to the new location.
- You will receive a success message that the stock has been moved to the new location.

To improve efficiency during the Scan Move process, scan the new location twice to confirm the move (if you are using a barcode scanner).

![scan-move-2-0-basic-move-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-basic-move-3.png?width=294&height=509&name=scan-move-2-0-basic-move-3.png)

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/CoH9KFBI8Kw

### Scan Move History

To access Scan Move history select the clock icon in the top left of the screen. You will be able to view all successful and unsuccessful Scan Move attempts.

Please note, Scan Move history is specific to the device being used. Even if the moves are completed within the same warehouse they will only show on the devices that the move was performed on.

Scan Move history will show the past 30 days of movements (specific to that device).

![scan-move-2-0-basic-move-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-basic-move-5.png?width=246&height=427&name=scan-move-2-0-basic-move-5.png)

**Successful move:**

![scan-move-2-0-basic-move-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-basic-move-6.png?width=295&height=525&name=scan-move-2-0-basic-move-6.png)
**Unsuccessful move:**

![scan-move-2-0-basic-move-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-basic-move-7.png?width=305&height=527&name=scan-move-2-0-basic-move-7.png)

![scan-move-2-0-basic-move-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-basic-move-8.png?width=292&height=519&name=scan-move-2-0-basic-move-8.png)

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/yv0Tngs56QI

---

<a id="scan-move-2-0-move-cart"></a>
## Scan Move 2.0 (Move Cart)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/scan-move-2-0-move-cart_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Use cases
- Benefits
- Prerequisites
- How to use the Move Cart

### Purpose

The Move Cart allows you to scan or manually enter a warehouse location barcode and select specific stock within that location to be moved (as opposed to moving all stock within the location). You can also scan or manually enter a Purchase Order Product (POP) barcode and move only a select quantity of that product (rather than the entire POP record).

The Move Cart is designed in a way that allows you to collect stock as you go, moving from location to location in the warehouse. You can then either scan the entire cart or part of it into a single destination location, or multiple destination locations.

### Use cases

- Replenishing pick face locations.
- Consolidating warehouse locations to make room for new stock.
- Putting away unassigned stock.

### Benefits

- Ability to move multiple products from multiple different locations in one movement.
- Ability to split product quantities and move only partial quantities.
- Ability to print product labels after moving the stock.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/a89yyZYWiiM

### Prerequisites

- You will need to be using a Mobile device (either a Mobile Phone (iOS or Android) or a [Mobile Computer](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Scanning-Devices)) to access Scan Move.
- If you are using a [Bluetooth Barcode Scanner](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Connect-a-Bluetooth-Barcode-Scanner), ensure it is connected and working correctly. See [Connect a Bluetooth Barcode Scanner](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Connect-a-Bluetooth-Barcode-Scanner) for more information.

**It is recommended that you utilise a barcode scanner to maximise the benefits and functionality of the Move Cart.**

### How to use the Move Cart

There are two ways you can utilise the Move Cart, either by using a **barcode scanner** or **manually** using the device's keyboard.

**If you are using a Bluetooth Barcode Scanner and need to use the keyboard on the application, click the button on the scanner twice to enable the keyboard.**

The below outlines the four stages that need to be followed when using the Move Cart:

**1. Enable the Move Cart**

- Once on the **Scan Move**screen, enable the Move Cart by sliding the **Use Move Cart**toggle.

You can enable the Move Cart at any point in time when using Scan Move. This means you can scan a location or Purchase Order Product barcode and then decide to enable the Move Cart.

**2. Add stock to the Move Cart**

- Scan or manually enter the Product Code, Purchase Order Product (POP), Location Barcode or Unit of Measure (UOM) Barcode into the **Scan POP/Location Barcode** field at the top of the application.
- You will now be able to view all stock associated with that barcode and individually select the POPID's you wish to either move or add to the Move Cart.
- To select stock either scan the POPID or select the tick circle next to the POPID.

Note, that even when the Move Cart is enabled you are still able to move stock without using the Move Cart. To do so, simply select the stock you wish to move and then scan the new location barcode in the **To Location** field at the bottom of the application.

**Scanning a location barcode:**

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-1.png?width=304&height=525&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-1.png)

 **Scanning a POPID barcode**

- To adjust the quantity of stock to be moved, select the box containing the POP's quantity.

**Check out the Move Cart -**[Split Quantity video](https://help.cartoncloud.com#MoveCartSplitQuantity) **for more information on how to use the Move Cart to split a POP quantity.**

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-3.png?width=299&height=519&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-3.png)

- Enter the quantity of stock you wish to move and then select **Add to Move Cart**. Or, you can scan the **Unit of Measure barcode** to select the quantity you wish to add to the Move Cart.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-4.png?width=293&height=513&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-4.png)

- If you scan the location again or unselect the POPID you will see the quantity that has been added to the Move Cart.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-5.png?width=306&height=530&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-5.png)

- For the selected stock that you have not changed the quantity of, click the **Add to Move Cart icon**to add stock to the Move Cart.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-6.png?width=324&height=560&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-6.png)

- The stock will now be in the Move Cart and can be viewed by selecting the Move Cart icon in the top right.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-7.png?width=315&height=548&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-7.png)

Until the stock is moved to a location, it will remain in the Move Cart for 30 days.

**To continue adding stock to the Move Cart:**

- Scan/enter a new location or Purchase Order Product barcode in the **Scan Location or Purchase Order Product** field at the top of the application.
- Repeat the above process and select the stock you wish to add to the Move Cart.

Check out the [Move Cart - Multiple Items video](https://help.cartoncloud.com#MoveCartMultipleItems)  for more information on how to use the Move Cart to collect stock from different locations and move them to either one or multiple locations.

**3. Complete the move**

- Select the Move Cart icon.
- From here, you can view all stock currently in the Move Cart and select the stock you wish to move.
- You can either select all stock in the cart at once (by clicking the **Select All** button) or individually select the stock (by selecting the circle next to the POPID).

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-8.png?width=311&height=534&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-8.png)

To remove stock from the Move Cart, select the stock you wish to remove and then click the **Remove** button at the top of the application.

- Once the stock is selected, scan/enter the new location barcode in the **Scan New Location Barcode field** and select **Move**or**scan the new location barcode a second time**to complete the move.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-9.png?width=302&height=522&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-9.png)

- You will receive a success message once the stock has been moved.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-10.png?width=321&height=555&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-10.png)

 **4.Print POP labels (optional)**

Printing a POP label when completing the move

- After successfully moving Purchase Order Products, you will have the option to print the POPID labels.
- If you are moving a part quantity of a POPID the **Requires New Label** icon will be displayed next to the POP and by default, it will be selected. To unselect, simply click the orange tick circle.
- If you are moving a full quantity POPID and would like to print a new label, select the orange circle next to the relevant POPID.
- To edit the number of labels printed, select the blue box next to the relevant POP and type in the number of labels you wish to print.
- Select **Print**.

Check out the [Move Cart - Split Quantity video](https://help.cartoncloud.com#MoveCartSplitQuantity)  for more information on how to print a new label using the Move Cart.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-11.png?width=246&height=425&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-11.png)

For more information regarding printing labels with CartonCloud see [Label Printing](https://help.cartoncloud.com/help/s/article/Label-Printing).

**Re-printing POP Labels**

If you forget to print the POP label when completing the move or the label is damaged you are able to re-print the label.

This functionality is useful if you are moving warehouses and need to re-print labels or you come across a label that is damaged.

**To re-print the POP label:**

- Scan the location in which the Purchase Order Product that you want to re-print the label for is located in.
- Select the relevant Purchase Order Product.
- Select the Print Label button at the top of the screen.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-12.png?width=297&height=528&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-12.png)

**Move Cart - Split Quantity**

The below video steps through how to use the Move Cart to split a product quantity and then print a new label.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/8buR04GWHrQ

**Move Cart - Multiple Items**

The below video steps through how to use the Move Cart when you need to add multiple items from different locations and move them to numerous different locations.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/YF8Fr2sf2Dc

**Move Cart History**

Please note, Move Cart history is specific to the device being used. Even if the moves are completed within the same warehouse they will only show on the devices that the move was performed on.

Scan Move history will show the past 30 days of movements (specific to that device).

**To access the Move Cart history:**

- Select the Move Cart icon in the top right corner.
- Select the clock icon in the left-hand corner.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-14](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-14.png?width=282&height=490&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-14.png)

From this screen, you will be able to view all successful and unsuccessful Scan Move attempts.

**Succesful move:**

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-15](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-15.png?width=324&height=576&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-15.png)

**Unsuccessful move:**

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-16](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-16.png?width=236&height=419&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-16.png)
When an item fails to move an orange clock icon will appear against the POP in the Move Cart. By clicking on the icon you will receive a message as to why the move failed.

![scan-move-2-0-move-cart-18-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scan-move-2-0-move-cart-18-1.png?width=259&height=451&name=scan-move-2-0-move-cart-18-1.png)

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/yv0Tngs56QI

​​​​​​​

---

<a id="scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking"></a>
## Scanning a barcode to verify against a POP Custom Field when Picking

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to enable verify when picking
- Limitations
- How to scan verify when picking a Sale Order
- Mobile app workflow

### Purpose

When you are picking an order on the CartonCloud mobile app, you have the ability to scan a custom field, such as a batch number, to verify that the product is being picked correctly. This ensures greater accuracy when picking items and that the correct product with the correct custom field, for example batch or serial number is being picked for the Sale Order.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/FGZcw2depyQ

### How to enable verify when picking

- You will need to create a Purchase Order Product Custom Field for the relevant field you wish to scan verify on picking. See here for steps on how to create a Purchase Order Product Custom Field.
- When creating the Custom Field you need to ensure the Verify when Picking option is selected.

![scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-0.png?width=670&height=366&name=scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-0.png)

If you are wishing to scan a barcode that contains more than one custom field data in it, for example a GS1 barcode, you will need to set up custom format barcode scanning using Regex Definitions. Please see  [Scanning Custom Format Barcodes.](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Scanning+Custom+Format+Barcodes)

### Limitations

- Multi capture unique reference scanning is not possible when picking. Unique references must be scanned individually.
- If you do not enforce unique values against the Purchase Order Product Custom Field, it will not split to the lowest unit of measure. See Setting up Custom Fields for Purchase Order Products for more information.

### How to scan verify when picking a Sale Order

- From the Task list view, select the Task you need to action (The sale Order you wish to pack).
- Start the Task by clicking Start Task .

![scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-1.png?width=294&height=586&name=scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-1.png)

- Click on the Product you wish to start packing first.

![scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-2.png?width=292&height=582&name=scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-2.png)

- You will then reach the verification page. For Custom Fields you have set to 'Verify when picking' you will need to verify the field here.

![scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-3.png?width=300&height=600&name=scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-3.png)

- Click on the first step to verify the field. You can either verify by scanning or manually typing the field in. To scan, click the scan icon in the bottom right.

**Scan to verify or verify using the keyboard:**

![scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-4.png?width=265&height=537&name=scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-4.png)

- Continue the same process for each verification step.

![scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-5.png?width=247&height=489&name=scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-5.png)

Note: if the Purchase Order Product Custom Field is marked as required, you will not be able to skip the verification step.

![scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-6.png?width=261&height=530&name=scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-6.png)

### Mobile app workflow

The mobile app workflow is as follows

![scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-7.png?width=670&height=57&name=scanning-a-barcode-to-verify-against-a-pop-customer-field-when-picking-verify-when-picking-7.png)

- The Picker selects the sales order they want to pick
- The Picker will identify the location of the first item on the list and then go to that location and scan the location barcode. This will filter the item list to only show items in that location (as there may be more than one)
- The Picker can then scan the barcode containing the Is Verifiable value (i.e. Batch Number) and the Mobile app will confirm that the correct product is being selected by opening the quantity calculator screen where the picker can enter the confirmed quantity picked or scan product unit of measure barcodes to capture the quantity being picked by carton or unit. If the picker scans a barcode that does not contain any values that match the product on the sales order for that location, the item list will become empty. In this case, the picker should clear the filters, scan the location barcode and then search for the correct product in that location.

---

<a id="ship-date-filter"></a>
## Ship Date Filter

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/ship-date-filter_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Filter access
- Filter options
- Applied filter

### Purpose

On the Sale Order task list on the mobile app, user are able to filter the list by the Sale Order's ship date.

The below documentation is only relevant if you are using Picking Legacy. It is highly recommended that you update your app and use the latest Picking mode on the mobile app. See [Accessing Picking Mode](https://help.cartoncloud.com/articles/Knowledge/Accessing-Picking-2-0-Mode) for steps on how to access the latest Picking mode.

### Picking Legacy

### Filter access

To access the ship date filter, select the filter (funnel) button in the top bar while in the Sale Order list.

![ship-date-filter-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/ship-date-filter-0.png?width=298&height=399&name=ship-date-filter-0.png)

Filter options

Once the ship date filter has been activated, a number of options are available.
![ship-date-filter-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/ship-date-filter-1.png?width=260&height=528&name=ship-date-filter-1.png)

**Today**

This filter will show all Sale Orders with a ship date of **up to and including today's date**.

**Tomorrow**

This filter will show all Sale Orders with a ship date of **tomorrow only**.

**Custom**

This filter allows the user to manually select a date range to filter on. Both the selected start and end date are included in the filtered date range.
![ship-date-filter-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/ship-date-filter-2.png?width=284&height=578&name=ship-date-filter-2.png)

### Applied filter

Once a ship date filter has been applied, a banner will be displayed at the top of the sale order list, highlighting the ship date filter that has been applied.

![ship-date-filter-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/ship-date-filter-3.png?width=449&height=179&name=ship-date-filter-3.png)
The applied filter can be cancelled by either tapping the X button on the banner or by once again opening the filter view and either adjusting the filter or switching off the ship date filter altogether.

### ![ship-date-filter-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/ship-date-filter-4.png?width=400&height=253&name=ship-date-filter-4.png)

---

<a id="wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only"></a>
## Split Pick and Pack Wave Pick - Packing (Pack by SKU)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Prerequisites
- Workflow
- How to steps

### Purpose

When completing the packing step of a Split Pick and Pack Wave Pick you can pack orders by the product unit of measure barcode.  This enables a more efficient packing process, especially for e-commerce workflows with most orders being single items.

### Prerequisites

- Product Unit of Measure barcodes are set up against the product.
- The Product Unit of Measure barcodes are available to scan on the items.

### Workflow

A workflow that can assist the picking and packing efficiency for single-item orders is to do the following:

- Create a Split Pick and Pack Wave Pick containing single-item orders to maximise the product cross-over between orders.
- Pick all of the orders in one go.
- Bring the orders back to the packing station and then rather than working through order-by-order, the packer can select an item, scan it and then pack it.
- As each item (Product Unit of Measure barcode) is scanned, this selects an unpacked order from the wave that contains that item. The Sale Order that is selected is based on a priority order of:
  - Sale Order with 1 quantity of the item
  - Sale Order with any quantity of the item
- The packer can then pack and print the shipping label and dispatch the order.

### How to steps

- From the Switch Mode page select Wave Picking.
- Select the relevant Wave Pick.
- Select Pick and complete the picking following the Wave Pick - Picking process.
- Once picking is completed you can move onto the packing stage.

![wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only-0.png?width=268&height=544&name=wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only-0.png)

- Scan the Product Unit of Measure barcode. The Sale Order with that product will be selected and you can complete the pack by selecting Finish Packing .

![wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only-1.png?width=284&height=574&name=wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only-1.png)

- Continue scanning the Product Unit of Measure barcodes to select and pack all of the orders on the Wave.

![wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only-2.png?width=270&height=548&name=wave-pick-packing-split-picks-only-2.png)

---

<a id="splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile"></a>
## Splitting a Purchase Order on Mobile

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Turning on the split purchase order feature
- Split receiving a purchase order
- Extra functionality on the web app

### Purpose

When receiving a Purchase Order, sometimes the quantity received does not match the Purchase Order's initial quantity due to some products being on backorder or the order is being split over multiple shipments. Split receiving will move the unconfirmed products onto a new Purchase Order, enabling Packers to process only what has arrived.

This feature is available for both Packers and Administrators through either the mobile app or the web app. Specifically, this feature is located within the Purchase Order verification stage.

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/5SVXik8nik0

### Turning on the Split Purchase Order Feature

This feature must be turned on for each Customer that you wish to allow the functionality for.

- To turn it on for a Customer, select Contacts in the main menu, followed by Customers in the drop-down menu.
- Now select the Customer you wish to turn the feature on for.
- From the View Customer page, select Edit near the bottom left of the page.
- Navigate to the Warehouse Management tab, followed by the Purchase Order subtab.
- Check the box shown in the image below to allow split receiving.

![splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-0.png?width=670&height=546&name=splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-0.png)

### Split receiving a purchase order

Once the feature has been enabled mobile users can split purchase orders during the putaway process.

To begin, navigate to the Purchase Order you are receiving that you wish to split and mark it is as received.

![splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-1.png?width=268&height=504&name=splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-1.png)

![splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-2.png?width=259&height=487&name=splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-2.png)

**Verification**

Stock is now ready to be verified. To verify a product select it on the screen.

![splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-3.png?width=249&height=469&name=splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-3.png)
In this example, we are going to mark the "Rich Columbia Blend" as verified, as the truck has arrived with the "Wonder of Brazil" pallet missing.

![splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-4.png?width=285&height=535&name=splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-4.png)
Now when verifying the Purchase Order Products on the screen if only a partial amount of stock is selected an option will appear to **Split Receiving**.

If the split receiving button does not appear for you at this stage, then you have not yet turned on split receiving for the customer in their settings. To do so, refer to the [Turning on the split receiving feature](https://help.cartoncloud.com/kb2/mobile-app-support/warehouse-pickers-packers-incoming/scan-putaway/splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile#SplittingaPurchaseOrderonMobile-TurningontheSplitPurchaseOrderFeature).

![splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-5.png?width=292&height=549&name=splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-5.png)
Upon selecting **Split Receiving**a prompt will appear to check if you wish to proceed with the split.

![splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-6.png?width=282&height=530&name=splitting-a-purchase-order-on-mobile-6.png)

To proceed, select **Create.**The split Purchase order will be created.

A message will appear at the bottom of the screen letting you know the Purchase Order ID of the order created by the split.

### Extra Functionality on the Web App

Further functionality surrounding splitting Purchase Orders, specifically for tracking them is available within the web app. For more information please see - [Splitting a Purchase Order](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Splitting+a+Purchase+Order)

---

<a id="switch-warehouse"></a>
## Switch Warehouse

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/switch-warehouse_

Should it be necessary to access Sale Orders or Purchase Orders from multiple warehouses, the current warehouse can be switched by accessing the Settings View within the app.

- Selecting the Settings View.

![switch-warehouse-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/switch-warehouse-0.png?width=242&height=431&name=switch-warehouse-0.png)

- Select the Warehouse Setting.

![switch-warehouse-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/switch-warehouse-1.png?width=269&height=478&name=switch-warehouse-1.png)

- Select the Warehouse.

![switch-warehouse-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/switch-warehouse-2.png?width=270&height=480&name=switch-warehouse-2.png)

---

<a id="task-list"></a>
## Task List

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/task-list_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Task list
- How to assign a task
- How to see who the task is assigned to
- How to take over a task
  - Use cases for take over a task
- Filters
- Sorting
- Grouping
- Searching for orders that are Packed or Dispatched

### Purpose

A unified place for users to see what tasks need to be performed in order of priority.

### Task List

The Task List lists all current tasks.

![task-list-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-0.png?width=287&height=580&name=task-list-0.png)

### How to assign a task

The task will be automatically assigned to you when you click into the order from the Task List.

- From the Task List, select the task you wish to assign yourself.
- Click Start Task .

![task-list-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-1.png?width=236&height=471&name=task-list-1.png)

You can also scan a product barcode to start the task immediately without needing to press 'Start Task'.

### How to see who the Task is assigned to

You can see who is assigned to the task from the initials on the Task List view. If the Task is assigned to you the initials will be blue and if it is for someone else the initials will be grey. In the web app, you can see on the details screen (under specifics) who the task is assigned to. The history will show if the person assigned to the task has changed.

![task-list-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-2.png?width=256&height=517&name=task-list-2.png)

### How to take over a Task

A task must be started by another user to have the option of 'Take Over Task'

#### **Use cases for take over Task:**

- User has been assigned a Task and has started it but is unable to complete packing so another user needs to take over the assigned packer role.
- User started a Task one one device but during the picking process the device loses power, user will be able to grab another device and take over the Task and complete it.

**How to steps**

- Click on the Task you wish to take over.
- Click Take Over Task .

![task-list-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-3.png?width=258&height=521&name=task-list-3.png)

- You will be prompted with a confirmation message, click OK.

![task-list-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-4.png?width=267&height=538&name=task-list-4.png)

- You will now be assigned to the Task.

![task-list-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-5.png?width=278&height=560&name=task-list-5.png)

If the Task is assigned to someone else but they have not yet started the Task then 'Start Task' will appear in orange rather than 'Take Over Task'. You will need to click Start Task to take over the task.

![task-list-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-6.png?width=278&height=558&name=task-list-6.png)

### Filters

You can filter tasks by order status - Awaiting Pick and Pack or Packing in Progress, Ship Date or Assignee.

- Click the filter icon in the top right corner.

![task-list-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-7.png?width=301&height=608&name=task-list-7.png)

![task-list-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-8.png?width=295&height=596&name=task-list-8.png)

Note, when searching the filters selected will not be applied.

![task-list-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-9.png?width=306&height=618&name=task-list-9.png)

### Sorting

- Click the filter icon in the top right corner.

![task-list-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-10.png?width=307&height=620&name=task-list-10.png)

- Select Default or Urgent . Urgent will show the orders marked as Urgent at the top of the list.

![task-list-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-11.png?width=303&height=614&name=task-list-11.png)

### Grouping

- Click the filter icon in the top right corner.

![task-list-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/task-list-12.png?width=312&height=631&name=task-list-12.png)

- Select None or Customer. To group by customer will show orders grouped by the customer in CartonCloud.

### Searching for orders that are Packed or Dispatched

On the list view you can search for orders that are in a Packed or Dispatched status by using the order reference. This allows the user to confirm order details including the delivery address and items picked. In addition, the user can print labels and submit adhoc charges.

---

<a id="sale-orders-tasks"></a>
## Tasks (Sale Orders)

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/sale-orders-tasks_

### Table of Contents

- What is a Task
- Benefits / use cases of tasks
- How to create a Task
- How to access the task picking mode (Sale Order picking)
- Process

### What is a Task

A task is an outbound order (Sale Order) that needs to be packed/actioned on the mobile application. In the future, tasks will also include Replenishments, Stocktakes and inbound orders, enabling the scheduling of multiple warehouse operations which are all actionable from the one screen.

### Benefits / use cases of Tasks

- Easily manage orders that need to be packed.
- Avoid users working on the same order.

### How to create a task

A task will automatically be created when a Sale Order is in “Awaiting Pick and Pack” status.

### How to access tasks (Picking Mode)

- To access Tasks (Sale Orders) you need to use the Picking Mode.
- Navigate to the Switch Mode screen. Select the menu navigation icon on the far left and then click Switch Mode .
- Select Picking .

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Mar-25-2026-03-21-25-4302-PM.png?width=257&height=547&name=image-png-Mar-25-2026-03-21-25-4302-PM.png)

### Process

- Start Task
- Task List
- Details page and items list
- Packing a Sale Order (Quantity Confirmation Page & verify and capture)
- Printing Sale Order labels (on the mobile app)
- Recording Sale Order Adhoc Charges (on the mobile app)

---

<a id="using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move"></a>
## Using pallet labels to move stock via Scan Move

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Use cases
- Prerequisites
- How to move stock via Scan Move with pallet labels
  - To warehouse locations
  - To pallets
- Splitting stock using Scan Move

### Purpose

Once a pallet has a pallet label you have the ability to move the pallet to different warehouse location or combine pallets by simply scanning the pallet barcode and using Scan Move. This allows you to move stock in bulk rather than having to manually scan and move each item on the pallet, you can conduct the move in one action.

### Use cases

- Locating pallets of Purchase Orders into warehouse location during the inbound process
- Moving pallets of stock from one warehouse location to another
- Combining stock from one pallet to another
- Consolidating stock
- Splitting stock onto multiple locations / pallets.

### Prerequisites

- Pallets are labelled with pallet barcodes
- See Pallet Handling for steps on how to print and assign pallet barcodes

### How to move stock via Scan Move with pallet labels

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/1BKXmIX85hs

**To warehouse locations**

To scan move pallets into a warehouse locations (or from an existing warehouse location into another)

- On the mobile app, select Scan Move.

![using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-6.png?width=248&height=494&name=using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-6.png)

- Scan the pallet label.
- Ensure the Use Move Cart option is enabled.
- Click Select All to move everything on the pallet.

![using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-1.png?width=251&height=498&name=using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-1.png)

- Scan the new location (twice to confirm).

![using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-2.png?width=288&height=577&name=using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-2.png)

- The pallet and all stock on it has now been moved to the new location.

**To pallets**

To combine stock from one pallet to another using Scan Move.

- On the mobile app, select Scan Move.

![using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-6.png?width=270&height=538&name=using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-6.png)

- Scan the pallet label.
- Click Select All to select all products on the pallet.

![using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-4.png?width=275&height=545&name=using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-4.png)

- Scan the new pallet label twice.

![using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-5.png?width=309&height=624&name=using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-5.png)

- This has now combined the two pallets into one.
- The old Pallet no longer contains any stock, however, the existing label can still be used if you wish to move stock back onto the pallet.

### Splitting stock using Scan Move

- On the mobile app, select Scan Move .

![using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-6.png?width=280&height=558&name=using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-6.png)

- Scan the pallet label of the pallet you wish to split.

![using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-7.png?width=248&height=492&name=using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-7.png)

- Select the stock on the pallet you wish to move/split and scan the pallet label  you wish to move/split the stock to. Double scan to confirm the move.

![using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-8.png?width=238&height=478&name=using-pallet-labels-to-move-stock-via-scan-move-8.png)

---

<a id="wave-pick-pack"></a>
## Wave Pick - Pack

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/wave-pick-pack_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to steps

### Purpose

How to complete the pack step on a Wave Pick.

### How to steps

- Once you have completed the pick step on a Wave Pick you can begin the Pack step by selecting the Pack option.

![wave-pick-pack-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-pack-0.png?width=295&height=586&name=wave-pick-pack-0.png)

- Tap an order to start packing. Alternatively, if you have Product Unit of Measure barcodes set up you can scan a barcode to start packing and follow this process - Split Pick and Pack Wave Pick - Packing (Pack by SKU) .

![wave-pick-pack-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-pack-1.png?width=318&height=645&name=wave-pick-pack-1.png)

- Tap into a product to confirm the product quantity.

![wave-pick-pack-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-pack-2.png?width=301&height=606&name=wave-pick-pack-2.png)

- Confirm the product quantity using the calculator view or scan to confirm.

![wave-pick-pack-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-pack-3.png?width=322&height=648&name=wave-pick-pack-3.png)

- Once confirmed tap Complete.

![wave-pick-pack-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-pack-4.png?width=310&height=624&name=wave-pick-pack-4.png)

- Repeat this process for each product until all are confirmed. Tap Finish Packing .

![wave-pick-pack-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-pack-5.png?width=308&height=624&name=wave-pick-pack-5.png)

- Record any Adhoc Charges.

![wave-pick-pack-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-pack-6.png?width=314&height=663&name=wave-pick-pack-6.png)

- Repeat this process for each order until all orders on the Wave Pick are packed.

![wave-pick-pack-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-pack-7.png?width=305&height=618&name=wave-pick-pack-7.png)

---

<a id="wave-pick-picking"></a>
## Wave Pick - Picking

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/wave-pick-picking_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to steps

### Purpose

How to complete the pick step on a Wave Pick on the mobile app.

### How to steps

- Select the Wave Pick you wish to complete.
- Click the Pick Mode.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-27-2025-02-46-32-5864-AM.jpeg?width=300&height=605&name=image-png-Nov-27-2025-02-46-32-5864-AM.jpeg)

- From here you can choose to pick via Location or Product.

![wave-pick-picking-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-1.png?width=296&height=593&name=wave-pick-picking-1.png)

**To pick via Location:**

- Select Locations from the top selection bar.

![wave-pick-picking-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-2.png?width=302&height=605&name=wave-pick-picking-2.png)

- Select a location to pick from.
- Tap into a product line for the selected location to pick it.

![wave-pick-picking-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-3.png?width=315&height=636&name=wave-pick-picking-3.png)

- Use the calculator to confirm the quantity or scan to confirm.

![wave-pick-picking-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-4.png?width=324&height=651&name=wave-pick-picking-4.png)

- Tap Complete .

![wave-pick-picking-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-5.png?width=283&height=571&name=wave-pick-picking-5.png)

- Tap Complete Pick once all products for that location have been picked.

![wave-pick-picking-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-6.png?width=274&height=550&name=wave-pick-picking-6.png)

![wave-pick-picking-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-7.png?width=288&height=582&name=wave-pick-picking-7.png)

- Complete the steps for each location.

**To pick via Product:**

- Select the Products option from the top selection bar.
- Click into the product you wish to pick first.

![wave-pick-picking-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-8.png?width=303&height=608&name=wave-pick-picking-8.png)

- Confirm the quantity using the calculator view or scan to confirm.

![wave-pick-picking-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-9.png?width=306&height=615&name=wave-pick-picking-9.png)

- Tap Complete to complete the pick.

![wave-pick-picking-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-10.png?width=320&height=646&name=wave-pick-picking-10.png)

- Complete the steps for each product line.

![wave-pick-picking-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-11.png?width=323&height=643&name=wave-pick-picking-11.png)

- Once all lines have been picked, select Continue to Pack. This will take you to the pack screen and step.

![wave-pick-picking-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-12.png?width=316&height=632&name=wave-pick-picking-12.png)

Pack screen:

![wave-pick-picking-13](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-pick-picking-13.png?width=290&height=587&name=wave-pick-picking-13.png)

---

<a id="wave-pick-list"></a>
## Wave Pick List

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/wave-pick-list_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- Refresh Wave Picks
- Wave Pick Type
- Search Wave Picks

### Purpose

The Wave Pick List displays all Wave Picks for the current warehouse (see [Switch Warehouse](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Switch+Warehouse) for information on how to change the current warehouse)**.**

The information displayed includes:

- **Number of Sale Orders in Wave Pick & Type Indicated by Colour**
- **Wave Pick Name**
- **Customer Name or Run Sheet Name**
- **Wave Pick Id**
- **Percentage Completed (Packing Progress): A** packing progress circle will be displayed, showing the packing progress of the Wave Pick's products as a percentage of the overall number of products to pack.

### Refresh Wave Picks

There are two ways to refresh the Wave Pick List from the server. Either swipe down while viewing the list (iOS only) or use the **Refresh Wave Picks** button in the menu.

### Wave Pick Type

There are three types of [Wave Picks](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Creating+a+Wave+Pick+for+a+single+Customer): Bulk, Split and Combination.

When viewing the Wave Pick list the Wave Pick's Process type is displayed in a red box.

As Split Picks separate Picking and Packing, they contain an extra progress circle to track each.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-28-2025-05-33-44-3491-AM.jpeg?width=339&height=682&name=image-png-Nov-28-2025-05-33-44-3491-AM.jpeg)

Sear

ch Wave Picks

To search the Wave Picks List, tap onto the search bar on top of the list and start typing your search terms such as the Customer or Run Sheet name. The list will be immediately filtered to fit your search text and matching text within the list rows will be highlighted in red.

![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-28-2025-05-34-19-4160-AM.jpeg?width=320&height=645&name=image-png-Nov-28-2025-05-34-19-4160-AM.jpeg)
 **Search Criteria**

Currently, the list search allows you to search for matching text in:

- Wave Pick Name
- Wave Pick Customer Name
- Wave Pick Run Sheet Name
- Wave Pick Id

The list view search for Wave Picks functions very much in the same way that it does for Consignments, so please refer to this article for more details: [List View Search](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/List+View+Search)

**Separate by Customer Name**

Should you prefer the Wave Pick List to be separated into sections by customer name, simply select the Filter button in the top navigation bar and activate the "Split by customer names" option and select the "Apply" button to confirm your selection. You will notice the list has now been updated to reflect your setting.

**After Splitting by Customer Name**
![](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/image-png-Nov-28-2025-05-36-04-8937-AM.jpeg?width=320&height=641&name=image-png-Nov-28-2025-05-36-04-8937-AM.jpeg)

---

<a id="wave-picking-by-location"></a>
## Wave Picking By Location

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/wave-picking-by-location_

### Table of Contents

- Navigation to wave picking by location
- Android
- iOS
- Location sorting
- Printing product labels by location
- Location pick sheet
- Product quantity confirmation view
- Completing a product pick
- Printing product labels
- Complete picking & leaving the location pick sheet view

### Navigation to wave picking by location

On selecting the

Locations

tab after choosing to enter the

Pick

mode for a Wave Pick, required product numbers will be displayed aggregated by Warehouse Location.

### Android

![wave-picking-by-location-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-0.png?width=213&height=401&name=wave-picking-by-location-0.png)

### iOS

![wave-picking-by-location-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-1.png?width=231&height=411&name=wave-picking-by-location-1.png)

### Location Sorting

Warehouse locations within the **Locations**tab can be sorted

- Alphabetically: **A-Z**
- **By Pick Efficiency**
- **By Pick Order**

**Android**![wave-picking-by-location-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-2.png?width=245&height=461&name=wave-picking-by-location-2.png)

**iOS**

![wave-picking-by-location-3](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-3.png?width=251&height=446&name=wave-picking-by-location-3.png)
Simply select the filter button from the top bar, choose the preferred sorting method and select **Apply**.

### Printing product labels by location

To print all product labels associated with a location, simply swipe on the desired location in the location list and select  **Print Labels.

Android**

![wave-picking-by-location-4](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-4.png?width=272&height=513&name=wave-picking-by-location-4.png)

**iOS**

### ![wave-picking-by-location-5](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-5.png?width=277&height=492&name=wave-picking-by-location-5.png)

Location pick sheet

On either manually selecting a location from the list, or by scanning a barcode associated with a unique location in the list, a Location Pick Sheet will be displayed.

**Android**

![wave-picking-by-location-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-6.png?width=276&height=519&name=wave-picking-by-location-6.png)

**iOS**

**![wave-picking-by-location-7](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-7.png?width=272&height=484&name=wave-picking-by-location-7.png)**

The Location Pick Sheet lists all the Wave Pick products to be picked from the selected location. Picked quantities for these products can either be confirmed by selecting them from the list and confirming their quantity in the Product Quantity Confirmation View (see below), or by scanning a related Product UOM barcode.

Products are aggregated by product type, expiry date and custom fields.

### Product Quantity Confirmation View

The Pack Confirmation View will either be automatically opened on scanning/entering a product barcode while in the Location Pick Sheet or is opened after tapping on a product in the list.

Within the Pack Confirmation View relevant information about the product is being displayed: warehouse location, product name, expiry date, base unit of measure and custom fields. Custom fields are initially hidden but can be expanded by tapping on the "Show Custom Fields" text.

For more information on how the quantity confirmation view displays information, check out this article: [Scan Picking Process - Pack Confirmation View](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Scan+Picking+Process#ScanPickingProcess-PackConfirmationView)[https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Scan+Picking+Process#ScanPickingProcess-PackConfirmationView](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Scan+Picking+Process#ScanPickingProcess-PackConfirmationView)

**Android**

![wave-picking-by-location-8](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-8.png?width=286&height=538&name=wave-picking-by-location-8.png)

**iOS**

![wave-picking-by-location-9](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-9.png?width=294&height=523&name=wave-picking-by-location-9.png)
On selecting **Save**, the pick quantity information will be stored without attempting to complete the pick for this product. The user can return to this product at a later stage, pick more products and complete the product pick.

On selecting **Complete**, the pick quantity information will be stored and the product pick completed, meaning no future alterations to the pick quantity can be made.

**Android**

**Saved Quantity**

![wave-picking-by-location-10](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-10.png?width=251&height=472&name=wave-picking-by-location-10.png)

**Completed Product**

![wave-picking-by-location-11](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-11.png?width=265&height=499&name=wave-picking-by-location-11.png)
**iOS**

**Saved Quantity**![wave-picking-by-location-12](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-12.png?width=289&height=514&name=wave-picking-by-location-12.png)
**Completed Product**

![wave-picking-by-location-13](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-13.png?width=282&height=502&name=wave-picking-by-location-13.png)

### Completing a Product Pick

If a product pick is not completed in the Product Confirmation View, it can be completed from the Location Pick Sheet itself, by swiping on the desired product in the list and selecting **Complete**. A requirement for this option to be available is that the correct quantity of the product has previously been saved as picked.

**iOS**

**Required Quantity Picked**

![wave-picking-by-location-14](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-14.png?width=271&height=482&name=wave-picking-by-location-14.png)
**Complete Product Pick**

![wave-picking-by-location-15](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-15.png?width=284&height=505&name=wave-picking-by-location-15.png)
**Completed Product Pick**

![wave-picking-by-location-16](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-16.png?width=300&height=533&name=wave-picking-by-location-16.png)

**Android**

**Required Quantity Picked**

![wave-picking-by-location-17](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-17.png?width=321&height=605&name=wave-picking-by-location-17.png)

**Complete Product Pick**

![wave-picking-by-location-18](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-18.png?width=295&height=559&name=wave-picking-by-location-18.png)

**Completed Product Pick**

![wave-picking-by-location-19](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-19.png?width=330&height=621&name=wave-picking-by-location-19.png)

### Printing Product Labels

To print all product labels for a product in the location pick sheet, simply swipe on the desired product in the list and select **Print Labels.

Android**

![wave-picking-by-location-20](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-20.png?width=250&height=472&name=wave-picking-by-location-20.png)
**iOS**

![wave-picking-by-location-21](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-21.png?width=278&height=494&name=wave-picking-by-location-21.png)

### Complete Picking & Leaving the Location Pick Sheet View

Once all products on the Location Pick Sheet have been completed, the Continue to Pack button will take the user to the pack process.

**Android**

![wave-picking-by-location-22](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-22.png?width=302&height=569&name=wave-picking-by-location-22.png)

**iOS**

![wave-picking-by-location-23](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-location-23.png?width=293&height=522&name=wave-picking-by-location-23.png)

On selecting **Complete**, the pick quantity information will be stored and the product pick completed, meaning no future alterations to the pick quantity can be made.

Complete Picking & Leaving the Products Pick Sheet View

Once all products on the Pick Sheet have been completed, the Continue to Pack button will take the user to the pack process.

**Android**

![wave-picking-by-product-6](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/wave-picking-by-product-6.png?width=305&height=574&name=wave-picking-by-product-6.png)

# Transport Management

<a id="containers"></a>
## Containers

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/containers_

### Table of Contents

- What is a Container?
- Container Custom Fields
- How to create a Container?

### What is a Container?

A container in CartonCloud is a container that is being received into your warehouse.

### Container Custom Fields

Before creating a Container it is suggested you create the required Custom Fields. Custom Fields allow you to record custom information against the Container. Two custom fields that you should create are Container Arrival Date and Container Departure Date. For steps on how to create a Custom Field click here -

Introduction to Custom Field

.

### How to create a Container?

- Type Containers into the Search for anything bar.
- Click +Add .

![containers-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/containers-0.png?width=670&height=362&name=containers-0.png)

- Enter the Container Arrival Date, Container Departure Date and related Shipment reference (if the shipment has been created/if you have a related shipment).

![containers-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/containers-1.png?width=670&height=252&name=containers-1.png)

- Once the Container has been created you can view the container details from the View Container screen. From here, you can update the status.

![containers-2](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/containers-2.png?width=670&height=160&name=containers-2.png)

- The different statuses the container can move through include: Not yet Received, Received, Unloaded, Pickup Booked, Picked Up.

---

<a id="setting-up-vehicles-in-cartoncloud"></a>
## Setting up Vehicles in CartonCloud

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/setting-up-vehicles-in-cartoncloud_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to enter Vehicles into CartonCloud

### Purpose

By entering your fleet's vehicles into CartonCloud, you can then track important information relating to those vehicles using Custom Fields (which you would have set up during the Custom Field's article).

CartonCloud also allows you to allocate Run Sheets to Vehicles and therefore track their performance by checking the revenue generated by an individual Vehicle. For example, by using Vehicles within CartonCloud they can then be displayed as a field on [Consignment Reports](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Consignment+Report).

Entering your fleet's vehicles into CartonCloud is entirely optional, however, we do recommend it for the benefits stated above.

### How to enter Vehicles into CartonCloud

To add a vehicle into CartonCloud begin by navigating to the  **Vehicles** page, the easiest way to do this is to type "Vehicles" into the  **Search for Anything** bar, then select when prompted.

![setting-up-vehicles-in-cartoncloud-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-up-vehicles-in-cartoncloud-0.png?width=670&height=293&name=setting-up-vehicles-in-cartoncloud-0.png)

Here you can see a list of Vehicles we have already set up (including Custom Fields to record their Rego number, minimum height clearance and rego expiry) in our demo tenancy, but your page will be empty for now until we begin entering Vehicles.

Next, click **+Add Vehicle** and you will be presented with a page of fields to fill out. At a minimum, these fields will be the Name of the Vehicle and which Warehouses it operates from. However, using Custom Fields it is also possible to create extra fields that you can then use to capture information against the vehicle. In the example provided you will notice we have created Custom Fields for the Registration Number and if the vehicle has a Tail Lift platform or not plus others. The fields you create is entirely up to your operational requirements and some users may not to utilise these at all.

![setting-up-vehicles-in-cartoncloud-1](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/setting-up-vehicles-in-cartoncloud-1.png?width=670&height=503&name=setting-up-vehicles-in-cartoncloud-1.png)
Once you have completed the fields select **Save** and the Vehicle will be created. A **Success!** banner will display on the browser to indicate this.

And that's it! Now when you allocate Consignments you will be able to select the Vehicle the consignment is being allocated to. You will also be able to reference the information entered against the vehicle when delivery with specific requirements arises. For example, a delivery to a parking lot with a height restriction, or to a shop front without a forklift.

For reporting purposes, the "Bulk Export Consignments" report will show which Vehicle was used for a Consignment, making it easy analyse how your fleet is performing.

Next Step: [Setting up CartonCloud to automatically allocate Consignments](https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/setting-up-cartoncloud-to-automatically-allocate-consignments)

---

# Transport Management > Allocations

<a id="allocating-consignments-to-drivers"></a>
## Allocating Consignments to Drivers

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/allocating-consignments-to-drivers_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- What are Run Sheets in CartonCloud
- Important information about allocating

### Purpose

Learning to use CartonCloud to efficiently allocate consignments within your transport operation is an important part of the TMS setup. Becoming comfortable with this area of the system can save operators hours of the day to focus on other tasks.

The articles within this section aim to make you familiar with the options CartonCloud provides for consignment allocation.

### What are Run Sheets in CartonCloud?

When consignments are allocated to drivers CartonCloud will automatically produce a new Run Sheet (unless you allocate to an already existing Run Sheet).

This Run Sheet is a list of the consignments going with a single driver. This means that the deliveries for a day going to the Delivery Run "Gold Coast" might be split up across multiple Run Sheets (East, North, West), as each Run sheet is allocated to a different driver.

To put it briefly, a run sheet is a list of jobs that are going on one driver for that day, and typically they're broken into different areas.

This could also mean for example that a driver could end up with multiple run sheets for a single day. For example, imagine a driver who services hospital deliveries in the morning using the run sheet "Gold Coast Hospital Run" and then services supplier collections in the afternoon, using a different run sheet which might be called "Gold Coast Supplier Collections".

### Important information about allocating

- To allow a driver to see a consignment and get an electronic POD - all that's required is the consignment allocated to that drivers name.
- Drivers are created through the Creating New Users process and must be marked as a Driver to be allocated to and for Mobile App access.
- Physically allocating a consignment to a driver can be done from a number of places. The following videos and articles will cover these and suggest what will work best for you based on your operation.

Next Step: [Checklist before Allocating](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Checklist+before+Allocating)

---

<a id="allocating-from-the-bulk-allocation-screen"></a>
## Allocating from the Bulk Allocation screen

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/allocating-from-the-bulk-allocation-screen_

### Table of Contents

- Purpose
- How to allocate in bulk
- Customising the bulk allocation screen

### Purpose

If your operation uses a large number of Delivery Runs, you may find the traditional way of allocating (going into each Delivery Run) time consuming and restrictive. It doesn't provide any way of seeing consignments outside of the current run you're allocating which can mean you miss valuable run combinations (mixing jobs from different runs to maximize efficiency).

Bulk Allocation addresses this by allowing you to simultaneously allocate multiple Delivery Runs by letting you search across all consignments due to go out on a certain day.

### How to allocate in bulk

**📹 Video:** https://www.youtube.com/embed/_uk1pdWnUy0

### Customising the Bulk Allocation Screen

**Optionally** the bulk allocation screen can be customised to your liking. Namely, you can:

- Enable/Disable the Map View for Bulk Allocation
- Change the Interface between Simple/Advanced Mode

For details on how to do this, see the article: [Customising the Bulk Allocation Screen](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Customising+the+Bulk+Allocation+Screen)

Next Step: [Optimising the delivery order of Consignments](https://help.cartoncloud.com/display/KB2/Optimising+the+delivery+order+of+Consignments)

---

<a id="automatically-allocate-consignments-to-drivers-from-delivery-runs"></a>
## Automatically allocate Consignments to Drivers from Delivery Runs

_Source: https://help.cartoncloud.com/knowledge/automatically-allocate-consignments-to-drivers-from-delivery-runs_

### How to steps

In order to automatically allocate consignments to drivers

- Select Contacts > Users .
- Select the User you wish to edit.
- Select Edit Delivery Runs at the bottom of the page.
- Tick the Delivery Runs you wish to link to the Driver.

![automatically-allocate-consignments-to-drivers-from-delivery-runs-0](https://help.cartoncloud.com/hs-fs/hubfs/automatically-allocate-consignments-to-drivers-from-delivery-runs-0.png?width=670&height=352&name=automatically-allocate-consignments-to-drivers-from-delivery-runs-0.png)

- Scroll down and select Save .
